english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ bɔtɛyi boǝ utidi ɔflɔɔ, bɔlɛ sikpi bote lesa nǝ betidi luuyifo, itsyise mǝmblɛ áfo bánɔ bǝ Yohanes eyifo Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: “Útǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Mǝmbǝ busu.’ Fɛɛ kamaa, ɔ́tsyi wǝ nsusu ɔ́wuninsǝ nya úsu.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: “Anto ǝmǝ ésu wǝ ubi nuǝfǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nko ke. Wǝ átɔnɔ nkǝ, ‘Yo o-o, nto, muusu.’ Fɛɛ wénsu.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: “Mǝ inuǝ bamǝ eto ntii, mǝ owoe díyifo lǝ mǝ anto eto lelabi ǝsuǝ?” Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Uninǝle ǝmǝ.” Nyaso Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi kakpasǝ minkǝ beyu ku basɔnɔdi lɛ mi bofe kaaka mi Onanto eto sekakedikɔ budufǝ.

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Itsyise Ntuflǝsǝtidi Yohanes ǝbǝ mi ɔflɔ nkǝ wǝǝbǝ ote mi kusu sɛɛ ko biɔyɛ, fɛɛ bíamfo wǝ bɛnɔ. Fɛɛ beyu ku basɔnɔdi bomu áfo wǝ bánɔ. Nya se bínyǝ nyamfo bítsyǝ lekpo tsyaa, bíamfloma mi nsusu biǝ biafo wǝ bɛnɔ.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Se lefe léyo bǝ balɛ eyibibi nyamǝǝ, ɔkuɛnsate ǝmfo ákpe wǝ ndomufɔ nkǝ besu bafo wǝ kedikɔ baboko wǝ.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: “Ɔkuɛmfo eto beyifoyifo bamfo ǝmuǝ wǝ ndomufɔ bamfo nya báblɛ ɔni, bálo ɔni, nya bákpɔ ɔni tsya aba.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, osate ǝmǝ esi ótsyese ndomufɔ bamba ba nso mba kɔ utu. Nya beyifoyifo bamǝ disi béyifo mǝ tsya nko ke.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Kalosǝkɔɔ, óla wǝ kosate eto ubi ótsyese mǝ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mfo nnɔ miǝ boobu mɔ ubi babe.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Nyaso bǝmuǝ wǝ nya báto wǝ bakpe lǝ ɔkpɔ ǝmǝ eto kama, nya bálo wǝ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔmɔɔ, lǝ eyibibi eto ɔkuɛnsate ǝmǝ ǝbǝǝ, be wuuyifo beyifoyifo bamǝ?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Woolo betidi bua bamfó kaminsǝ mǝnyǝmǝnyǝ, ufi eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ okpe beyifoyifo bamba ba luutǝ wǝ, wǝ kutsyǝ lǝ balɛ eyibibi nyamǝ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bíankla tɔ lesa nǝ nkpe lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Leba nǝ mba laato ayo lɛni bǝ dimbǝ kɔnɔɔ, ni bomu díbǝ lefiani nǝ nso bofe lǝ afɛfɛ kenke nti. Bonamute ni nwǝ díyifo nyamfo. Sitintii, iyifo lesa nǝ laanɔ bobe tinti!’ ”

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: Nya Yesu disi ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Nyaso nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ boofo Onanto eto sekakedikɔ lǝ mi ani befi bǝtǝ betidi ba lɔɔnwɔ ebibi nnya lɛlɛkɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: Nya utsyuǝ saa ke lɔɔtsyinini udu ɔnɔ lǝ leba nǝ báni mfó ǝsuǝǝ, wuufǝsǝ blublublu. Nya lǝ ibǝ bǝ leba nǝmǝ tsya asɔ utsyuǝ lǝ ǝsuǝǝ, dioba wǝ dikuǝ wǝ blublublu.”

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: Nyaso bála bǝ bǝmuǝ wǝ. Fɛɛ báté sitiditu siamǝ sikpi, itsyise mǝmblɛ kenke ébu Yesu fe Onanto eto Unyɛlǝkǝtidi.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lélosǝ nkǝ, “Onanto ákpadi betidi kpǝ, fɛɛ esiani ete úlǝkǝ lǝ mǝ nti.” Yesu Édi Etiki Itsyi Lǝ Umǝnkofofo Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ ésu bákpe disi lǝ kase bɔɔyɛ bǝmuǝ Yesu ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ eto etikididi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: Bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Oteasa, Moses átɛyi bo nkǝ, lǝ osani utsyuǝ ǝkpǝ nya óyani wǝ usiǝ utsyǝ nya wonko wǝ dímanle bebii, elebɛ osani ǝmǝ eto ɔyimisani fi ɔkɔsiǝ ǝmǝ ufi. Lǝ wonko wǝ ale bebii, boofi bebi bamǝ fe osani ǝmǝ díkpe eto bebi.

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Yesu esi óté mǝ asa ɛyɛ lǝ akpá ǝsuǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “Onanto eto sekakedikɔ te fe mmǝ. Lefe lɔnii, Oka utsyuǝ édi kusiǝkpakpa eto diyisɛɛ útǝ wǝ ubisaibi.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: Óla wǝ beyifoyifo ókpe nkǝ besu bakpo betidi ba ókla utsyǝ nkǝ bafe bǝbǝ diyisɛɛ nǝmǝ kedikɔ. Fɛɛ bánla bǝ bǝǝbǝ.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: “Nyaso óla wǝ beyifoyifo bamba ókpe nkǝ batɛyi betidi ba ókla utsyǝ nkǝ, ‘Lesalidife nǝmǝ eboyo! Bálo mɔ banantsyui ku banatsyui eto akpɔ nnya nkpe ayinkua bufi bɔlɛ mi kama bobe. Nya búkalɔ lesa saa bútsyǝ. Nyaso bibǝ kusiǝ komǝ kakpakɔ!’

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ mbamǝ bákla eto kuutsyuǝ saa mémbu wǝ úbǝ. Bomu bédu bésu nlebe bamba bo bála. Ɔni édu úsu wǝ ɔkuɛ, nya obamba tsya dídu usu wǝ bikǝkatekɔ.

english_verse: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
translation_verse: Nya babamba tsya dímuǝ beyifoyifo bamǝ nya báblɛ mǝ nya bálo mǝ.

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: Oka ǝmǝ ǝnyǝ ɔblɔ tinti, nya ótsyese wǝ bamamanyǝ nya bésu bálo bewudifɔ bamǝ kenke nya bátonsǝ mǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: “Nyaso ókpadi wǝ beyifoyifo nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Mɔ ubi eto kusiǝkpakpa eto diyisɛɛ didi nǝmǝ eto lefe éboyo. Fɛɛ betidi ba mínkla miǝ bǝbǝ diyi nǝmǝ kedikɔ mǝnńyǝ itǝ diyisɛɛ nǝmǝ.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Nyaso mɔmɔɔ, bisu umǝ eto kesunte lǝ bekpadi utsyuǝ saa wǝ ke biunyǝ biǝ bǝbǝ diyisɛɛ nǝmǝ kedikɔ mfo.’

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: “Nya se Oka ǝmǝ díbǝ diyo nǝmǝ nkǝ wǝǝbǝ obe betidi ba díbǝ mfóo, únyǝ osani wǝ dímankpe awu nnya baakpe kesu kusiǝkakpakɔ.

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Nyaso tɛyi bo lesa nǝ febu. Bo kɔfi ǝtǝ bo kusu nkǝ bofó umǝnko butǝ Romafɔ eto Okankplɛ lee, yǝntǝ kusu nko?”

english_verse: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
translation_verse: Nko ibǝ ɔyimisani nuǝfǝ ku utsyǝfǝ isu ese okuansele mǝmblɛ dífi usio ǝmǝ bafia nya mǝ nti kuutsyuǝ ku usio ǝmǝ mánle fɛ békpǝ.

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Nyaso bátsyese mǝ bakasebi bǝtsyuǝ ku betidi ba ntekeko Herodes eto ditsyukpa kama lǝ Yesu ɔflɔ. Nya se bésuu, bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Oteasa, buyi boǝ eyifo ɔnukualɛtidi nya faate Onanto bofokanɔ eto asa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ sitinti, itsyise aambe utidi eto ǝnǝmi keyifo lesa saa. Nya ememfi kukǝtsyuǝ lǝ betidi eto etikididi ǝsuǝ tsya.

english_verse: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
translation_verse: Bete mɛ atabi nnya biefi kafo umǝnko ǝmǝ lǝ mbe.” Béfi atabi nyamǝ báboko wǝ.

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Okankplɛ Kaisare eto ale ini.” Nyaso Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bifi lesa nǝ ni Kaisare eto ale bitǝ Kaisare. Lǝ bífi lesa nǝ ni Onanto eto léle bitǝ Onanto.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Se bánɔ nyamfo, íyifo mǝ yanii nya bédu wǝ lǝ ɔflɔ. Yesu Áte Nkǝ Bekpǝnkpǝ Si Bootaka.

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Lǝ kalosǝkɔɔ, usio ǝmǝ tsya ǝbǝ úkpǝ.

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: Moses eto kɔfi komǝ ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto asatete kenke kpǝsǝ lǝ afi ǝnuǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝ.” Owoe Ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ?

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Diyi nǝmfo kee, Sadukifɔ ba laatɛyi bǝ bekpǝnkpǝ ensiembotaka betsyi ɔkɔmǝǝ, ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ ku etiki ǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu ǝtǝ mǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Bénya kusu tinti. Itsyise biannɔ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue komǝ kasɔ, nya binyi kulesa lǝ Onanto eto ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
translation_verse: Lǝ bekpǝnkpǝ esi bataka bǝbǝ nkpǝǝ, bootsyǝ fe Onanto eto batɔnkpe ba nkpe lǝ Onanto eto kato, nya bembufi bǝsiǝ nye bǝsiǝ besǝ tsya.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
translation_verse: Nya bíankla asa nnya Onanto lɛtɛyi mi itsyi lǝ bekpǝnkpǝ eto botaka ketsyi ɔkɔmǝ ǝsuǝ? Ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: ‘Mɔ ni Abraham eto Onanto ku Isak eto Onanto ku Yakob eto Onanto!’ Uyifo mba nkpe nkpǝ eto Onanto inni mba díkekpǝ.”

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Se sitiditu siamǝ lɛnɔ asa yamfoo, íyifo mǝ yanii, lǝ wǝ asatete ǝsuǝ. Yesu Édi Etiki Lǝ Kɔfi Ko Nso Bofe Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Se Farisifɔ lɛnɔ bǝ Yesu asɔ Sadukifɔ kasɔɔ, bésu bátsya.

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: “Oteasa, nte kɔfi ko nso bofe lǝ afi nyamǝ kenke nti?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Kɔfi komfo ni kɔkplɛ kenke ko nso bofe tsya nɛ.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Kunuǝfǝ ko lefe lele ko ni, ‘La fǝ ɔyimitsyuǝ fe kase faala fǝ ǝsuǝ.’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Se Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ létsyaa, Yesu átɔ mǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ‘Bonamute átɛyi mɔ Onamute nkǝ, “Siǝ mfo lǝ mɔ kodiani isu ese lefe nǝ muutǝ efi fǝ akpa ǝtǝkǝ fǝ balo lǝ disinti.” ’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Ntsyǝ ni mi nsusu lǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ Ǝsuǝ? Owoe eto ubi uni?” Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Oka David eto ubi uni.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ nko inii, ntsyǝ fɛ Oka David ɛyɛ lǝ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝǝ, lékpadi wǝ nkǝ Onamute. Oka David átɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Oka David ákpo Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkǝ Onamutee, ntsyǝ ibǝ bǝ Nwǝ ǝmǝ esi uyifo Oka David eto ubi?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Mǝ nti eto kuutsyuǝ mámfo Yesu mbuayɛ butǝ. Nya idu dinǝmfo entɔɔ, mǝ kuutsyuǝ ménsifo wǝ ditiki saa bɔtɔ.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ laabesǝ ǝsuǝ katoo, boobokosǝ wǝ kasɔ. Nya nwǝ laabokosǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔɔ, boobesǝ wǝ kato. Boonǝ Ǝnǝmi Ǝnǝmi Eto Betidi Koto Kekleke.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya esoo, fǝ Disumuyo ebubu kǝtsyǝ fufu.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Nwǝ ni ɔkplɛnyǝ lǝ mi nti yifo nwǝ ni mi uyifoyifo.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem, fǝ lélo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi nya fákpɔ Onanto eto batɔnkpe ba ótsyese aba! Nla sesia sesia miǝ maasɔ fǝ betidi lebo lǝ mɔ akɔ, fe kase ukusɛsiǝle laakpa wǝ bebi kakpe lǝ wǝ mbamba kalɔ, fɛɛ biǝntǝ kusu nko.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Yesu édi sitiditu siamǝ ku wǝ bakasebi etiki.

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ kpe ǝsuǝale, bǝ baté Moses eto kɔfi komǝ.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Nyaso ele bǝ biyifo lǝ lesa nǝ ke batɛyi mi ǝsuǝ kukɔnɔ. Fɛɛ biǝntǝkǝko mǝ eyifosa kama itsyise bǝǝnsiǝ nkpǝ lǝ asa nnya baate ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: Baakle bikǝ bia nkpe kalende ku nkpe ale ku butsyi ketsyisǝ betidi, tsya fɛɛ bembokpe fǝ lɛ kɔni etsyi bikǝ biamǝ.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: “Beeyifo lesa saa bǝ lǝ betidi lǝ bǝnyǝ mǝ. Be Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto etiki nnya báwɔni bákpe lǝ mǝ nto ku anii, be kase báwɔni nya ɛmɔɔ, ku kase bankpimi nnyaa! Be kase tsya aboyo nnya mfaka lǝ mǝ awu nnya bankpe eto nyɛ ǝsuǝ dikulǝǝ!

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Baala nkplɛkɔ sɛɛ busiǝ lǝ eyi kedikɔ ku nsiǝkɔ bo bebu bǝtsyǝ bakplɛnyǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ.

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Baala bǝ betidi kǝsiǝsǝ mǝ kǝbǝ kasɔ ku dibu lǝ asi bakpadi mǝ bǝ Oteasa.

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: “Yanle bǝ bakpadi mi bǝ Oteasa itsyise mimblɛɛ, bayimi bini lǝ bǝtsyuǝ nya Oteasa ɔni kplo nkpe mi.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Nya yanle bǝ bekpadi kuutsyuǝ saa lǝ kasɔ mfo biǝ mi Anto, itsyise Anto ɔni hã nkpe mi lǝ kato.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Biǝntǝ bakpadi mi Ɔtɔnkotɔnko, itsyisee Ɔtɔnkotɔnko ɔni hã ete nkpe bo, wǝ ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ! Mi ni ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi. Biesu bakɔbǝsiǝ ɔflɔ biǝ biawunsǝko mǝ, fɛɛ biawɛ mǝ kalɛ mǝ lesa saa kafo. Nya biato kala kɛntsyɛ bimba. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, mi kotonǝnǝ ebɔkɛ ale eso!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ! Ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi bini! Biayila lekpo akpa akpa ku biayɛ kafe lǝ nsɔ nsɔ ǝsuǝ lǝ bilǝkǝ utidi ɔni eto okla bitǝ Onanto. Nya lǝ befo utsyuǝ buyifo nkoo, biatǝ woabia nnya lǝǝtǝ bǝ udufǝ lǝ ditefɛnku nǝme kǝmiǝ enso mi kosate kulaa tsya!

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi, bǝnǝmba ba ni batɔnkotɔnko! Biatɛyi biǝ, ‘Lǝ utsyuǝ akpadi Disumuyo eto diyee, yeambiasǝ kulesa. Fɛɛ lǝ okpadi sika sia nte lǝ Disumuyo mfóo, nnya akle wǝ bǝ uyifo lǝ akpadi nyamǝ ǝsuǝ kokooko.’

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Bǝnǝmba eto bamumu! Nte nnya nso bofe, sika siamǝ lee, Disumuyo ka dítǝ sika siamǝ díyifo kɛnkɛ?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Biate tsya biǝ, ‘Lǝ utsyuǝ ataninko Disumuyo eto lebaa, kataninko kamǝ mankle wǝ, fɛɛ lǝ otaninko ǝtǝsa nnya ntǝkǝ lǝ Disumuyo eto leba nǝmǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nnya akle wǝ.’

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Nya ntsyǝ béba ǝnǝmi mmǝ! Nte nnya nso bofe, ǝtǝsa nnyamǝ lee, Disumuyo eto leba nǝ dítǝ ǝtǝsa nnyamǝ díyifo kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ óka ntamu okpadi Disumuyo Kplɛɛ, ɔlɛ mu boka kakpadi Disumuyo ku Onanto we nsi lǝ mfó tsya.

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ utsyuǝ ákpadi katoo, ɔlɛ Onanto eto lekankpomɛ ku nwǝ lǝǝsiǝ lǝ ni bokpadi.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: “Mǝǝtǝ mi mbɔbɔɛ, mi afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ! Mi ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi! Biefi kayoma eto bekolenkǝ bia laanyi fififi bia biakpe eto lekpafosile kǝtǝ Onanto, fɛɛ bɛni nya bienyifo lǝ kɔfi eto asa tete nnya nso bofe ǝsuǝ, nnya ni bɔyɛ lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ, kaminsǝnyǝnyǝ, ku ɔnukualɛdidi. Nyamfo ele bǝ biyifo bifi bekpe lǝ afɛfɛ nyamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Mi batɔnkotɔnko ba ni bǝnǝmba! Biayɛ ekubi kǝlǝkǝ lǝ nte ǝsuǝ, fɛɛ asa nnya biamini amɔ eso ɔtɔmini ku lekoko.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ! Ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi! Biǝflǝ mi akɔbe ku kakpɛ eto kama, fɛɛ nnya kafoo eyi ku asa bua fe kase mi tsya lebia nya biyi ku asa nnya béfo lǝ ǝsuǝale ku lǝ mɔ hã ete eto lɛlɛkɛsǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Fǝ Farisinyǝ unǝmba, tsyiko fǝ lɛkɔbe buflǝ lǝ kafo, ete ni kama tsya ebole kɛnkɛnkɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ! Ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi bini! Bite fe edi nnya bákua bǝńyǝnsǝ laanɔ bobe. Fɛɛ nnya kǝmiǝ eyi ku bakɔ bubu ku ekubi biabia.

english_verse: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
translation_verse: Nko ke bele betidi ba lɛlɛkɛ lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmi, fɛɛ biyi ku kawɛ ku ditsyǝbi bua. Yesu Átɛyi Afi Eto Bateasa Ku Farisifɔ Eto Kotonǝnǝ Utsyǝ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi afi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ! Ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi! Bieku edi sɛɛ kǝtǝ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba dísiǝ nkpǝ sɛɛ nya bialɔ nnya kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: Nya biatɛyi biǝ, ‘Lǝ nnya ete busiǝ nkpǝ lǝ lefe nǝ bo banamǝ dísiǝǝ, eni bienyifo lesa nǝ béyifo, balo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi.’

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Nyaso befo nnya sitinti biǝ, mi eyifosa afo nnya ekpe bǝ biyifo mba lélo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto befluflu.

english_verse: So keep on doing everything they did.
translation_verse: Nyaso bisukosǝ lǝ belosǝ lesamǝ mi banamǝ dítsyǝ kasɔ.

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: “Mi betidi bua, biyifo fe bawa eto bebi eto bebi! Bibu biǝ biofo butsyetsyi kedu lǝ Onanto eto kotonǝnǝ lǝ ditefɛnku manlo ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ kɔtɔ komfó kenke tǝkǝ mmi mba nsi nkpǝ mmi lǝ ǝsuǝ. Yesu Eto Lelabi Amɔ Itǝ Yerusalem.

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Idu mɔmɔ ɛntɔɔ, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi miǝ, biensi embunyǝ mɛ bio isu se lefe nǝ bɛtɛyi biǝ, ‘Onanto kpo nwǝ nlɛ bubǝ lǝ Bonamute eto diye ǝsuǝ atsyɔ.’ ”

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Se Yesu lɛbɔ lǝ Disumuyo mfo ɔntɔɔ, wǝ bakasebi ǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ bǝ baaté wǝ kase báto Disumuyo nǝmǝ ku ke ayo kenke.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bɛlɔ bebe nyamfo kukɔnɔ. Itsyise nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ booba ayo nyamfo kenke, bǝ lɛ leba lɔni embubu ditǝkǝ lǝ ni utsyuǝ ǝsuǝ. Boosensǝ nyamblɛ bewui.” Yesu Átɛyi Wǝ Bakasebi Lefe Nǝ Bulǝ Ku Kamatiti Duutu Mǝ.

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Betidi kpǝ ebubǝ ba luufi mɔ diye bǝbǝ batɛyi mi bǝnkǝ, ‘Mɔ ni Nwǝ Ǝmǝ Bákpo Ɔhile Bǝtsyǝ,’ bǝ lǝ bawɛ betidi kpǝ bakpe.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Biɔnɔ ama eto kudu ku ama eto etiki lǝ katsyontsyokɔ ku kaka lɛfi. Fɛɛ bíǝntǝ lǝ nyamfo lǝ ekpe mi sikpi. Ele bǝ asa nyamfo bǝ, fɛɛ yante bǝ kalosǝkɔ eboyo tɔ.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Se Yesu dísu unsi lǝ Nnuǝbi eto Eyi eto Kɔtini ǝsuǝǝ, wǝ bakasebi ǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ buɛɛ nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Tɛyi bo lefe nǝ kawunsiǝ eto kalosǝkɔ luubǝ ku lesa nǝ luubǝ nnya loote bǝ fǝ businkli kǝbǝ eto lefe eboyo.”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Nsɔ eto betidi ebɔkpɔ ama ku mǝ bǝtsyuǝ. Nya sekakedikɔ ebotaka sińyǝ lǝ se utsyuǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya kukpɛnsɛ luubǝ lǝ nlebe kpǝ. Nya nsɔ tsya luutinkǝ lǝ nlebe nlebe.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ bafonlɛ ku bebiambe ba nlɛ bebisambi bɔyimisǝ lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bebe kukɔnɔ biǝ kuutsyuǝ saa manwɛ mi okpe.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Nyamfo kenke ebuyifo fe kase kalelefo lǝǝtsyǝ usio kasɔ ku bunǝ.

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, boomuǝ mi befi mi bakpe lǝ ani bǝtǝ bǝ bate mi ohiã, nya boolo mi kulaa tsya. Kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke ebukisi mi lǝ mɔ disi eso.

english_verse: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
translation_verse: Betidi kpǝ ebonyansǝ mǝ bofokanɔ. Boofi mǝ bǝtsyuǝ bakpe lǝ ani bǝtǝ balo nya bookisi mǝ bayimi bǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Betidi kpǝ ebɔbɔ batɛyi bidia bǝnkǝ mǝ ni Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi nya bɔɔwɛ betidi kpǝ bakpe.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ Onanto lekpakpa biǝ mmi ditsyetsyife nǝmǝ mǝntǝkǝ lǝ lɛyɔ lefe nye Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: “Buinyǝ ekpinsa nyamǝ luubǝ ińyǝ lǝ kakɛnkɛkɔ kamǝ fe kase Onanto eto unyǝlǝkǝtidi Daniel lɛtɛyi útsyǝ. Yuuńyǝ lǝ kakɛkɛnkɔ kamǝ. Nya fǝ ke lékla kukue komfoo, nɔ nyamfo kasɔ wuee.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: “Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, mimba nkpe lǝ Yuda eto kasɔ, tsyetsyi besu atini ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Utidi wǝ nkpe lǝ ɔkuɛ mensi sinkli ubǝ oto nkǝ wǝǝbǝ ufi wǝ kafia.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Onanto ébose nnya uyi útsyǝ nkǝ wuulǝkǝ eyi nyamǝ ubu. Lenni nko esoo, eni utidi sa embubu usiǝ lǝ nkpǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ betidi ba Onanto díkǝlǝkǝ utsyǝ esoo, wuulǝkǝ eyi nyamǝ ubu.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Nko ke lǝ binyǝ asa nnya kenke mínkatɛyi míntɔnsǝǝ, bete biǝ kalosǝkɔ kamǝ eboyo kalo.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Utidi Eto Ubi eto bubǝ ebuyifo fe kase íbǝ lǝ Noa eto lefe.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Kuutsyuǝ saa enyi diyi nye lefe nǝmǝ kalosǝkɔ luubǝ. Onanto eto Batɔnkpe nye Ubi ǝmǝ kulaa tsya enyi. Anto hã nyi.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ lɛtɔ ntu eto buyi bomǝǝ, betidi ékedi, bǝkǝnǝ, basani ku besio ákakpa bǝtsyuǝ isu ese diyi fɛfɛ nǝ Noa dídufǝ lǝ kufue komǝ kafo.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bánte lesa nǝ nlɛ bubǝ isu ese lefe nǝ ɔsɔmi lɛni, ntu díyi bókpa mǝmblɛ kenke búdunko. Nko ke yuutsyǝ lǝ Utidi Eto Ubi eto dibǝfe tsya nɛ.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, basani inuǝ ebuńyǝ lǝ ɔkuɛ bekeyifo keyifo fɛ wookpa ɔni udunko nya uubu ɔni bǝtsyǝ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Besio inuǝ ebuńyǝ bǝkǝkuǝ antabi, fɛɛ Onanto ebutsyi ɔni udunko nya uubu ɔni utsyǝ.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: “Nyaso bitǝkǝ ǝnǝ, itsyise binnyi diyi nǝ mi Onamute luubǝ.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Lǝ oyosate áte dinǝ uyu luubǝ wǝ keyukɔ, eni wuutǝkǝ ǝnǝ ukase ni. Nya weembutǝ kusu nkǝ uyu ǝmǝ dufǝ, oba wǝ diyo uyu wǝ.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Nko ke ele bǝ fintsya muǝ ǝsuǝ ǝtsyǝ, itsyise Utidi Eto Ubi ǝmǝ ebubǝ lǝ lefe nǝ fannlɛ wǝ kusu bobe. Ele Bǝ Uyifoyifo Yifo Asa Lǝ Kusu Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ uyifoyifo bua unii, wɔɔtɛyi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, ‘Mɔ Onamute embubǝ mɔmɔ!’

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: “Owoe ni uyifoyifo sɛɛ wǝ ni obesibe? Wǝ ni uyifoyifo wǝ, wǝ Onamute dífi útsyǝ lǝ wǝ beyifotsyuǝ eto kɛnyɛ, nkǝ úkǝtǝ mǝ lesa lǝ lefe ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Nte disuǝyuǝ nǝ eto okle yuudi itǝ uyifoyifo ǝmfo lǝ wǝ Onamute ǝbǝ unyǝ wǝ ɔnlɛ buyifo lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Nlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ Onamute ǝmfo ebufi uyifoyifo ǝmfo utsyǝ lǝ wǝ bikǝ kenke eto kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: Nyaso wuutsyiko wǝ beyifoyifotsyuǝ bɔblɛ, nya ukedi, ukǝnǝ ku betekpe.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: Nya wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ ebubǝ lǝ diyi lɔni nǝ ɔnlɛ wǝ kusu bobe ku lefe nǝ unnyi.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Onamute ǝmǝ ebunǝ wǝ koto sitinti. Nya wuunǝ wǝ koto kekleke fe kase ińyǝ itǝ ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi seka! Mfó nya wuuwi keku wɔɔyimi animi!

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: Ɛnɔ ba leeninǝ étsyi kakandiɛ, fɛɛ béntsyi nnuǝbi lǝ asa ǝsuǝ báwunisǝ.

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: Nya babesibe bamǝ alɛɛ, mǝ étsyi nnuǝbi báwunsǝ lǝ mǝ asa ǝsuǝ ku kakandiɛ kamǝ.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: “Ntsyenti lefee, bánɔ kudu bǝnkǝ, ‘Nnyǝ okafɔse ǝmǝ nlɛ bubǝ! Betaka, bibǝ betsyako wǝ!’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: “Mfó nya mbitɛ bomǝ létaka nya bábesǝ mǝ kakandiɛ eto ute kato.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Nya mba leeninǝ lɛtɛyi babesibe bamǝ bǝ, ‘Bitǝ bo mi nnuǝbi bomǝ eto ntsyuǝ itsyise bo kakandiɛ lɛ bɔni.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: “Nya babesibe bamǝ lɛtɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Bo nnuǝbi emboyo itǝ bo ku mi, nyaso bisu mba laate ɔflɔ, lǝ bisu beya butsyuǝ bitǝ ǝsuǝ.’

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: “Nya okafɔse ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Kulefe, minnyi mi!’ ”

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lélosǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nyaso bimuǝ ǝsuǝ bitsyuǝ, itsyise binyi diyi nye lefe nǝ muubǝ. Ele Bǝ Bɔlɛ Asa Nnya Onanto Dífi Ókpe Bo Lǝ Ani Kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe kɛntsye eto kamaa, beyifoyifo bamfo eto Onamute ǝmǝ ésinkli úbǝ nya údufǝ lǝ nkunta ku mǝ.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: Nyalɛ beso fémemfi mɔ atabi ésuko atabi kǝtsyǝkɔ bǝ lǝ mbǝǝ, lǝ nnyǝ nnya kutǝkǝ?’

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: “Lefe nǝmfóo, Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ebutsyǝ fe mmǝ. Lefe lɔnii, Osani utsyuǝ akɛ, wǝ léla nkǝ lǝ usu kusu kutsyuǝ nyaso ókpadi wǝ beyifoyifo nya úfi wǝ bikǝ ókpe mǝ lǝ ani.

english_verse: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
translation_verse: Uyifoyifo wǝ léfo atabi eto akpi anɔɔ, ésu ɔ́tsyi ɔ́wuninsǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ nya únyǝ akpi anɔ útǝkǝsǝ.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Alɔ éyifo, uyifoyifo sɛɛ. Éyifo asa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ nya fálɛ asa sɛkɛ ola, nyaso muufi fǝ ntsyǝ lǝ asa kpǝ eto kɛnyɛ. Dufǝ ǝbǝ lǝ ǝbǝ ǝnyǝnko mɛ disuǝyuǝ!’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Uyifoyifo sɛɛ, éyifo lesa saa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ tinti! Se fálɛ asa sɛkɛ ola lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ esoo, muufi fǝ ntsyǝ lǝ kpǝ eto kɛnyɛ. Bǝ lǝ ǝbǝ ǝnyǝnko mɛ disuǝyuǝ!’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Nya se mínte sikpi esoo, mfi fǝ atabi nyamǝ nya mínku dibiǝ mímfi nnya mínkpe. Nyaso nyǝ fǝ atabi ni nyamfo.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: “Wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Fǝ uyifoyifo bua, ɔflɔ́. Eyi fǝ meefi lesa kaka lǝ mánkpe nya mǝǝfǝsǝ asa lǝ kaka mánto kulesa.

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: “Mfó nya onamute ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mɔmɔ, lɛ atabi nyamfo afo lǝ wǝ ani lǝ befi nnya bitǝ nwǝ nkpe akpi lefosi.’

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Itsyise utidi wǝ nkpe asaa, wǝ ke bonsi boofi ditsyuǝ bakpe bawunsǝ nɛ. Nya utidi wǝ kulesa lentee, sɛkɛɛ nte wǝ tsyaa, bɔɔlɛ ni bafo wǝ lǝ ani.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Nya uyifoyifo bimba ǝmfo alɛɛ, beto wǝ bekpe lǝ ketuntu ka nkpe lǝ lekple. Kaka mfóo, wuwi keku unǝ wǝ animi. Utidi Eto Ubi Ebokponko Kawunsiǝ Eto Betidi Lekpo.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: “Lefe nǝ Utidi Eto Ubi ǝmǝ luubǝ lǝ wǝ diyǝnde ǝsuǝ ku Onanto eto batɔnkpe kenkee, wuusiǝ lǝ wǝ lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: Nya kawunsiǝ eto ese eto betidi kenke lɔɔbɔ bǝńyǝ lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi. Nya wuusǝnsǝ mǝ lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ fe kase nwǝ laayɛnko bebuki leesǝnsǝ basamu kǝlǝkǝ lǝ bate nti.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: Wuuńyǝsǝ betidi sɛɛ lǝ wǝ kodiani, nya wuutǝ mba ni betidi bua ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kǝmǝni.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: “Nya Oka ǝmǝ lɔɔtɛyi mba ńyǝ wǝ lǝ kodiani nkǝ, ‘Bibǝ, mi mba mɔ anto lékpo atsyɔ! Bibǝ bidufǝ lǝ sekakedikɔ ka bálɔ bǝtsyǝ itǝ mi idu taa kawunsiǝ eto ketsyikokɔ ke.

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: Kukpɛnsɛ álɛ mɛ nya bítǝ mɛ lesa míndi, utukuɛ álɛ mɛ, nya bítǝ mɛ ntu mínnǝ. Nyifo ɔfɔ nya béfo mɛ békpe lǝ mi ayo.

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ mínyɛ leyaa, bítǝ mɛ kafia nti. Nya lefe nǝ mínlɛ bufii, bɛlɛ mɛ ola. Lefe nǝ bákpe mɛ lǝ diyo, bíbǝ bebe mɛ.’

english_verse: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
translation_verse: “Mfó nya betidi sɛɛ bamǝ lɔɔtɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Bonamute, dimanfe búnyǝ kukpɛnsɛ nlɛ fǝ fɛ bútǝ lesa fédi, nye bútǝ fǝ ntu fǝnǝ?

english_verse: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
translation_verse: Dimanfe búnyǝ fǝ ɔfɔ fɛ bófo fǝ nye fányɛ leya bútǝ fǝ kafia féti?

english_verse: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Dimanfe bɔ́nɔ bǝ alɛ bufi nye bǝ akɛ lǝ diyo fɛ búbǝ bóbe fǝ?’

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: “Mfó nya Oka ǝmǝ luutǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, lefe saa nǝ fáwunsǝko utsyuǝ wǝ lǝmbǝ dibu saa lǝ mɔ bayimi bamfo ntii, mɔ féyifo fǝtǝ.’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: “Mfó wɔɔtɛyi mba ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kǝmǝni nkǝ, ‘Bidu mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ, mi mba Onanto díkudi! Bisu bidufǝ lǝ ditefɛnku manlo nǝ Onanto létsyaka útǝ Obonsam ku wǝ batɔnkpe!

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Kukpɛnsɛ álɛ mɛ, fɛɛ bɛni bíǝntǝ mɛ lesa ndi. Utukuɛ álɛ mɛ, fɛɛ bíǝntǝ mɛ ntu nko ke.

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: Nyifo ɔfɔ wǝ díbǝ ɔ́sɔnɔ mi, tsya bíamfo mɛ. Nyɛ leya tsya bíenti mɛ kafia. Nnɔ bufi, bákpe mɛ lǝ diyo, fɛɛ bíǝmbǝ bisiǝsǝ mɛ.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: “Mfó buutǝ wǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, ‘Boanto, lemanfe búnyǝ kukpɛnsɛ lɛlɛ fǝ, nye ututkuɛ lɛ fǝ, nye féyifo ɔfɔ, nye fányɛ leya, nye fanlɛ bufi, nye fakɛ lǝ diyo fɛ bóǝmbǝ bobe nye bɔ́wunsǝko fǝ?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: “Oka ǝmǝ ebutǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, lefe nǝ eto lefe bíenyifo kasɛɛ bítǝ betidi ba bíembu kulesa lǝ mɔ bǝtsyuǝ bamfo ntii, mɔ uni bíenyifo kulesa bitǝ!’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi ólosǝ nkǝ, “Nyaso boofi mba mfo batsyese kanyi kǝnyǝkɔ itǝ manlo manlo. Nya betidi sɛɛ bamfóo, mǝ ebunyǝ nkpǝ bo lǝmbɔkɛ kalokɔ kulefe kulefe.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Iyifo mi bote bǝnkǝ ibu eyi ǝnuǝ lǝ Ukpǝ Asa Bo Ɔ́yila eto Diyi nǝmǝ lǝ leyoo, nya boofi Utidi Eto Ubi bǝtǝ bǝ basɔ wǝ bamanklasǝ lǝ kuyi ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Nya úsinkli úbǝ bakasebi bamǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bisi belabe selabe bɛlɛ ɔnwɛ butsyǝ? Bebe! Lefe nǝmǝ eboyo bǝ befi Utidi Eto Ubi ǝmǝ bakpe lǝ betidi bua eto ani.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: Befi nsusu bǝ boomuǝ Yesu kalɔkalɔ lǝ balo wǝ.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Bátɛyi tsya bǝnkǝ, “Yanle bǝ bumuǝ wǝ lǝ diyi nǝmǝ budi kǝmiǝ, lenni nko betidi bamǝ ebunyǝnki bataka bǝńyǝ bo lǝ ǝsuǝ.” Usio Utsyuǝ Édiki Yesu Nnuǝbi Fifi Lǝ Betania.

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: Boofo nnuǝbi bomfó boté itǝ koya kplɛ, befi atabi nyamǝ bǝtǝ bakombo!”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Se Yesu eto bakasebi dínyǝ nyamfoo, kafo ányi mǝ. Bátɔ ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Beso óbiasǝ nnuǝbi bomfo nko?

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, bésǝ kusǝ bátɛsǝ Onanto, nya bédu bésu Nnuǝbi eto Eyi eto Kɔtini ǝsuǝ. Yesu Átɛyi Utsyǝ Nkǝ Petro Ǝbɔni Wǝ.

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: Idu lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, útsyiko esu bɔbɛbɛ kase wuudu Yesu utǝ mǝ. Yesu Ku Wǝ Bakasebi Édi Ukpǝ Ása Bo Ɔ́yila Eto Diyi.

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: Lǝ lesa nǝmǝ kedikɔɔ, Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, mi nti eto ɔni ebudu mɛ utǝ.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: Lǝ Abolo Menyenki eto Eyididi eto diyi tutɛ ǝsuǝǝ, bakasebi bamǝ ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Sie faala fǝ bɔlɔ butsyǝ butǝ fǝ itǝ Ukpǝ Ása Bo Ɔ́yila eto diyi nǝmǝ budi?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Se banlɛ lesa budii, Yesu éfi abolo, nya ɔ́sɔ ani nya úbudi wǝ nya úfi útǝ wǝ bakasebi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Befo lǝ bidi. Nyamfo ni mɔ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ nɛ.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: Nyǝ mɔ kɔtɔ ko ni kakle lǝ Onanto ku wǝ betidi eto nti. Kɔtɔ ko minfi minlɛ bokpo kasɔ lǝ betidi kpǝ eto disi, bǝ lǝ Onanto lǝ ufi mǝ abua ɔtsyɛ mǝ.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ, mensiembunǝ nte bomfo eto okle isu ese lefe nǝ mɔ ku mi luunǝ nte fɔfɔ lǝ mɔ Anto eto sekakedikɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mmi eto ketsye kamfoo, mimblɛ ebutsyetsyi bidu mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ, nya mɔ mate luubu. Itsyise Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue átɛyi nkǝ, ‘Onanto ebolo nwǝ lǝǝtsyǝ basamu bamǝ nya basamu bamǝ luusǝnsǝ.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi Petro nkǝ, “Nlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi minkǝ ketsye kamfo fɛ ukusɛ luutǝ kɛnyɛ, ǝbɔtɛyi sitsyǝ fǝnkǝ enyi mɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Nya Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi dísu kaka baakpo Getsemane, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bisiǝ mfo lǝ nsu sɛtɔ mǝ nsu nto ola.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Kanyi ka nkpe mɛ lǝ utu fui tinti bǝ kába mɛ ǝsuǝ. Bisiǝ mfo lǝ bikase ku mɛ.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Petro éfe kɛnyɛ ɔ́tɛyi Yesu nkǝ, “Lǝ bafɛfɛ kenke ani fǝ tsyaa, memboyani fǝ ntsyǝ kulefe!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Petro ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mǝmbɔni fǝ kulefe, lǝ ibǝ bǝ bonko fǝ kpe kulaa tsya.” Nya bakasebi fɛfɛ kenke tsya lɛtɛyi nko. Yesu Áto Ola Lǝ Getsemane.

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Yesu esi úsu dinuǝfǝ úsu oto ola ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔ Anto, lǝ kanyi eto lɛkɔbe nǝ mǝǝbǝ bunǝ mfo embofe mɛ, nya iyifo fǝ lelabi bǝ nnǝ ni kokooko, nyalɛ tǝ lǝ ibǝ itǝ nko.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Ɔ́yɛ úsu sɛtɔ sɛkɛ nya ókpo lǝ kasɔ úfusǝ ǝnǝmi, nya óto ola nkǝ, “Mɔ Anto, lǝ yɔɔlɛkɛɛ, takasǝ bulǝ bomfo lǝ mɛ ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ menyifo lǝ mɔ lelabi ǝsuǝ bomu lǝ fǝ lelabi ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Betaka lǝ budu. Bebee, nwǝ nlɛ mɛ budu kǝtǝ ebɔfi.” Yudafɔ Eto Beninǝ Ǝmuǝ Yesu.

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Ókpa Petro ku Sebedeo eto bebi inuǝ bamǝ ɔ́yɛnko. Údufǝ lǝ kawɛndo ǝsuǝ, nya útsyiko butinkǝ kekleke.

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Se úsinkli úsu bakasebi itsyǝ bamǝ ɔflɔ, únyǝ nkǝ bálabe selabe, nya ɔ́tɔ Petro nkǝ, “Nyaso mi basini bamfo embofo ǝnǝ butǝkǝ ku mɛ itǝ lebombosɔsɔ lɔni hã ete?

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Bitǝkǝ ǝnǝ lǝ beto ola bǝ biembudufǝ lǝ botokabe ǝsuǝ. Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ lɛ bola, fɛɛ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ayɔkpɔ.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Yesu esi údu lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ ditsyǝfǝ úsu óto ola fe kase óto ibǝ éfe.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Yesu ǝtǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Ɔhɔ, kɔse lǝ eyifo lesa nǝ fǝbǝ keyifokɔ!” Mfó nya betidi bamǝ díbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ nya bǝmuǝ wǝ nya bákɔbe wǝ kekleke.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Yesu ǝńyǝ ɔlɛ mǝ etiki budinko fɛ Yuda wǝ ni lefosi inuǝ bamǝ eto ɔni díbǝ údufǝ. Betidi ba ni besumunyǝ ninǝ ku batɔnkotɔnko ba bǝlǝkǝ ǝtǝkǝko wǝ, nya béntsyi nlande ku keyi.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: Udundukǝtǝ ǝmǝ ǝtǝ báte batɔnsǝ bǝ, “Utidi wǝ mɔɔbuta nsiǝsǝǝ, wǝ ni utidi wǝ bɛnlɛ bɔbɛbɛ nɛ. Bimuǝ wǝ.”

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Yuda ésu Yesu ɔflɔ nya lǝ kase únyǝ wǝ koo, ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Oteasa, diyuǝsǝ itǝ fǝ.” Mfó nya ɔ́buta wǝ.

english_verse: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Mba ntǝkǝko Yesu eto ɔni ǝlǝkǝ wǝ kalande nya ówe Usumunyǝ Ninǝ eto ndomunyǝ eto koto.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Fi fǝ kalande akpe lǝ ke kakpekɔ. Betidi ba leetsyi kalande lǝ betidi ǝsuǝǝ, kalande ǝsuǝ baayɛ kǝkpǝ.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝǝ, Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke ǝmǝ áfuadi wǝ awu ku lɛblɔfi nya ófa ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Édi mbusiotiki! Boensihiã badansiɛ saa! Bíɔnɔ mbusiotiki nnya údi mfo!

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Yesu édi bi, wóantɛyi kulesa. Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke ǝmǝ esi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ Onanto wǝ nsi nkpe eto diye ǝsuǝǝ, nlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi minkǝ tɛyi bo lǝ fǝ ni Onanto eto Ubi wǝ Bákpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ ǝmǝ.”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Nya údu mfó úsu úńyǝ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ eto kesie. Usumubisiobi bamba tsya ǝnyǝ Petro nya ɔ́tɛyi mba díńyǝ mfó nkǝ, “Wuntsya ǝtǝkǝko Yesu Nasaretnyǝ ǝmǝ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: Mba dímuǝ Yesu ákpa wǝ bésuko Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke Kayafa eto diyo kaka besumunyǝ ninǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ ku afi eto bateasa létsya bensi.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Petro ǝtǝko mǝ kama kama úsu taa Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke eto lekpata kafo. Úsu úsiǝ lǝ bamamanyǝ bamǝ ɔflɔ nkǝ lǝ obe kase ditiki nǝmǝ dubǝ ibǝ.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Besumunyǝ ninǝ ku bakponkpo ba dísiǝ mfó kenke ábɛbɛ betidi ba luubǝ bala etiki bǝtǝkǝ Yesu lǝ bǝnyǝ kusu balo wǝ.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bǝnnyǝ kulesa saa, ite bǝ betidi kpǝ ǝbǝ bála bidia bǝtǝkǝ wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ tsyafɛ. Betidi inuǝ bǝtsyuǝ ǝbǝ

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
translation_verse: nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Utidi ǝmfo átɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mooba Onanto eto kesumuyo nǝmfo, nya lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ ǝsuǝǝ, muusinkli nto ni nńyǝnsǝ.’ ”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Ee, ite fǝ kase fátɛyi. Fɛɛ nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ idu mɔmɔ ɛntɔɔ, buinyǝ Utidi Eto Ubi se unsi lǝ Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke eto kodiani. Nya biunyǝ se unsi lǝ asɔmiloku ǝsuǝ utsyi kato ɔnlɛ bubǝ!”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Nya bétufǝ ntufǝ bákpo wǝ lǝ ǝnǝmi nya báblɛ wǝ. Nya mba lɛsɔ wǝ lǝ atosi,

english_verse: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: átɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Lǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ fenii, tɛyi bo futsyǝ lɛsɔ fǝ!” Petro Étudi Nkǝ Únyi Yesu.

english_verse: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Bio Petro esi ɔ́ni nya óka ntamu ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Minyi osani ǝmǝ ko!”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Petro ǝsiǝ lǝ lekple lǝ kpakpaakpe mfó fɛ Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke eto usumubisiobi díbǝ mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Fintsya ǝtǝkǝko Yesu wǝ ditsyi Galilea eto kasɔ.”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ɔ́ni nnya lǝ betidi bamfo kenke eto ǝnǝmi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Mante lesa nǝ fanlɛ bɔtɛyi mfo!”

english_verse: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Yanklɛ fɛ mba ńyǝ mfo díbǝ bátɛyi Petro bǝnkǝ, “Ɛkpɛ mǝ ɔni feni, itsyise fǝ etikididi ǝǝlǝkǝ fǝ kate wuee.”

english_verse: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
translation_verse: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ lesa saa bǝ ɔnukualɛ minlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto lǝ unǝ mɛ koto lǝ sidia minlɛ bɔtɛyi!” Lefe nǝmfo tutuutu nnya ukusɛ dítǝ kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Nya Petro lékloma lesa nǝ Yesu lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Fɛ ukusɛ luutǝ kɛnyɛɛ, ǝbɔtɛyi sitsyǝ fǝnkǝ enyi mɛ.” Ɔ́bɔ úsu lekple nya úwi keku kekleke.

english_verse: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Úyifo nko itsyise óte nkǝ kekisi eso Yudafɔ eto beninǝ bamfo lékpa wǝ báboko.

english_verse: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bédu lǝ umǝ báyɛ bantɔɔ, bátsyako osani utsyuǝ wǝ ditsyi Kirene wǝ baakpo Simon. Bamamanyǝ bamǝ áhiɛ wǝ bǝ ofo Yesu eto uyikabie komǝ utsyi.

english_verse: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
translation_verse: Ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nyifo kabua bǝ mfi utidi wǝ dímenyifo kabua ntǝ miǝ balo!” Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Nyamfo manka bo nkpǝ. Fǝ etiki ni nyamfó!”

english_verse: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
translation_verse: Se bákpe disii, báse nya uyi bǝnkǝ befi atabi nyamǝ bésu báya Asa eto Otonto eto Kasɔ itǝ bafɔ eto kebikekɔ.

english_verse: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Nyamfo esoo, bǝtǝkǝ kasɔ kamǝ diye bǝ Kɔtɔ eto Kasɔ ibǝ ese lǝ mmi.

english_verse: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Itǝ lesa nǝ Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi Yeremia lɛtɛyi útsyǝ díbǝ itǝ nkǝ, “Béfi sika flubia eto atabi fosi ǝtsyǝ, nya Israelfɔ lɛtɔnɔ bǝ boofo lǝ wǝ disi

english_verse: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: béfi báya Otonto eto Kasɔ, fe kase Bonamute léla nkǝ nyifo.” Pilato Átɔ Yesu Etiki.

english_verse: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Bákpa Yesu báboko Romafɔ eto Aba eto Unyɛsiǝ Ninǝ Pilato eto ǝnǝmi. Nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Fǝ ni Yudafɔ eto Oka ǝmǝ?” Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Ee. Átɛyi kukɔnɔ.”

english_verse: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ wántɛyi kulesa itsyi lǝ besumunyǝ ninǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ eto nyɛ bo banlɛ bola kǝtǝkǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
translation_verse: Fɛɛ íyifo Aba eto uninǝ ǝmǝ yani tinti se Yesu lɛni wémfe kɛnyɛ útǝ mbuayɛ saa. Pilato Álo Yesu Dikpǝnkpi.

english_verse: Pilate set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross. Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Mfó nya Pilato léyani Baraba útǝ mǝ. Nya se útǝ báblɛ Yesu bálosǝ, óyani wǝ útǝ nkǝ básɔ wǝ lǝ uyi ǝsuǝ. Romafɔ Eto Bamamanyǝ Ákpe Yesu Kofani.

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
translation_verse: Betidi ba kenke lɛyɛ mfó étinkǝsǝ mǝ esi nya báka Yesu fefe.

english_verse: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
translation_verse: Besumunyǝ ninǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ ábiasǝ betidi ba létsya mfo eto ato bǝnkǝ batɔ Pilato lǝ oyani Baraba utǝ mǝ lǝ balo Yesu.

english_verse: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
translation_verse: Se Pilato dínyǝ nkǝ wensiembofo kulesa buyifo lenni bulǝ ebubǝǝ, úfi ntu nya úflǝ ani lǝ sitiditu nǝmǝ eto ǝnǝmi nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nnyǝ mɔ ani, nnyǝ mɔ akpa, mɔ ani ǝmbǝ lǝ osani ǝmfo eto bolo ǝsuǝ! Mi etiki ini.”

english_verse: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
translation_verse: Sitiditu siamǝ kenke ǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Wǝ ukpǝ bǝ utǝkǝ lǝ bo ku bo bebi lǝ ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: Nya béfi biyu bédi lekpakpa bétsyisǝ wǝ lǝ disi fɛ béfi uyi bákpe wǝ lǝ kodiani. Nya báse akonki lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi fe oka seka nya bǝmǝ wǝ bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Yuu, yuuduu,! Yudafɔ eto Oka lǝ usiǝ nkpǝ kɛntsyɛɛ!”

english_verse: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
translation_verse: Ófó Onanto ɔ́nɔ, nya waakpadi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ Onanto eto Ubi. Nyaso bitǝ lǝ bobe lǝ Onanto aala wǝǝ, lǝ ulǝkǝ wǝ mɔmɔ lǝ bobe!”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Se bǝmǝ wǝ bálosǝ nkoo, bǝlǝkǝ wǝ awu sia nyamǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ nya besi béfi wǝ awu nyamǝ bákpe wǝ. Nya bákpa wǝ báyɛnko bésu wǝ kasɔkɔ lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝ. Básɔ Yesu Bamanklasǝ Lǝ Uyikabie Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ Yesu nte bo bátsyakasǝ ku kofa ko nkpe kalo bǝ unǝ. Fɛɛ se útu mu óbee, ɔ́ni wǝnnǝ mu.

english_verse: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Mfó nya bamamanyǝ bamǝ lɛsɔ wǝ bamanklasǝ lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝ, nya bákpo asa basɛɛ fɛ bésie wǝ awu bǝtǝ bǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: Then they sat down to guard him.
translation_verse: Nya kamaa, bǝsiǝ mfó bákase wǝ.

english_verse: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Lǝ wǝ disi katoo, báwɔni asa bámanklasǝ lǝ uyi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ. Nnya báwɔni bǝnkǝ lesa nǝ eso bálo wǝ ni bǝ, “Nnyǝ Yesu, Nwǝ Ni Yudafɔ Eto Oka.”

english_verse: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
translation_verse: Bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Fǝ ni nwǝ lɛtɛyi fǝnkǝ eboba Desumuyo Kplɛ akpo lǝ kasɔ, esinkli ato ni ǝńyǝnsǝ lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ ǝsuǝ nɛ! Lǝkǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ, lǝ Onanto eto Ubi feni! Yifǝ lǝ uyikabie komǝ ǝsuǝ ǝbǝ kasɔ.”

english_verse: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ betidi bamba átɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bińyǝ lǝ bobe! Bitǝ lǝ bobe lǝ Elia ebubǝ ulǝkǝ wǝ utsyiko kuyi komfo ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
translation_verse: Idu lǝ kakɔmi eto abombo lefosi ǝnuǝǝ, kawunsiǝ kenke ána tũũ isu ese lǝ ketsyitsyokɔmi eto abombo ǝtsyǝ.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Fe abombo ǝtsyǝ etii, Yesu áfa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani?” Nyamfo eto kasɔ ni, “Mɔ Onanto, mɔ Onanto! Beso fáyani mɛ fǝtsyǝ?”

english_verse: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
translation_verse: Mǝ ɔni étsyetsyi nya úfi okosa wǝ bákpe lǝ nte eto sonkpe, nya úfi mu ku uyi úsuko nkǝ lǝ utǝ wǝ lǝ unǝ.

english_verse: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, Disumuyo Kplɛ eto kafia kplɛ dísie lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ itsyi kato ibǝ kasɔ. Nya kasɔ kamǝ kenke dítinkǝ, nya aba léba.

english_verse: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
translation_verse: Edi ásini, nya Onanto eto betidi kɛnkɛ kpǝ ba díkekpǝ létaka bǝbǝ nkpǝ.

english_verse: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
translation_verse: Bábɔ lǝ mǝ edi kafo nya se Yesu létaka útsyi ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ nkpǝǝ, bésu Yudafɔ eto Umǝ Kɛnkɛ ǝmǝ kafo kaka betidi kpǝ dínyǝ mǝ.

english_verse: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
translation_verse: Se Omamanyǝ ninǝ ku bamamanyǝ ba díńyǝ bákase Yesu dínyǝ kase kasɔ dítinkǝ ku asa nnya díbǝ mfó kenkee, sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ tinti! Nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Sitintii, utidi ǝmfo éyifo Onanto eto Ubi.”

english_verse: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
translation_verse: Besio kpǝ ba dítsyi Galilea ba díkawunsǝko Yesu ǝńyǝ lǝ kɔsɛsɛ bákabe asa nnya díbǝ.

english_verse: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
translation_verse: Se éyo ditsyitsyo lefee, otabinyǝ utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Yosef, nya úyifo Yesu eto okasebi wǝ ditsyi Arimatea ǝbǝ.

english_verse: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
translation_verse: Úsu Pilato ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ lǝ ofo Yesu eto ɔkɔ. Pilato ǝtǝ kusu nkǝ befi ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bǝtǝ Yosef.

english_verse: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
translation_verse: Nyaso Yosef áfo ɔkɔ ǝmǝ nya úfi kafia flubia kǝtsyuǝ úmli wǝ.

english_verse: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
translation_verse: Nya úfi wǝ úsu úbikǝ lǝ kebikekɔ lǝ wǝ kosate eto kudi fɔfɔ ko bǝlǝkǝ lǝ koba kǝmiǝ. Nya ominansǝ leba nǝ lɛmɔ úti ko kɛnyɛ nya údu.

english_verse: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
translation_verse: Maria Magdalene ku Maria nuǝfǝ ǝsiǝ mfó balɛ kudi komǝ bobe. Bamamanyǝ Áse Yesu Eto Kudi.

english_verse: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
translation_verse: Nya se kale disiǝ nya ini Ɔnwɛditsyǝyii esoo, usumunyǝ ninǝ ku Farisifɔ ésu Pilato ɔflɔ.

english_verse: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
translation_verse: Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Uninǝ, lefe nǝ odiantidi ǝmǝ dísiǝ nkpǝǝ, ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Lǝ nkpǝǝ, bootakasǝ mɛ betsyiko ɔkɔmǝ lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ eto kama.’

english_verse: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
translation_verse: Nyaso tǝ lǝ bamamanyǝ lǝ base kudi komǝ kukɔnɔ isu ese diyi tsyǝfǝ nǝmǝ, kase wǝ bakasebi lembusu beyu ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bedunko batɛyi betidi bǝnkǝ ótaka utsyi ɔkɔmǝ. Lǝ ibǝ nkoo, sidia sia bɔɔtɛyi ǝbɔkɛ seso nsia kɔ.”

english_verse: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Pilato lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ lǝ bamamanyǝ lǝ besu base kudi komǝ fe kase boofo.”

english_verse: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya eto kamaa, bakasebi lefosi ɔnwɛ bamǝ ábe bésu kɔtini ko nkpe lǝ Galilea ǝsuǝ kaka Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ utsyǝ.

english_verse: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
translation_verse: Besumunyǝ ninǝ bamǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ ákpe disi béfi nsusu. Bǝtǝ bamamanyǝ bamǝ atabi eto akplɛ,

english_verse: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
translation_verse: nya bete mǝ bǝ betsyue lesa saa nǝ mínte mi koto. Nya lǝ sitintii, mɔɔkɛ ku mi lefe saa, isu ese lǝ kawunsiǝ eto kalosǝkɔ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Biante sikpi. Bisu bɛtɛyi mɔ bayimi biǝ besu Galilea, mfó buunyǝ mɛ nɛ.” Bamamanyǝ Ba Lése Kudi Komɛ Átɛyi Lesa Nǝ Bǝnyǝ.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, kasɔ étinkǝ kekleke. Onanto eto Ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ áyifǝ utsyi kato úbǝ ɔ́minansǝ leba kplɛ nǝmǝ nya úsiǝ lǝ ni ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
translation_verse: Wǝ ditsyǝbi ékeyǝnde fe kase ɔsi laato nya wǝ awu diflubi futututu fe aklala seka.

english_verse: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
translation_verse: Sikpi ǝmuǝ mba nlɛ mfó bose nya bétinkǝ tinti, nya bákpo kasɔ mfó fe bukpǝ békpe.

english_verse: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Onanto eto Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmfó édinko besio bamǝ etiki nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Biante sikpi! Nnyi miǝ Yesu wǝ básɔ bámanklasǝ lǝ kuyi ǝsuǝ bɛnlɛ bɔbɛbɛ.

english_verse: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, bisu mlǝ lǝ bisu bɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi biǝ, ‘Bátakasǝ wǝ betsyiko ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ nkpǝ, nya ɔntɔ mi sɛtɔ úsu Galilea. Mfó biunyǝ wǝ nɛ.’ Bekloma nnya míntɛyi mi mfo.”

english_verse: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, Yesu átsyako mǝ, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Diyuǝ itǝ mi.” Báyɛ bétu wǝ bábokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ bákɔbe wǝ lǝ akpa nya bésumu wǝ.

english_verse: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ besio bamfó dísinkli bantɔɔ, bamamanyǝ ba lése kudi komǝ ésu umǝ nya bátɛyi besumunyǝ ninǝ bamǝ asa nnya kenke díbǝ mfó.

english_verse: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
translation_verse: nya bátɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, “Bɛtɛyi biǝnkǝ lefe nǝ bélabe selabe ku ketsyee, wǝ bakasebi ǝbǝ béyu wǝ ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bédunko.

english_verse: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
translation_verse: Bamamanyǝ bamǝ áfo atabi nyamǝ nya béyifo fe kase bátɛyi mǝ. Nyaso nyamfo ni ditiki nǝ lɛyɛ létsyilama Yudafɔ ntii taa mmi nɛ. Yesu Ǝlǝkǝ Ǝsuǝ Óte Wǝ Bakasebi.

english_verse: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
translation_verse: Yesu ányi ɔfi mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Onanto éfi kato ku kasɔ kenke eto ǝsuǝale útǝ mɛ.

english_verse: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
translation_verse: Nyaso bisu kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke ɔflɔ lǝ biyifo mǝ mɔ bakasebi. Biflǝsǝ mǝ ntu lǝ Anto ku Ubi ku Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto diye ǝsuǝ,

english_verse: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
translation_verse: Se bǝnyǝ wǝǝ, bésumu wǝ, ite bǝ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ áyaki tsya.

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfo báyani mǝ asabu bǝtsyǝ nya bátaka bǝtǝkǝko wǝ.

english_verse: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
translation_verse: Ɔ́yɛ úsu sɛtɔ sɛkɛ nya únyǝ bayimisani inuǝ bǝtsyuǝ ba ni Sebedeo eto bebi ba ni Yakobo ku Yohanes. Besi mǝ ɔklɔ kafo balɛ mǝ asabu bɔlɔ.

english_verse: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Itǝ esoo, Yesu eto diye áyɛ letsyo lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ kenke. Yesu Átsya Betidi Kpǝ Bufi.

english_verse: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe kɛntsyɛ nǝ léfe mǝǝ, Onanto eto unyǝlǝkǝtidi Yesaya áwɔni utsyǝ nkǝ Onanto átɛyi wǝ ubi nkǝ, “Mootsyese ɔtɔyɛ ntɔnsǝ. Wǝ lɔɔlɔ betidi utsyǝ itǝ fǝ bubǝ.

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Yohanes ákpe ɔflimu eto bisǝ eto awudindi nya úfi kubukikuǝ eto abɔsu ókle wǝ nkɛ. Didisa nǝ úkǝdi didi bablɔ ku bǝkpǝsǝ.

english_verse: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
translation_verse: Wǝ etiki nnya ɔnlɛ ditsya bobe ni nkǝ, “Utidi wǝ ntǝkǝ mɛ kama ɔnlɛ bubǝǝ, so mɛ famfamfa nya yǝńyǝ itǝ bǝ ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ nyifo wǝ keyifo blɛmblɛ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu ku mba ntǝkǝko wǝ ésu bédufǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Kapernaum. Nya lǝ Yudafɔ eto Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi nǝ ntǝkǝ lǝ mǝ mfó eto busu ǝsuǝ eto kamaa, Yesu ésu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ nya útsyiko mǝ Anantotiki bote.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Iyifo mba kenke lɛnɔ wǝ asatete nyamǝ yanii, itsyise ɛbɔ lete lǝ mba laate asa itsyi lǝ Yudafɔ eto afi ǝsuǝ. Bomu wǝ asatete eyifo nnya nkpe kalende nya itsyi taa wǝ utu kǝmiǝ.

english_verse: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Yanklɛ fɛ Yesu ditsyi umǝ wǝ baakpo Nasaret lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ, ubǝ udufǝ, nya Yohanes díyifo wǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa lǝ Yordan.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Lǝ kase Yesu nlɛ bɔbɔ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ kabe katoo, únyǝ nkǝ Onanto eto kato lɛ bɔsini nya Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ lɛyifǝ dísiǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ lɛblɛkpa eto ditsyǝbi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Osani utsyuǝ wǝ nlɛ bufisia bufi ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ. Óse akonki ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa nkǝ lǝ ɔwunsǝko wǝ. Ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ alaa, ebofo mɛ botsya nya yuuyuǝ mɛ tsya.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔkɛ mfó katɛyi nkǝ, “Lefe sɛɛ nǝmǝ eboyo. Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eto lefe ǝbɔfi. Bisinkli lǝ mi asa bua eto kama lǝ befo Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo bɛnɔ.”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Kase Yesu dínyǝ mǝ koo, ókpadi mǝ nkǝ bǝtǝkǝko wǝ. Mǝ tsya átaka akpa akpa báyani mǝ anto Sebedeo ku wǝ beyifoyifo bǝtsyǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo bǝtǝkǝko Yesu. Yesu Ála Lɛwɔnɔ Bua Lǝ Osani Utsyuǝ Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Nyaso Yesu ésu wǝ ɔflɔ, nya ɔ́kɔbe wǝ lǝ kɔni ótakasǝ úńyǝnsǝ. Lamfolamfo wǝ bufi bomǝ áyɛ wǝ nya ótaka útǝ mɛ lesa bédi.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ bǝnyǝ wǝǝ, bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Utsyuǝ saa ǝbǝ ɔlɛ fǝ bɔbɛbɛ.”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmǝ tutuutuu, osani utsyuǝ lɛwɔnɔ bua nsi wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ akɛ lǝ katsyakɔ kamǝ mfó,

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: nya ófa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Yesu Nasaretnyǝ, be fanlɛ bɔbɛbɛ ketsyiko lǝ bo ɔflɔ mfo? Bubǝ fǝbǝ fǝ ǝǝbǝ atsylɔsǝ bo lee? Nyi futsyǝ feni. Fǝ ni Onanto eto Ɔkɛnkɛnyǝ wǝ ótsyese nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Yesu éti lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ kusu nya ókpe ni kɔfi nkǝ, “Dika, nya lǝ abɔ lǝ osani ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmfo étinkǝsǝ osani ǝmǝ keklekle nya dítǝ ófa bǝmbǝǝmbǝ fɛ lɛbɔ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Itǝ iyifo mba kenke díńyǝ mfó yanii, nya bátɔ mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Nte asatete fɔfɔ nnya eto okle nɛ? Utidi ǝmfo kpe ǝsuǝale woafo etiki budi ku awɔnɔ bua nya beetsyue wǝ etiki ato.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ lǝ busu umǝ bamba. Efe bǝ ndi Anantotiki lǝ mfó tsya, itsyise nyamfo ni mɔ keyifo ka mimbǝ keyifokɔ lǝ kawunsiǝ nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Wǝ etiki nyamǝ ǝnǝ Yesu kafo, nyaso únǝ wǝ kɔni nya útidi wǝ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Nla nko. Fǝ ǝsuǝ kenke lǝ yele.”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi ku Yakobo ku Yohanes édu lǝ katsyakɔ kamǝ nya bésu Simon ku Andreas eto diyo.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Se bésu bédufǝ mfóo, betidi bǝtsyuǝ átɛyi Yesu bǝnkǝ, Simon eto usiǝambe áyila, atikesi ǝmuǝ wǝ olabe.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Se ditsyitsyo lefe léyoo, betidi ákpa befimfi kenke ku mba awɔnɔ bua díkakpe bulǝ kpǝ báboko Yesu bǝ lǝ otsya mǝ bufi.

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: Nya umǝ ǝmǝ kenke eto betidi díbǝ bǝńyǝ lǝ kesie mfó bánlɛ bobe.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
translation_verse: Yesu átsya betidi kpǝ ba nlɛ bufi ateate bufi nya óla awɔnɔ bua lǝ betidi kpǝ ǝsuǝ ku mba awɔnɔ bua nsi lǝ ǝsuǝ, itsyise Yesu yi nkǝ awɔnɔ bua nyamǝ yi futsyǝ uni, nyaso wántɔnɔ nkǝ awɔnɔ nyamǝ tɛyi kulesa itsyi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Yesu Ábe Ditiki Sɛɛ Nǝnǝ Letsya Lǝ Galilea Eto Kasɔ.

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Kolensiǝnto fɛ kale luusiǝǝ, Yesu átaka ɔ́bɔ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ. Úsu kaka kutsyuǝ saa lǝmbǝ nya óto ola útǝ Onanto.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Kamaa, Simon ku wǝ bayɛtsyuǝ ésu bábɛbɛ Yesu.

english_verse: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Nyaso ɔ́yɛ ótsyilama Galilea eto kasɔ kenke, nya úkedi Anantotiki nyamǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ kpǝ nya óla awɔnɔ bua ɔ́bɔsǝ lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ tsya. Yesu Átsya Osani Utsyuǝ Wǝ Nlɛ Bufisia Bufi.

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfo bufi bomǝ ǝyuǝ wǝ.

english_verse: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ udu, nya ókpe wǝ kɔfi keklekle nkǝ,
