english_verse: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
translation_verse: Bisiǝ lǝ diyo nǝ ke bɛsɔnɔ. Nya lesa saa nǝ ke boofi bǝtǝ mi, bidi lǝ binǝ faa. Itsyise uyifoyifo saa, ǝńyǝ itǝ kayofofo. Biekedu lǝ diyo lɔni bisu lebamba.

english_verse: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
translation_verse: “Nya umǝ saa wǝ báfo mii, bidi lesa saa nǝ bootǝ mi.

english_verse: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
translation_verse: Betsya befimfi ba nkpe lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ. Nya bɛtɛyi umǝ ǝmǝ eto betidi biǝnkǝ, ‘Onanto eto sekakedidi ebɔfi mi.’

english_verse: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ bisu umǝ utsyuǝ nya báni bámfo mii, bidu bisu obia nti lǝ bɛtɛyi biǝnkǝ,

english_verse: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
translation_verse: Nya nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti kekleke minkǝ lǝ diyi nǝ bookponko betidi lekpo ayoo, Onanto ebunyǝ Sodom eto umǝ kulaa tsya kaminsǝ eso umǝ ǝmfo. Kǝmǝ Kǝtsyuǝ Mámfo Yesu Eto Ditiki.

english_verse: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
translation_verse: Fɛɛ yenyifo mi disuǝyuǝ bǝ awɔnɔ bua etsyue mi ditiki ato. Bomu binyǝ disuǝyuǝ bǝ Onanto áwɔni mi aye ókpe lǝ wǝ sekakedikɔ kato.” Yesu Ǝnyǝ Disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
translation_verse: Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi kpǝ ku baka tsya ála bǝ lǝ bǝnyǝ lesa nǝ bɛnlɛ bunyǝ, fɛɛ bǝnnyǝ, nya lǝ bánnɔ lesa nǝ bɛnlɛ bɔnɔ, fɛɛ bɔ́nnɔ.” Yesu Átɛyi Mǝ Samarianyǝ Wǝ Diyifo Utidi Utsyuǝ Uni Kafoɛkpɛ Eto Lekpa.

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
translation_verse: Onanto ebunyǝ Tiro ku Sidon eto kǝmǝ kaminsǝ eso mi lǝ Lekpoyi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
translation_verse: Nya fǝ Kapernaum eto umǝ, bubu fanlɛ fǝ babesǝ fǝ ku fǝ betidi kato eso? Bɔɔtsylɔsǝ mi, bato mi bakpe lǝ ute!”

english_verse: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Nnyǝ Obonsam se ófankli útsyi kato úbǝ kasɔ fe ofamfa seka.

english_verse: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
translation_verse: Bɛnɔ kukɔnɔ! Ntǝ mi ǝsuǝale bǝ biofo buńyǝ lǝ bawa ku bakpoe ǝsuǝ. Ku lǝ beso olontidi eto ǝsuaǝle kenke ani nya kulesa saa embuyifo mi.

english_verse: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Kɔfi eto oteasa utsyuǝ ǝbǝ nkǝ woato Yesu obe. Nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Oteasa, be ele bǝ nyifo fɛ lǝ nyǝ nkpǝ manlo?”

english_verse: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
translation_verse: Nya osani ǝmǝ dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “La Bonamute Onanto ku fǝ utu kenke, ku fǝ lɛwɔnɔ kenke ku fǝ ǝsuǝale kenke ku fǝ nsusu kenke tsya, nya laala fǝ ɔyimitsyuǝ fe fǝ ǝsuǝ ke.”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝǝ, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ákpe disuǝyuǝ lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ kenke nya Yesu lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Anto, kato ku kasɔ eto Onamute, maasɔ fǝ ani, itsyise asa nnya féfi fákula bekuente ku babesibe, ǝlǝkǝ nnya áte mba lenni kulesa ku mba nte fe bekpefi. Sitintii, Anto, nko nkpe fǝ ɔkɔɛ nɛ.”

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: Nya usi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔ Anto éfi asa nnya kenke utǝ mɛ. Kuutsyuǝ saa enyi nwǝ ni Onanto eto Ubi ǝmǝ, enso Onanto mate hã. Nya kuutsyuǝ saa lenyi utidi wǝ Anto ǝmǝ tsya ni, enso wǝ Ubi ɔni kplo wǝ mate hã ku betidi ba Ubi ǝmǝ dílǝkǝ nkǝ bate Anto ǝmǝ tsya.”

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lɛtsyi ɔ́wuninsǝ óbe wǝ bakasebi bamǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ mate buɛɛ nkǝ, “Ǝsuǝ yuǝ mi sitinti bǝ bɛlɛ asa nnya bɛnlɛ bunyǝ mfo bunyǝ!

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Be Asawɔniwɔni Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyi? Nya ntsyǝ faanɔ ditiki nǝmǝ kasɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Átɛyi ɛlɛkɛ nko. Yifo nko ete ebunyǝ nkpǝ manlo.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se kɔfi eto oteasa ǝmǝ léla lǝ utǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ lɛbɛ esoo, usi ɔ́tɔ Yesu nkǝ, “Owoe ni mɔ ɔyimitsyuǝ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Lefe ditsyuǝ nǝ léfe mǝǝ, osani utsyuǝ étsyi Yerusalem ɔ́tɔ lǝ Yeriko nya beyu dímuǝ wǝ lǝ kusu. Báblɛ wǝ nya bǝlǝkǝ wǝ bikǝ kenke lǝ ǝsuǝ nya báyani wǝ betsyǝ mfó lǝ ukpǝ ku mǝnkpǝ eto nti.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Íbǝ bǝ Onanto eto usumunyǝ utsyuǝ ǝbǝ ɔlɛ bɔyɛ kafe lǝ kusu komǝ mfó. Fɛɛ se únyǝ utidi ǝmfo lǝ kusu ɔflɔ, ɔ́yɛ lǝ lekpake bamba fe údu.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Nko kǝ Lewinyǝ, utsyuǝ tsya díbǝ ɔ́yɛ mfo ɔnlɛ bofe. Nya se únyǝ osani ǝmǝ, wuntsya áyɛ lǝ ɔflɔ bamba ófe údu.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Samarianyǝ utsyuǝ wǝ dibǝ ɔ́yɛ lǝ kusu mfo ɔnlɛ bofe, ǝbǝ útu utidi ǝmǝ. Nya se únyǝ wǝ, wǝ asa ǝnǝ wǝ kafo.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Ɔ́yɛ úsu wǝ ɔflɔ nya úfi nnuǝbi ku ntǝ ókpo lǝ wǝ afabe nyamǝ ǝsuǝ nya ókle nnya kenke, nya útsyi osani ǝmǝ útǝkǝ lǝ wǝ ubuki ǝsuǝ. Nya úfi wǝ úsuko bafɔ eto kasɔnɔkɔ nya ɔ́lɛ wǝ ola óbe wǝ.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Se kale disiǝ, úlǝkǝ atabi ǝtsyuǝ útǝ nwǝ laabe bafɔ eto kasɔnɔkɔ kamǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Kalɛ wǝ ola. Nya lǝ nsinkli mbǝ nlɛ bofe mfoo, moofo fǝ lesa nǝ ke fesi fábiasǝ lǝ wǝ bobe ǝsuǝ.’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Yesu lɛtɔ afi eto oteasa ǝmǝ nkǝ, “Nya lǝ fǝ nsusu ǝsuǝ, betidi itsyǝ bamfo nti eto owoe lǝ fǝ ǝnyǝsa ǝsuǝ, ni utidi wǝ didufǝ lǝ bewudifɔ bamfo eto ani eto ɔyimitsyuǝ?”

english_verse: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
translation_verse: Afi eto oteasa ǝmǝ ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Utidi wǝ dinyǝ wǝ kaminsǝ.” Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Fintsya su lǝ esu eyifo nko.” Yesu Ésu Marta Ku Maria Ɔflɔ.

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Se Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi lɛyɛ lǝ kusu bantɔ, bésu bedufǝ kebuto kǝtsyuǝ. Usio utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Marta ákpa wǝ úsuko wǝ diyo.

english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Ɔyimisio utsyuǝ akɛ Marta wǝ baakpo Maria. Usio ǝmfo ǝsiǝ lǝ Yesu ɔflɔ nya ɔnlɛ wǝ asa nnya ɔnlɛ bote bɔnɔ.

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: Diyo bobe eto asa nnya dísu ítǝkǝ lǝ Marta mate ǝsuǝ étsyiko wǝ bulǝ. Nya úbǝ ɔ́tɛyi Yesu nkǝ, “Bonamute, yenyifo fǝ kulesa bǝ mɔ ɔyimisio áyani diyo bobe eto asa buyifo nyamfó kenke utǝkǝ mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ? Tɛyi wǝ lǝ ubǝ ɔwunsǝko mɛ!”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “O Marta! Be kase fanlɛ ǝsuǝ bulǝ lǝ asa kpǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lesa lɔni ete loohiã bǝ babɛbɛ. Nya lesa nǝmǝ ni nǝ Maria dílǝkǝ nɛ. Nya bensiembofo ni lǝ wǝ ani.”

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: “Bɛtɔ asa lǝ befi bǝtǝ mi. Bɛbɛbɛ asa lǝ binyǝ. Bɛsɔ siku lǝ basini mi.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Mimba ni babuayifo kulaa tsya yi kase buifi asa sɛɛ bitǝ mi bebi. Nya ntsyǝ yuutsyǝ ku Boanto wǝ nkpe kato itǝ bo? Ntsyǝ yoofó bubǝ bǝ wǝmbufi wǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ utǝ betidi ba laatɔ wǝ?” Betidi Bǝtsyuǝ Ǝnǝ Etiki Itsyi Lǝ Yesu Ku Beelsebul Eto Ǝsuǝale Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
translation_verse: “Nya fǝ ɔyɛtsyuǝ ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi fǝ nkǝ, ‘Mǝmbǝ ǝlǝ mɛ! Bonko mɔ bebi ebolabe nya búti siku lǝ diyo. Mensi embofo botaka mɔmɔ kǝbǝ kǝtǝ fǝ kulesa.’

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: “Nya mimba ni bebiantomǝǝ, biefi owa kǝtǝ lǝ lɛkpɛ nǝ ubi lɛtɔ mi eto kɛnyɛ?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ bɛlɛ ola botoo, bɛtɛyi biǝnkǝ, ‘Bo Anto, tǝ lǝ bakpasǝ fǝ diye. Fǝ sekadidi lǝ sibǝ. Beyifo lǝ fǝ lelabi ǝsuǝ lǝ kasɔ mfo fe kase inte kato.

english_verse: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ efi bo abua atsyɛ bo, fe kase bo tsya leefi katsyɛ utidi saa wǝ laata bo kebu. Nya mǝntǝ lǝ budufǝ lǝ botokabe ǝsuǝ nya le ǝlǝkǝ bo etsyiko lǝ abua ǝsuǝ.’ ”

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Itsyise utsyuǝ saa wǝ laatɔ asaa, bǝǝtǝ wǝ. Nya utsyuǝ saa wǝ laabɛbɛɛ, wǝǝnyǝ. Nya utsyuǝ saa wǝ laasɔ sikuu, bɔɔsini wǝ.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Fe kase Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi Yona eto ale díbǝ itǝ lǝ Ninivefɔ ǝsuǝǝ, nko ke Utidi eto Ubi tsya eto ale luubǝ fe ditsyǝbi itǝ mmi eto betidi nɛ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ Yesu lɛtɛyi asa nyamfoo, usio utsyuǝ wǝ lɛkɛ lǝ betidi bamǝ nti áfa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Onanto lǝ okpo usio wǝ lélé fǝ ku nwǝ dítǝ fǝ lɛmi fáyimi atsyɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Lefe lɔnii, Yesu ákala lɛwɔnɔ bua nǝ lǝǝndi etiki ketsyiko lǝ osani utsyuǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso se lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ lɛbɔ lǝ utidi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ ko, utidi ǝmǝ étsyiko etiki budi. Nyamfo éyifo betidi ba díńyǝ mfó yanii.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Nya bǝtsyuǝ tsya léla bǝ baato Yesu babe eso bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ úyifo ayanisa ǝtsyuǝ lǝ ulǝkǝ ote nkǝ Onanto ɔflɔ utsyi.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Yesu wǝ ate mǝ nsusu lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Sekakedikɔ saa sia lema akpa akpa nya mǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃ nlɛ bɔkpɔ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ ǝsuǝ, sekakedikɔ siamfo eboba. Nya diyo nǝ ke eto betidi lǝǝńyǝ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ ǝsuǝ, diyo nǝmǝ eboba.

english_verse: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
translation_verse: Nyaso lǝ boma kpe lǝ Obonsam eto sekakedikɔɔ, ntsyǝ fɛ wǝ sekakedikɔ siamǝ luunyǝ? Bɛlɛ bɔtɛyi biǝnkǝ maala awɔnɔ bua kǝlǝkǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Beelsebul eto ǝsuǝale nnya wǝǝtǝ mɛ eso.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Beelsebul ǝsuǝ maala mǝǝ, bawoe ǝsuǝ mba ntǝkǝko mi laayɛ kala awɔnɔ bua kǝlǝkǝ lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ? Mi kosate eto mba ntǝkǝko mi lɛ bulǝkǝ kate nkǝ bidia baatɛyi.

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: “Lǝ ǝsuǝaletidi utsyuǝ ǝmuǝ ǝsuǝ ku wǝ bemamakǝ uńyǝ ɔlɛ wǝ diyo bosee, te fǝ bike bia nte wǝ embonya.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi Farisifɔ! Itsyise biala biǝ biǝsiǝ lǝ nsiǝkɔ kplɛ bo nso lǝ ntsyakɔ. Nya biala biǝ betidi kǝsiǝsǝ mi ku dibu lǝ obia.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ kɔfi eto oteasa ɔni lǝ mǝ nti átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Oteasa, lǝ atɛyi nyamfo nkoo, áka bontsya.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: “Lǝ lɛwɔnɔ bua ábɔ lǝ utidi ǝsuǝǝ, diesu katsyilama nlebe nlebe bo lekoe kabɛbɛ kaka diɔkɛ ditsyǝ ɔnwɛ. Nya lǝ díǝnnyǝ kedufǝkɔɔ, diatɛyi ǝsuǝ nkǝ, ‘Muusinkli nsu mɔ diyo nǝ mínsiǝ kɔ.’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Nyaso diesinkli kesu kabe ǝkǝnyǝ nkǝ bákpa diyo nǝmǝ kafo kɛnkɛnkɛ nya lesa saa nte kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya esoo, diabɛbɛ awɔnɔ bua akuanse nnya nkpe disiale enso ni nya bǝǝbǝ bǝ lǝ bǝsiǝ mfo. Lǝ kalosǝkɔ, utidi ǝmfo eto nkpǝ bo woǝsiǝ abia kaso mbo úsiǝ kɔ kulaa tsya.” Yesu Áte Asa Itsyi Lǝ Utidi Wǝ Nkpe Le Disuǝyuǝ Kosate Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Nya Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Betidi ba laanɔ Onanto eto ditiki nya beeyifo lǝ ni ǝsuǝ, mǝ bomu ele bǝ bǝnyǝ disuǝnyuǝ nɛ!” Yesu Áni Nkǝ Weembuyifo Ayanisa.

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Íyifo Farisinyǝ ǝmǝ buɛ se únyǝ nkǝ Yesu mǝmflǝ kɔni fe kase mǝ lekpa lete fɛ útsyiko budi.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi Farisifɔ! Biefi mi edisa ku mi nyoma eto bekolenkǝ ku seko ku afantosi akpa akpa, nnya laanyi fififi eto lekpafosile kǝtǝ Onanto. Fɛɛ bɛni asa sɛɛ buyifo lǝ kɔnukualɛsu ǝsuǝ nya biannla Onanto tsya. Nyamfo ni lesa nǝ ele bǝ biyifo bɛkɔ lǝ nnya bímfi bɛnlɛ buyifo koko, nya yanle bǝ bilǝkǝ ku kuǝtsyuǝ bitsyɛ kama.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Nya se betidi bamǝ dítsyiko bufui lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝǝ, úsukosǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bebe kase mi mmi eto betidi nkpe sibusufɔ! Ayanisa eto bulǝkǝ kate nya baala bǝ bǝǝnyǝ, fɛɛ bembunyǝ anyinisa saa enso nya díbǝ lǝ Yona ǝsuǝ seka.

english_verse: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ diyi fɛfɛ eto Lekpoyi ǝsuǝǝ, Besio eto Oka wǝ dítsyi Ɔnwɛdikue ebotaka uńyǝ nya woote mi lɛnimi lǝ lɛwɔ olo mi dikpi, itsyise ótaka útsyi lǝ wǝ kasɔ ka letso famfamfa úsu nkǝ lǝ ɔnɔ Oka Salomo eto kobetete eto asa. Fɛɛ nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ utidi wǝ nso Salomoo, wǝ ni nwǝ mfo nɛ.

english_verse: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Ninivefɔ ebotaka bǝńyǝ lǝ Lekpoyi Fɛfɛ ǝsuǝ nya boolo mi mmi eto betidi bamfo dikpi. Itsyise lefe nǝ Yona lɛtɛyi mǝ Onanto eto ditikii, bátsyi báwuninsǝ nya báyɛ mǝ abua buyifo. Fɛɛ nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ, utidi ɔni utsyuǝ kpe lǝ mi nti mfo wǝ lɛmɔ onso Yona kulaa tsya! Yesu Ǝlǝkǝ Lesa Nǝ Lǝǝtǝ Utidi Lǝǝyǝnde Óte.

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: “Utidi kuutsyuǝ saa anto okandiɛ kefi kakula nye kakpe lǝ asa kalɔ. Bomu weefi wǝ kǝtǝkǝ lǝ wǝ kǝtǝkǝkɔ kaka betidi ba lǝǝbǝ mfó luunyǝ asa wue.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Utidi eto ǝnǝmi eyifo fe okandiɛ itǝ wǝ utidi ǝmǝ. Lǝ fǝ ǝnǝmi ǝnyǝ asa kukɔnɔɔ, fǝ utidi ǝmǝ kenke tsya, yuutǝ ǝnyǝ asa ekpa kenke. Fɛɛ lǝ fǝ ǝnǝmi ǝmbǝ kɔnɔɔ, te fǝ fǝ utidi ǝmǝ kenke kpe lǝ ketuntu ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
translation_verse: Bieninǝ! Inni Onanto ǝmǝ ete ni nwǝ díyifo asa nnya kenke eto kafo ku kama lee ntsyǝ?

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi sitinti, itsyise béto edi kukɔnɔ bitǝ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi. Fɛɛ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi bamfoo, lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝǝ, mi bana mǝ lélo mǝ.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Se Yesu dídi etiki ólosǝǝ, Farisinyǝ ɔni utsyuǝ ákpadi wǝ nkǝ ubǝ udi lesa lǝ wǝ diyo. Mfó nya úsu úsiǝ lǝ kasɔ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ lǝ udi lesa.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Nyaso Bonamute Yesu átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Mɔmɔɔ, mi Farisifɔ biǝflǝ mi kedikpɛ ku akɔbe eto kama, fɛɛ lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝǝ, biyi ku lefomfi ku asa buayifoyifo sɔ̃ɔ̃.

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Bifi lesa saa nǝ nkpe lǝ mi kakpɛ ku akɔbe kafo bitǝ bakombo bǝ lǝ mi lesa saa lǝ ele kɛnkɛnkɛ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi, itsyise bite fe edi nnya mbikǝ lǝ kasɔ bǝǝnyǝ kǝtsyǝ lekpo, nya betidi laayɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ kafe itsyise baante nko.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Nya Yesu dítǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi afi eto bateasa tsya. Itsyise bietsyisǝ betidi bikǝ bia bembofo butsyi. Nya biemfi nsusu kabe biǝ biawunsǝko mǝ lǝ mi butsyi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Nya mi kosatee, bɛtɔnɔ lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝ biǝ lesa nǝ mi anto mǝ díyifo álɛkɛ. Mǝ lélo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi bamǝ nya mi léto mǝ edi.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso Onanto lǝ wǝ kobetete ǝsuǝ lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mootsyese mɔ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ku batɔnkpe nya boolo mǝ bǝtsyuǝ nya bootǝkǝko bǝtsyuǝ kama.’

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Nyaso mi mmi eto betidi kenkee, boonǝ mi koto lǝ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba kenke bálo idu taa kawunsiǝ eto kasɔ ketsyikokɔ kǝ.

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: Idu lefe nǝ bálo Abel ibǝ eyo Sakaria wǝ bálo lǝ kedikǝtǝkɔ eto leba ku kakɛnkɛkɔ eto nti. Sitinti minlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, Onanto ebunǝ mi, mmi eto betidi ato lǝ nyamfo kenke eso!

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ mi kɔfi eto bateasa! Itsyise diyo nǝ kafo kobe nkpee, bíti ni siku bílǝkǝ safui bídunko. Fɛɛ mi kosate áni biǝ biembudufǝ lǝ ni kafo. Nya bieti betidi bamba ba nlɛ bokplatsya bǝ beedufǝ lǝ ni tsya kusu.”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Se Yesu dídu lǝ kaka mfóo, Farisifɔ ku kɔfi eto bateasa étsyiko bunyǝnki nya bátɔ wǝ etiki kpǝ ku lɛblɔfi.

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Nya banlɛ kusu bɔbɛbɛ lǝ kase bɔɔyɛ bǝmuǝ wǝ lǝ wǝ ditiki ǝsuǝ bǝ ɔtɛyi kabua.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: Se betidi akpi akpi bǝtsyuǝ díbǝ bátsya mfóo, itǝ bǝ bétsyiko bǝtsyuǝ bukǝ kamanklii, Yesu étsyiko wǝ bakasebi etiki budi katɛyi nkǝ, “Bebe sinini lǝ Farisifɔ eto bidiabidia eto asayifoyifo nnya laayansǝ betidi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Lǝ Onanto lǝǝtǝ sefa kɔlɔkɔ komfo eto oklee, sefa sia lǝǝńyǝ mmi ete nya lǝ kǝsiǝsǝ ete baala se kato kakpé lǝ ditefɛnku kǝmiǝǝ, ntsyǝ yuubǝ bǝ Onanto ǝmfo kǝ embutǝ mi alɛsa nnya lǝmboso sefa eto ale nyamǝ? Be dítǝ eso mi bofokanɔ lǝmbǝ ale mmǝ?

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Bomu bikebu disi ketsyiko lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ǝsuǝ nya uufi nyamfo kenke utǝ mi. Bifi Mi Kusubebe Bitǝkǝ Lǝ Onanto Eto Sekakedikɔ Eto Asa Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: “Bayɛtsyuǝ, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, bianté sikpi betidi ba laafo etidisuǝ ete bolo nya bensiembofo kulesa buyifo lǝ nnya kama.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Mi disi eto sɛwɔni kulaa tsya, Onanto yi se leklambi. Nyasoo, biensite sikpi itsyise befo koya beso baklɔbɛ kpǝ. Bińyǝ Lǝ Yesu Eto Kama Kekleke.

english_verse: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
translation_verse: “Mi ditsyukpa kɛkɛ nǝ ntǝkǝko mɛ, biante sikpi. Itsyise iyifo Onanto eto lelabi bǝ utǝ mi kǝsiǝkɔ lǝ wǝ sekakedikɔ.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Bifi mi bikǝ bia nte mi bisu bete lǝ bifi atabi nyamǝ bitǝ bakombo. Lǝ bɛbɛbɛ otabitobe wǝ lǝǝmbobia bitsyǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ Onanto eto. Mfóo, nya mi bikǝ bia lefo koya luutsyǝ kpoo nya biembolo kulefe. Itsyise uyu saa ǝmbofo budufǝ mfo nya balɛ lembobiasǝ mi.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: Itsyise Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ebote mi lesa nǝ biɔtɛyi lǝ lefe nǝmǝ ayo.” Lekpa Itsyi Lǝ Otabinyǝ Wǝ Leeninǝ Utsyuǝ Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ Onanto átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Fǝ utidimumu! Ketsye kamfo hã moofo fǝ nkpǝ lǝ fǝ ani. Nya owoe luudi asa nnya fála fǝ alɔ ǝtsyǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ mfo?’ ”

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi kasini minkǝ, utidi wǝ ke lɔɔtɛyi lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmi nkǝ uyifo mɔ olee, Utidi Eto Ubi tsya ebofo wǝ fe wǝ ole lǝ Onanto eto batɔnkpe eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ utidi wǝ lɔɔni mɛ lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmii, Utidi Eto Ubi tsya ebɔni wǝ lǝ Onanto eto batɔnkpe kenke eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: “Lǝ ibǝ bǝ bakpa mi besuko Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ nye baka eto ǝnǝmi bǝ baakponko mi lekpoo, biǝntǝ lǝ ilǝ mi lǝ lesa nǝ biusu bɛtɛyi ǝsuǝ nye kase biota mi ǝsuǝ disi.

english_verse: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
translation_verse: Utidi utsyuǝ ákɛ mfo lǝ betidi nti wǝ lɛtɛyi Yesu nkǝ, “Oteasa, tɛyi mɔ ɔyimi fǝnkǝ usie bo anto eto bikǝ bia utsyǝ bo lǝ mfo mɔ bele.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Mfó Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Osani, owoe dítǝ mɛ kusu nkǝ nsie bikǝ nye nyifo okponkpo lǝ mi inuǝ eto bikǝ busie ǝsuǝ?”

english_verse: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝmblɛ nkǝ, “Bitǝkǝ ǝnǝ lǝ bebe kukɔnɔ lǝ bikǝtanta ku kunǝnkpǝ saa eto okle ǝsuǝ, itsyise utidi eto nkpǝ sitinti manyɛ lǝ bikǝ bia nkpe wǝ, lǝ uyifo otabinyǝ kulaa tsya ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eso úfi nsusu nkǝ, ‘Se mǝnnyǝ kaka muutsyǝ mɔ bidikǝ biamfoo, be ele bǝ nyifo?

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: “ ‘Lesa nǝ ele bǝ nyifo ni bǝ mooba mɔ kenwu ka kɔ nya mooto kafɔfɔ ka lɔɔmɔ eso nka kɔ. Mfó muunyǝ kǝtsyǝkɔ itǝ mɔ bidikǝ ku bikǝ kenke.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Nyaso nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ biankpɔnko ǝsuǝ lǝ lesa nǝ biudi bisiǝ nkpǝ nye lesa nǝ biɔlɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: “Bibu bebe kase dituwinimi lǝǝmǝ kanɔ bobee. Kaanta olokotutu nya keendi kafia kefi keti ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ Oka Salomo kulaa tsya lǝ wǝ kɔlɔkɔ eto diyǝnde ǝsuǝǝ, mánnɔ bobe fe dituwunimi nǝ lǝǝmǝ mfó.

english_verse: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
translation_verse: Nyaso biante sikpi lǝ bɛsɛsǝ ǝnǝmi bikatɔ biǝnkǝ be biudi nye be biunǝ.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Itsyise betidi ba dímamfo Onanto bánɔɔ, mǝ ni mba leefi mǝ ǝnǝmi kǝtǝkǝ lǝ asa nyamfo ǝsuǝ nɛ. Mi Anto yi nkǝ asa nyamfo kenke afe itǝ mi.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ lǝ uyifoyifo ǝmǝ átɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mɔ Onamute ésu kusu wǝǝmbubǝ mlǝ,’ nyaso útsyiko budi kǝnǝ kǝkpǝ ntǝ nya woablɛ beyifoyifo fɛfɛ ba ni basani ku besio kenke ku disialee,

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Bíbu biǝ mbǝ kawunsiǝ miǝ mǝǝtǝ mi diyuǝ? Owo lo! Inni diyuǝsǝ, bomu nti busǝnsǝ eso.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Beyifoyifo ba Onamute luubǝ unyǝ nkǝ bǝńyǝ balɛ bose itǝ wǝ bubǝǝ, ebunyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti! Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, wɔɔwuninsǝ kafia lǝ lɛmɔkɔsi ɔtsyimi, nya wuutǝ nkǝ bǝsiǝ lǝ wǝ kedikɔ, nya wǝ kosate luutǝ mǝ lesa bedi.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: Lǝ ibǝ bǝ ntsyenti nye kolensiǝnto kplɛ úbǝ nya únyǝ mǝ se bǝńyǝ bensǝkǝ wǝ bubǝǝ, nte disuǝyuǝ nǝ eto okle wookpe mǝ!

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, bete biǝ lǝ oyosate ebote lefe nǝ uyu luubǝ uyu wǝǝ, eni wǝmbutǝ wǝ kusu nkǝ oba udufǝ wǝ diyo.

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Petro lɛtɔ Yesu nkǝ, “Bonamute, bo fanlɛ lekpa nǝmfo bɔtɛyi kǝtǝ, lee dińyǝ itǝ utidi saa?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Nya Yesu ditǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Uyifoyifo ni nwǝ ntǝkǝ ǝnǝ nya weeyifo keyifo lǝ ɔnukualɛ eto kusu ǝsuǝ, wǝ ni nwǝ wǝ Onamute leefi kǝtsyǝ lǝ wǝ beyifoyifo fɛfɛ eto kɛnyɛ nɛ. Nya wǝ ni futsyǝ luutǝ wǝ beyifoyifo fɛfɛ mǝ didisa lǝ lefe sɛɛ ǝsuǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Bebe, lǝ uyifoyifo ǝmǝ afo nya woeyifo ke kukɔnɔ fɛ wǝ Onamute díbǝǝ, wuunyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti lǝ uyifoyifo ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Sitintii, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ wǝ Onamute ǝmfó ebufi uyifoyifo ǝmǝ utsyǝ lǝ wǝ bikǝ kenke eto kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: wǝ Onamute ebubǝ lǝ lefe nǝ wǝ uyifoyifo lenyi, ɔnnlɛ kusu bobe. Mfó Onamute ǝmǝ ebunǝ wǝ koto kekleke ko dinyǝ itǝ mba ditidi ato bamfó eto okle.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: “Uyifoyifo wǝ nyi lesa nǝ wǝ Onamute laala nkǝ uyifo utǝ wǝ fɛ woani ni buyifo se woanla nkǝ woeyifo lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ esoo, ablɛ hã buukablɛ wǝ nya luutǝ ofo afabe lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Fɛɛ uyifoyifo wǝ lennyi lesa nǝ wǝ Onamute laala nkǝ uyifo fɛ uyifo lesa nǝ dińyǝ itǝ kotonǝnǝ, sɛkɛ ete boonǝ wǝ koto. Utidi saa wǝ bǝtǝ asa kpǝǝ, nko ke ele bǝ uyifo asa kpǝ nɛ. Nyaso utidi saa wǝ bǝtǝ asa kpǝ isi ensoo, nko ke ele bǝ uyifo keyifo isi eso nɛ. Betidi Eto Nti Ebusǝnsǝ Lǝ Yesu Eso.

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: “Mfi Onanto eto kotonǝnǝ ko nte fe ute seka mboko kawunsiǝ. Nya nla sitinti minkǝ eni utsyiko buńyǝ koko.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto bulǝ kplɛ butsyuǝ kpe ele bǝ mfo fe kase ketu leetsyi utidi kedunko. Nya membunyǝ lɛwɔnɔ nsiǝ isu ese lefe nǝ bulǝ bomfó díbǝ bófe.

english_verse: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
translation_verse: Nya idu mɔmɔ ɛntɔɔ, ayo nnya fe kaka betidi ɛnɔ nkpee, boosensǝ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ ǝsuǝ. Inuǝ ebotaka bǝńyǝ lǝ itsyǝ ǝsuǝ nya itsyǝ lootaka bǝńyǝ lǝ inuǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Ubianto ebotaka uńyǝ lǝ wǝ bebisaibi ǝsuǝ, nya bebisaibi lootaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ anto ǝsuǝ. Ambemǝ ebotaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ bebisiobi ǝsuǝ, nya bebisiobi tsya lootaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ ambemǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya bǝsǝambe mǝ lootaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ bebisaibi eto bǝsiǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bebi eto bǝsiǝ lootaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ bǝsǝambemǝ ǝsuǝ.” Yesu Ǝkǝ Betidi Ba Údinko Etiki Ato.

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi betidi bamfó tsya nkǝ, “Lǝ binyǝ biǝ lɛsɔmiloku ana ditsyi kofe eto diyo kedufǝkɔ lǝ difule lɛlɛ bubǝǝ, biatɛyi mfó ǝmǝ biǝ ɔsɔmi ebubǝ ɔni. Nya yǝǝbǝ kǝtǝ nko.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ binyǝ biǝ ufiebi lɛ bɔsɔ ketsyi Ɔnwɛdukuǝ eti ɔnlɛ bubǝǝ, biatɛyi biǝ utefofo nye diwi ɛbɔkɛ. Nya yǝǝbǝ kǝtǝ nko.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Ǝnǝmi, ǝnǝmi eto betidi, lǝ binyǝ kato ku kasɔ eto ditsyǝbii, biate lefe nǝ ɛnlɛ bote, beso bɛnnlɛ mɔmɔ eto lefe kasɔ bɔnɔ kǝlǝkǝ kate? Yesu Áte Nkǝ Balɔ Betidi Inuǝ Eto Nti Bǝtsyǝ.

english_verse: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
translation_verse: “Beso biamfo budufǝ lǝ asa kǝmiǝ kabe lɛsɛ nǝ ele bǝ biyifo?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Lǝ utidi utsyuǝ ákpadi fǝ etiki lǝ lekpokakpokɔ nkǝ fonko wǝ ákɛ etiki ǝtsyuǝǝ, yɛ lǝ kusu saa ǝsuǝ lǝ alɔ etiki nyamǝ ǝtsyǝ fɛ lǝ bidufǝ lekpokakpokɔ. Leni nkoo, wuufi fǝ utǝ bakponkpo nya mba lǝǝmuǝ betidi luufi fǝ bato bakpe lǝ diyo.

english_verse: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
translation_verse: Nya nlɛ fǝ butǝ bote minkǝ ebɔkɛ mfó isu ese lefe nǝ fafó fǝ kotonǝnǝ eto kayo kenke falosǝ.”

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, betidi bǝtsyuǝ ákɛ mfó ba lɛtɛyi Yesu kase Pilato lélo Galileafɔ bǝtsyuǝ lǝ lefe nǝ bǝkǝtǝ Onanto kedi.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Bibu biǝnkǝ se bálo Galileafɔ bamfo nko esoo, mǝ abua nnya béyifo amɔ eso Galileafɔ fɛfɛ kenke?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: Owo lo! Inni nko ini kon! Fɛɛ nnya míntɛyi mi ni bǝ, lǝ bianyɛ mi asa bua buyifoo, nko ke mi tsya luubǝ bikpǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Biyi kase Onanto eto Sekadidi laayɛ kesensǝ? Nya be ele bǝ bufi nnya bokatesǝ ko?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: Owo lo! Inni nko ini kon! Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ lǝ bientsyi mi akɔɛsi bɛwuninsǝ, nko ke mimblɛ kenke luubǝ bikpǝ fe mǝ ke nɛ.” Yesu Átɛyi Lekpa Itsyi Lǝ Kudiyi Ko Laannwɔ Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ lekpa nǝmfo nkǝ, “Lefe ditsyuǝ nǝ lefe mǝ, osani utsyuǝ ákɛ mfó wǝ lɛkɛ kudiyi kutsyuǝ lǝ wǝ ɔkuɛ. Úsu nkǝ lǝ usu ɔbɛbɛ kuyi komǝ eto ebibi, fɛɛ wǝnnyǝ ku ǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ nwǝ laabe ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Bonamute, yani ko lǝ kusi kunyǝ lɛlɛ lɔni kplo lǝ bobe. Muuku ko kalɔ ntsyilama nkpo asa nnya lɔɔlɛkǝsǝ kasɔ kamǝ lǝ bobe.

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Nya lǝ kofó ebibi kpǝ bɔnwɔɔ, ɛlɛkɛ. Fɛɛ lǝ koamfo bɔnwɔ lɛlɛ nǝ luubǝ, ebofo ko bola kakpo kasɔ.’ ” Kǝnǝ Bio Esi Kǝbǝ Itsyi Lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi lɔni ǝsuǝǝ, Yesu ákɛ mfó úkate asa lǝ Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ kǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Se Yesu dínyǝ wǝǝ, ókpadi wǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Usio, báyankli fǝ lǝ fǝ bufi ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Bonamute dítǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mi, ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi, mii, biǝlǝkǝ bebuki ba leeyifo mi keyifo nye leetsyi mi bikǝ ketsyiko lǝ kakuɛ nya biakpa mǝ kesuko ntu kǝnǝkɔ lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi.

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Etiki nnya Yesu dílǝkǝ ɔ́tɛyi mfo ǝtǝ bǝ kofani ǝmuǝ wǝ balo, nya betidi bamba dínyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ anyinisa nnya kenke ɔnlɛ bulǝkǝ kate eso. Yesu Éfi Onanto Eto Sekakedikɔ Ókatesǝ Ko Diyibibi Nǝ Nso Bɔfɛ.

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfo kǝmiǝ, Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Kɔse lǝ abɔ mfo edu mɔmɔ mɔmɔ lǝ esu kalebe bamba, itsyise Oka Herodes ala nkǝ wǝ alo fǝ.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Nyaso ele bǝ ndiǝ ndiǝ ndiǝ nyɛ nsu sɛtɔ mmi isu ese kusɛɛ ku kǝsiǝsǝ. Itsyise yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ balo Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi ku utsyuǝ sa lǝ kalebesa enso lǝ Yerusalem eto umǝ kafo.

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Nya betidi bǝtsyuǝ ba ni bafɛfɛ, mǝ luubǝ bedi betutɛ. Nya mba ni betutɛ eto bǝtsyuǝ, mǝ luubǝ bafiani bafɛfɛ nɛ.” Yesu Kpe Lelabi Utǝ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Mfo ǝmǝ nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bisu bɛtɛyi wǝ obiasǝtidi ǝmfó biǝnkǝ mɔ nlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ, ‘Mmi, moola awɔnɔ bua nlǝkǝ nya mootsya betidi efimbi, efimbi nya banlɛ isu ese kusɛɛ. Nya lǝ diyi tsyǝfǝ ǝsuǝ, moolosǝ mɔ keyifo.’

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Bebe! Osani utsyuǝ éfi diyibibi ditsyuǝ nǝ nso bɔfɛ óto lǝ wǝ ɔkuɛ. Se diyibibi nǝmǝ díkǝǝ, díbǝ lébe léfiani kuyi kplɛ kutsyuǝ eto kɔlɔmɔ ǝsuǝ baklɔbɛ díbǝ báto ayo bakpe.” Yesu Éfi Onanto Eto Sekakedikɔ Ókatesǝ Ko Ǝnyǝnkisǝsa.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Yesu esi ɔ́tɛyi mǝ lekpa bamba nkǝ, “Be muufi Onanto sekadidi nkatesǝ ko?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Yesu ésu ɔ́yɛ lǝ kǝmǝ ku mbuto kafo nya óte betidi asa lǝ lefe nǝ ɔntɔ lǝ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Bekpe ale lǝ bɛyɛ lǝ kesie ka nso bɔfɛ bidufǝ. Itsyise betidi kpǝ ebola sitinti bǝ baayɛ mfo lǝ bedufǝ, fɛɛ bǝmbofo budufǝ.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Lefe ebubǝ leyo itǝ buudufǝ nya Onamute ǝmǝ lootaka uńyǝ nya wuuti kesie kamǝ. Nya mimba dibu biińyǝ lǝ lekple biu kasɔ siku katɛyi biǝnkǝ, ‘Onamute, sini bo!’ “Fɛɛ Onamute ǝmǝ ebulǝkǝ kɛnyɛ ɔtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Minnyi mi ku kaka bitsyi!’

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: “Nya biɔtɛyi biǝnkǝ, ‘Bo ku fǝ lídi búnǝ, nya fáte asa lǝ bo umǝ!’

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ usi ɔɔtɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Minnyi mi! Bidu mɛ lǝ akɔ mi babuayifotidi.’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: “Mfo ǝmǝ nya nku buwi ku animi bunǝ lɔɔkɛ lǝ lefe nǝ biunyǝ Abraham ku Isak ku Yakob Ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi kenke lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ lǝ lefe nǝ Onanto léla mi ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ biendufǝ mfó.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Betidi ebutsyi kofekatokɔ ku diyo kedufǝkɔ bǝbǝ. Nya mba ditsyi ɔnwɛ kato ku ɔnwɛ dikuǝ ebotsya nya boodi bǝnǝ lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem! Fǝ betidi laalo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi, nya biakpɔ aba Onanto eto batɔnkpe ba ótsyese mi. Lǝ lefe kpǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nla miǝ lǝ nkpa fǝ betidi ntsyǝ ditsyu fe kase ukusɛsiǝle leesensǝ wǝ mbamba keti wǝ bebi, fɛɛ áni.

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, Onanto ebulǝkǝ wǝ ato utsyiko lǝ umǝ ǝmfo. Nya lǝ ntɛyi mi sitinti minkǝ, biensiembunyǝ mɛ bio isu ese lefe nǝ biudi etiki bɛtɛyi biǝnkǝ, ‘Atɛsǝ itǝ nwǝ luubǝ lǝ Boanto Onanto eto diye ǝsuǝ!’ ”

english_verse: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
translation_verse: Mfó nya osani utsyuǝ wǝ eto ani ku akpa lɛfɔɔ, díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lɛtɔ kɔfi eto bateasa ku Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ nkǝ, “Bo kɔfi ǝtǝ bo kusu nkǝ batsya utidi lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi ǝsuǝ, lee koǝntǝ bo kusu?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bámfo kɛnyɛ bufǝ katɛyi kulesa. Mfó ǝmǝ nya Yesu lɛkɔbe utidi ǝmfo ókpa wǝ úsuko kutsyǝ nya ótsya wǝ nya útǝ wǝ kusu údu.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bámfo wǝ ditiki eto mbuayɛ butǝ bio. Yesu Átɛyi Kase Baabokosǝ Ǝsuǝ Kasɔ Ku Kase Baafo Bafɔ.

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Se Yesu dínyǝ kase betidi ba bákpadi báboko mfó eto bǝtsyuǝ nlɛ nsiǝkɔ kplɛ bota kǝtǝ ǝsuǝ, ɔ́tɛyi mǝ lekpa ditsyuǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Lǝ utidi utsyuǝ ákpadi fǝ nkǝ bǝ wǝ kusiǝkakpakɔ, mensu ǝsiǝ lǝ kakplɛkɔ. Itsyise ǝtsyuǝ ókpadi utidi wǝ nkpe dibu onso fǝ tsya oboko mfó.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Itsyise utidi saa wǝ labesǝ ǝsuǝ kato, Onanto ebobokosǝ wǝ kasɔ. Nya utidi saa wǝ laabokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ, Onanto ebobesǝ wǝ kato.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi utidi wǝ lékpadi wǝ nkǝ ubǝ kedikɔ mfó nkǝ, “Lǝ akpadi betidi fǝ bǝbǝ bedi lǝfǝ diyo, mankpadi fǝ bayɛtsyuǝ nye fǝ bayimi nye fǝ betidi nye fǝ bayotsya ba ni batabinyǝ. Itsyise mǝ tsya ebokpadi fǝ nko kǝ bǝ, bǝ lǝ ǝbǝ edi. Nya lǝ ibǝ nko, nyalɛ te fǝ lesa nǝ féyifo fǝtǝ mǝǝ, eto oko banlɛ bulǝkǝ kǝtǝ fǝ nɛ.

english_verse: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
translation_verse: Nyamfo luutǝ Onanto kpo fǝ atsyɔ nɛ. Itsyise betidi bamfó ǝmbofo fǝ kayo bofo lǝ nnya kɛnyɛ kulesa. Onanto ebofo fǝ nya kayo lǝ diyi nǝ ǝsuǝ betidi sɛɛ lootaka betsyi ɔkɔmǝ bǝbǝ nkpǝ.” Bákpadi Betidi Bǝ Bǝbǝ Diyisɛɛ Kedikɔ.

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: “Mfo ǝmǝ nya uyifoyifo ǝmǝ dísu úyifo nko. Nya úsinkli úbǝ ɔ́tɛyi wǝ Onamute nkǝ ókpadi mǝ, fɛɛ nsiǝkɔ kpǝ esi bubu bute.

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Se betidi ba nsi lǝ kedikɔ mfó eto ɔni lɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfo, ɔ́tɛyi Yesu nkǝ, “Sitinti, disuǝyuǝ ebɔkɛ lǝ betidi ba luubǝ bedi lesa lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ lefe nǝ léfe mǝǝ, osani utsyuǝ ákpadi betidi kpǝ lǝ wǝ diyi kplɛ ditsyuǝ eto lesakedikɔ.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Se lefe léyo bǝ betsyiko budi kǝnǝǝ, óla wǝ beyifoyifo ókpe nkǝ batɛyi wǝ betidi ba kenke ókla utsyǝ nkǝ bǝbǝ. Itsyise lesa saa ebɔflɔ!

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ mǝmblɛ kenke étsyiko taflatsyɛ boto. Utidi tutɛ átɛyi uyifoyifo ɔni ǝmǝ nkǝ, ‘Nya kasɔ kǝtsyuǝ nya ele bǝ nsu mbe kǝ. Nyaso tɛyi wǝ fǝnkǝ, “Nlɛ wǝ lekpakpa, mǝmbofo bubǝ.” ’

english_verse: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Obamba tsya nkǝ, ‘Ńnya banantsyui lefosi ba lɔɔwunsǝko mǝ lǝ ɔkuɛ. Nya kusu mintɔ miǝ lǝ nsu nto mǝ mbe lǝ boofo keyifo buyifo. Nyamfo esoo, tɛyi wǝ fǝnkǝ, “Nlɛ wǝ lekpakpa, mǝmbofo bubǝ.” ’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: “Uyifoyifo ɔni ǝmǝ esinkli úsu, úsu ɔ́tɛyi wǝ Onamute asa nyamfo kenke. Mfó kafo ányi wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ tinti nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ uyifoyifo ǝmǝ nkǝ, ‘Kɔse lǝ esu ǝńyǝ lǝ esu ku nlebe nlebe kenke lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ lǝ akpa bakombo ku betidi ba lɛtsyini ku bǝnǝmba ku mba kenke leba bankpo lǝ kasɔ aboko mfo.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Onamute lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Su lǝ esu ayɛ lǝ umǝ ku mbuto eto esu lǝ akpe nkɛale lǝ akpa betidi ba kenke aboko itǝ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ kafo kenke lǝ diyi.

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: Nya nlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi kakpasǝ minkǝ betidi ba kenke mínkpadi miǝ bǝbǝ lesakedikɔ fɛ báni, mǝ kuutsyuǝ saa embutidi mɔ lesa nǝmfo kɛnyɛ babe!’ ” Ebunyǝ Kanyi Lǝ Eyifo Okasebi.

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Lefe ditsyuǝ se betidi kpǝ bǝtsyuǝ dítǝkǝko Yesu, ɔ́tsyi ɔ́wuninsǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Utidi saa wǝ luubǝ mɔ ɔflɔɔ, ǝmbofo mɔ okasebi budi lǝ waanla mɛ kaso wǝ anto ku wǝ usiǝ ku bebi ku wǝ bayimisani ku bayimisio ku wǝ kosate eto ǝsuǝ kulaa tsya.

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa ke dimentsyi wǝ uyikabie ɔyɛ mɔ kayɛkɔ utǝkǝko mɛɛ, mǝnńyǝ utǝ bǝ uyifo mɔ okasebi.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
translation_verse: “Lǝ utidi utsyuǝ aala nkǝ woato diyo kplɛ ditsyuǝ, tutɛ ele bǝ usiǝ kasɔ ubu bikǝ bia kenke loofe ku mi koya kenke obe lǝ okpe atabi nya loohiãa, bǝ woofo diyo nǝmǝ boto kalosǝ fɛ utsyǝ ni kasɔ.

english_verse: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ weenyifo nyamfo, wǝmbofo diyo nǝmǝ boto kalosǝ, lǝ ɔ́sisǝ ni kasɔ kekleke ku abambi fɛ utsyǝ kafati kasɔ boto kulaa tsya. Nya utsyuǝ saa wǝ luunyǝ nii, ebumǝ wǝ katɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
translation_verse: ‘Utidi ǝmfo étsyiko diyo nǝmfo boto kasɔ, fɛɛ wǝmbofo ni boto kalosǝ.’

english_verse: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ unyǝ nkǝ umbǝ ǝsuǝale nyamfo eto okle, wootsyese wǝ batɔnkpe nya boosu batsya ku oka ǝmǝ lǝ lefe nǝ batsyoko bǝtsyuǝ nya bɔɔlɛ wǝǝ lekpakpa nkǝ bayɛ bɔkpɔ lǝ bafiani bayɛtsyuǝ bomu, fɛ lǝ oka ǝmfó lǝ ɔnyi ɔfi kulaa tsya.”

english_verse: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
translation_verse: “Mba kpe alɛkɛ. Fɛɛ lǝ kamini kamǝ alo lǝ mba bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, kusu ku kutsyuǝ saa ensimbǝ ǝsuǝ bookpe kamini lǝ mu bio.

english_verse: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
translation_verse: Yensilɛkɛ bǝ befi mu bakpe balɛkɛsǝ kasɔ, nya yanlɛkɛ itǝ kuku kulaa tsya. Buwi ele bǝ bewi mu. “Nyaso fǝǝ ke ato nnya laanɔ asa kpee, tsyue ato lǝ anɔ!”

english_verse: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
translation_verse: Mootaka ńyǝ nsikli nsu mɔ anto ɔflɔ, lǝ ntɛyi wǝ minkǝ, “Mɔ Anto, nyifo kabua nya mínta Onanto ku fintsya mimbu.

english_verse: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
translation_verse: Nya dikpefile lɛtɛyi wǝ anto nkǝ, ‘Nto, fi mɔ kedikɔ ka luutu mɛ lǝ fǝ bikǝ nti ǝtǝ mɛ mɔmɔ mɔmɔ.’ Lǝ nyamfo eso osani ǝmǝ éfi wǝ bikǝ biamǝ nya úsie mi lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ útǝ wǝ bebi inuǝ bamǝ.

english_verse: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
translation_verse: Údi lesa saa nǝ dítsyǝ wǝ ólosǝ. Lǝ nyamfó eto kamaa, kukpɛnsɛ kekleke kutsyuǝ ǝbǝ lǝ kasɔ kamǝ kenke nyaso kukpɛnsɛ ǝbǝ kúmuǝ wǝ nya kulesa ménsibu wǝ lǝ udi.

english_verse: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
translation_verse: Diyi lɔnii, mba laafo umǝnko kpǝ ku babuatidi bamba ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ bǝ baanɔ wǝ ditiki.

english_verse: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Farisifɔ ku afi eto bateasa dítsyiko dinyǝfi bokpe ku wǝ katɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Utidi ǝmfo aafo babuatidi nya wǝ ku mǝ leedi lesa kulaa tsya!”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: Nyaso Yesu átɛyi mǝ lekpa nǝmfo nkǝ,

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ǝnyǝ wǝǝ, ebunyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti, nya fuutsyi wǝ ǝtǝkǝ lǝ simuǝ.

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Nya fuufi wǝ aboko oto. Nya fookpadi fǝ bayɛtsyuǝ ku mba lǝ koyotsya nya fɔɔtɛyi mǝ fǝnkǝ, ‘Nlɛ disuǝyuǝ bunyǝ tinti, itsyise nnyǝ mɔ osamu wǝ lénya nyaso binyǝnko mɛ disuǝyuǝ!’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way there is more happiness in heaven because of one sinner who turns to God than over
translation_verse: Nya nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, nko ke disuǝyuǝ tinti lɔɔkɛ lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ lǝ obuatidi ɔni wǝ hã lɛtsyi lɛkɔɛsi ɔwuninsǝ enso disuǝyuǝ nǝ boonyǝ lǝ fe betidi awosi ɔwɔnɔmbǝ ɔwɔnɔmbǝ ba dimensihiã bɔtsyi kawuninsǝ ǝsuǝ. Bǝnyǝ Sika Flubia Sia Lénya.

english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: “Nya lǝ usio utsyuǝ kpe sika flubia lefosi nya ónyansǝ sɔnii, be ele bǝ uyifo? Wooto okandiɛ, okpa wǝ diyo kafo kenke, ɔbɛbɛ se lǝ diyo eto akpɔ nnya kenke kukɔnɔ isu ese lefe nǝ unyǝ atabi nyamǝ.

english_verse: Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ unyǝ see, wookpadi wǝ bǝtsyuǝ ku mba nkpe lǝ koyotsya, nya wɔɔtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Nlɛ disuǝyuǝ bunyǝ tinti itsyise nnyǝ mɔ atabi nnya lénya nyaso binyǝ ku mɛ disuǝyuǝ.’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
translation_verse: Nya nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ, nko ke Onanto eto batɔnkpe tsya luunyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ obuatidi ɔni wǝ ditsyi lɛkɔɛsi ɔwuninsǝ eso.” Ubi Wǝ Lénya, Ésinkli Úbǝ Wǝ Anto Ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Yesu ésukosǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Osani utsyuǝ akɛ wǝ bebisaibi inuǝ lɛkɛ.

english_verse: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
translation_verse: “Nya lǝ eyi asiani eto kamaa, dikpefile nǝmǝ éfi asa nnya kenke dítu wǝ óte nya úfi atabi nyamǝ nya údu úsu kusu famfamfa kaka úsu úsiǝ údi nkpǝ bua bua, nya óbiasǝ wǝ atabi nyamǝ kenke.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Nyamfo esoo, úfi ǝsuǝ úsu ɔ́tsyɛ osani utsyuǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ wuukeyifo keyifo saa ka ke eto okle wuutǝ wǝ nke uyifo. Nya osani ǝmǝ tsya dífi wǝ ókpe lǝ kaka wǝ bakplako nkpe banlɛ kota bɔnwɔ lǝ wǝ kebuto kǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: Kukpɛnsɛ esoo, óla nkǝ eni lǝ ɔkɛ bakplako eto didisa nǝ bǝǝtǝ mǝ mfó eto ditsyuǝ lǝ udi, fɛɛ utidi saa mǝntǝ wǝ ni.

english_verse: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
translation_verse: “Lǝ kalosǝkɔɔ, úsinkli údufǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Betidi ba ni mɔ anto eto beyifoyifo, baakɛ lesa kedi kemi kulaa tsya diebu. Nya mɔ nsi mfo nya kukpɛnsɛ luubǝ kolo mɛ.

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: Mensińyǝ ntǝ bǝ kpadi mɛ fe fǝ ubi. Bomu fi mɛ fe betidi ba nlɛ keyifo buyifo kǝtǝ fǝ eto ɔni.” ’

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya ótaka nya úfi wǝ bikǝ nya ɔ́yifǝ kusu ɔntɔ lǝ wǝ anto ɔflɔ. “Se wǝ anto dínyǝ wǝǝ, óte nkǝ wǝ ubi uni nkpe fãã ɔnlɛ bubǝ. Wǝ ubi eto asa álɛ wǝ kaminsǝ, nya útsyetsyi úsu ɔ́buta wǝ ku disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: “Mfo nya wǝ úbi lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Mɔ Anto, nyifo kabua nta Onanto ku fintsya mbu. Nya mensinyǝ ntǝ bǝ akpadi mɛ fe fǝ ubi tsya!’

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ wǝ anto ákpadi wǝ beyifoyifo nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Bɛkɔse! Bifi awu nnya laanɔ bobe bibǝ bekpe wǝ. Nya lǝ bifi lɛsukpɛ ku afokpa nnya laanɔ bobe tsya bekpe wǝ lǝ ani ku akpa.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, bisu bimuǝ lenantsyuikpɔ nǝ nkpe ayinkua lǝ belo ni. Nya bitǝ lǝ budi bunye disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Itsyise mɔ ubi ǝmfoo, búfi wǝ fe nwǝ díkpǝ. Fɛɛ úsinkli úbǝ nkpǝ. Ónya nya busi búnyǝ wǝ.’ Nyamfo eso bétsyiko diyi budi kǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe nǝmfo kenke ǝsuǝǝ, wǝ ubi ninǝ ésu nkǝ lǝ usu obe mǝ kakuɛ. Se utsyi ɔnlɛ bubǝ nya ibu sɛkɛ lǝ udufǝ otoo, ɔ́nɔ ǝsǝ sǝnsǝ ku nwui wuinwui eto kɔsɔ.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Mfo nya ókpadi beyifoyifo eto ɔni lǝ mǝ nti nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, ‘Be nlɛ bubǝ lǝ oto?’

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: “Uyifoyifo ǝmǝ ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Fǝ ɔyimisani úsinklii úbǝ oto nya fǝ anto dítǝ bálo lenantsyuikpɔ nǝ nkpe ayinkua, itsyise usinkli ubǝ fɛ kulesa dímǝnyifo wǝ eso.’

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: “Nyamfo ǝtǝ kafo ányi wǝ ɔyimisani ninǝ ǝmǝ tinti eso ɔ́ni nkǝ wǝmbudufǝ diyo kulaa tsya. Lǝ nnya eso wǝ anto ábɔ úbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó, nya úbǝ ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa nkǝ udufǝ diyo.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ wǝ ubi ǝmǝ ésinkli ɔ́tɛyi wǝ anto ǝmǝ nkǝ, ‘Beso mińyǝ minlɛ keyifo buyifo kǝteǝ fǝ alɛ kpǝ ni nyamfo, fe fǝ uyifoyifo nya mǝnńyǝ lǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ kulefe kulefe, nya ǝmǝntǝ mɛ kulesa fe ote kulaa tsya, fǝ lǝ nyǝ disuǝyuǝ ku mɔ bayɛtsyuǝ?

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se fǝ ubi wǝ lébiasǝ fǝ bikǝ nya úsu útǝkǝko batsyetɔ dísinkli úbǝ otoo, álo lenantsyuikpɔ nǝ dísiǝ ayinkua ǝtǝ wǝ!’

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: “Mfó ǝmǝ nya wǝ anto lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mɔ ubi, fǝ kpe mfo lǝ mɔ ɔflɔ lefe saa nya asa nnya nte mɛ kenke, eyifo fǝ ale.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ɛlɛkɛ bǝ budi diyi bunyǝ disuǝyuǝ, itsyise búfi boǝ iyifo fe bukpǝ fǝ ɔyimi díkpǝ nya úsinkli úbǝ nkpǝ. Iyifo fe bonya ónya nya busi búnyǝ wǝ bio.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Lefe nǝ léfe mmǝǝ, otabinyǝ utsyuǝ akɛ mfó wǝ lɛkɛ uyifoyifo ɔni wǝ díkabe wǝ bikǝ. Nya bǝbǝ bátɛyi otabinyǝ ǝmǝ bǝ nwǝ laabe wǝ bikǝ mfó lɛ wǝ atabi bobiasǝ.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eso otabinyǝ ǝmǝ ákpadi nwǝ laabe wǝ bikǝ mfo nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Be ni nnya eto okle mínɔ itsyi lǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ mmǝ? Yifo nkunta itsyi lǝ kase fanlɛ mɔ bikǝ bobe ǝsuǝ kenke aboko mɛ, itsyise ensiembofo mɔ bikǝ bobe bio tsya.’

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: “Mfó nya uyifoyifo ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, ‘Mɔ Onamute ǝbǝ mɛ bola lǝ mɔ keyifokɔ. Be muuyifo? Mimbǝ ǝsuǝale nnya mukeyifo kakuɛnyifo. Nya asa bɔtɔ tsya aalɛ mɛ kofani.

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: Ahaa! Mbote lesa nǝ muuyifo! Itǝ bǝ lǝ mɔ keyifo ábɔ mɛ lǝ ani tsyaa, lǝ mɔ bayɛtsyuǝ lǝ bafo mɛ lǝ mǝ ayo.’

english_verse: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
translation_verse: “Oyonyǝ ǝmǝ ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Nnuǝ eto atase nnya lɛmɔ kolofa kɔni.’ “Mfó ǝmǝ nya nwǝ laabe bikǝ biamǝ lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Be fǝ kayo eto kukue mfo. Siǝ lǝ kasɔ lǝ awɔni awosi anɔ fe nnya fensi nɛ.’

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: “Nko ke ɔ́tɔ oyonyǝ bamba nkǝ, ‘Ntsyǝ ni fǝ ale? Kayo kasia fensi?’ “Oyonyǝ ǝmǝ tsya nkǝ, ‘Iyifo antabi eto atoku kplɛ kolofa kɔni.’ “Mfo nya nwǝ laabe bikǝ ǝmfo lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Be fǝ kayo eto kukue mfo. Wɔni awosi mflamfla ǝtsyǝ.’

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: “Nyamfo esoo, Onamute ǝmǝ átɛsǝ nwǝ laabe bikǝ wǝ dímenyifo asa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ ǝmǝ lǝ wǝ kobe ko óte mfo eso. Sitintii, betidi ba lǝǝsiǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo eto asa eto kɛnyɛɛ, mǝ etsyuesa lǝ mǝ asayifoyifo ǝsuǝ kulaa tsya so mba difi mǝ etsyuesa bǝtǝkǝ lǝ Onanto eto sekadidi ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu dísukosǝ ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bifi mi kawunsiǝ eto bikǝ bɔkɛ bɛwunsǝ ko betidi. Itǝ lǝ béloo, Onanto ebofo fǝ okpe lǝ wǝ kǝsiǝkɔ.

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ Abraham átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Lǝ bǝbɔnɔ Moses ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto ditikii, lǝ yuubǝ bǝ utidi utsyuǝ ataka utsyi ɔkɔmǝ mfo usu mǝ ɔflɔ kulaa tsyaa, bembofo wǝ ditki banɔ!’ ”

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ Farisifɔ lɛnɔ asa nnya Yesu lɛtɛyii, bǝmǝ wǝ itsyise bála atabi fefe.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Biyifo betidi ba laate ǝsuǝ fe betidi sɛɛ lǝ babamba eto ǝnǝmi. Fɛɛ Onanto yi mi akɔɛsi kafo kenke. Itsyise asa nnya lefo koya lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmii, eyifo asinsa lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
translation_verse: “Moses eto kɔfi eto kukue ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto asa nnya báwɔni bǝtsyǝǝ, éyifo mǝ keyifo isu ese Ntuflǝsǝtidi Yohanes eto lefe. Nya idu lǝ lefe nǝmfó ǝsuǝǝ, bianɔ se baatɛyi dikiti sɛɛ nǝ ditsyi lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ǝsuǝ. Nya utidi saa nlɛ bokplatsya nkǝ lǝ udufǝ mfó.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: “Osani saa wǝ lɔɔni wǝ usiǝ nya uufi obambaa, okpo kusiǝ. Nya osani wuufi kusiǝ ko bánii, okpo kusiǝ. Yesu Átɛyi Etiki Itsyi Lǝ Otabinyǝ Utsyuǝ Ku Lasaro Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe ditsyuǝǝ, otabinyǝ sani ɔni akɛ wǝ díkalɛ bikǝ bia lefo koya tinti, nya úkǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ lefe saa lǝ wǝ nkpǝ kenke ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house.
translation_verse: Nya okombo utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo bǝ Lasaro tsya lɛkɛ mfo. Afabe édi okombo ǝmfo ǝsuǝ kenke nya béketsyi wǝ bǝkǝtsyǝ lǝ kasɔ lǝ otabinyǝ ǝmǝ eto diyo eto kesie.

english_verse: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
translation_verse: Úkabe kusu nkǝ lǝ otabinyǝ ǝmǝ lɛ budii, ewuliwuli nnya díkakpo lǝ kasɔ eboyo wǝ lǝ udi. Bakpa kula tsya kǝbǝ kadia wǝ afabe nyamǝ.

english_verse: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
translation_verse: “Íbǝ íbǝ bǝ Okombo ǝmǝ ésu úkpǝ nya Onanto eto bɔtɔnkpe díbǝ bétsyi wǝ bésu bǝtsyǝ lǝ Abraham eto akɔ kato. Otabinyǝ ǝmǝ tsya ǝbǝ úkpǝ nya bébikǝ wǝ.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ɔkɔmǝǝ, únyǝ nkǝ ɔ́kɛ lǝ kanyi kǝnyǝkɔ. Mfó se óbesǝ ǝnǝmi kato, únyǝ Abraham famfamfa nya Lasaro dísiǝ wǝ lǝ akɔ.

english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya ókpadi bembembe ɔtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Anto Abraham! Nyǝ mɛ kaminsǝ lǝ ǝtǝ lǝ Lasaro lǝ ufi lɛnimi oto lǝ ntu ntsyuǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ ufi ubǝ utǝkǝ lǝ mɔ odiakami ǝsuǝ lǝ iyuǝ ko mɛ. Itsyise nlɛ kanyi bunyǝ lǝ ute ǝsuǝ mfo!’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ Abraham átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Ubi, kloma fǝnkǝ lǝ fǝ nkpǝ disiǝfee, áfo asa sɛɛ kpǝ tinti. Nya Lasaro wǝ alɛɛ, asa bua sɔ̃ɔ̃ ófo. Fɛɛ mɔmɔɔ, ɔlɛ disuǝyuǝ bunyǝ nya fǝ nlɛ kanyi bunyǝ.

english_verse: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
translation_verse: “Mfó otabinyǝ ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Anto Abraham, nlɛ fǝ lekpakpa kpe Lasaro lǝ usu mɔ anto eto.

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: Mfóo, nkpe bayimisani ɛnɔ. Tǝ lǝ usu ɔlɛ mǝ kɔlɛ kukɔnɔ itǝ lǝ mǝ lǝ bayɛ kaka minkpe bubǝ, kaka kanyi nkpe mfo.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: “Mfó ǝmǝ nya Abraham lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Fǝ bayimisani kpe Moses ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi lǝ ɔflɔ. Nya ele bǝ betsyue mǝ koto.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: “Mfó nya otabinyǝ ǝmǝ dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Anto Abraham, bǝmbɔnɔ mǝ ditiki! Fɛɛ lǝ utidi utsyuǝ ataka utsyi ɔkɔmǝ mfo úsu mǝ ɔflɔɔ, bɔɔnɔ wǝ ditiki bayɛ mǝ asa bua buyifo.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Ntsyǝ ibǝ bǝ utidi wǝ lenni Yudanyǝ ǝmfo hã ni nwǝ dísinkli úbǝ nkǝ wǝǝbǝ ɔsɔ Onanto ani?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Bonamute lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ bekpe bofokanɔ bo leyo fe diyibibi nǝ nso bɔfɛ kenkee, biofo kuyi komfo bɔtɛyi biǝ, ‘Su lǝ mfo lǝ esu edufǝ lǝ lekpo kǝmiǝ mǝ.’ Nya kuuyifo nko mǝ. Yesu Átɛyi Kase Uyifoyifo Sɛ Leeyifo Keyifo.

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: “Bibu nyamfo be be. Lǝ mi utsyuǝ kpe uyifoyifo wǝ laafo ɔkuɛ kǝtǝ wǝ nye ɔnlɛ fǝ basamu bobe nya utsyi ɔkuɛ ubǝ udufǝǝ, bɔtɛyi fɔɔtɛyi wǝ fǝ ubǝ usiǝ lǝ kasɔ mlǝ lǝ udi wǝ lesa?

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Kuutsyuǝ ǝmbɔtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ se mfo!’ nye ‘Nnyǝ se mmǝ.’ Itsyise Onanto eto Sekadidi siamǝ fe sekpe mfo lǝ mi nti.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Lefe ditsyuǝ ebubǝ nǝ biɔbɛbɛ biǝ biǝnyǝ diyi nǝ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi, luubǝ. Fɛɛ biembunyǝ!

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Yanle bǝ sɔ wǝ ani se ini wǝ uyifoyifo eto keyifo ete ini.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Asa nnya lǝǝtǝ utidi leeyifo lebua, ebubǝ kokooko. Fɛɛ mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ utidi wǝ ǝsuǝ asa nyamfo lɔɔyɛ ibǝ!

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Nyaso lɔ abe asa nnya feeyifo kukɔnɔ. “Nya lǝ fǝ ɔyɛtsyuǝ éyifo fǝ kabua kǝtsyuǝǝ, kǝ wǝ ato. Nya lǝ údi dikpi nya ɔnɔ ǝsuǝ kasɔɔ, nyalɛ fi atsyɛ wǝ.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Lǝ úyifo lesa bua óta fǝ úbu sekuanse lǝ diyi lɔni ǝsuǝ nya usi usinkli úbǝ, úbǝ ɔlɛ fǝ lekpakpa sekuanse diyi nǝmǝ ke ǝsuǝǝ, fi atsyɛ wǝ.” Bálɛ Yesu Lekpakpa Bǝ Utǝ Mǝ Bofokanɔ Lǝ Bumuǝ Ale Boso.

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Yesu eto batɔnkpe álɛ Yesu lekpakpa bǝnkǝ, “Bonamute, tǝ lǝ bo bofokanɔ lǝ bumuǝ ale boso.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Owo lo! Bomu ebɔtɛyi wǝ fǝ, ‘Muǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ atsyasǝ kafia lǝ nkɛ, lǝ anwa lesa, lǝ efi mɔ didisa ǝtsyǝ mɛ lǝ ndi nlosǝ fɛ lǝ fintsya lǝ edi.’

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Nko ke ini itǝ mi tsya nɛ. Lǝ biyifo lesa saa nǝ mintɛyi mi miǝ biyifoo, ele bǝ bɛtɛyi biǝ, ‘Beyifoyifo ete buni. Nya lesa nǝ buyifoo, eyifo lesa nǝ ete ele bǝ buuyifo.’ ” Yesu Átsya Betidi Lefosi Bǝtsyuǝ Ba Nlɛ Bufisia Bufi.

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Nya se Yesu dídu ɔntɔ lǝ Yerusalem, ɔ́yɛ Samaria ku Galilea eto kumǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Nya se ɔnlɛ kebuto kǝtsyuǝ budufǝǝ, basani lefosi bǝtsyuǝ ba nlɛ bufisia bufi ǝbǝ batsyako wǝ. Befimfi bamfo ǝńyǝ kɔsɛsɛ.

english_verse: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Nya báfa bembembe bátɛyi bǝ, “Anto Yesu, be bo lǝ ǝnyǝ bo kaminsǝ!”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Yesu ǝnyǝ mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bisu lǝ bifi mi ǝsuǝ bisu bete Onanto eto nwǝ lǝǝtǝ wǝ kedi lǝ ɔlɛ ola obe mi.” Se bányɛ bantɔɔ, bǝnyǝ bǝ mǝ bufi áyɛ mǝ.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se mǝ ɔni dínyǝ nkǝ wǝ bufi ǝyuǝ wǝǝ, úsinkli úsu nya ɔnlɛ Onanto bɔtɛsǝ ku ɔkɔɛ bembembe.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: Nya úsu óse akonki lǝ Yesu eto ǝnǝmi nya ɔ́sɔ wǝ ani. Nya utidi ǝmǝ eyifo Samarianyǝ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɔ nkǝ, “Nya inni betidi lefosi míntsya fɛɛ? Se ɔwɔnɔmbǝ fɛfɛ léfe?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Taka ǝńyǝ, lǝ edu. Fǝ bofokanɔ átsya fǝ bufi.” Yesu Átɛyi Kase Onanto Eto Sekadidi Luutsyǝ.

english_verse: The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky.
translation_verse: Itsyise fe kase ofamfa laafa ku ute ketsyi lekpake nǝ kesu lebamba, nko kǝ sitinti Utidi Eto Ubi eto bubǝ luutsyǝ nɛ.

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Betidi bǝtsyuǝ ebubǝ mi nti ini mba lɔɔtɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe, nnyǝ wǝ mmǝ!’ nye ‘Bebe, nnyǝ wǝ mfo!’ Fɛɛ biensu bɛbɛbɛ mɛ mfó!

english_verse: When Lot lived, people were also eating and drinking. They were buying, selling, planting, and building.
translation_verse: “Nko kǝ yuubǝ itǝ fe kase íbǝ lǝ Lot eto lefe tsya nɛ. Betidi ékedi, bǝkǝnǝ, nya bákasa asi tsya. Bǝtsyuǝ ǝnyǝ bákakpe bidikǝ, nya bǝtsyuǝ díkato ayo.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Mfó ǝmǝ nya bakasebi bamǝ lɛtɔ Yesu bǝnkǝ, “Bonamute, lǝ sie?” Nya Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Kaka lesakpǝnkpǝ ntǝ, mfó ebintsyǝ laakpo nɛ.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus when God's kingdom would come. He answered, “God's kingdom isn't something you can see.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ átɔ Yesu bǝnkǝ bala bǝ baate lefe nǝ kon Onanto eto sekakedikɔ luubǝ itǝ. Mfó nya Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Onanto eto Sekadidi embubǝ lǝ kusu ko ǝsuǝ betidi loofo bunyǝ.

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ele bǝ ibǝ bǝ wooka kanyi bunyǝ tinti nya mmi eto betidi lɔɔni wǝ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: “Asa nnya díbǝ lǝ Noa eto lefe, nnya kǝ nnya luubǝ itǝ le lefe nǝ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi, luubǝ bio nɛ.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Betidi ékedi, bǝkǝnǝ. Nya basani díkakpa bǝsiǝ nya bǝkǝtǝ besio bese isu ese diyi nǝ ǝsuǝ Noa dídufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ kplɛ ǝmǝ kafo. Nya ɔsɔmi lɛni nya ntubibi díyi bóba bókpo kasɔ ka kenke nya bólo betidi kenke.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Nya diyi nǝ Lot lɛbɔ lǝ Sodom, ute ku etebambi nnya nkpe lɛyimbi bua lɛwunini itsyi kato íbǝ ékpo lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ nya étonsǝ mɛmblɛ kenke.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: “Nko ke yuutsyǝ lǝ diyi nǝ ǝsuǝ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi, lɔɔbɔ mbǝ nɛ.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: Diyi nǝmfoo, utidi wǝ ńyǝ lǝ wǝ letsya ǝsuǝǝ, mensitɛyi nkǝ weesu diyo lǝ usu ufi kulesa. Nko ke ini lǝ ɔkuɛ utsyǝ ńyǝǝ, wensitɛyi nkǝ weesinkli ubǝ oto lǝ ubǝ ufi kulesa.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Bekloma lesa nǝ díbǝ lǝ Lot eto usiǝ ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Utidi saa wǝ lela nkǝ wǝǝlǝkǝ wǝ nkpǝǝ, wooyansǝ mu. Nya utidi saa wǝ leyansǝ wǝ nkpǝǝ, wuusinkli unyǝ mu.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Nya ketsye kamfoo, betidi inuǝ ebolabe lǝ bikǝ biɔni ǝsuǝ, nya Onanto ebofo ɔni nya wooyani ɔni olabesǝ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Basani inuǝ ebuńyǝ bakafo nya wuutsyi ɔni udunko nya wooyani unuǝfǝ uńyǝnsǝ mfó.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi lekpa ditsyuǝ óte mǝ nkǝ ele bǝ bakato ola lefe saa nya yanle bǝ elo mǝ ani.
