english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo kenkee, bafokanɔfɔ kenke eyifo kɛnyɛ kani lǝ ditsyǝbi ku nsusu ǝsuǝ mǝ kuutsyuǝ saa membu wǝ hã eto lɛlɛkɛsǝ eto disi, bomu mǝ lesa saa eyifo mǝmblɛ eto lele.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Nya batɔnkpe bamfo kenke dífi ǝsuǝale kplɛ bábe Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ ditsya itsyi lǝ Bonamute Yesu eto botaka ketsyi ɔkɔmǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya Onanto eto atsyɔkpokpo kplɛ dísiǝ ko mǝ kenke.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Mǝ kutsyuǝ saa mánhiã kulesa, itsyise mba ayo nye nsɔ nkpee, efi asa nyamfo báte nya béfi atabi nnya bǝnyǝ,

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: bésuko batɔnkpe bamǝ nya bésie nnya bǝtǝ utsyuǝ saa lǝ kase ińyǝ itǝ wǝ.

english_verse: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
translation_verse: Nya osani utsyuǝ tsya dísiǝ wǝ baakpo Ananias ku wǝ usiǝ Safira. Mǝ tsya éfi mǝ kasɔ kǝtsyuǝ báte.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Nya úfi atabi nyamǝ eto ǝtsyuǝ ɔ́kula fɛ úfi afɛfɛ óboko batɔnkpe bamǝ. Ditsyǝbi nǝmfo eyifo wǝ usiǝ Safira eto bote nya ɔ́tɔnɔ nkǝ beeyifo nko.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Nya Petro lɛtɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Ananias, beso fǝtǝ Obonsam dítǝkǝ fǝ kato, nya útǝ fátɛyi Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ sidia? Nya féfi fǝ kasɔ ka fáte eto atabi ǝtsyuǝ fákula fǝtsyǝ ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Kasɔ kamǝǝ, fǝ kale keni fɛ fáte ke, nya atabi nnya báfo fǝ kayo tsya eyifo fǝ ale. Be dítǝ eso féfi nsusu bua bomfó eto okle? Inni bo betidi bamfo fátɛyi sidia, bomu Onanto!”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Kase Ananias lɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfo koo, lamfolamfo úduɔnɔ ókpo kasɔ, úkpǝ! Nyamfo ǝtǝ sikpi kplɛ ǝmuǝ mba kenke lɛnɔ nnya.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Nya bakosobi ba lɛkɛ mǝ lenti dífi kafia beti wǝ nya béfi wǝ bésu bébikǝ.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Fe abombosɔsɔ ǝtsyǝ eto kamaa, wǝ usiǝ tsya ǝbǝ údufǝ mfó. Fɛɛ wóannɔ tɔ lesa nǝ díkǝbǝ.

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Nya Petro lɛtɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Tɛyi mɛ, se fǝ ku fǝ usǝ dífi mi kasɔ bétee, atabi nnya kenke báfó mi kayo ni nyamfoo?” Nya usio ǝmǝ dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Ee, nyamblɛ nɛ.”

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Beso fonko fǝ usǝ lékle kɛnyɛ bɛnlɛ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ boto kabe? Bebe bakosobi ba dítsyi fǝ usǝ bésu bébikǝ ni mba mfo nɛ. Mǝ ke luutsyi fintsya besuko nɛ.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfo, wuntsya édu ɔ́nɔ nya úkpǝ. Nya se bakosobi bamǝ díbǝ bǝńyǝ se úkpǝ onlabee, bétsyi wuntsya bésu bébikǝ lǝ kaka bébikǝ wǝ usǝ ke.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Itǝ eso sikpi kplɛ ǝmuǝ mba kenke díkafo Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ bánɔ ku betidi ba kenke lɛnɔ lesa nǝ díbǝ mfó. Batɔnkpe Bamǝ Éyifo Ayanisa Kpǝ.

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfo kenkee, batɔnkpe bamǝ éyifo ayanisa kpǝ lǝ betidi bamǝ nti. Bafokanɔfɔ ba lǝ mfóo, ákatsya lǝ Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo kaka baakpo Solomo eto Lekpata.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Ite bǝ betidi bamba kpǝ éfi lelabi ku díbu bǝtǝ bafokanɔfɔ tsyaa, báte sikpi bámfo budufǝ lǝ mǝ nti.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Tsya fɛɛ betidi kpǝ, basani ku besio ésinkli lǝ nkpǝ bua bo bensi kama nya báfo Bonamute bánɔ.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Nya betidi lékpa mǝ befimfi bálabesǝ lǝ mǝ belabekǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ kpakpaakpe kaka bébu bǝ Petro ebɔyɛ lǝ ɔlɛ botsyilama, lǝ wǝ koyo lǝ kokpo lǝ befimfi bamfó ǝsuǝ lǝ bǝmuǝ ale.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Nya betidi kpǝ lǝ kǝmǝ ka letsyilama Yerusalem lékpa mǝ befimfi ku mba awɔnɔ bua nlɛ bule báboko mǝ ɔflɔ nya bátsya mǝmblɛ bufi. Yudafɔ Eto Beninǝ Ǝnǝ Etiki Ku Yesu Eto Batɔnkpe.

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Nya Yudafɔ eto Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke ku wǝ betidi ba ni Sadukifɔ eto ditsyukpa lɛsɛsǝ ǝnǝmi kekleke lǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, bǝmuǝ mǝ bákpe lǝ umǝ eto diyo.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Lǝ kǝtsyǝ kamfóo, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝbǝ ɔ́sini diyo nǝmǝ eto siku nya úlǝkǝ mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: “Bidu bisu Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo lǝ bisu bidi betidi ba kenke etiki itsyi lǝ nkpǝ fɔfɔ bomǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Nyaso se kale dísiǝǝ, lǝ kase Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe lɛtɛyi mǝ ǝsuǝǝ, bésu bédufǝ Disumuyo mfó nya bétsyiko mǝ asa bote. Lǝ lefe nǝmfoo, Usumunyǝ Ninǝ ku wǝ betidi ákpadi Yudafɔ kenke eto beninǝ itǝ botsya nya bákpe betidi bǝtsyuǝ bǝ besu bǝlǝkǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ baboko.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se bésu mfóo, bǝnnyǝ mǝ! Nyaso bésinkli bǝbǝ bátɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Se búsuu, búnyǝ buǝ diyo nǝmǝ eto biku kenke ti, nya mba laase kesie ńyǝ mfó. Fɛɛ se bɔ́sini bikuu biamǝǝ, bóǝnnyǝ betidi bamǝ eto kuutsyuǝ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ kafo.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ mba laase Disumuyo nǝmǝ eto uninǝ ku besumunyǝ ninǝ bamfo lɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfoo, íyifo mǝ yanii tinti, itsyise bánte lesa nǝ díbǝ lǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, utsyuǝ ǝbǝ ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Betidi ba bímuǝ békpe lǝ diyoo, bebe, nyǝ mǝ ńyǝ lǝ Disumuyo nǝmǝ banlɛ betidi asa botee!”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, mba laase mfo eto uninǝ ku wǝ betidi édu bésu Disumuyo mfo bésu bǝmuǝ mǝ baboko. Fɛɛ se báte sikpi bǝ betidi bamǝ ebubǝ bato mǝ aba esoo, bǝnnǝ mǝ lǝ ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Se bákpa mǝ bésuko, bésu bǝńyǝnsǝ mǝ lǝ bakponkpo bamǝ eto ǝnǝmi. Usumunyǝ Ninǝ átɔ mǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Bóantɛyi mi boǝnkǝ biensidi etiki bɛtɛyi kulesa itsyi lǝ Yesu ǝmfo ǝsuǝ? Fɛɛ bíusensǝ wǝ ditiki nǝmǝ békpo lǝ Yerusalem kenke. Nya bela biǝ biefi wǝ ukpǝ eto dikpilolo bitǝkǝ bo lǝ ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Yesu wǝ bélo se bítǝ básɔ bámanklasǝ lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝǝ, bo bana eto Onantoo, ni nwǝ létakasǝ wǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ nkpǝ nɛ.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Onanto ǝtsyǝ wǝ lǝ wǝ sediani lǝ kato kaka ni kakplɛkɔ kenke fe ɔtɔnkotɔnko ku Ofomfokǝtǝ itǝ lǝ Israelfɔ lǝ bǝnyǝ kusu batsyi mǝ akɔɛsi bawuninsǝ lǝ Onanto lǝ ufi mǝ abua ɔtsyɛ mǝ.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Bo ku Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ, nǝ Onanto leefi kǝtǝ mba leetsyue wǝ koto ni mba ni ditiki nǝmfo eto badansiɛ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Se bakponkpo bamǝ lɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfo lǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ eto kɛnyɛɛ, kafo esi kányi mǝ tinti, nyaso báse nnya uyi bǝnkǝ ele bǝ balo mǝ.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Farisifɔ eto osani ɔni wǝ lɛkɛ lǝ bakponkpo bamǝ nti baakpo Gamaliel áni ko mǝ. Úyifo utidi wǝ betidi kenke díkebu tinti, nya úyifo afi eto oteasa tsya. Wǝ ni futsyǝ létaka uńyǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, bǝtǝ lǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ lǝ babɔ, fɛ lǝ befi nsusu lǝ etiki nyamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Israelfɔ, bɛlɔ bebe sinini lǝ lesa nǝ bela biǝ bieyifo betidi bamfo!

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, lǝ betidi eto leklafee, Yuda wǝ ni Galileanyǝ ákpa betidi ókpo lǝ ǝsuǝ fe wǝ bǝtǝkǝkotsyuǝ. Nko ke se úkpǝǝ, wǝ bǝtǝkǝkotsyuǝ mfó ko dísensǝ.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Nyaso lesa nǝ minyǝ mɔɔtɛyi mi ko ni bǝ, beyani betidi bamfo. Lǝ asa nnya banlɛ bote nye asa nnya banlɛ buyifo eyifo mǝ kosate eto etsyuesaa, yǝmbɔklɛ fɛ yuusensǝ

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ Onanto eto etsyuesa inii, boǝmbofo mǝ kusu buti. Lǝ boambe kukɔnɔ kulaa tsyaa, buubǝ bunyǝ boǝ bɔlɛ botaka kǝńyǝ lǝ Onanto Kosate ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Bakponkpo bamǝ áfo Gamaliel eto nsusu bo útǝ mǝ. Nyaso bákpadi batɔnkpe bamǝ báboko mǝ nti, nya báblɛ mǝ. Nya besi bǝkǝ mǝ ato bǝ bensi tɛyi kulesa saa itsyi lǝ Yesu eto ditiki ǝsuǝ bio. Nya báyani mǝ bédu.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Se batɔnkpe bamǝ dídu lǝ lekpokakpokɔ bantɔɔ, bǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti, itsyise bǝnyǝ bǝnkǝ Onanto ákla mǝ ókpe lǝ betidi ba baakpe kofani lǝ Yesu eto disi nti.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nya diyi saa, bákate Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝme kátɛyi lǝ Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo ku ayo ayo kafe bǝnkǝ Yesu ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfo se bakasebi bamǝ dítsyiko bufuii, kǝnǝ ǝbǝ kédufǝ lǝ Yudafɔ ba laatɛyi Sigriki ku mba laatɛyi Sihebri eto nti. Nnya ni bǝ lǝ bésie diyi eto lesaa, díankayo mba laatɛyi Sigriki eto bakɔ besiǝ.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, lefosi inuǝ bamǝ ákpadi bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ kenke, nya bátɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, “Yanlɛkɛ bǝ bobiasǝ lefe lǝ didisa siesie eto etiki ǝsuǝ lǝ lefe nǝ ele bǝ bufi bo lefe kenke butǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ eto budi.

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Ditiki nǝmǝ álɛkɛ itǝ mǝmblɛ kenke, nyaso bǝlǝkǝ Stefano wǝ ni utidi wǝ nkpe bofokanɔ nya Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nsi ko wǝ lefe saa. Nya bǝlǝkǝ Filipo ku Prokoro ku Nikanɔ ku Timona ku Parmena ku nwǝ díkafiani Yudanyǝ wǝ baakpo Nikolas wǝ ditsyi Antiokia.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Bǝlǝkǝ betidi ekuanse bamfo báte batɔnkpe bamǝ. Nya báto ola bǝtǝkǝ mǝ ani lǝ disi itǝ keyifo kamǝ eto buyifo.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ dísukosǝ busǝnsǝ kakpe. Nya bafokanɔfɔ lǝ Yerusalem dífui bakpe. Yudafɔ eto besumunyǝ kpǝ tsya átsyi báwuninsǝ báfo Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ. Yudafɔ Eto Beninǝ Ǝmuǝ Stefano.

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Nyaso Stefano wǝ Onanto lékpo atsyɔ útǝ ǝsuǝalee, éyifo ayanisa kpǝ lǝ betidi bamǝ nti.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Yudafɔ ba díkafo mǝ ǝsuǝ ǝsuǝ eto busiǝ ba dítsyi Kirene ku Aleksandria ku bǝtsyuǝ ba dítsyi Kilikia ku Asia eto ditsyukpa étsyiko etiki bunǝ ku Stefano.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mǝ kutsyuǝ saa mámfo buńyǝ lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ kobe ko Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ dítǝ wǝ eso.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Nyaso bákpe disi ku betidi bamba bǝ lǝ batɛyi bidia lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ bǝ, “Bɔ́nɔ se úkedi etiki uńyǝ lǝ Moses ku Onanto tsya ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Kɛnyɛlala kamfo ǝnǝ lefonyi lékpe lǝ betidi bamfo ku beninǝ ku afi eto bateasa bamǝ kenke ǝsuǝ. Nya bǝmuǝ Stefano nya béfi wǝ bésuko bakponkpo eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Nya báboko betidi bǝtsyuǝ ba lékle kɛnyɛ bátɛyi sidia bǝnkǝ, “Osani ǝmfo ékedi etitiki lefe saa kǝńyǝ lǝ Disumuyo Kɛnkɛ kamfo ku lǝ Moses eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Nya bɔ́nɔ bǝ ɔ́tɛyi tsya nkǝ utidi ǝmǝ ditsyi Nasaret baakpo Yesu mfóo, átɛyi nkǝ wooba Onanto eto Disumuyo Kɛnkɛ kamfo nya woofloma akpa nnya Moses dítǝ bo!”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Nya mba kenke lɛkɛ lǝ lekpokakpokɔ mfó létakasǝ ǝnǝmi bákpo lǝ Stefano ǝsuǝ, nya bǝnyǝ bǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi ǝkǝyǝnde fe kato eto ɔtɔnkpe eto ale.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Nya Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke lɛtɔ Stefano nkǝ, “Etiki nnya banlɛ budi mfoo, sitinti ini banlɛ bɔtɛyi?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Nya Stefano dítǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Boantomǝ ku mɔ bayimi, bitsyue koto kukɔnɔ lǝ bɛnɔ! Diyǝnde eto Onanto ǝlǝkǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ óte bo ona Abraham lǝ Mesopotamia eto kasɔ fɛ údu mfo úsu úsiǝ lǝ kasɔ ka baakpo Haran.

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: Ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Taka lǝ edu lǝ kasɔ kamfo ku lǝ fǝ betidi ɔflɔ lǝ esu kasɔ ka moote fǝ ǝsuǝ!’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: “Nyaso údu lǝ wǝ kasɔ nya úsu úsiǝ lǝ Haran. Lǝ Abraham eto anto eto ukpǝ eto kamaa, nya Onanto lékpa wǝ óboko kasɔ ka binsi mfo mmi nɛ.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Onanto mémbudi kasɔ kamfo eto dikuku sɛkɛ saa útǝ Abraham fe wǝ kale, inni lɛ kokpa kɔni. Bomu ɔ́tɛyi wǝ ɔtɔnsǝ nkǝ kasɔ kamǝ ebofiani wǝ ku wǝ befluflu eto kale, ite bǝ ibǝ ese lefe nǝmfóo, ubi saa mánkɛ wǝ.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Nnya Onanto lɛtɛyi wǝ ni nkǝ, ‘Fǝ befluflu ebofiani bafɔ lǝ kasɔ ka lenni mǝ kalɛ ǝsuǝ. Boosiǝ mfo fe ndomufɔ nya boote mǝ ohiã itǝ alɛ alofa ana flɔflɔflɔ.’

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Nya usi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ tsya nkǝ, ‘Moolo betidi ba díyifo mǝ mǝ ndomufɔ dikpi. Nya lǝ nnya kamaa, buudu lǝ kasɔ kamfó bǝbǝ basumu mɛ lǝ kaka mfo.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Mmǝǝ ini Onanto lékle kɛnyɛ ku Abraham ini lelolala eto kɛnyɛklekle nɛ. Nyaso Abraham ála Isak lelo lǝ diyi flamflale se bále wǝ. Isak tsya ála Yakob lelo, nya Yakob tsya léla lelo lefosi bebi inuǝ ba bále díbǝ báfiani Yudafɔ eto ese lefosi ǝnuǝ ba lefo diye.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: “Yakob eto bebi bamfo ásɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ mǝ ɔyimi Yosef ǝsuǝ. Nya bǝmuǝ wǝ báte fe ndomunyǝ lǝ Egipte eto kasɔ. Fɛɛ Onanto ǝsiǝko wǝ,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: nya úlǝkǝ wǝ útsyiko kanyi ku bulǝ saa ǝsuǝ. Se Yosef lɛbɔ úńyǝ lǝ Egipte eto Oka Farao eto ǝnǝmii, Onanto ǝtǝ wǝ ditsyǝbi sɛɛ ku kobe, itǝ Oka ǝmǝ éfi wǝ útsyǝ nwǝ laabe Egipte eto kasɔ ku wǝ diyo ku bikǝ kenke.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe nǝmfoo, kukpɛnsɛ kekleke ǝbǝ lǝ Egipte ku Kanan eto nsɔ kenke, nya ínǝ kanyinyǝnyǝ kpǝ éboko. Bo bana mánkɛ didisa nǝ buudi.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Nya se Yakob lɛnɔ nkǝ didisa kpe lǝ Egipte, ókpe wǝ bebi ba ni bo bana ditutɛ nkǝ besu baya didisa baboko wǝ.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Lǝ mǝ dísu nuǝfǝǝ, Yosef ǝlǝkǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ óte wǝ bayimi nya Egipte eto Oka tsya díbǝ óte Yosef eto bayimi bamǝ.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Nyaso Yakob édu úsu Egipte kaka wǝ ku wǝ bebi díkpǝ.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Kamaa, bétsyi bǝkpǝnkpǝ bamǝ bésinkli bésuko umǝ wǝ baakpo Sikem bésu bébikǝ mfó. Bébikǝ mǝ lǝ kudi ko Abraham léya ku sika flubia eto atabi ǝtsyuǝ lǝ Hamɔ eto bebi ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “Se kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ ka Onanto dítsyǝ útǝ Abraham eto lefe nlɛ boyoo, wǝ befluflu ba mfe nkpe lǝ Egipte ébole béfui tinti.

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Kafɛfɛkɔɔ, Oka fɔfɔ wǝ dímante Yosef étsyiko seka budi lǝ Egipte eto kasɔ.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Farao emfo ǝlǝkǝ nsusu bua úyifo Abraham eto befluflu bamfo disiale nya úkisi mǝ tsya. Itǝ ɔ́hiɛ mǝ bǝlǝkǝ mǝ bebisambi ba bále lǝ ayo báto bákpe lǝ kase buukpǝ.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: “Lefe nǝmfo eto lefe nnya bále Moses nɛ. Nya bǝnyǝ bǝ úyifo ubi wǝ laanɔ bobe tsya. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, wǝ antomǝ éfi wǝ bákula afa ǝtsyǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃ lǝ diyo.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bǝnyǝ bǝ bensiembofo wǝ bɔkulaa, bǝlǝkǝ wǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya Farao eto ubisiobi dísu ɔ́kɛ wǝ nya úfi wǝ ófianisǝ wǝ kafo eto ubi.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Óbe wǝ nya békluǝsǝ wǝ fe Egiptenyǝ lǝ mǝ esu ku mǝ asayifofo ǝsuǝ. Nya óbe ófiani utidi wǝ lɛkɛ ǝsuǝale lǝ wǝ etikididi ku wǝ eyifosa kenke ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ úsu mfoo, únyǝ kase Egiptenyǝ nlɛ mǝ ɔni disiale buyifo. Esoo, úsu ɔ́wunsǝko wǝ ɔ́sɔ Egiptenyǝ ǝmfo ólo.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Moses ébu nkǝ wǝ kosate eto betidi ǝbɔnɔ kasɔ nkǝ Onanto ebɔyɛ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ úlǝkǝ mǝ, fɛɛ mǝ mánnɔ nnya kasɔ nko.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Kǝsiǝsǝǝ, únyǝ Israelfɔ inuǝ ba nlɛ bɔkpɔ. Úsu nkǝ lǝ usu okpla mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Bebee! Bayimi, bini! Yanle bǝ bikakpɔ keyifo lesafimfi mi bǝtsyua nko!’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: “Nya mǝ inuǝ bamǝ eto nwǝ nlɛ wǝ ɔyimi disiale buyifo dínǝ Moses útsyǝ kutsyǝ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, ‘Be leka fǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ? Owoe dílǝkǝ fǝ bo ɔtɔnkotɔnko ku okponkpo lǝ bo etiki nyamfo ǝsuǝ fɛ?

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Lee, ala fǝ aalo mɛ fe kase fálo Egiptenyǝ ǝmǝ kǝnǝ?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Se Moses lɛ́nɔ ditiki nǝmfoo, útsyetsyi úsu ɔ́biɔ lǝ Midia eto kasɔ fe ɔfɔ. Nya mfó óle bebisaibi inuǝ.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: “Lǝ alɛ fosi ana eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte wǝ fe ute wǝ nlɛ biyu eto kuyi botonsǝ eto ditsyǝbi lǝ Sinai eto kɔtini ɔflɔ lǝ mfiminti mfo.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Se Moses dínyǝ nyamfo nkoo, íyifo wǝ naa. Nyaso lefe nǝ ɔ́nyi ɔ́fi nkǝ lǝ obe lesa nǝ inii, nnya Onanto eto ɔkɔɛ ábɔ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Mɔ ni fǝ banamǝ eto Onanto, Abraham ku Isak ku Yakob eto Onanto.’ Moses étinkǝ ku sikpi eso wénsifo mfó bobe.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: “Onanto ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Lǝkǝ fǝ afokpa! Itsyise kaka fǝńyǝǝ, kasɔ kɛnkɛ keni.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Mbunyǝ kase mɔ betidi nlɛ kanyi bunyǝ lǝ Egipte eto kasɔ mfó. Mbɔnɔ mǝ bɔwɔnɔnɔ ku nku bo banlɛ kakpadi mɛ. Nyaso mbubǝ miǝ lǝ mbǝ nlǝkǝ mǝ. Bǝ, mookpe fǝ kɔtɔ lǝ Egipte!’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “Moses emfo ni utidi wǝ Israelfɔ lɛ́ni nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Owoe dílǝkǝ fǝ bo ɔtɔnkotɔnko ku okponkpo lǝ bo etiki nyamfo ǝsuǝ fɛ?’ Wǝ ni nwǝ Onanto létsyese nkǝ udi seka lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ ku mǝ Ofomfokǝtǝ, ɛyɛ lǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ dílǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte lǝ ute wǝ díkǝnyǝ lǝ mfiminti mfó nɛ.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Ókpa mǝ betidi bamǝ údunko lǝ Egipte nya úyifo ayanisa úlǝkǝ asa kpǝ óte lǝ Egipte eto kasɔ ku lǝ Lekpo Sia eto kɛnyɛ ku lǝ lefe nǝ banyɛ lǝ mfiminti alɛ fosi ana sɔ̃ɔ̃.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: “Moses ǝmfo ni nwǝ lɛtɛyi Israelfɔ nkǝ, ‘Onanto ebotsyese mi wǝ unyɛlǝkǝtidi fe kase ótsyese mɛ, nya wuulǝkǝ wǝ utsyiko lǝ mi kosate eto nti.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Wǝ ni nwǝ lɛtɔnko Israelfɔ ba lɛkɛ lǝ mfiminti mfó. Wǝ lɛkɛ mfó ku bo banamǝ. Wǝ ni nwǝ dídinko Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe etiki lǝ Sinai eto kɔtini ǝsuǝ. Wǝ nsi ni futsyǝ léfo Onanto eto ditiki nǝ lǝǝtǝ nkpǝ óboko bo.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ bo banamǝ mámfo Moses eto etiki nyamǝ nyaso bǝkǝ wǝ bǝtsyǝ kutsyǝ nya bála bǝ eni lǝ beesinkli besu Egipte eto kasɔ.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso báto ufiǝ fe onantsyui eto ubi. Wǝ bǝkǝtǝ kedi nya bǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ diyi nǝmfo lǝ ufiǝ ǝmǝ béfi mǝ ani béyifo mfó eso.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eso Onanto étsyi kama ókpe mǝ. Nya oyani mǝ utǝ kofe ku kofande ku befandebi eto kesumu, fe kase báwɔni bákpe lǝ bɛnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto kukue nkǝ, ‘Israelfɔ, inni mɔ bélo bebuki bítǝ kedi lǝ alɛfosi ana nnya bɛkɛ lǝ mfiminti mfó.

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Bomu Molok eto dibú ku Romfa eto befandebi bia béfianinsǝ bǝfiǝ ba bíkǝtǝ kedi nɛ. Lǝ nyaso muutǝ bǝmuǝ mi besuko kasɔ fãã lǝ Babilonia eto kama bisu bɛkɛ mfo.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “Bo banamǝ étsyi Onanto eto diyo nya ɔ́tɔ, ɔ́yɛnko mǝ lǝ mfiminti kafo. Báto diyo nǝmǝ fe kase Onanto dílǝkǝ óte Moses nkǝ bato ni pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Kama kamaa, bo bana ba léfo diyo nǝmǝ betsyiko lǝ mǝ antomǝ ɔflɔɔ, étsyi ni ku mǝ lǝ lefe nǝ báyɛ ku Yosua, nya bésu báfo kasɔ kamǝ betsyiko lǝ ese nnya Onanto lélà lǝ mfó se bántɔ. Nya lɛkɛ lǝ mǝ nti mfó isu ese Oka David eto lefe.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Onanto ála David eto asa tinti. Lǝ nnya eso David átɔ Onanto nkǝ utǝ wǝ kusu lǝ oto kǝsiǝkɔ utǝ wǝ, Yakob eto Onanto.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Salomo ko díbǝ óto diyo nǝmǝ.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ Onanto Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke ǝnsiǝ lǝ ayo nnya kawunsiǝ mfo eto betidi léto. Onanto áyɛ lǝ wǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ǝsuǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: ‘Kato ni mɔ sekakedikɔ eto lekpomɛ, nya kasɔ ni mɔ akpa eto kǝtǝkǝkɔ. Lǝ nnya esoo, nte diyo nǝ eto okle footo ǝtǝ mɛ. Sie ni kaka ele bǝ nsiǝ?

english_verse: You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso Stefano ési ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nke, “Letotidi kon eto betidi bini! Ntsyǝ bimbǝ bofokanɔ mǝ! Bíti ato lǝ Onanto eto ditiki ǝsuǝ fe kase mi antomǝ díyifo. Lefe saa biataka kǝńyǝ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Nte Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba mi banamǝ dímǝńyǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ? Bálo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba díbǝ bábe letsya taa kɔ bǝ Ɔsɛɛtidi Wǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ lɛ bubǝ. Nya Ɔsɛɛtidi Wǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ ǝmǝǝ, wǝ bɛni nya bíkǝ wǝ bítǝ bálo wǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Mi ni mba léfo Onanto eto kɔfi ko útǝ mi ɛyɛ lǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe eto ani, fɛɛ bɛni bíentsyue ko koto!” Bákpɔ Stefano Aba.

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Se bánɔ etiki nnya Stefano lɛtɛyi lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝǝ, kafo ányi mǝ tinti nya báyimi mǝ anyi ku lɛblɔfi.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nsiko Stefano esoo, óbesǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi kato fãã, nya únyǝ Onanto eto diyǝnde ku Yesu wǝ ńyǝ lǝ wǝ sediani.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bebe! Nnyǝ se kato lɛsini nya mínyǝ Utidi eto Ubi ǝmǝ se uńyǝ lǝ Onanto eto sediani!”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Nya báfã bembembe nya béti mǝ ato eto kafo nya bédufǝ wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ bǝmuǝ wǝ.

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: Nya bǝnǝ wǝ bábɔnko lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ, nya bétsyiko wǝ aba. Nya mba ǝmǝ dínǝ wǝ bábɔnkoo, bǝlǝkǝ mǝ awukplɛ nnya bankpe bǝtsyǝ lǝ okosobi wǝ baakpo Saulo eto akɔ.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ banlɛ wǝ aba bɔkpɔɔ, Stefano ákpadi Bonamute nya óto ola nkǝ, “Bonamute Yesu, fo mɔ lɛwɔnɔ!”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Óse akonki nya ófa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bonamute, fi mǝ abua nnya béyifo mfo atsyɛ mǝ!” Nya se ɔ́tɛyi nyamfo ólosǝǝ, úkpǝ.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, Saulo ǝtǝkǝko nya úlǝ bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ kekleke nya údufǝ ayo kafo úkabɛbɛ basani ku besio ta kǝnǝ kǝlǝkǝ kakpe mǝ lǝ ayo. Filipo Ábe Onanto Eto Ditiki Sɛɛ Nǝmǝ Letsya Lǝ Samaria.

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Nya bafokanɔfɔ lǝ mfó ésensǝ bésu kalebe saa nya banlɛ Boanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya bobe.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Nya Filipo wǝǝ, údu úsu Samaria nya ɔ́tɛyi betidi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ itsyi lǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Betidi kpǝ étsyue ato bánɔ etiki nnya ɔnlɛ budi itsyise bǝnyǝ ayanisa kpǝ nnya úyifo eso báfo wǝ ditiki nǝmǝ.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Óla awɔnɔ bua kpǝ utsyiko lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ, nya nkomǝ eto awɔnɔ bua nyamfó lékpo kudu bembembe fɛ bábɔ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya ótsya mba eto ani ku akpa díkpǝ ku mba léba bákpo lǝ kasɔ tsya nya bátaka báyɛ.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Nyamfo esoo, disuǝyuǝ akɛ lǝ Samaria eto umǝ tinti!

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Osani utsyuǝ akɛ lǝ Samaria baakpo Simon, uyifo utsyuǝ wǝ leeyifo afantosi eto afa. Lǝ lefe kɛntsyɛ nǝ léfe ǝsuǝǝ, uyifo utsyuǝ wǝ díkatsya betidi kekleke nya íkeyifo betidi yanii. Nya úkǝbú wǝ ǝsuǝ ɔkplɛnyǝ wǝ lefé lǝ wǝ eyifosa ku etiki nnya úkedi eso.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Samariafɔ eto bekpefi ku beninǝ kenke ékebu wǝ lǝ kase úkate ǝsuǝ keyifo eso. Le nnya esoo, béfi wǝ etikididi fe, “Wǝ ni Onanto eto ǝsuǝale nya baakpo Ǝsuǝale Kplɛ.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Se úkeyifo afa éfo lefe kɛntsyɛ esoo, béfi mǝ ǝnǝmi bǝtǝkǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Itsyise beetsyuǝ wǝ koto kanɔ wǝ etiki.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mɔmɔɔ, báfo Filipo eto ditiki nǝ ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi banɔ itsyi lǝ Onanto eto Sekadidi ku Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ. Nya basani ku besio kpǝ léfo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo eto diye ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Nya Simon ǝmǝ mfo tsya léfo ɔ́nɔ. Nya lefe nǝ ófo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa nǝmfoo, úkǝtǝkǝko Filipo kesu kalebe saa ka ɔntɔ. Nya lǝ ǝnyǝsa ku ayanisa nnya úkeyifo esoo, íyifo wǝ naa!

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ batɔnkpe ba lɛkɛ lǝ Yerusalem lɛnɔ bǝ Samariafɔ ébofo Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ banɔɔ, bákpe Petro ku Yohanes lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Se bésu bédufǝǝ, báto ola lǝ bafokanɔfɔ fɔfɔ bamǝ eto disi bǝ lǝ bafo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Nko ini nɛ itsyise isu ese lefe nǝmfoo, bámfo tɔ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ. Ntu ete bǝflǝsǝ mǝ lǝ Bonamute Yesu eto diye ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Nyamfo esoo, Petro ku Yohanes éfi mǝ ani bǝtǝkǝ mǝ lǝ esi, nya mǝ tsya léfo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Se Simon dínyǝ nkǝ lǝ batɔnkpe bamfo éfi ani bǝtǝkǝ mǝ lǝ esi koo, báfo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛɛ, úfi atabi nkǝ lǝ utǝ Petro ku Yohanes nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: “Bitǝ mintsya ǝsuǝale nyamfo itǝ bǝ utsyuǝ saa ke mimfi ani mintǝkǝ lǝ ofo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Ekudi itǝ fǝ ku fǝ atabi kenke! Membu fǝ ebofo Onanto eto atɛsǝ boya ku atabi.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Embofo lekpa saa bokpa lǝ keyifo kamfo ǝsuǝ itsyise lɛkɔɛsi mankpa fǝ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Itsyise nnyǝ miǝ akpe kǝnǝnsɛ kplɛ nya asa bua eto buyifo tsya eyi fǝ utu, nya fafiani ndomunyǝ itǝ lebua.”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Nya Simon lɛtɛyi Petro ku Yohanes nkǝ, “Beto ola bɛlɛ Onanto lekpakpa bitǝ mɛ bǝ kotonǝnǝ kekleke ko bɛtɛyi mfo eto okle embubǝ mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Petro ku Yohanes díkedi mǝ sedansiɛ nya bédi etiki itsyi lǝ Bonamute eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ Samaria mfóo, báyɛ lǝ Samaria eto kǝmǝ kpǝ bédi mǝ etiki lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ lefe nǝ besinkli bantɔ lǝ Yerusalem. Filipo Édi Etiki Ku Etiopia Eto Keyifo Eto Uninǝ.

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Itǝ Filipoo, Bonamute eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ dítsyi kato ǝbǝ ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Taka lǝ esu ɔnwɛdikueti lǝ mfiminti eto kusu ko lidu lǝ Yerusalem kɔntɔ lǝ Gasa.”

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Mfo ótaka nya ɔ́yɛ kusu komǝ. Lefe nǝ ɔ́ntɔɔ, ótsyako utidi utsyuǝ lǝ kusu nlɛ bubǝ. Utidi ǝmfo éyifo uyifoyifo ninǝ wǝ nsi lǝ Etiopia eto Besio eto Oka baakpo Kandake eto sekakedikɔ eto atabi eto kɛnyɛ. Uyifoyifo ninǝ ǝmfo étsyi olakatokɔ lǝ Yerusalem.

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: Ɔ́tɔ lǝ oto nya unsi lǝ nkenke bo ɔtɔmini nlɛ bunǝ kafo, nya lǝ kase ɔnyɛ ɔntɔɔ, úkakla asa itsyi lǝ Unyɛlǝkǝtidi Yesaya eto Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Nya Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ dídi Filipo etiki ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Yɛ mlǝ lǝ etu ɔtɔmini eto nkeke bomfó.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Filipo akɔse ɔ́yɛ útu wǝ nya ɔ́nɔ se uyifoyifo ninǝ ǝmfo nlɛ asa bokla ketsyi ko lǝ Yesaya eto kukue ko ɔ́wɔni kafo. Nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Asa nnya fanlɛ bokla mfo anɔ nnya kasɔ?”

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Nya etiki nnya úkakla lǝ Kukue Kɛnkɛ eto Awɔnisa nnya nkpe mfó ni nkǝ, “Bákpa wǝ báyɛnko fe kase baayɛnko osamu kesuko wǝ kalokɔ. Nya unsi kpoo fe osamu wǝ dímemfi nnya kulesa lǝ bákɔ wǝ bisǝ, nya wémfe kɛnyɛ ɔ́tɛyi kulesa.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Bákpe wǝ kofani, nya bǝntǝ ota wǝ ǝsuǝ disi. Kuutsyuǝ saa embofo kulesa bɔtɛyi itsyi lǝ wǝ befluflu ǝsuǝ, itsyise bála wǝ nkpǝ diku lǝ kasɔ mfo.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Nya uyifoyifo ninǝ ǝmfo lɛtɛyi Filipo nkǝ, “Tɛyi mɛ, wǝ kosate Unyɛlǝkǝtidi Yesaya ɔnlɛ, lee utidi bamba utsyuǝ?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Mmǝ nnya Filipo dítsyiko, ɔ́tɛyi wǝ asa lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue komǝ ǝsuǝ, nya úlǝkǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ kasɔ ku kase ɛnlɛ bote itsyi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Nya se banyɛ lǝ kusu bantɔɔ, bésu báyo kaka ntu ntsyuǝ nńyǝ lǝ usunte ɔflɔ. Nya uyifoyifo ninǝ ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi Filipo nkǝ, “Be, nyǝ ntu ntsyuǝ! Beso fǝmbofo mɛ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa nǝmǝ buyifo mfó?” Nya Filipo lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ afo anɔ ku fǝ utu kenkee, ebofo mu bofo.”

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Nya uyifoyifo ninǝ ǝmfo lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Mfo nnɔ miǝ Yesu Kristo ni Onanto eto Ubi.” Nya útǝ nkǝ bǝńyǝsǝ ɔtɔmini eto nkenke bomǝ, nya bédufǝ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ, nya Filipo díflǝsǝ wǝ ntu.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝ bábɔ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Bonamute eto Lɛwɔnɔ ákpa Filipo dídunko, nya uyifoyifo ninǝ ǝmfo ménsinyǝ wǝ bio. Úsukosǝ wǝ kusu ko ɔnyɛ ku disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, Filipo ǝńyǝ nkǝ uńyǝ lǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Asoto. Idu mfóo, óbe Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letysa lǝ kǝmǝ ka nkpe lǝ kusu mfó kenke isu ese lefe nǝ údufǝ Kaisarea eto umǝ.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nya Saulo dísukosǝ wǝ etiki sɛtɔ ókpasɛ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ sitinti ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi. Yudafɔ ba lɛkɛ lǝ Damasko mfó eto kuutsyuǝ mámfo kulesa bɔtɛyi kǝńyǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Ókpasɛ mǝ nkǝ Yesu ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, taka lǝ esu umǝ ǝmǝ. Bɔɔtɛyi fǝ lesa nǝ ele bǝ yifo.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Mɔ kosate ebote wǝ kase sɔ̃ɔ̃ ele bǝ ɔyɛ udi ohiã lǝ mɔ disi.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Se úsu údufǝ Yerusalem, óla nkǝ lǝ usu ɔkɔ ku bakasebi ba nkpe mfó. Fɛɛ bámfo bánnɔ bǝ uyifo okasebi eso mǝ tsya áte wǝ sikpi.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ bulǝkǝkate bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, únyǝ osani utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Ananias wǝ díbǝ úfi ani útǝkǝ wǝ lǝ disi itǝ bǝ usinkli nyǝ asa.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, iyifo Saulo fe bufǝ bǝfǝ lesatsyuǝ nǝ nte fe akpɛ eto dikpǝ seka lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi nya útsyiko asa bunyǝ. Nya se ótaka uńyǝǝ, béyifo wǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa lǝ Yesu eto diye ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Le lefe nǝmfo kenke ǝsuǝǝ, Saulo áti kama Bonamute eto bafokanɔfɔ kekleke nkǝ lǝ olo mǝ. Nya úsu Yudafɔ eto Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke ɔflɔ.

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Mfó úsu ɔ́tɔ nkǝ bǝtǝ wǝ ekue lǝ ufi ɔlɛ usuko Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ lǝ Damasko lǝ ɔkɛ ǝsuǝale umuǝ basani ku besio ba kenke díkafo Ditiki Sɛɛ eto kusu fɔfɔ komfo bánɔ lǝ okpa mǝ usuko Yerusalem.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Saulo díkanyi ɔfi Damasko ku wǝ nsusu bomfoo, dikunǝ diyǝnde ditsyuǝ ko dítsyi kato lékpo wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Údu ɔ́nɔ nya ɔ́nɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ nlɛ wǝ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Saulo, Saulo, beso fanlɛ mɛ bɔkpɔnko kekleke nko?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Nya Saulo lɛtɔ nkǝ, “Bonamute, fǝ owoe uni?” Ɔkɔɛ ǝmǝ ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mɔ Yesu wǝ fanlɛ bɔkpɔnko kekleke uni.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Nya betidi bamǝ ku Saulo nyɛ lǝ kusu bantɔ mfó léle bǝńyǝ, bánte lesa nǝ baatɛyi. Bánɔ nkomǝ eto etikididi, fɛɛ bǝnnyǝ kuutsyuǝ saa.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Saulo létaka úńyǝ fɛ úminkli ǝnǝmii, wénsifo bunyǝ. Nya bálɛ wǝ lǝ kɔni bákpa wǝ bédufǝnko Damasko eto umǝ.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Úsiǝ mfo eyi ǝtsyǝ wǝnńyǝ asa, wéndi lesa, wǝnnǝ kulesa.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, okasebi wǝ léfo ɔ́nɔ ákɛ lǝ Damasko wǝ baakpo Ananias. Bonamute ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte wǝ ókpadi wǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Ananias!” Nya ɔ́tɔnɔ nkǝ, “Bonamute, nnyǝ mɔɔ!”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Nya Bonamute lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Taka lǝ ayɛ lǝ kusu ko baakpo Kusutsyɔtsyɔ, nya lǝ esu Yuda eto diyo lǝ atɔ nwǝ baakpo Saulo wǝ ditsyi Tarso eto disi. Okpe mfo ɔlɛ ɔla boto.

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Mfo nya Ananias dílǝkǝ kɛnyɛ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bonamute, mbɔnɔ sesiasesia bulǝ bo eto okle utidi ǝmfo nlɛ bafokanɔfɔ bokpe lǝ Yerusalem!

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Nya míkanɔ tsya bǝ ófo ǝsuǝale eto ekue utsyiko lǝ besumunyǝ ninǝ ɔflɔ nkǝ ubǝ umuǝ bafokanɔfɔ kenke lǝ Damasko!”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Bonamute átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Su lǝ esu eyifo lesa nǝ míntɛyi fǝ. Nnya ni bǝ mbulǝkǝ utidi ǝmfo miǝ lǝ udi mɔ ɔyɛkpa wǝ luufi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ usuko mba lenni Israelfɔ ku baka ku Israelfɔ kenke lǝ bate mɛ.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nyaso Ananias ésu údufǝ diyo nǝ Saulo lɛkɛ, nya úfi wǝ ani útǝkǝ lǝ wǝ, nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Mɔyimi Saulo, Bonamute Yesu wǝ dílǝkǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ óte fǝ lǝ kusuu, wǝ Yesu kosate ke ni futsyǝ létsyese mɛ fǝ ɔflɔ nkǝ lǝ esinkli ǝnyǝ asa nnya lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ lǝ disiǝko fǝ.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Nya se údi lesa ólosǝǝ, usinkli údufǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ úmuǝ ale. Saulo Ábe Dikiti Sɛ Nǝmǝ Letsya Lǝ Damasko. Saulo ǝsiǝ lǝ bafokanɔfɔ ɔflɔ lǝ Damasko itǝ eyi asiani.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝǝ, údu úsu Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ úsu údi etiki úlǝkǝ óte nkǝ Yesu eyifo Onanto eto Ubi.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Iyifo mba kenke lɛnɔ wǝ ditiki nǝmfo yanii. Nya bátɔ mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Inni utidi ǝmfo létsya ófe úkalo Yesu eto mba ntǝkǝko wǝ lǝ Yerusalem lefe nǝ léfe mǝǝ? Nya inni bubǝ úbǝ nkǝ wǝǝbǝ umuǝ bafokanɔfɔ usuko besumunyǝ ninǝ ɔflɔ?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe kpǝ eto kamaa, Yudafɔ bamǝ átsya báse uyi bǝnkǝ boolo Saulo.

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bǝtsyuǝ ǝbǝ bátɛyi wǝ itsyi lǝ ketsyue kamǝ ǝsuǝ. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, bákase umǝ ǝmǝ eto mbɔsie ketsye ku kakɔmi itǝ kase buunyǝ wǝ balo eso.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Nyaso lǝ ketsye kanii, Saulo eto bayɛnkotsyuǝ éfi wǝ bákpe lǝ ditsyitsya kafo nya báyɛko wǝ ku ukuǝ lǝ dibiǝ nǝ nkpe lǝ ofati ǝsuǝ úbǝ kasɔ ɔ́bɔ údu lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ. Saulo Ésu Údufǝ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Nyaso Barnaba áwunsǝko wǝ ókpa wǝ úsuko batɔnkpe bamǝ ɔflɔ ɔ́teyi mǝ kase ótsyako Bonamute lǝ kusu, nya Bonamute dídi wǝ etiki. Usi ɔ́tɛyi mǝ kase Saulo dílǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte údi etiki lǝ kpakpaakpe itsyi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ lǝ Damasko.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, báfo wǝ bákpe lǝ mǝ nti, ɔ́kɛ ku mǝ nya báyɛ bábé Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya lǝ Yesu eto diye ǝsuǝ lǝ Yerusalem.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Nya kama údi etiki nya ókpe kǝnǝ ku Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ ba laatɛyi Sigriki, fɛɛ bábɛbɛ kusu bǝ lǝ balo wǝ.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfóo, se wǝ bayimi ba ni bafokanɔfɔ lɛnɔ nnya nkoo, bákpa Saulo bésuko Kaisarea nya bákpa wǝ bátsyese wǝ umǝ Tarso.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, bafokanɔfɔ kenke ba lɛkɛ lǝ Yudea ku Galilea ku Samariaa, ála báwɔnɔ bǝsiǝ, sikpi eto bulǝ saa mensikɛ mǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bǝnyǝ sɛtɔsusu nya bǝsiǝ nkpǝ lǝ Bonamute eto kototsyuetsyue ǝsuǝ, nya béfi mǝ ǝsuǝ bákpe lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto ǝsuǝale eto bɔwunsǝko ǝsuǝ. Petro Ésu Lida Ku Yopa.

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Petro áyɛ ótsyilama nlebe kpǝ óbe bafokanɔfɔ kenke. Nya úsu únyǝ bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe lǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Lida tsya.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Mfó úsu únyǝ osani utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Ainea wǝ nlɛ bufi nlabe lǝ kalebe kani, ani ku akpa díkpǝ alɛ mfamfla sɔ̃ɔ̃.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Nya se Petro dínyǝ wǝǝ, ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Ainea, Yesu Kristo átsya fǝ bufi! Taka ǝńyǝ lǝ ǝkǝnǝ fǝ belabeke!” Lamfolamfo Ainea átaka uńyǝ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Nya se betidi ba kenke nkpe lǝ Lida ku Saron eto kǝmǝ dínyǝ bǝ Ainea átaka ɔlɛ bɔyɛ kafe mfóo, bátsyi bawuninsǝ béfi ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Bonamute ǝmǝ.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Usio utsyuǝ wǝ léfo ɔ́nɔ akɛ lǝ Yopa wǝ baakpo Tabita nya lǝ Sigriki ǝsuǝ bǝ Dorkas eto kasɔ ni kafɛ. Úfi wǝ lefe kenke úyifo kasɛɛ kpǝ nya úyifo kafo ɛkpɛ útǝ bakombo.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Úbǝ ɔ́nɔ bufi nya úkpǝ. Se bǝflǝsǝ wǝ ntu bálosǝǝ, bésu bálɔ wǝ bákpo lǝ abanslo kato.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfó se bánɔ bǝ Petro ǝbǝ údufǝ Lida itsyise Lida mantsyo lǝ Yopa ɔflɔɔ, bafokanɔfɔ lǝ Yopa ákpe wǝ betidi inuǝ bǝ bakpa wǝ baboko Yopa akpa akpa.

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Nyaso Petro ǝmuǝ ǝsuǝ nya útǝkǝko mǝ besu kaka balabesǝ wǝ kato. Nya bakɔbǝsiǝ ba nkpe mfóo, díńyǝ batsyilama Petro banlɛ nku buwi, nya béfi awu akpa akpa nya Dorkas léka lǝ wǝ nkpǝ lefe banlɛ wǝ bote.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Petro ǝtǝ nkǝ mǝmblɛ bɔ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ kafo nya óse akonki óto ola. Nya útsyi ǝnǝmi útǝ ɔkɔ ǝmǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Tabita, taka!” Lamfolamfo úmǝnkli wǝ ǝnǝmi, nya kase únyǝ Petro koo, ótaka úsiǝ.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Nya Petro ǝnǝ kɔni utsyuǝ nya ɔ́wunsǝko wǝ ótaka únyǝ. Petro esinkli ókpadi bafokanɔfɔ ku bakɔbǝsiǝ bamǝ bǝbǝ, nya úfi wǝ ókpe mǝ lǝ ani ku nkpǝ.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nya ditiki nǝmfo dísensǝ lékpo lǝ Yopa mfó, itǝ betidi kpǝ áfo Bonamute bánɔ.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, Petro ǝsiǝ lǝ Yopa mfó itǝ eyi kpǝ lǝ nwǝ baakpo Simon wǝ díkeyifo kubukikue eto keyifo mfó ɔflɔ.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, osani utsyuǝ ǝsiǝ lǝ Kaisarea eto umǝ wǝ baakpo Kornelio. Uyifo uninǝ utǝ Romafɔ eto bamamanyǝ eto ditsyukpa nǝ baakpo Italiafɔ.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Uyifo utidi sɛɛ nya wǝ ku wǝ diyo eto betidi kenke díyifo mba leesumu Onanto. Utidi ǝmfo ákawunsǝko Yudafɔ eto bakombo, nya úkato ola lefe saa kǝtǝ Onanto.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Diyi lɔni lǝ ketsyitsyokɔmi eto abombo ǝtsyǝ sekaa, únyǝ bulǝkǝkate butsyuǝ. Únyǝ lǝ bulǝkǝkate ǝsuǝ bomǝ nkǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝbǝ, nya ókpadi wǝ nkǝ, “Kornelio!”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Kornelio dínyǝ wǝǝ, óbe wǝ tũũ ku sikpi nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Onamute, be ini?” Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmfo átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Kase faato ola ku kase feeyifo kafo ɛkpɛ kǝtǝ betidi édufǝ Onanto ǝnǝ.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Kpe betidi bǝtsyuǝ lǝ besu Yopa lǝ besu bakpadi osani wǝ baakpo Simon wǝ bensi baakpo Petro baboko.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Okpe lǝ Simon wǝ nsi lǝ lekpo ɔflɔ wǝ leeyifo kubukikuǝ eto keyifo mfo.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Se Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmfo díduu, Kornelio ákpadi wǝ diyo eto betidi inuǝ ku bamamanyǝ eto mba laase wǝ tsya eto ɔni wǝ ni utidi sɛɛ.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ lesa nǝ díbǝ, nya ókpe mǝ nkǝ besu Yopa.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Se kale dísiǝ nya banyɛ lǝ kusu bantɔ nya ibu sɛkɛ lǝ bedufǝ Yopa mfóo, Petro ábe úsu diyo nǝ bankpe eto kato itǝ kakɔmi eto lefosi abombo ǝnuǝ eto olatoto.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Kukpɛnsɛ étsyiko wǝ bɔlɛ, fɛɛ mfe banlɛ lesa bonwa nɛ. Nya únyǝ bulǝkǝkate butsyuǝ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: Únyǝ se kato lɛsini, nya únyǝ lesa nǝ fe kafia flubia kplɛ kǝtsyuǝ ka lɛmɔ, nya banlɛ kǝ lǝ esi ana nyamǝ ketsyi kato kanlɛ kasɔ bubǝ.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Lǝ kafia kamǝ kafo aakǝ bebuki akpa akpa ku bawa ku baklɔbɛ.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Mfó ɔ́nɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Petro, taka, lo lǝ edi!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Petro édi etiki ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Owo lo! Kulefe! Bonamute, méndi tɔ kulesa nǝ Yudafɔ leekisi budi nyǝ!”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɔkɔɛ emfo esi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lesa nǝ Onanto díyifo kɛnkɛɛ, mantɛyi fǝ iyifo ekisisa!”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Únyǝ bulǝkǝ kate bomfo sitsyǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃. Nya ditsyǝfǝ kamaa, bétsyi kafia kamfo besuko kato.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfoo, ditsyǝbi nǝ dílǝkǝ ǝsuǝ léte mfó éyifo Petro yanii, nyaso ɔ́tɔ wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, “Be nnya mínyǝ mfo lete, nnya kasɔ nkpǝ?” Lefe nǝmfo kee, betidi ba Kornelio lékpe mfó ǝbǝ bédufǝ umǝ ǝmǝ. Nya se bǝnyǝ diyo nǝ banlɛ bɔbɔbɛɛ, bǝbǝ bǝńyǝ lǝ ni kesie.

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: Nya bǝńyǝ banlɛ kaka Simon Petro nkpe disi bɔtɔ.

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Nya se Petro dísiǝ úkǝbu disi itsyi lǝ bulǝkǝkate bo únyǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Lɛwɔnɔ ídi wǝ etiki lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Bebe basani itsyǝ ǝbǝ balɛ fǝ bɔbɛbɛ.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Taka, muǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ esu ko mǝ. Mante sikpi ku mǝ butǝkǝko kesu. Mɔ uni létsyese mǝ.”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Nyaso Petro ésu únyǝ mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔ ni nwǝ bɛnlɛ bɔbɛbɛ. Bomfo buɛɛ.”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ wǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Butsyi Kornelio wǝ ni Romafɔ eto omamanyǝ ninǝ ɔflɔ. Uyifo utidi sɛɛ wǝ lǝǝsumu Onanto. Nya Yudafɔ kenke laala wǝ asa tinti. Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ okpe fǝ utidi lǝ ǝbǝ wǝ diyo lǝ ɔnɔ fǝ kɛnyɛ eto etiki nya fanyɛnko.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso Petro átɔnɔ ókpa mǝ údufǝnko kaka onkpe nya ófo mǝ fe wǝ bafɔ útǝ bátsyimɛ wǝ ɔflɔ. Se kale dísiǝǝ, ótaka útǝkǝko mǝ. Nya bafokanɔfɔ ba ditsyi Yopa mfo eto bǝtsyuǝ dítǝkǝko mǝ bésu.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Bésu bédufǝ Kaisarea kǝsiǝsǝ. Lefe nǝmfóo, Kornelio ébokpo wǝ diyo eto betidi utsyǝ ku wǝ bayɛtsyuǝ bǝtsyuǝ nya bensi bensǝkǝ Petro eto bubǝ kedufǝ.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Kase Petro nlɛ diyo nǝmǝ budufǝǝ, Kornelio átsyako wǝ nya óse akonko lǝ Petro eto ǝnǝmi nya óbokosǝ wǝ disi kasɔ.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Petro átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Taka ǝńyǝ! Mintsya utidi ǝtǝ mini.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Nya lǝ kase bantɔ banlɛ etiki budii, Kornelio ákpa wǝ úsuko kaka betidi fɛfɛ létsya bensi.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mi kosate yi biǝ bo Yudafɔ eto kɔfi mǝntǝ kusu nkǝ ndufǝ utidi wǝ lenni bo ɔni eto diyo nye nsu wǝ ɔflɔ kulaatsya. Fɛɛ Onanto ǝlǝkǝ óte mɛ nkǝ membu kuutsyuǝ saa fe utidi wǝ dimeńyǝ utǝ lee menyifo kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso se fákpe fǝ bakpadi mɛɛ, mánni nya míntaka mímbǝ nɛ. Nya beso bɛnlɛ mɛ bokpadi?”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Kornelio átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Fe eyi ana ni nnya léfee mmǝǝ, nya lǝ lefe nǝmfo sekaa, se minlɛ ola boto lǝ kakɔmi eto ǝtsyǝ eto lefe lǝ mɔ diyoo, lefua utidi utsyuǝ ko lɛbɔ úńyǝ mɛ lǝ ǝnǝmi, okpe awu nnya díkǝyǝnde!

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Kornelio, Onanto ánɔ fǝ olatoto ku kase feeyifo kafo ɛkpɛ kǝtǝ betidi. Nyaso kpe betidi lǝ besu Yopa lǝ bakpadi nwǝ baakpo Simon wǝ bensi baakpo Petro wǝ nkpe lǝ Simon wǝ leeyifo keyifo kubukikue eto lǝ lekpo ɔflɔ baboko.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Nko eso lamfolamfo mínkpe fǝ ɔtɔnkpe miǝ ubǝ okpadi fǝ lǝ ǝbǝ nya fintsya lekplatsya fátaka fákpe akpa kusu fǝbǝ nɛ. Nyaso bomblɛ si lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi mfo busǝkǝ fǝ bǝ lǝ ǝbǝ atɛyi bo ditiki nǝ Bonamute lɛtɛyi fǝ nkǝ batɛyi bo.” Petro Édi Etiki Úlǝkǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ Nǝmǝ Kasɔ Óte Mǝ.

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Nyaso Petro édi mǝ etiki nkǝ, “Nnyǝ ntsyǝ lekpo sitinti minkǝ Onanto aambe betidi eto ǝnǝmi lǝ wǝ eyifosa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Bomu kusǝ saa eto utidi wǝ laabokosǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ kǝtǝ wǝ, keyifo kasɛɛ, wǝ waala nɛ.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Mfo nnɔ miǝ bɛnɔ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ Onanto dílǝkǝ óte Israelfɔ bǝ, ɛyɛ lǝ Yesu Kristo wǝ ni betidi kenke eto Onamute ǝsuǝǝ, boonyǝ diyuǝ.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Biyi lesa kplɛ nǝ díbǝ Israel eto kasɔ kenke idu lǝ lefe nǝ Yohanes lébe letsya lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ itsyi lǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa nǝmɔ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Nya bɛnɔ tsya kase Onanto lékpo Yesu wǝ ditsyi Nasaret ɔhile utsyǝ lǝ lefe nǝ úfi Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ku ǝsuǝale útǝ wǝ. Nya ótsyilama úkeyifo asa sɛɛ, nya ótsya mba Obonsam díkelǝ kɛlɛkɛ mǝ ketsyiko lǝ bulǝ bomǝ ǝsuǝ itsyise Onanto ǝsiǝ ko wǝ.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Búdi adansiɛ lǝ asa nnya kenke úyifo lǝ Israel eto kasɔ ku Yerusalem. Mfó ǝmǝ básɔ wǝ lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝ bálo nɛ.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Onanto esi ótaka wǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ nkpǝ lǝ diyi tsyǝfǝ ǝsuǝ nya úlǝkǝ wǝ óte betidi dínyǝ.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ inni betidi ba kenke dínyǝ wǝ. Bomu bo mba Onanto dílǝkǝ nkǝ budi wǝ badansiɛɛ, bo ni mba dínyǝ wǝ nɛ. Nya búdi nya búnǝ ku wǝ lǝ wǝ botaka kǝńyǝ eto kama.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Bo ni mba ótsyese nkǝ busu bɔtɛyi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ kalebe saa butǝ betidi nɛ. Nya lǝ bulǝkǝ bote boǝnkǝ, Onanto ni futsyǝ dílǝkǝ Yesu fe Okponkpo itǝ bǝkpǝnkpǝ ku kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: Nya Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba kenke dídi etiki itsyi lǝ wǝ bǝnkǝ utsyuǝ saa kee léfo wǝ ɔ́nɔɔ, Onanto ebufi wǝ abua ɔtsyɛ wǝ lǝ Yesu eto diye eto ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ.” Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ Áyifǝ Dísiǝ Lǝ Mba Lenni Yudafɔ Tsya Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Petro ńyǝ ɔnlɛ etiki nyamfo budii, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ áyifǝ dísiǝ lǝ mba kenke lɛkɛ mfo banlɛ Petro eto ditiki nǝmǝ bɔnɔ ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ eto bafokanɔfɔ ba dítǝkǝko Petro betsyi Yopa bǝbǝ mfóo, etiki nyamǝ éyifo mǝ yanii! Itsyise Onanto ǝtǝ wǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nǝmǝ áyifǝ díbǝ dísiǝ lǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ tsya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bǝmbofo bɔtɛyi bǝ inni nko ini. Itsyise bánɔ se banlɛ bele bamba kpǝ bɔtɛyi katɛsɛ Onanto. Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Betidi bamfo tsya áfo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ fe kase bo tsya lefo esoo, boǝmbofo bɔni boǝ boǝmbuflǝsǝ mǝ ntu.”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Nyaso útǝ kusu nkǝ bǝflǝsǝ mǝ ntu lǝ Yesu Kristo eto diye ǝsuǝ. Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, bálɛ Petro lekpakpa bǝ usiǝ lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ itǝ eyi asiani.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: Yanklɛ fɛ batɔnkpe bamǝ ku bafokanɔfɔ bamba lǝ Yudea lɛnɔ bǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ tsya ébɔtsyi báwuninsǝ báfo Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ bánɔ.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Nyaso lefe nǝ Petro dísinkli úsu Yerusalem eto umǝǝ, Yudafɔ eto bafokanɔfɔ ba lɛkɛ mfóo, mántɔnɔ ko wǝ. Nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ,

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: “Beso fésu fásɔnɔ lǝ bafɔ ba dímanla alo nya fákpe kɔni lǝ ɔkpɛ ɔni ǝsuǝ fédi lesa ku mǝ?”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Nyaso Petro ǝlǝkǝ etiki nyamfo kenke kasɔ óte mǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe nǝ minkɛ lǝ Yopa minlɛ ola botoo, nnyǝ bulǝkǝkate butsyuǝ. Mfó mínyǝ kafia kplɛ kǝtsyuǝ ka banlɛ lǝ esi ana kétsyi kato banlɛ kǝ kasɔ bobokosǝ lǝ mɔ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ete mínɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ nlɛ etiki budi katɛyi nkǝ, ‘Petro! Taka, lo lǝ edi!’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Nyaso nlǝkǝ kɛnyɛ ntɛyi minkǝ, ‘Kula o-o! Kulefe! Bonamute, méndi tɔ kulesa nǝ Yudafɔ leekisi budi nyǝ.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ ɔkɔɛ ǝmfo esi údi etiki útsyi kato ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Lesa nǝ Onanto diyifo kɛnkɛɛ, mantɛyi fǝ diyifo dimankpa.’

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfó tututuu, betidi itsyǝ ba bákpe bǝ bakpa mɛ baboko Kaisarea ǝbǝ bédufǝ diyo nǝ minkɛ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Nya Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ lɛtɛyi mɛ nkǝ ntǝkǝko mǝ lǝ busu. Mɔ bayimi ekua bamfo ditsyi Yopaa, ǝtǝkǝko mɛ busu Kaisarea búsu búdufǝ osani wǝ lekpe mɛ batɔnkpe ini Kornelio eto diyo.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ɔ́tɛyi bo kase Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe dílǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte wǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Bɛbɛ utsyuǝ akpe Yopa lǝ usu ɔbɛbɛ nwǝ baakpo Simon Petro.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Wuudi mi etiki nnya luutǝ bǝ fǝ ku fǝ diyo eto betidi kenke luunyǝ bofo.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: “Fɛ míntsyiko Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ bɔtɛyii, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ébubǝ dídufǝ mǝ mǝmblɛ kenke lǝ ǝsuǝ fe kase dídufǝ bo lǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ kasɔ ketsyikokɔ kamǝ!

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nyamfo dítǝ mínkloma lesa nǝ Bonamute lɛtɛyi lefe nǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Sitinti Yohanes efi ntu uflǝsǝ betidi. Fɛɛ Onanto ebufi Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ uflǝsǝ mi.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Onanto ǝtǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ ǝtǝsa nyamfo fe kase úfi útǝ bo lǝ lefe nǝ bófo Bonamute Yesu Kristo bɔ́nɔɔ, owoe mini bǝ lǝ mmfo Onanto kusu buti nko?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Se bánɔ etiki nyamfoo, bénsite lesa nǝ bɔtɛyi. Bomu bátɛsǝ Onanto bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Sitinti Onanto ǝtǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ tsya kusu nkǝ batsyi bawuninsǝ lǝ bǝnyǝ nkpǝ.” Bafokanɔfɔ Lǝ Antiokia Efui Bakpe.

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ asa nyamfo nlɛ bubǝ nkoo, bafokanɔfɔ ba dítsyǝtsyi bédu lǝ bulǝ bo dítu mǝ itsyi lǝ kase bálo Stefano ǝsuǝǝ, bésu bédufǝ Foinike ku Kipro ku Antiokia eto kǝmǝ. Nya bátsyilama banlɛ ditiki sɛɛ nǝmǝ bɔtɛyi Yudafɔ hã.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Nya bafokanɔfɔ ba ditsyi Kipro ku Kirene dísu Antiokia bésu bátɛyi betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ Bonamute Yesu eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Nya se Bonamute eto ǝsuǝale lɛkɛ ko mǝ esoo, bafokanɔfɔ ba dífi ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Bonamute ǝbǝ befui bakpe.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe lǝ Yerusalem lɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfoo, bátsyese Barnaba bǝ usu lǝ usu obe lesa nǝ nlɛ bubǝ lǝ Antiokia.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Se úsu údufǝ mfó nya únyǝ kase Onanto lɛwunsǝ ko mǝǝ, únyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti nya usi ókpe mǝ nkɛale nkǝ bǝńyǝ kekleke lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ nya lǝ bedi ɔnukualɛ bǝtǝ Bonamute ku mǝ utu kenke.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Barnaba eyifo utidi sɛɛ wǝ nkpe bofokanɔ nya Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nsi ko wǝ tinti. Ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ bɔwunsǝko ǝsuǝǝ, bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ ǝbǝ befui bakpe.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, Barnaba ésu Tarso, úsu ɔ́bɛbɛ Saulo mfo.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Se únyǝ wǝǝ, ókpa wǝ bédu bǝbǝ Antiokia nya mǝ inuǝ bamǝ dísiǝ mfó lɛlɛ wɔlɔlɔ. Bakɛ mfó bákate betidi kpǝ asa. Antiokia mfóo, nya betsyiko bafokanɔfɔ bokpo bǝ Kristofɔ nɛ.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo tututuu, Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi bǝtsyuǝ etsyi Yerusalem bǝbǝ Antiokia.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Mfó mǝ nti eto ɔni wǝ baakpo Agabo, létaka uńyǝ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ kukpɛnsɛ kekleke ebubǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke. Nyamfo ǝbǝ ítǝ lǝ lefe nǝ Romafɔ eto Okankplɛ wǝ baakpo Klaudio nlɛ seka budi.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: Nyaso bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ éfi nsusu bǝ baako asa lǝ befi batsyese bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe lǝ Yudea eto kasɔ. Nya mǝ utsyuǝ saa léko kase woofo.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nya se báko bikǝ biamǝǝ, béfi mi bǝtǝ Barnaba ku Saulo nya béfi mi bésu bǝtǝ Yudea eto bafokanɔfɔ eto beninǝ.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝ, Oka Herodes ǝmuǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto bǝtsyuǝ nkǝ lǝ ókpe mǝ bulǝ kekleke.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Útǝ balo Yakobo wǝ ni Yohanes eto ɔyimisani ku lesiabi.
