english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Nya se únyǝ nkǝ nnya úyifo ákɛ Yudafɔ ɔkɔɛɛ, úmuǝ Petro tsya lǝ lefe nǝ beedi Abolo nnya Ǝyǝnkisǝsa Lǝmbǝ eto Diyi.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Nyaso mba laase mfó ákase Petro lǝ diyo nǝmǝ. Fɛɛ bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ ákato ola kekleke ákalɛ Onanto lekpakpa lǝ wǝ disi. Onanto Ǝlǝkǝ Petro Wǝ Bákpe Lǝ Diyo.

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ketsye ka eto kǝsiǝsǝ ka Herodes díbu disi nkǝ wútǝlǝkǝ Petro lǝ diyo utǝ betidi bamǝǝ, Petro álabe selabe lǝ mba laase eto inuǝ eto nti. Nya bákpe wǝ lǝ kɔnyɔ kplɛ ko béfi batsyasǝ lǝ mǝ kosate eto ani ǝsuǝ. Nya fɛ mba laase mfó eto bǝtsyuǝ díńyǝ banlɛ siku siamǝ bose.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Akpaakpa koo, Kato eto Ɔtɔnkpe ko lɛbɔ nya lekpasǝ léfo diyo nǝmǝ kenke. Nya Kato eto Ɔtɔnkpe dífi wǝ kɔni útǝkǝ Petro lǝ lekpake ósanklisǝ wǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Kɔse mlǝ lǝ ataka ǝńyǝ!” Kɔnyɔ kplɛ ko nkpe wǝ lǝ kɔni lɛsini kɔ́bɔ kókpo lǝ kasɔ.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Nya Kato eto Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Fi fǝ awu ku afɔkpa akpe.” Nya Petro díyifo nko. Nya Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ disi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Fi fǝ awu kɛntsyɛ akpe lǝ ǝtǝkǝko mɛ.”

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Nya báyɛ báfe mba laase eto ditsyukpa tutɛ ku bǝnuǝfǝ. Nya se báyo situ eto kesie kplɛ ka baayɛ kedufǝ umǝ ǝmǝǝ, siku siamǝ kosate ko lɛsini ǝsuǝ. Nya báyɛ lǝ kesie kamǝ tsya báfe. Nya se báyɛ bésu sɛtɔ sɛkɛɛ, Kato eto Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ ko lénya údu lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, fɛ Petro léte lesa nǝ díbǝ wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, “Mɔmɔ mbote miǝ sitinti ini bǝ Bonamute létsyese wǝ Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmfo nkǝ ubǝ ulǝkǝ mɛ utsyiko lǝ Herodes eto ani ku asa nnya Yudafɔ díbu bǝ booyifo mɛ tsya lǝ diyo nǝmfo kafo!”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Nya se únyǝ nnya díbǝ utsyǝ lekpoo, údu úsu Maria wǝ ni Yohanes Marko eto ambe eto diyo kaka betidi kpǝ létsya banlɛ ola boto.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Nya se Petro léyo diyo nǝmǝ eto kǝsiǝǝ, ɔ́sɔ siku siamǝ. Nya uyifoyifosiobi utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Roda ábɔ úsu nkǝ woabe futsyuǝ uni.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Se ɔ́nɔ Petro eto ɔkɔɛ utsyǝ lekpoo, únyǝ disuǝyuǝ ofensǝ! Itǝ wánsini wǝ siku siamǝ, bomu útsyetsyi úsu wǝ betidi ɔflɔ úsu ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ Petro ǝbǝ uńyǝ lǝ siku kama.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Ntɔ disi lébia fǝ?” Nya usi úńyǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ nkǝ sitinti ini. Nyaso bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Nyalɛ ǝtsyuǝǝ, wǝ kato eto ɔtɔnkpe uni!”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo kenke ǝsuǝǝ, Petro esi úńyǝ ɔlɛ kesie eto siku bɔsɔ. Nya lefe nǝ básini se nya bǝnyǝ wǝǝ, íyifo mǝ yanii.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Úyifo mǝ kɔni nkǝ bánkpo kudu. Ɔ́tɛyi mǝ asa nnya díbǝ ku kase Bonamute díbǝ úlǝkǝ we utsyiko lǝ diyo nǝmǝ kafo. Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bisu bɛtɛyi Yakobo ku bayimi bafɛfɛ asa nnya díbǝ.” Nya udu mǝ lǝ ɔflɔ úsu kalebe bamba.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Nya se kale dísiǝǝ, íyifo mba laase mfó yaa, nya éboko bulǝ kpǝ. Bátɔ mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Se tutuutu Petro lefe mɔmɔ?”

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Nya Herodes ǝtǝ bábɛbɛ wǝ tinti, fɛɛ bǝnnyǝ wǝ. Útǝ bǝmuǝ mba laase diyo nǝmǝ bákpoko mǝ lekpo, nya útǝ nkǝ balo mǝ. Lǝ nnya kamaa, Herodes édu lǝ Yudea úsu úsiǝ lǝ Kaisarea itǝ lefe ditsyuǝ. Oka Herodes Eto Ukpǝ.

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, Herodes ǝnyǝ ɔblɔ tinti ókpe Tiro ku Sidon eto betidi, nyaso bésu lǝ sitiditu ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ besu bǝnyǝ wǝ. Bésu bátɔ bɔwunsǝko betsyi lǝ Blasto wǝ ni Herodes eto nwǝ laabe diyo ɔflɔ bǝ ɔlɛ Herodes lekpakpa lǝ utǝ lǝ lɔniyifoyifo lǝ dibǝ lǝ mǝ inuǝ bamǝ ku Herodes eto nti. Ibǝ nko itsyise Herodes eto kasɔ bǝkǝnyǝ mǝ didisa ketsyiko.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ diyi nǝ Oka Herodes ditsyǝ mǝǝ, ókpe wǝ bekankǝ nya úsiǝ lǝ wǝ lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ údi betidi bamǝ etiki.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Se údi etiki ólosǝǝ, betidi bamǝ áfa básɔ ani bǝtǝ wǝ nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Onanto Kosate eto ɔkɔɛ uni! Inni utidi!”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, Bonamute eto Ɔtɔnkpe ákpe Herodes bufi lǝ ǝsuǝ, itsyise wémfi atɛsɛ Onanto. Nya batsyini dídi wǝ ǝsuǝ kenke, nya úkpǝ.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Barnaba ku Saulo lélosǝ mǝ keyifo ka bákpe mǝ bǝ besu beyifoo, bésinkli betsyi Yerusalem bǝbǝ nya bákpa Yohanes wǝ tsya baakpo Marko báyɛnko báboko.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Lǝ Antiokiaa, Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi bǝtsyuǝ ku bateasa ákɛ lǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa nǝmǝ nti. Mǝ ni Barnaba ku Simon wǝ baakpo utidi naa ku Lukio wǝ ditsyi Kirene ku Manaen nwǝ ku Oka Herodes békluǝsǝ bakɔ taa mǝ sikpefi lefe ku Saulo.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ banlɛ Bonamute bɔtǝsǝ nya bakɛ lǝ kɛnyɛklekle ǝsuǝǝ, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝbǝ dídi mǝ etiki nkǝ, “Bilǝkǝ Barnaba ku Saulo bitsyǝ itǝ keyifo ka eso mínkpadi mǝ.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, beninǝ bamǝ ákle kɛnyɛ nya báto ola nya bǝtǝkǝ mǝ ani, nya bǝtsyǝ mǝ kusu. Barnaba Ku Saulo Ésu Kipro.

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Se Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ditsyǝ mǝ kusu lélosǝǝ, bésu umǝ wǝ baakpo Seleukia. Mfó béfi ɔklɔ báyila lekpo nǝmǝ bésu kasɔ ka ntu letsyilama eto umǝ wǝ baakpo Kipro.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Se bésu bédufǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Salamii, bésu Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ bésu bédi mǝ ditiki sɛɛ nǝmǝ. Nya Marko Yohanes díkǝko mǝ fe mǝ ɔwunsǝkotsyuǝ.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Se bédu mfóo, báyɛ lǝ kasɔ kamǝ bésu bédufǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Pafo. Mfó bátsyako Yudanyǝ wǝ ni ofate wǝ ni sedia eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi wǝ baakpo Bar-Yesu eto ubi.

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: Ofate ǝmfo ku Aba eto unyɛsiǝ wǝ baakpo Sergio Paulo eyifo bayɛtsyuǝ. Nko eto Aba eto unyɛsiǝ ǝmfo wǝ diyifo obesibe ákpadi Barnaba ku Saulo nkǝ bǝbǝ bǝnyǝ wǝ, itsyise óla nkǝ lǝ ɔnɔ Onanto eto ditiki nǝmǝ.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nya se bésuu, ofate ǝmǝ wǝ baakpo tsya bǝ Elima lǝ Sigriki ǝsuǝǝ, ǝnǝ etiki ku mǝ. Óla nkǝ lǝ ulǝkǝ Aba eto unyɛsiǝ ǝmfo eto nsusu utsyiko lǝ etiki nnya Saulo ku Barnaba lela bǝ beedi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Saulo wǝ tsya bensi baakpo Pauloo, ábe ofate ǝmfo ku lɛblɔfi kekleke. Nya úlǝkǝ óte wǝ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto ǝsuǝale nkǝ, “Fǝ Obonsam eto ubi, eyifo odia ku nwǝ laawɛ betidi kakpe. Nya feekisi kasɛyifoyifo, nya faatsyi Bonamute eto ɔnukualɛtiki kawuninsǝ keyifo bidia!

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Bonamute ebutǝkǝ fǝ kɔni mɔmɔ nya wuutǝ fǝ ǝnǝmi ba, nya fensiembunyǝ kofe bio isu ese lǝ lefe ditsyuǝ eto kama!” Lamfolamfo lɛtɔntɔ eti Elima lǝ ǝnǝmi, nya wénsifo asa bunyǝ bio. Úkawunya ani kabɛbɛ utidi nkǝ lǝ ɔyɛnko wǝ.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfo se Aba eto unyɛsiǝ ǝmfó dínyǝ nyamfo nkoo, íyifo wǝ yanii, nya ófo Bonamute eto ditiki nǝmǝ ɔ́nɔ. Barnaba Ku Saulo Ésu Antiokia Lǝ Pisidia.

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, Paulo ku mba ntǝkǝko wǝ ésu bédufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ nya bédu lǝ Pafo bésu Perge wǝ ni umǝnkplɛ wǝ nkpe lǝ Pamfilia eto kasɔ. Mfó nya Marko Yohanes dídu mǝ lǝ ɔflɔ úsinkli úsu Yerusalem.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Se bédu lǝ Pergee, bésu Antiokia, umǝnkplɛ wǝ nkpe lǝ Pisidia. Nya lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyii, bésu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ nya bǝsiǝ kasɔ lǝ betidi bamǝ nti.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bákakla asa betsyiko lǝ Moses eto kɔfi eto kukue ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto asawɔniwɔni bálosǝǝ, katsyakɔ eto batɔnkotɔnko ǝtǝ Paulo ku wǝ bǝtsyuǝ bote bǝ, “Bayimi, lǝ utsyuǝ kpe ditiki ditsyuǝ nkǝ woatɛyi lǝ ufi okpe bo nkɛalee, lǝ ubǝ ɔtɛyi.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Mfó Paulo létaka uńyǝ nya óte kɔni kato lete bǝ bedi kaa, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Israelfɔ ku mimba kenke ba lenni Israelfɔ ba leetsyue Onanto koto, bitsyue ato lǝ bɛnɔ!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Israelfɔ eto Onanto ni nwǝ dílǝkǝ bo banamǝ nya ókpo mǝ atsyɔ. Nya úyifo mǝ kusǝ kplɛ eto betidi lǝ lefe nǝ bǝsiǝ kafɔle ka ni Egipte. Onanto ǝlǝkǝ mǝ utsyiko Egipte ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝale kplɛ nyamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Nyaso bebe kukɔnɔ! Biǝntǝ lǝ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto etiki nnya bátɛyi bǝtsyǝ lǝ ɛyɛ bubǝ kǝtǝ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ nko.

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Se ɔ́tsylɔsǝ ǝsǝ akuanse lǝ Kanan eto kasɔɔ, úfi mǝ kasɔ kamǝ utǝ Israelfɔ fe mǝ kedikɔ.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, Onanto átakasǝ wǝ lǝ seka ǝsuǝ nya úfi David úsiǝsǝ lǝ lekankpomɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ. David ǝmfo ni utidi wǝ ǝsuǝ Onanto dídi etiki utsyiko nkǝ, ‘Yese eto ubi David ni utidi wǝ mɔ utu laala, itsyise wuuyifo lesa nǝ maala.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: “Nko ke ini bǝ Onanto áyɛ lǝ David eto kafo, nya úlǝkǝ Ofomfokǝtǝ wǝ ni Yesu utǝ Israelfɔ fe kase utsyǝ bo kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Nya fɛ Yesu luubǝǝ, Ntuflǝsǝtidi Yohanes ǝbǝ ɔ́tɛyi Israelfɔ kenke nkǝ batsyi mǝ nkpǝ bawuninsɛ lǝ mǝ akɔɛsi bayɛ abua buuyifo lǝ uflǝsǝ mǝ ntu.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ Yohanes eto keyifo nlɛ kalosɛkɔ bubǝǝ, ɔ́tɛyi betidi bamǝ nkǝ, ‘Owoe bibu biǝ wǝ mini? Mɔ ni nwǝ bɛnlɛ kusu bobe le? Owo lo! Fɛɛ wǝ kpe lǝ kusu ɔlɛ bubǝ mɛ kama, nya mǝnńyǝtǝ bǝ nyankli wǝ afukpa eto kekue kulaa tsya!’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Mɔ bayimi, Israel bebi, mi mba ni Abraham eto bebi ku mimba lenni Yudafɔ fɛ biesumu Onanto, nkpǝ manlo eto bofo bomfo ǝbǝ itǝ bomblɛ kenke.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Betidi ba nkpe lǝ Yerusalem ku mǝ beninǝ mámfo Yesu bánɔ bǝ wǝ ni mǝ Ofomfokǝtǝ ǝmǝ. Nya bánnɔ kasɔ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto ditiki nǝ baakla kate bo lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi saa ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ bǝtǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto etiki ǝbǝ itǝ se bálo wǝ dikpǝnkpi eso.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: “Nya lefe nǝ békeyifo lesa nǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyi utsyǝ itsyi lǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ bálosǝǝ, bǝlǝkǝ wǝ lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝ bésu bebikǝ.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Onanto átakasǝ wǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ.

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ eyi kpǝ ǝsuǝǝ, úlǝkǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ óte betidi ba wonko mǝ lɛyɛ itsyi Galilea isu Yerusalem. Mǝ ni betidi ba nlɛ adansiɛ budi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ Israel eto kusǝ nɛ.

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Nya nyamfo ni lesa nǝ Onanto lɛtɛyi itsyi lǝ wǝ botakasǝ ketsyiko ɔkɔmǝ kǝbǝ ni nkǝ wembubu lǝ kudi kafo. ‘Mookpo fǝ atsyɔ nnya diyifo kɛnkɛ fe kase mínse uyi mintsyǝ miǝ mookpo David.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Nko usi ɔ́tɛyi lǝ David eto kusǝ bamba nkǝ, ‘Mǝmbutǝ bǝ fǝ uyifoyifo kɛnkɛ ǝmfo kɛ lǝ kudi kafo ebú.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: “Ditiki nǝmfo manyɛ lǝ David eto kutsyǝ itsyise usiǝ nkpǝ úyifo Onanto eto lelabi ǝsuǝ, nya ukpǝ nya bébikǝ wǝ ku wǝ bana mǝ nya wǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ tsya díbú.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nwǝ Onanto létakasǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝǝ, wǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ mémbú lǝ kudi kafo.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: “Nyaso bayimi, bola boǝ bete lǝ ekpa bǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Yesu ǝmfo ǝsuǝǝ, nya bábe abuabufikatsyɛ eto ditiki nǝmǝ letsya bǝtǝ mi nɛ.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto átɛyi nkǝ, ‘Bebe mimba lǝǝmǝ mɔ ditiki. Bitǝ lǝ iyifo mi yanii lǝ bǝtsylɔ! Itsyise lesa nǝ minlɛ buyifo mmii, iyifo lesa nǝ biembofo bɛnɔ, lǝ utsyuǝ ǝlǝkǝ ni kasɔ ote mi kulaa tsya.’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Se Paulo ku Barnaba nlɛ bɔbɔ lǝ disikakpekɔ mfóo, betidi bamǝ átɛyi mǝ bǝ besi bǝbǝ bedi mǝ etiki nyamfo lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyuǝ eto diyi nǝ luubǝ.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bésensǝ lǝ disikakpekɔ mfóo, Yudafɔ kpǝ ku bafokanɔfɔ ba léfiani Yudafɔ nya baate Onanto sikpi ba lɛkɛ lǝ disikakpekɔ mfóo, ǝtǝkǝko Paulo ku Barnaba. Batɔnkpe bamǝ édi mǝ etiki nya balɛ mǝ kɔlɛ bǝ bǝńyǝ kekleke lǝ bǝsiǝ nkpǝ le lefonǝ Onanto ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi nǝ disi dítǝkǝko nǝmfóo, ibu sɛkɛɛ eni umǝ ǝmǝ kenke eto betidi ǝbǝ disikakpekɔ nǝmǝ lǝ banɔ Bonamute eto ditiki nǝmǝ.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Yudafɔ eto beninǝ dínyǝ sitiditu sia díbǝ disikakpekɔ mfóo, básɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bétsyiko etiki bunǝ ku Paulo lǝ etiki nnya ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi ǝsuǝ nya báka wǝ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Paulo ku Barnaba ákpe utu besi bédi etiki bátɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, “Ele bǝ budi Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo itǝ Yudafɔ bɔtɔnsǝ. Nya se bɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfo nya bɛtɛyi biǝnkǝ bianla nkpǝ manlo bomfoo, búfi ni bɔlɛ mba lenni Yudafɔ butǝ.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Nyamfo ni kɔfi ko Bonamute dítǝ bo nkǝ, ‘Nnyifo fǝ fe okandiɛ ntǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ bǝ lǝ efi bofo aboko kawunsiǝ eto lɛkpɔ saa.’ ”

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ lɛnɔ lesa nǝ bátɛyi mfoo, bǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti, nya bátɛsǝ Bonamute eto ditiki nǝmǝ. Nya mba kenke Onanto dílǝkǝ itǝ nkpǝ manloo, áfo bánɔ.

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Nya Bonamute eto ditiki nǝmǝ dísensǝ dísu kasɔ kamǝ kenke.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ édi ute bákpe lǝ besio ba nkpe dibu ba leesumu Onanto ku umǝ eto beninǝ eto nti. Nya bétsyiko Paulo ku Barnaba bulǝ kǝńyǝ mǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bála mǝ babɔsǝ lǝ mǝ lekpake mfó.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lefe nǝ banlɛ budu lǝ umǝ ǝmǝǝ, báflɛ utembu wǝ nkpo mǝ lǝ akpa bákpo lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ nnya léte bǝ umǝ ǝmǝ eto betidi áni mǝ. Nyamfo eto kamaa, bédu mfo bésu Ikonion.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Nya bafokanɔfɔ ba lɛkɛ lǝ Antiokia mfó dínyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti nya béyi ku Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nya se bédufǝ Ikonion tsyaa, Paulo ku Barnaba ésu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ mfó. Nya se bátɛyi Yudafɔ ku mba lenni Yudafɔ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝǝ, mǝ nti eto betidi kpǝ áfo bánɔ.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nya Yudafɔ ba dímamfo Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo bánɔɔ, ǝnǝ etiki ku mǝ nya bábiasǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ eto nsusu nya bǝtǝ bǝ bátaka bǝńyǝ lǝ Paulo ku Barnaba lǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ batɔnkpe bamǝ ǝsiǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ itǝ lefe kɛntsyɛ ditsyuǝ ákate Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ ku ute. Nya Onanto tsya dílǝkǝ wǝ lefonǝ eto etiki eto sitinti óte ɛyɛ lǝ ayanisa kpǝ nnya útǝ béyifo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Ibǝ bǝ umǝ ǝmǝ eto betidi áma lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ, bǝtsyuǝ ǝtǝkǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto kama nya bǝtsyuǝ tsya dítǝkǝ lǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ eto kama.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Se bésu Listraa, osani utsyuǝ akɛ mfó wǝ lɛtsyini taa lefe nǝ bále wǝ nya wámfo tɔ botaka kayɛ kulefe.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Úsiǝ mfó úkanɔ ditiki sɛɛ nǝ Paulo ńyǝ ɔnlɛ budi. Nya se Paulo lébe wǝ tũũ, únyǝ nkǝ ófo ɔ́nɔ nya wǝ bufi bomǝ luuyuǝ wǝǝ,

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: Nyaso Paulo áfã bembembe ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Taka ǝńyǝ lǝ fǝ akpa ǝsuǝ!” Nya osani ǝmfo lésa ótaka úńyǝ nya útsyiko bɔyɛ kafe.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Se betidi ba díńyǝ mfó banlɛ ditiki nǝmǝ ato butsyue dínyǝ lesa nǝ Paulo díyifoo, báfã bembembe lǝ Likaoniafɔ eto sele ǝsuǝ bátɛyi nkǝ, “Bananto beni! Báyifǝ bǝbǝ bo nti fenkǝ betidi!”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso bǝtǝkǝ Barnaba diye bǝ Seus. Nya bǝtǝkǝ Paulo tsya bǝ Herme, itsyise wǝ ni futsyǝ díńyǝ nlɛ etiki budi nɛ.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Nya Seus eto usumunyǝ, nwǝ eto kesumukɔ ńyǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ eto kabanto mfóo, éfi banantsyui ku kaflawasi óboko nkǝ lǝ wǝ ku wǝ betidi bamǝ lǝ befi bǝtǝ batɔnkpe bamfo kedi lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ eto kabɔsie.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Paulo ku Barnaba lɛnɔ lesa nǝ baala bǝ beeyifoo, bǝnǝ mǝ kosate eto awu nnya bankpe báfuadi, nya bétsyetsyi bédufǝ lǝ betidi bamǝ nti. Mfó nya báfã

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Boantomǝ, be bɛnlɛ buyifo mfó? Bontsya kasɔ kamfo eto betidi ete buni fe mi ke. Búbǝ mfo boǝ boefi ditiki sɛɛ nǝmfo boboko mi. Bisinkli bɛyɛ asa bimba nyamfo eto kakle buyifo lǝ bifi atɛsǝ bitǝ Onanto wǝ dítǝ bo nkpǝ. Wǝ ni futsyǝ díyifo kato ku kasɔ ku lekpo ku asa nnya nkpe lǝ nnya kenke!

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Kɔɔ Onanto ǝtǝ kusu betidi kenke nkǝ bǝsiǝ nkpǝ lǝ kase mǝ léla ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Tsya usi wǝǝlǝkǝ kate mi nkǝ okpe sitinti ɛyɛ lǝ asa sɛ nnya weeyifo kǝtǝ mi. Wǝǝtǝ mi ɔsɔmi ku bekelenkǝ lǝ lefe sɛɛ ǝsuǝ nya wǝǝtǝ mi edisa ku disuǝyuǝ tsya.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Ite bǝ bátɛyi mǝ nyamfo kenke tsyaa, ilǝ fɛ betidi bamfo lɛyɛ kedi kamfo mǝ butǝ.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Nya Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ ditsyi Antiokia, umǝ wǝ nkpe lǝ Pisidia ku Ikonion bǝbǝ bábiasǝ betidi bamfo eto nsusu, nya bátɔnɔ ku mǝ báto Paulo aba nya bákpi wǝ lǝ kasɔ bábɔnko lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ, nya bébu bǝ úkpǝ.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bafokanɔfɔ ba ńkpe mfo díbǝ bǝńyǝ batsyilama wǝǝ, ótaka nya usinkli úsu umǝ ǝmǝ. Nya se kale disiǝǝ, wǝ ku Barnaba édu bésu Derbe. Paulo Ku Barnaba Esinkli Besu Antiokia Wǝ Nkpe Lǝ Siria.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Nya besu bedi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ Derbe. Nya se betidi kpǝ bǝtsyuǝ díkafo bánɔ, bési besinkli bésu Listra ku Ikonion ku Antiokia lǝ Pisidia eto kasɔ.

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mfoo, besi bákpe bafokanɔfɔ nkpe mfo nkɛale bǝ bǝnyǝ kekleke lǝ mǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ. Nya bátɛyi mǝ tsya bǝ, “Ele bǝ bunyǝ kanyi kpǝ fɛ lǝ bunyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kama Paulo ku Barnaba tsya ǝlǝkǝ batɔnkotɔnko bǝtǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto etsyukpa nyamǝ kenke, nya lǝ olatoto kǝtǝ Onanto ku lǝ kɛnyɛklekle ǝsuǝǝ, béfi mǝ bákpe lǝ Bonamte wǝ báfo bánɔ eto ani.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bábɔ lǝ kǝmǝ ka nkpe lǝ Pisidiaa, bésinkli bésu Pamfilia eto kasɔ.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Nya batɛyi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ Perge fɛ báfe bésu Atalia.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Mfó bǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ báyɛ bésu Antiokia, kaka eto batɔnkotɔnko díkefi batɔnkpe bamǝ bákpe lǝ Onanto eto ani itǝ keyifo ka bákalosǝ mɔmɔ.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Se bésu bédufǝ Antiokiaa, bákpadi bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe mfó kenke bǝtsyǝ ditsyu nya bátɛyi mǝ kusu ko báyɛ eto etiki. Nya bátɛyi mǝ asa nnya kenke Onanto lɛyɛnko mǝ béyifo ku kase ɔ́sini bofokanɔ eto kusu utǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Nya bǝsiǝ lǝ Antiokia ku bafokanɔfɔ mfo itǝ lefe kɛntsyɛɛ ditsyuǝ.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ Paulo ku Barnaba lɛkɛ lǝ Antiokiaa, betidi bǝtsyuǝ ba ditsyi Yudea ǝbǝ mfó nya bákate bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ asa nnya ni bǝnkǝ, “Lǝ bánla fǝ lelo fe kase Moses eto lekpa letee, embunyǝ bofo.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Paulo ku Barnaba ku betidi bamfo ǝnǝ ditiki nǝmfo ɛklɛ. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, bǝtǝ bǝnkǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto bǝtsyuǝ tǝkǝko Paulo ku Barnaba lǝ besu Yerusalem lǝ mǝ ku batɔnkpe bamǝ ku bafokanɔfɔ eto beninǝ ba nkpe lǝ mfó lǝ balɛ lelolala eto etiki nyamfo babe.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Nyaso se bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa nǝmǝ dítsyǝ mǝ kusu bálosǝǝ, bédu bésu Foinike ku Samaria eto kǝmǝ nya bésu bǝnyǝ bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe mfó. Bátɛyi mǝ bǝ mɔmɔɔ, mba lenni Yudafɔ tsya éfi mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Onanto, nnya ni bǝnkǝ mǝ tsya áfo bánɔ. Ditiki nǝmfo ákpe mǝmblɛ disuǝyuǝ tinti.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Nya se bésu bédufǝ Yerusalem, mǝ beninǝ bamǝ ku bafokanɔfɔ ku batɔnkpe bamǝ áfo mǝ kukɔnɔ. Nya Paulo ku Barnaba dífi mǝ kusu ko bésu eto amaniǝ bábɔ mǝ ku asa nnya kenke Onanto díyifo ɔ́yɛ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Farisifɔ ba díkafo Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ eto ditsyukpa eto bǝtsyuǝ átaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ nti bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Ele be bayimi ba lenni Yudafɔ tsya la lelo nya lǝ beyifo lǝ Moses eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nyaso mǝ beninǝ ku batɔnkpe bamǝ átsya fe ditsyukpa nya bédufǝ lǝ ditiki nǝmfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ katsyakɔ kamfóo, lǝ lefe nǝ bákalɛ etiki nyamǝ bábe ɛklɛɛ, Petro átaka úńyǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bayimi, mi kosate yi biǝ Onanto dílǝkǝ mɛ taa kɔ utsyiko mi nti nkǝ ntɛyi betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ tsya Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo lǝ mǝ tsya lǝ bafo banɔ.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Nya Onanto wǝ nyi betidi eto utu kǝmiǝǝ, ǝlǝkǝ óte nkǝ ófo mǝ nya útǝ mǝ wǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ fe kase úfi útǝ bo tsya.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Wánkpe kabakǝfǝ lǝ bonko mǝ nti, nyaso ófo mǝ lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ. Nya úfi mǝ abua ɔ́tsyǝ mǝ fe bo ke.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, beso biato Onanto kabe mmǝ? Bo ku bo banamǝ mámfo bikǝ bia nkpe kalende butsyi! Beso bɔnlɛ mi bufi ketsyisǝ bafokanɔfɔ mfo?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Owo lo! Nnya ni bǝ bófo bɔ́nɔ boǝ kase Onanto dínyǝ bo kaminsǝ nya ófo bo lǝ nkpǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu eto lefonǝ ǝsuǝǝ, woofo mǝ tsya fe kase mǝǝtsya léfo bánɔ.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, mǝmblɛ kenke ésinkli báyɛ etiki budi nya bǝsiǝ bétsyue ato lǝ nnya Barnaba ku Paulo lɛtɛyi bǝnkǝ, Onanto áyɛ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ úyifo ayanisa kpǝ itǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bákalosǝǝ, Yakobo tsya átaka úńyǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bayimi, bitsyue ato lǝ bɔnɔ.

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon Petro éboklomasǝ bo ibǝ efe ditutɛ, lǝ kase Onanto dílǝkǝ wǝ lelabi óte betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ nya úlǝkǝ bǝtsyuǝ útsyiko lǝ mǝ nti nkǝ bafiani wǝ bale.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, muusinkli mbǝ nya muusinkli nto Oka David eto sekakedikɔ. Nya muusinkli nto asa nnya lébia mfó bǝ lǝ isinkli ɛlɔ itsyǝ kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Itǝ bǝ betidi ba díbu, ku mba lenni Yudafɔ kenke ba mínkpadi minkǝ bedi mɔ betidi ebubǝ mɔ ɔflɔ. Nko ini nɛ bǝ Bonamute éyifo lǝ lesa saa nǝ ɔ́tɛyi útsyǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I promised it long ago.”
translation_verse: Onanto lɛtɛyi ditiki nǝmfo betidi léte taa kɔ ke.’

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: “Nyaso mɔ, Yakobo, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ mfo nya béfi mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Onantoo, yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ buti mǝ kusu lǝ asa nyamfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Bomu bitǝ lǝ bɔwɔni mǝ kukue buǝ bayɛ asa nnya beefi kǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi nnya lǝǝtǝ baanansǝ ǝsuǝ budi ku sɛsɔnɔdi ku bebuki ba báwaninsǝ simuǝ bálo ku kɔtɔ eto budi.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Itsyi taa kɔɔ, asa nnya Moses lɛwɔni utsyǝǝ, baakla nnya nya baatɛyi nnya lǝ Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi saa lǝ umǝ saa.” Báwɔni Kukue Bǝtǝ Bafokanɔfɔ Ba Lenni Yudafɔ.

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nya batɔnkpe ku beninǝ ku bafokanɔfɔ kenke eto ditsyukpa lɛtɔnɔ bǝlǝkǝ betidi bǝtsyuǝ bǝ bǝtǝkǝko Paulo ku Barnaba besu Antiokia. Betidi inuǝ ba bǝlǝkǝ eyifo bafokanɔfɔ eto beninǝ. Mǝ ni Yuda wǝ bensi baakpo tsya bǝ Barsaba ku Sila.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Nya kukue ko bálɛ bésuko eto etiki nnya nkpe lǝ ko ǝsuǝ ni nkǝ, Bo, batɔnkpe ku beninǝ ba ni mi bayimi ba nkpe lǝ Yerusalem, boǝ boǝsiǝsǝ mi, bo bayimi ba lenni Yudafɔ mba nkpe lǝ Antiokia ku Siria ku Kilikia.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Bɔ́nɔ boǝ bo mfo eto bafokanɔfɔ eto bǝtsyuǝ ǝbǝ mfó bǝbǝ bǝnǝ etiki ǝtsyuǝ ku mi itsyi lǝ bo Yudafɔ eto lelolala eto lekpa ǝsuǝ. Nyaa, inni bo létsyese mǝ bǝ bǝbǝ batɛyi mi etiki nyamfo eto okle.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Yuda ku Sila tsya ebɔtɛyi mi asa nnya bɔ́wɔni bokpe mfo.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Itsyise iyifo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto lelabi ku bo lele tsya boǝnkǝ yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ bufi Yudafɔ eto afi buyifo bitsyikǝ butsyisǝ mi enso asa nyamfo.

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: Bɛni asa nnya béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi ku kɔtɔ budi ku bebuki ba báwaninsǝ simuǝ bálo eto budi ku sɛsɔnɔdi. Lǝ biɔlɛ mi ǝsuǝ mǝǝ, lesa saa ebuyuǝ ko mi. Bisiǝ kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Nyaso batɔnyɛ bamfo áma kusu bésu Antiokia. Nya se bésu bédufǝǝ, bákpadi bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ kenke bǝbǝ bátsya. Nya béfi kukue ko bétsyiko Yerusalem mfó bǝtǝ mǝ.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Nya mba kenke lɛkɛ mfo ǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ ku nkɛale itsyi lǝ etiki nnya bákla lǝ kukue komǝ ǝso.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nya se Yuda ku Sila díyifo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi esoo, bǝlǝkǝ asa kasɔ báte mǝ, bálɛ mǝ kɔlɛ, nya bákpe mǝ nkɛale.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Nya se bǝsiǝ mfó eyi asianii, bafokanɔfɔ bamǝ ǝtǝ mǝ kusu bǝ besinkli besu Yerusalem lǝ diyuǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Nya Paulo ku Barnaba dísiǝ lǝ Antiokia mfó nya mǝ ku babamba kpǝ ákɛ mfo bákate mǝ asa kedi mǝ etiki utsyi lǝ Bonamute ǝsuǝ. Paulo Ku Barnaba Ésie Bǝtsyuǝ Esu Nti.

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi ǝtsyuǝ kamaa, Paulo átɛyi Barnaba nkǝ, “Tǝ lǝ busi busu bobe bo bayimi ba nkpe lǝ kǝmǝ ka kenke búkesu budi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfó ku nka bɔ́yɛ bófe lǝ bobe kase banlɛnko.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Barnaba átɔnɔ nya óla nkǝ lǝ okpa Yohanes nwǝ tsya besi baakpo Marko ɔyɛnko.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Paulo mántɔnɔko wǝ kulaa! Itsyise lefe nǝ mǝ ku wǝ dísu Pamfiliaa, Marko Yohanes ésikli lǝ mǝ kama údu, nya wǝnsiǝ bálosǝ mǝ keyifo.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Nyaso Marko Yohanes eto etiki nyamfo ǝbǝ ékpe mǝ inuǝ bamǝ kǝnǝ ɛklɛ, itǝ bǝ bésense lǝ bǝtsyuǝ. Barnaba ákpa Marko ɔ́yɛnko nya béfi ɔklɔ bésu Kipro.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lékpa Sila ɔ́yɛnko, nya bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe mfó dífi mǝ bákpe lǝ Onanto eto ani fɛ bédu.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Nya báyɛ lǝ Siria ku Kilikia nya bákpe bafokanɔfɔ eto etsyuka nnya kenke nkpe mfó nkɛale.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo ku Sila dídu bésu umǝ wǝ baakpo Derbe, nya báyɛ mfo bésu Listra. Lǝ mfóo, bésu bǝnyǝ ofokanɔnyǝ utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Timoteo. Wǝ ambe éyifo Yudanyǝ wǝ díkafo Kristo ɔ́nɔ. Fɛɛ wǝ anto éyifo Grikinyǝ.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Bafokanɔfɔ kenke ba nkpe lǝ Listra ku Ikonion, ékebu Timoteo tinti.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Wǝ ni futsyǝ Paulo lela kekleke nkǝ lǝ okpa wǝ ɔyɛnko. Nyaso útǝ bala wǝ lelo fe kase Yudafɔ eto lekpa léte, itsyise Yudafɔ ba kenke nkpe lǝ lɛkpɔ nǝmfo yi bǝnkǝ wǝ anto éyifo Grikinyǝ.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Nya se báma kusuu, báyɛ lǝ kǝmǝ ka kenke nkpe mfó báfe, nya bátɛyi mǝ ketsyue ka batɔnkpe eto beninǝ ba nkpe lǝ Yerusalem dítsyue bǝtsyǝ. Nyaso bátɛyi mǝ bǝ lǝ beyifo lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso betidi bamǝ ǝbǝ befui bakpe, nya nko ke tsya mǝ bofokanɔ tsya dimuǝ ale enso nɛ. Paulo Ǝnyǝ Bulǝkǝkate Lǝ Troa.

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, báyɛ bésu Frigia ku Galatia, itsyise Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ mǝntǝ mǝ kusu nkǝ bátɛyi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ Asia eto kasɔ.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bésu bédufǝ Misiaa, bála bǝ lǝ besu Bitinia eto kasɔ. Fɛɛ mfó tsyaa, Yesu eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ mǝntǝ kusu nkǝ besu mfó.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Nyaso bésinkli báyɛ lǝ Misia eto kasɔ ɔflɔ nya bésu bédufǝ umǝ wǝ nkpe lǝ lekpo ɔflɔ baakpo Troa.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Ketsye kamfóo, Paulo ǝńyǝ kǝlǝkǝkate. Únyǝ nkǝ osani utsyuǝ wǝ ditsyi Makedonia nya únyǝ ɔnlɛ wǝ lekpakpa bɔlɛ ákatɛyi nkǝ, “Yila ǝbǝ Makedonia lǝ ǝbǝ mfo lǝ ǝbǝ awunsǝko bo o-o!”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Nya lǝ bulǝkǝkate bomfo esoo, búmuǝ ǝsuǝ akpa kpa nya búdu búsu Makedonia. Itsyise nyamfo lǝ bo bote nkǝ Onanto ála nkǝ lǝ otsyese bo lǝ busu bɔtɛyi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ mfó. Lidia Éfi Wǝ Ǝsuǝ Útǝ Onanto Lǝ Filipi.

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Nyaso búsu búfi ɔklɔ lǝ Troa, nya bɔ́yɛ bófe búsu Samotrake. Nya se kale dísiǝǝ, búdu búsu Neapoli.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Se búdu mfóo, búsu búdufǝ Filipi wǝ ni Makedonia eto umǝ nkplɛ. Nya mfó tsya eyifo Romafɔ eto umǝ. Nya búsiǝ mfó itǝ eyi ǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyii, búdu lǝ umǝ ǝmfó búsu utuflɔ utsyuǝ kaka búbu boǝ mfó eto bediti aato ola kǝtǝ Onanto. Nya búsiǝ mfó bɔnlɛ besio ba díbǝ mfó Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ bɔtɛyi.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Nya ɔni akɛ mǝ lenti baakpo Lidia Tiatira. Woate ago eto mfia nya uyifo utidi wǝ léfo Onanto ɔ́nɔ kekleke. Nya lefe nǝ unsi mfó ɔnlɛ etiki nya bɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi ato butsyuee, Onanto ásini wǝ nsusu nya ófo etiki nnya Paulo nlɛ budi mfó kenke ɔ́nɔ.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Nya se wonko wǝ diyo eto betidi kenke léfo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa nǝmǝǝ, ɔ́lɛ bo lekpakpa nkǝ, “Lǝ befo bɛnɔ biǝ mintsya eyifo Bonamute eto ofokanɔnyǝ sitintii, nyalɛ bibǝ bɛsɔnɔ lǝ mɔ ɔflɔ.” Nya únǝnko bo ditiki nǝmfo isu ese lefe nǝ bɔ́tɔnɔ búsu bɔ́sɔnɔ lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ. Bákpe Paulo Ku Sila Lǝ Aba Eto Diyo.

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Nya diyi lɔni se bɔ́ntɔ lǝ ola katokɔɔ, bótsyako ndomunyǝ siobi utsyuǝ wǝ lɛwɔnɔ bua nsi lǝ ǝsuǝ wǝ lǝǝnyǝ asa katɛyi. Nya woayɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ keyifo atabi kǝtǝ wǝ banamute mǝ.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Nya usiobi ǝmfo dítǝkǝko bo ku Paulo kama kafã katɛyi nkǝ, “Betidi bamfo eyifo Onanto eto beyifoyifo, nya bǝbǝ mfo bǝ lǝ bate mi konantosu ko luutǝ Onanto tǝ mi nkpǝ.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Nko eto eyifosa ǝtsyǝ nko éfo eyi kpǝ. Nya se ditsyǝbi nǝmǝ díbe dílǝ Pauloo, útsyi ɔwuninsǝ nya údi lɛwɔnɔ nǝ nsi lǝ usiobi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, “Nlɛ fǝ bola lǝ Yesu Kristo eto diye ǝsuǝ miǝ bɔ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ!” Nya lamfolamfoo, lɛwɔnɔ bua nǝmǝ ko lɛbɔ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Nya se wǝ banamute dínyǝ bǝ mǝ kudisu étii, bǝmuǝ Paulo ku Silas nya bǝnǝ mǝ bésuko umǝ ǝmǝ eto beninǝ ɔflɔ lǝ obia nti.

english_verse: They told the officials, “These Jews are upsetting our city!
translation_verse: Nya bátɛyi lǝ obia nti mfó bǝnkǝ, “Betidi bamfo eyifo Yudafɔ, nya bǝbǝ mfo banlɛ bo umǝ bobiasǝ.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: Bǝbǝ mfo balɛ asa nnya díńyǝ lǝ Romafɔ eto afi ǝsuǝ bote.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Nya betidi kpǝ díbǝ bátsyilama mǝ, nya bétsyiko Paulo ku Sila bunǝ kesuko sɛtɔ ku kama. Nya bakponkpo bamǝ dítǝ kusu bǝnkǝ bǝlǝkǝ mǝ bikǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bǝtǝ kusu bǝnkǝ bablɛ mǝ ku akete.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ báblɛ mǝ báfo afabe lǝ ǝsuǝ tintii, béfi mǝ bésu bákpe lǝ Aba eto diyo. Nya bátɛyi utidi wǝ laase mba bákpe lǝ diyo mfó bǝnkǝ ɔlɔ obe Paulo ku Sila kekleke.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Nya se bǝkǝ wǝ ato nko esoo, ókpa mǝ úsu ókpe diyo nǝmǝ eto kaka nso ale nya úti mǝ ókpe mfó, ókpe mǝ lǝ amana.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ntsyǝnti lefe nǝ Paulo ku Sila lɛkɛ mfo bákato ola kǝtǝ Onanto nya banlɛ ǝsǝ busǝ katɛsǝ wǝ nya mba nkpe lǝ diyo nǝmǝ ku mǝ ǝkǝtsyuǝ ato.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Dikunǝ, kasɔ ko dítinkǝ kekleke, nya diyo nǝmǝ kenke tsya dítinkǝ itǝ bǝ diyo ni biku kenke ásini, nya amana nnya bákpe mba bákape lǝ diyo nǝmǝ dífǝsǝ ɛbɔ.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Se nwǝ laabe mba bákpe lǝ diyo lésanklii, únyǝ nkǝ ayo nyamǝ eto biku kenke ásini bite leya. Nyaso se úbu nkǝ mba bákpe lǝ diyo nǝmǝ kenke étsyǝtsyi béluu, úlǝkǝ wǝ lesiabi nkǝ lǝ olo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Nyaso utidi ǝmfo ákpadi nkǝ befi okandiɛ baboko. Nya lǝ ketinkǝ ku difufu ǝsuǝǝ, úsu óse akonki lǝ Paulo ku Sila ɔflɔ.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Nya se ókpa mǝ ɔbɔnko lǝ diyo nǝmǝ kafoo, ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Boantomǝ, be ele bǝ nyifo lǝ nyǝ bofo?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Fo Bonamute Yesu anɔ nya ɛyɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝǝ, ebunyǝ bofo itǝ fǝ ku fǝ diyo eto betidi kenke.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Nya bésu bátɛyi wǝ ku wǝ diyo eto betidi kenke Bonamute eto ditiki nǝmǝ.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Nya ketsye kamfo ke nya úsǝ mǝ afabe nnya báfo, nya wǝ ku wǝ diyo eto betidi kenke léfo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa nǝmǝ.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, ókpa Paulo ku Sila úsuko wǝ diyo nya útǝ mǝ lesa bédi. Nya wǝ diyo eto betidi kenke dínyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti, itsyise báfo Onanto bánɔ.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Se kale dísiǝǝ, umǝ ǝmǝ eto bakponkpo ákpe wǝ bamamanyǝ nkǝ besu bátɛyi nwǝ laabe mba bákpe lǝ diyo eto uninǝ nkǝ, “Nyani bafɔ bamǝ lǝ bedu.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Nyaso mǝ obebe ǝmfo átɛyi Paulo ku Sila bǝnkǝ, “Bakponkpo bǝnkǝ nyani mi lǝ bidu. Nyaso bisu lǝ diyuǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lɛtɛyi bamamanyǝ bamfo bǝnkǝ, “Bablɛ bo lǝ umǝ ta eto ǝnǝmi lǝ lefe nǝ eto lefe bánkponko bo tɔ lekpo nya béfi bo bésu bákpe lǝ Aba eto diyo. Fɛɛ Romafɔ buni! Nya mɔmɔɔ, beso bala bǝnkǝ beyu bǝlǝkǝ kaa lǝ budu bǝ lǝ betidi mante? Kulefe! Tǝ lǝ mǝ kosate lǝ bǝbǝ bǝlǝkǝ bo kusu!”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Nyaso bamamanyǝ bamfo ésinkli bésu bátɛyi bakponkpo bamǝ kɛnyɛ kamfo. Nya se bánɔ bǝ Paulo ku Sila eyifo Romafɔɔ, sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ tinti!

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Lǝ nya mfo eso bésu bálɛ mǝǝ lekpakpa bǝyuǝsǝ mǝ kafo, fɛ bǝlǝkǝ mǝ lǝ aba eto diyo nǝmǝ. Nya bálɛ mǝǝ lekpakpa bǝ bédu lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ lǝ besu kalebe bamba.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Nyaso Paulo ku Sila édu bésu Lidia eto. Mfó nya bésu bǝnyǝ bafokanɔfɔ bamba. Nya bési bákpe mǝ nkɛale enso, fɛ bédu lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Nya se bésu báyɛ lǝ Amfipoli ku Apoloniaa, bésu bédufǝ Tesalonika. Nya mfóo, Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ ákɛ.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Nya fe kase Paulo laala buyifoo, úsu mfó nkǝ lǝ úsu udi mǝ etiki lǝ disikakpekɔ kamǝ. Nya lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi ǝtsyǝ nya dítǝkǝ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ ǝsuǝǝ, úsukate mǝ asa itsyi lǝ etiki nnya nkpe lǝ Awɔnisa kɛnkɛ eto kukue ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: Nya ókpasǝ mǝ bulǝ bo bátɛyi bǝtsyǝ bǝ Kristo ebubǝ unyǝ ku kase lǝ nnya kamaa, wootaka utsyi ɔkɔmǝ ubǝ nkpǝ. Paulo átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Utidi wǝ eto etiki minlɛ mi bɔtɛyi mfo ni Yesu wǝ ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nɛ.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Etiki nnya ókpasǝ óte mǝ mfó átsyi mǝ bǝtsyuǝ eto nsusu bɔ́wuninsǝ, nya báfo bánɔ bǝtǝkǝko Paulo ku Sila. Nya Grikifɔ kpǝ ba laate Onanto sikpi ku besio kpǝ ba nkpe díbu tsya átsyi bawuninsǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ bǝtǝkǝko mǝ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Yudafɔ asɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso babɛbɛ betidi bua bǝtsyuǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ ba bǝtǝ bǝ umǝ ǝmǝ eto betidi lǝ bataka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso bákpa betidi kpǝ ba lányǝ lǝ ditsyǝbi saa ǝsuǝ ku kudu besu batsyilama Yason eto diyo ku nsusu bǝ lǝ bǝmuǝ Paulo ku Sila lǝ bakpa mǝ besuko umǝ ǝmǝ eto katsyakɔ.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Nya se bǝnnyǝ mǝ esoo, bǝmuǝ Yason ku bafokanɔfɔ bǝtsyuǝ nya bǝnǝ mǝ besuko katsyakɔ kamǝ. Mfó banlɛ bofa katɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Betidi bǝtsyuǝ lɛ bɔyɛ katsyilama kakpe bulǝ lǝ kalebe saa, nya bǝbǝ mfo tsya bǝ lǝ bakpe, bulǝ.

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: Nya Yason tsya léfo mǝ ókpe lǝ wǝ diyo. Nya betidi bamǝ létaka bǝńyǝ lǝ Roma eto Okankplɛ eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ. Nya mǝ asatete nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ oka bamba wǝ baakpo Yesu si okpe!”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Nya se bánɔ nyamfoo, betidi bamǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ ǝnyǝnki nyanklaa.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ ele bǝ Yason ku wǝ betidi fo kayo fe mba díńyǝ mǝ kama fɛ lǝ bayani mǝ. Paulo Ku Sila Édu Bésu Beroia.

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Nyaso diyi nǝmfo eto ketsyee, bafokanɔfɔ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ ákpa Paulo ku Sila bétsyetsyiko bésuko Beroia. Nya se bésu bédufǝ mfóo, bésu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Mba lǝ Beroiaa, mǝ nsusu ásini lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ esó Tesalonikafɔ. Nyaso bétsyue ato bánɔ lesa nǝ banlɛ bɔtɛyi. Nya diyi saa, nya banlɛ budufǝ lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue komǝ ǝsuǝ kabe lǝ etiki nnya Paulo nlɛ bɔtɛyi eyifo sitinti.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Itǝ eso mǝ nti eto bǝtsyuǝ kpǝ áfo bánɔ. Grikifɔ eto besio kpǝ ba nkpe díbu ku basani kpǝ tsya áfo bánɔ bátsyi bawuninsǝ.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lefe nǝ Yudafɔ ba nkpe lǝ Tesalonika lɛnɔ bǝ Paulo kpe ɔlɛ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ budi lǝ Beroiaa, bésu mfó bésu bákpe kǝnǝ ku bulǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Nyaso bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe mfoo átaka kpla bǝńyǝ nya bákpa Paulo bésuko lekpo kɛnyɛ. Nya íbu Sila ku Timoteo lǝ Beroia mfó.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Mba lékpa Paulo bayɛnko, áyɛnko wǝ bédufǝnko Atene. Se bétsyi mfo banlɛ bubǝǝ, Paulo ǝtǝkǝ mǝ kɛnyɛ nkǝ bǝbǝ batɛyi Sila ku Timoteo lǝ bakɔse bǝbǝ betu wǝ mfó. Paulo Ákɛ Lǝ Atene.

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ Paulo lɛkɛ lǝ Atene unsi unsǝkǝ Sila ku Timoteo, iyifo lesa nǝ dílǝ wǝ tinti se únyǝ kase betidi bamfo dítsyǝ bǝfiǝ beesumu, nya umǝ ǝmǝ kenke díyi ku bǝfiǝ.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, úsu mfó nkǝ weesu udi Yudafɔ ku betidi bamba ba laate Onanto sikpi tsya etiki lǝ mǝ katsyakɔ. Nya diyi saa, úkedi mba úkǝnyǝ lǝ asi tsya etiki.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Nya lefe lɔnii, betidi bǝtsyuǝ ba ni Epikurofɔ ku Stoafɔ ba ni kawunsiǝ eto babesibe kplɛ ǝnǝ etiki ku Paulo nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Be kon utikidi wǝ leenyi kulesa ǝmfo nlɛ bɔtɛyi?” Nya babamba tsya lɛtɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Iyifo fenkǝ nsɔ bamba eto bǝfiǝ ǝsuǝ ɔnlɛ etiki budi ketsyiko.” Nya betidi bamfó édi etiki nko itsyise bánɔ bǝ Paulo édi etiki itsyi lǝ Yesu ku wǝ botaka ketsyi ɔkɔmǝ kǝbǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, bákpa wǝ bésuko kaka baakpo akpo lǝ Areopago ini umǝ eto beninǝ eto disikakpekɔ, nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bola boǝ boate asafɔfɔ nnya fanlɛ bote mfo eto kasɔ.

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Itsyise asa nnya fanlɛ bote eyifo bo yanii. Nyaso bola boǝ boanɔ nnya kasɔ.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Nya Atenefɔ kenke ku bafɔ ba nkpe lǝ kasɔ mfóo, eefi mǝ lefe kǝtǝ asafɔfɔ saa eto etiki bɔtɛyi ku nnya bɔnɔ.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Nyaso Paulo átaka únyǝ lǝ disikakpekɔ kamǝ lǝ betidi bamfó eto ǝnǝmi, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ kusu saa ǝsuǝǝ, nyǝ ntsyǝ lekpo miǝ mi Atenefɔɔ, biyifo betidi ba laala kesumu tinti.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Itsyise se minlɛ botsyilama kalɛ asa ola kabee, nya se mínyǝ mi nsumukɔɔ, nnyǝ kesumukɔ kani ka báwɔni bǝtǝkǝ lǝ kǝ leba ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝǝ, ‘Búfi nyamfo bútǝ bo Anto wǝ bunyi.’ Nya Anto wǝ binyi fɛ bɛnlɛ wǝ busumu mfoo, wǝ ǝsuǝ minla miǝ meedi mi etiki mɔmɔ nɛ.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: “Anto ǝmfo ni Bonamute wǝ diyifo kawunsiǝ, kato ku kasɔ ku asa nnya kenke nkpe lǝ ke. Nya woǝnsiǝ kpoo lǝ disumuyo fe nǝ kasɔ mfo eto betidi dífi mǝ ani báto.

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: Nya woanhiã bǝ udi kulesa itsyi lǝ betidi eto ani ɛyɛ lǝ mǝ keyifo tsya ǝsuǝ, itsyise wǝ kosate ni futsyǝ lǝǝtǝ utsyuǝ saa nkpǝ ku lesa saa nǝ baahiã.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Wǝ dílǝkǝ utidi saa ɛyɛ lǝ utidi ɔni ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ busiǝ nkpǝ fe kɔtɔ kɔni eto bebi lǝ kasɔ kamfo kenke ǝsuǝ. Wǝ kosate dítsyǝ lefe ku kedikɔ útǝ mǝ tsya.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Nya Boanto dítsyue nyamfo utǝ bo betidi itǝ bǝ lǝ bɔbɛbɛ wǝ esu lǝ bunyǝ wǝ, ekpe bǝ lǝ sintintii, woantsyonko bo.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Itsyise fe kase mi mba laatɛyi akpa lɛtɛyii, ‘Wǝ ni nwǝ nsi nkpǝ nyaso bo tsya nsi nkpǝ, nya bunsi nkpǝ ku wǝ nya boalabe selabe lǝ wǝ.’ Nya baatɛyi tsya bǝnkǝ, ‘Bɔ́bɔ butsyi lǝ wǝ.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: “Nya se buni Onanto eto bebi esoo, yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ bubú boǝ Onanto ǝmfo te fe lesa nǝ bo betidi dífi sika sia nye siflubia nye kuyi nye leba búyifo ku bo ani nye lesaatsyuǝ nǝ baawoe nye lǝ ditsyǝbi saa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Nya idu lǝ kawunsiǝ eto kasɔ ketsyikokɔ ibǝ ese mɔmɔɔ, Onanto mǝnnǝ utidi saa koto itsyise bánte wǝ eso. Fɛɛ mɔmɔ mfoo, úkǝ bo ato nkǝ bulǝkǝ bo ǝsuǝ lǝ asa nyamfo eto kakle ǝsuǝ lǝ butsyi ǝnǝmi butǝ wǝ.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Itsyise útsyǝ diyi nǝ utidi wǝ úlǝkǝ utsyǝ luubǝ okponko bo lekpo itsyi lǝ bo ditsyǝbi ǝsuǝ ɛyɛ lǝ kɔtsyɔntsyɔsu ǝsuǝ Nya úlǝkǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ óte bo idu lǝ lefe ne ótakasǝ wǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ ubǝ nya unsi nkpǝ.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bánɔ bǝ Paulo edi etiki itsyi lǝ botakaketsyi ɔkɔmǝ kǝ bǝ ǝsuǝǝ, mǝ nti eto bǝtsyuǝ ǝmǝ wǝ. Fɛɛ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ tsya ǝmǝ wǝ bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bola boǝ busi boanɔ lesatsyuǝ itsyi lǝ utidi eto businkli kataka ketsyi ɔkɔmǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Nyaso se íbǝ nko esoo, Paulo édu mǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ betidi bǝtsyuǝ ǝtǝkǝko wǝ nya bétsyi báwuninsǝ báfo bánɔ. Nya utsyuǝ uni ákɛ mǝ lenti wǝ díyifo Areopago eto uninǝ wǝ baakpo Dionisio ku usio wǝ baakpo Damari ku babamba bǝtsyuǝ tsya áfo bánɔ.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, Paulo édu lǝ Atene úsu Korinto.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Mfó úsu únyǝ Yudanyǝ wǝ baakpo Akwila, nwǝ bále lǝ Ponto. Wǝ ku wǝ usiǝ Priskila étsyi Italia bǝbǝ mfó yanklɛ tɔ, itsyise kasɔ kamǝ eto Okankplɛ wǝ baakpo Klaudo átɛyi nkǝ Yudafɔ kenke du lǝ Roma. Paulo ésu úsunyǝ mǝ,

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: nya úsiǝ lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ ɔnlɛ mǝ bɔwunsǝko lǝ kubukikue kanka eto keyifo ka woeyifo kedi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi saa, Paulo ékesu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ nya úkǝlǝkǝ asa kasɔ kate Yudafɔ ku Grikifɔ kenke itǝ lǝ bǝ lǝ batsyi mǝ akɔɛsi bawuninsǝ.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Sila ku Timoteo dítsyi Makedonia bǝbǝǝ, Paulo ǝnyǝ lefe kpǝ úfi útǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ bote ku ni kasɔlǝkǝlǝkǝ kate Yudafɔ bamǝ nkǝ Yesu ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ tinti.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝ Yudafɔ bamfó létaka bǝńyǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nya bésie wǝ abie lǝ Yesu eto disi esoo, Paulo áflɛ sitǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ bikpǝ nye benyaa, nyǝ mɔ ani, nyǝ mɔ akpa minyi nnya kulesa saa. Idu mɔmɔ mfoo, ndu ntɔ lǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ ɔflɔ lǝ nsu ntɛyi mǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Nya se údu mfóo, úsu ɔ́kɛ lǝ osani utsyuǝ wǝ lenni Yudanyǝ wǝ baakpo Tito Yusto wǝ laate Onanto sikpi nya wǝ diyo nkpe lǝ Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ katsyakɔ kamǝ eto uninǝ wǝ baakpo Krispo ku wǝ diyo eto betidi kenke áfo Bonamute bánɔ, nya béyifo mǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa. Nko ke, béyifo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa bǝtǝ bafokanɔfɔ kpǝ bamba tsya lǝ Korinto mfó nɛ.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Nya lǝ diyi lɔni eto ketsyee, Bonamute ǝtǝ Paulo ǝnyǝ kǝlǝkate ka ǝsuǝ ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Mante sikpi! Nya mensinkli lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo bɔtɛyi eto kama.

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: Itsyise nsiko fǝ, nya kutsyuǝ saa ǝmbofo fǝ kabua buyifo. Betidi kpǝ kpe lǝ umǝ mfó ba ni mɔ bale.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Nyaso Paulo ǝsiǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmfo lɛlɛ lɔni ku kutsyǝ kedi Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo kate betidi.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ bǝtsyǝ Galio fe Roma eto Aba eto unyɛsiǝ lǝ Akaia eto kasɔɔ, Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ ásɔ mǝ ǝsuǝ lebo nya báwoe etiki lǝ Paulo ǝsuǝ nya bǝmuǝ wǝ bésuko lekpokakpokɔ.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Nya bátɛyi Aba eto unyɛsiǝ ǝmǝ nkǝ, “Utidi ǝmfo lɛ betidi eto nsusu bobiasǝ nkǝ basumu Onanto lǝ kusu ko nńyǝ lǝ bo afi ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Nya kase Paulo nkǝ lǝ ulǝkǝ kɛnyɛ lǝ udi etiki koo, Aba eto Unyɛsiǝ Galio ǝmfo ko dídi etiki ɔ́tɛyi Yudafɔ bamǝ nkǝ, “Mi Yudafɔ bɛnɔ! Lǝ bǝnkǝ etiki nnya bɛyɛnko béboko mfo eyifo etiki kekleke nnya léta kɔfi ibuu, nyalɛ mɔɔkɛ nnya lefe ntsyue mi etiki koto.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se iyifo kǝnǝ itsyi lǝ etiki ku aye ku mi afi eto asa ini esoo, mi kosate su bɛtɛyi nnya. Bǝntsyǝ mɛ okponkpo lǝ mi asa nyamfo ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Nyaso óla mǝ ɔ́bɔnko lǝ wǝ lekpokakpokɔ mfó.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Nyaso betidi eto ditsyukpa nǝmfo ǝmuǝ Sostene wǝ ni mǝ katsyakɔ kamǝ eto ununǝ nya báblɛ wǝ lǝ lekpokpakpokɔ ɔflɔ mfo. Fɛɛ Galio mánkpe mǝ kɛnyɛ. Paulo Ésinkli Úsu Antiokia.

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Paulo esi úsiǝ lǝ Korinto eto umǝ mfó éfo eyi kpǝ. Lǝ nnya kamaa, ókla bafokanɔfɔ bamfó nya wǝ ku Akwila ku Priskila léyila lekpo bésu Siria. Tsya fɛ úduu, Paulo ǝtǝ nkǝ bakpi wǝ disi lǝ Kenkrea itsyi lǝ ntamu bo óka utsyǝ eso.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Se úsu údufǝ Efesoo, óyani Priskila ku Akwila útsyǝ mfo. Nya wǝ dídu úsu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ lǝ úsu ɔlɛ asa obe ku Yudafɔ ba nkpe mfo.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Betidi álɛ wǝ lekpakpa bǝ lǝ ɔkɛ lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ sɛkɛ, fɛɛ wántɔnɔ.

english_verse: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso se ɔnlɛ buluu, ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ Onanto atɔnɔɔ, muusinkli mbǝ mi ɔflɔ.” Nya údufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝsuǝ nya údu lǝ Efeso.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Se úsu údufǝ lǝ Kaisareaa, ófe úsu Yerusalem nya úsu úsiǝsǝ bafokanɔfɔ mfo. Nya usi ófe úsu Antiokia.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Se úsiǝ mfo sɛkɛɛ, usi údu ɔ́yɛ ɔbɔ lǝ Galatia ku Frigia. Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfó ǝsuǝǝ, ótsyilama mfó kenke óbe bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe lǝ dikuku mfó, nya ókakpe mǝ nkɛale. Apolo Ésu Efeso.

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Nya Yudanyǝ utsyuǝ díbǝ Efeso. Wǝ ni Apolo. Bále wǝ lǝ Aleksandria. Nya uyifo utidi wǝ nkpe kɛnyɛ laafo etiki budi, nya unyi Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto kúkue eto etiki tsya.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Ekpe bǝ wɔ́ɔnɔ Bonamute eto diye ɔnɔ nya woedi etiki itsyi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ ku ute tsyaa, Yohanes eto ntu flǝsǝflǝsǝ eto etiki hã ete unyi.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Nya se úńyǝ ɔnlɛ etiki budi ku wǝ utu ku ǝsuǝale lǝ Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ mfó fɛ Priskila ku Akwila lɛnɔɔ, bákpa wǝ bésuko mǝ diyo bésu bǝlǝkǝ Boanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ kasɔ kukɔnɔ báte wǝ nya báte wǝ asa bawunsǝ.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Nya se Apolo díbu disi nkǝ woesu Akaiaa, Efeso eto bafokanɔfɔ ésu báwunsǝko wǝ nya báwɔni kukue bátsyese bafokanɔfɔ lǝ Akaia mfó bǝ lǝ úbǝ mfóo, lǝ bafo wǝ kukɔnɔ. Se úsu údufǝ mfóo, ɔ́wunsǝko betidi kpǝ ba léfiani bafokanɔfɔ ɛyɛ lǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nya óso Yudafɔ ani lǝ kase unǝ etiki ku ku ute lǝ betidi nti. Nya etiki nnya nkpe lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝǝ, úfi úlǝkǝ óte ékpa wuee bǝ Yesu ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ Apolo lɛkɛ lǝ Korintoo, Paulo tsya áyɛ lǝ akpa eto kusu nya úsu údufǝ lǝ Efeso. Mfo úsu únyǝ bafokanɔfɔ bǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Idu lefe nǝ béfo bɛnɔɔ, bíofo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ?” Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Owo lo! Be ni Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ fɛ? Bóannɔ tɔ ni kulesa saa.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Nya usi ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Nyalɛ nte ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa beyifo mi?” Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Yohanes eto ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Nya Paulo dílǝkǝ kasɔ óte mǝ nkǝ, “Yohanes ǝflǝsǝ betidi ba ditsyi báwuninsǝ lǝ mǝ abua ǝsuǝ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ ele bǝ bafo utidi wǝ luubǝ lǝ wǝ kama banɔ. Nya utidi ǝmfo ni Yesu.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Nya se bánɔ ditiki nǝmfoo, bátɔnɔ nya úflǝsǝ mǝ ntu lǝ Bonamute Yesu eto diye ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Nya se nǝ Paulo dífi ani útǝkǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝbǝ dísiǝ lǝ mǝmblɛ ǝsuǝ, nya bétsyiko bele bele bɔtɛyi nya bǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto ditiki kɛnyɛ báte.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Nya basani bamǝ eto utu lídi fe lefosi inuǝ.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Nya se Paulo dísu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔɔ, údi mǝ etiki ku ute lǝ afa ǝtsyǝ ǝsuǝ, úlǝkǝ asa kasɔ óte mǝ ku kasɔnɔnɔ nya ókplatsya kekleke lǝ kase wookpa mǝ nsusu usuko Onanto eto Sekakedikɔ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ méntsyue wǝ ditiki sɛɛ nǝmǝ ato nya báni bofokanɔ, nya lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmii, bédi etiki bua nnya dińyǝ lǝ Onanto eto kusu sɛ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso Paulo édu lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ nya ókpa bafokanɔfɔ ɔ́yɛnko nya bédu bésu Tirano eto suku eto diyo. Mfóo, nya ókatsyako mǝ diyi saa, kédufǝ lǝ etiki ǝsuǝ ku mǝ.

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Nya Onanto dítǝ Paulo ǝsuǝale nnya úfi keyifo ayanisa.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ béfi wǝ ofiamɛ nye wǝ ǝsuǝ eto awu bétidi ufimfi utsyuǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ koo, úkǝnyǝ bufitsyatsya, nya ékala awɔnɔ bua tysa kabɔnko lǝ betidi ba ǝsuǝ ɛkɛ.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Nya Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ ba ni bafate ba laatsyilama idu kalebe isu kalebee, ébu bǝnkǝ boofo bɔyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu eto diye ǝsuǝ bala awɔnɔ bua lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ. Nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Nnla mi lǝ Yesu wǝ eto diye ǝsuǝ Paulo laayɛ kala mi mbɔsǝ lǝ utidi ǝmfo ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se bésu bǝ beedi lɛwɔnɔ bua ditsyuǝ etikii, lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔɔ nnyi Yesu, nya minyi Paulo tsya. Fɛɛ mimii, bawoe bini?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Nya utidi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ lɛwɔnɔ bua nǝmfo nsii, lɛblɛ mǝ! Nya báfo afabe tinti, nya bétsyetsyi bábɔ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ leyaleya!

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
