translation_verse: Nnya díbǝ mfó ésensǝ efia Efeso kenke itǝ bǝ Yudafɔ ku mba lenni Yudafɔ kenke ánɔ nnya. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ tinti, nya Bonamute Yesu eto diye disi lebe kato lekpe.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Nya mba lɛtsyi akɔɛsi báwuninsǝ eto bǝtsyuǝ kpǝ díbǝ bǝlǝkǝ mǝ asa bua nnya béyifo kasɔ lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
translation_verse: Nya mba ni bafate lǝ mǝ nti dífi mǝ afa eto ekue ku kekue ku bɛbɛ báboko nya bátonsǝ nnya lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmi mfó. Ekue ku asa nyamfo kenke eto koya ebuyifo fe sika flubia eto akpi fosi anɔ.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Nyamfo lǝ bote kase Bonamute eto ditiki dísensǝ ku ǝsuǝale, nya éfo mfo kenke. Bulǝ Ǝbǝ Lǝ Efeso.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝlǝkǝ léte Paulo nkǝ ɔyɛ usu Makedonia ku Akaia lǝ usu Yerusalem. Nya Paulo lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Lǝ ndufǝ mfóo, ele bǝ nyɛ nsu Roma tsya.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Nya ótsyese wǝ bawunsǝkotsyuǝ inuǝ, Timoteo ku Erasto nkǝ bákatɔ wǝ lǝ Makedonia lǝ ubǝ. Nya wǝ lɛtsyimɛ ɔkɛ kama lǝ Asia mfó itǝ lefe sɛkɛ.

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, bulǝ kplɛ ǝbǝ lǝ Efeso itsyi lǝ kusu ko ǝsuǝ Kristo nkǝ bǝǝsiǝ nkpǝ eso.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Nwǝ dínǝ bulǝ bomfo óboko ni utidi wǝ baakpo Demetrio wǝ ni ɔtsyuɛntsyuɛ wǝ leefi sika flubia katsyuɛ Artemi eto esumuyo kɛkɛ. Nya nnya bote kǝnǝ kutǝkǝ kpǝ kaboko wǝ ku wǝ bawunsǝkotsyuǝ.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: Diyi lɔnii, Demetrio ákpadi batsyuɛntsyuɛ ba kenke bátsya nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bayimi, biyi biǝ buǝnyǝ kutǝkǝ kpǝ ketsyi lǝ bo keyifo kamfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Nya bɛnlɛ bɔnɔ, kǝnyǝ sitinti kase Paulo ǝmfo nlɛ bo keyifo kamfo bobiasǝ kakpe bo kofani, nya ɔnlɛ betidi kpǝ eto nsusu bobiasǝ nkǝ befiǝ eto asa nnya boeyifo kenke menyifo lesa nǝ ele bǝ bakpe disi kasɔ bǝtǝ! Lǝ nyamfo eso bo bikǝtete ébubǝ kasɔ! Nya inni Efeso mfo mate ni kaka nyamfo nlɛ bubǝ. Bomu ɛlɛ bubǝ lǝ Asia eto kasɔ kenke!

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Nya inni bo mate eto keyifo ete lɔɔsɔɔ kasɔ! Sitintii, Boambe Ufiǝ Kplɛ Artemi eto diyo ku wǝ dibu ebonya lǝ Asia mfo ku lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke!”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Se bánɔ Demetrio eto etiki nyamfoo, ɔblɔ éfi mǝ nya bényenki tinti nya báfã bembembe bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Efoso eto Ambe Artemi ni Ufiǝ Kplɛ Kenke!”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Nya kudu ko bákpo dísensǝ kófo umǝ ǝmǝ kenke. Nyaso bátsya fe ditsyukpa nya bǝmuǝ Paulo eto bayɛtsyuǝ inuǝ bǝtsyuǝ ba baakpo Gaio ku Aristarko ba ditsyi Makedonia. Nya bákpa mǝ bésuko umǝ ǝmǝ eto katsyakɔ.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo tsya léla nkǝ lǝ wontsya lǝ usu mfo, fɛɛ bafokanɔfɔ fɛfɛ mántɔnɔko wǝ nko.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Nya lekpake nǝmfo eto beninǝ ba díyifo Paulo eto bayɛtsyuǝ tsyaa, léla ɔtɔnkpe bákpe wǝ bálɛ wǝ lekpakpa bǝnkǝ woensu mfó lǝ usu ufi wǝ ǝsuǝ okpe lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ kulaa!

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfó ǝsuǝǝ, diyo nǝmǝ kafo eyi ku kudu. Nya betidi ba díbǝ bátsya mfóo eto utsyuǝ saa ku wǝ lesa nǝ ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi. Nya mǝ nti kpǝ mánte lesa nǝ eso bǝbǝ mfo.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Nya Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ díkǝ Aleksandro bǝ usu unyǝ sɛtɔ lǝ okpasǝ lesa nǝ díbǝ. Nya Aleksandro lébesǝ ani kato nkǝ utsyuǝ saa yɛ kudu bokpo lǝ ɔtɛyi mǝ lesatsyuǝ.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Nya se betidi bamfo dínyǝ bǝ Yudanyǝ unii, besi betsyiko kudu bokpo kafa bembembe itǝ fe abombosɔsɔ ǝnuǝ. Nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Efoso eto Ambe Artemi ni Ufiǝ Kplɛ Kenke!”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Efo lefe fɛ Aba eto unyɛsiǝ ninǝ wǝ nkpe mfo léfo mǝ bɔkɔnsǝ, nya údi etiki ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔ bayimi, Efesofɔ, utsyuǝ saa lǝ mi nti yi nkǝ Efeso mfo ni Ufiǝ Kplɛ Artemi eto kǝsiǝkɔ. Nya wǝ kakpakɔ eto leba kɛnkɛɛ, áfankli ditsyi kato díbǝ kasɔ, nya lenkpe lǝ bo nti.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Nya kutsyuǝ saa ǝmbǝ loofo ditiki nǝmfo sidia butǝkǝ. Nyaso biyuǝsǝ mi akɔɛsi nya bieyifo kulesa saa lǝ lɛblɔfi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Itsyise betidi ba bímuǝ békpa béboko mfoo, bényu kulesa betsyiko lǝ Disumuyo nye babiasǝ bo Ufiǝ Artemi eto diye tsya.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ Demetrio ku wǝ batsyuɛntsyuɛ ǝnyǝ bǝ bakpe ditiki ditsyuǝ lǝ utsyuǝ ǝsuǝǝ, bakponkpo kpe mfo. Nya bonkpe eyi nnya ǝsuǝ baakpo akpo. Lǝ béyifo kabua kǝtsyuǝ, boofo ke bulǝkǝ kate mǝ mfó.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ lesa bamba ditsyuǝ kpe bela biǝ biefi bisukoo, bitǝ lǝ bufi busuko umǝ eto beninǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Itsyise nyamfo lɛ bote fenkǝ bɔlɛ botaka kǝńyǝ lǝ Aba ǝsuǝ. Lǝ Romafɔ eto bamamanyǝ ǝbǝ bǝnyǝ mmi eto diyi nǝmfo eto asayifoyifoo, boǝmbofo kɛnyɛ bulǝkǝ, nya buunǝ bo koto.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Nya se ɔ́tɛyi etiki nyamfo nkoo, útǝ bésensǝ bédu.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bulǝ bomǝ díyuǝ kɛnyɛɛ, Paulo ákpadi bafokanɔfɔ bǝbǝ bátsya lǝ kalebe kani. Nya se ókpe mǝ nkɛale lǝ mǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ ólosǝǝ, ókla mǝ fɛ údu úsu Makedonia.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Se údu ɔntɔɔ, Paulo áyɛ lǝ kǝmǝ ka nkpe lǝ lekpake nǝmfó kenke nya údi mǝ etiki kpǝ úfi ókpe mǝ nkɛale fɛ úsu údufǝ Akaia.

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: Úsiǝ mfó fe afa ǝtsyǝ. Lefe nǝ ɔnlɛ ǝsuǝ bumuǝ nkǝ lǝ usu Siria, únyǝ útsyǝ lekpo nkǝ Yudafɔ ba nkpe mfó lǝ wǝ ukpǝ eto esu bɔbɛbɛ. Nyaso úfi nsusu bio nkǝ wesinkli usu ɔyɛ Makedonia.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ ɔnlɛ buduu, betidi kpǝ ǝtǝkǝnko wǝ. Betidi bamǝ ni Sopater wǝ dítsyi Beroia wǝ ni Piro eto ubi ku Aristarko ku Sekundo ba ditsyi Tesalonika ku Gaio wǝ dítsyi Derbe ku Tihiko ku Trofimo ba ditsyi Asia eto lekpake ku Timoteo.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Mba mfo átɔ̃ bo bǝ beesu bǝńyǝ bǝsǝkǝ bo lǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Troa.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Yudafɔ eto Nnamfokuã Eto Abolo Eto Eyididi eto kamaa, búfi ɔklɔ nya búdu lǝ Filipi, nya búsu bútu mǝ lǝ Troa lǝ eyi anɔ eto kama. Nya búsiǝ mfó éfo eyi akuanse. Paulo Ésu Troa Lɛfɛfɛ.

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Kɔsida eto diyi tutɛɛ, bótsya lǝ kalebe kani boǝ lǝ budi Bonamute eto didisa lǝ bǝtsyuǝ. Nya se Paulo lǝǝbǝ budu kǝsiǝsǝ esoo, údi etiki ikulǝ isu taa ntsyenti.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Nya báto kakadiɛ kpǝ bǝtsyǝ lǝ abanslo eto diyo nǝ ǝsuǝ bótsya mfó.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Nya se Paulo eto etiki nlɛ bukulǝ esoo, selabe éfi okosobi wǝ baakpo Eutiko lǝ lefe nǝ unsi lǝ diyo nǝmǝ eto mfɛnsle ǝsuǝ. Ófankli utsyi lǝ abanslo eto botokǝtǝkǝ tsyǝfǝ mfó úbǝ ɔ́sɔ lǝ kasɔ. Nya se bésu bétsyi wǝǝ, bǝnyǝ bǝ úkpǝ.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Paulo áyifǝ úsu kasɔɔ, úsu dikpefi nǝmǝ ɔflɔ nya úbǝ kasɔ ɔ́buta ni nya útsyi ni óboko wǝ akɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Biankpo kudu! Okpe nkpǝ!”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Nya mǝmblɛ dísinkli bésu kato mfó bésu bédi Bonamute eto lesa nǝmǝ. Lǝ nnya kama Paulo esi úsiǝ údi etiki isu ese kalesiǝsiǝ fɛ údu.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Nya se bákpa okosobi ǝmǝ ku nkpǝ besuko diyoo, ǝsuǝ ǝyuǝ mǝ tinti. Paulo Etsyi Troa Úsu Mileto.

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo dífi akpa eto kusu úsu Aso. Fɛɛ lǝ kase útsyue ǝsuǝǝ, bo éfi ɔklɔ bɔ́tɔ wǝ sɛtɔ bǝ lǝ busu butsyi wǝ lǝ mfó.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Nyaso se úbǝ útu bo lǝ Aso mfóo, údufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ nya bonko wǝ kenke lídu búsu Mitilene.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Búdu lǝ mfó nya lǝ kǝsiǝsǝǝ, búsu bóyo Kio. Nya lǝ diyi nǝmfo eto kǝsiǝsǝ tsyaa, bóyo Samo.Lǝ diyi tsyǝfǝ ǝsuǝǝ, búsu búdufǝ Mileto.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Paulo mánla nkǝ bobiasǝ lefe kpǝ lǝ Asia mfó nyaso útǝ bókpia Efeso bófe, itsyise ɔlɛ bɔkɔse. Nya utsyue nkǝ lǝ woofoo, ele bǝ udufǝ Yerusalem lǝ Pentekoste eto diyi ǝsuǝ. Paulo Ákla Efeso Eto Bafokanɔfɔ.

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Nya se búsu búdufǝ Miletoo, ókpe batɔnkpe lǝ Efesofɔ eto bafokanɔfɔ eto beninǝ ɔflɔ nkǝ bǝbǝ bǝnyǝ wǝ mfó.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Nya se bǝbǝǝ, ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bayimi, mimblɛ ta yi kase minkɛ lǝ mi le nti lefe nǝ kenke minkɛ mfo ǝsuǝ, idu lǝ diyi tutɛ nǝ mímbǝ míndufǝ lǝ Asia eto kasɔ mfo ibǝ ǝsǝ mɔmɔ.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: Binyǝ kase mímbokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ míntǝ Bonamute, lǝ kanyi ku binǝntu ǝsuǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Yudafɔ eto kase bála bǝ balo mɛ eso.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ ola bebe mɛ. Minni futsyǝ nkpe disiale. Bonamute eto asa nnya minyi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nnya ni lesa nǝ mínte mi nɛ, nya mémfi kulesa nkula mi. Nte Bonamute eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ betidi nti ku lǝ mi ayo.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Nya kalebe saa mínsuu, ntɛyi Yudafɔ ku mba lenni Yudafɔ kekleke minkǝ bayɛ asa bua buyifo lǝ batsyi bawuninsǝ befi mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Onanto, lǝ bafo Bonamute Yesu banɔ.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: “Nya mɔmɔɔ, Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ lɛ mǝ bɔtɛyi nkǝ nsu Yerusalem, nya minnyi lesa nǝ lǝbǝ mɛ busu ketu mfó.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ kǝmǝ ka kenke mínyɛ mínfee, nyi miǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝlǝkǝ óte mɛ nkǝ moolabee diyo nyǝ kanyi.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ meenfi mɔ nkpǝ fe lesa nǝ lefe itǝ mɛ. Lesa nǝ lefe itǝ mɛ ni bǝ nlosǝ keyifo ka Bonamute Yesu lékpe mɛ kɔni nkǝ nyifo. Nya keyifo ka ókpe kɔni nkǝ nsu nyifo ni bǝ ntɛyi betidi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ itsyi lǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Nyasoo, nyi miǝ mimba kenke lɛnɔ mɔ asatete itsyi lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ǝsuǝǝ, ensie embotsya ku mɛ bio.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Nyaso mmii, mǝǝtǝ mi bote minkǝ lǝ utsyuǝ áyansǝ wǝ nkpǝ eto kusuu, beembufi utidi ǝmfo eto kabua balo mɛ dikpi.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Sitintii, mémfi Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eto kusu eto asa nkula mi.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya esoo, bebe mi ǝsuǝ kukɔnɔ. Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ díyifo mi mba ni batɔnkotɔnko bǝ lǝ bɛlɛ wǝ bebi ba Onanto dífi útǝ mi nkǝ bɛlɛ mǝ ola. Bɛlɛ mǝ kukɔnɔ, itsyise Onanto eto ubi dífi wǝ kɔtɔ ókpo kasɔ lǝ mǝ disi úfi úlǝkǝ mǝ utsyiko lǝ mǝ abua ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Nnya minyi ekpa ko mǝ ni bǝnkǝ lǝ nyani mi ntsyǝǝ, betidi ebubǝ bataka bǝńyǝ bakpɔ mi bǝlǝ mi nya boosensǝ mi fe kase bekiti laakpɔ ku basamu kesensǝ mǝ.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ mi nti kulaa tsyaa, mi bǝtsyuǝ ébusinkli lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ kama bawɛ mi, bákpa betidi kpǝ báyɛnko lǝ mǝ kosate eto bidiabidia eto asatete ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Bitǝkǝ ǝnǝ bikabe lǝ bekloma kase mínkplatsya ku binǝntu lǝ mi disi nya mínte mi asa ketsye ku kakɔmi itǝ alɛ ǝtsyǝ nnya minkɛ mi lenti ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: “Nya mɔmɔ mfoo, mfi mi nkpe lǝ Onanto eto ani bǝ lǝ ose mi. Lǝ wǝ ditiki nǝ laawunsǝko betidii lǝ lekpe mi nkɛale lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ bo luutǝ biunyǝ kedikɔ lǝ Onanto eto, fe mba úlǝkǝ ókpo atsyɔ utsyǝ itǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: Mánsɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ kuutsyuǝ saa eto sika sia nye sika flubia nye bikǝlɛlɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Mi kosate yi biǝnkǝ mɔ ani nyamfo mímfi mínyifo keyifo fɛ minlɛ budi nya mimfi minlɛ betidi ba lǝǝtǝkǝko mɛ bobe.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Nyifo nyamfo kenke lǝ mfi nte mi kase ele bǝ bukawunsǝ ko bakombo, nya lǝ bekloma ditiki nǝ Bonamute Yesu lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Asa butǝ ǝtǝ disuǝyuǝ enso bofo.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Paulo dídi etiki nyamfo ólosǝǝ, óse akonki nya wǝ ku mǝ léto ola.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Nya mǝmblɛ lɛkɔbǝ wǝ bálɛ nya béwi nku itsyise balɛ budu lǝ bǝtsyuǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Nya nnya kulaa leso mǝ nkuɛnlo bokpe ni nnya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ bensiebunyǝ wǝ bio eso. Nya bákpa wǝ bésu bǝtsyǝ kusu lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ ɔflɔ lǝ lekpo kɛnyɛ mfó.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Nya se bǝtsyǝ bo kusu bálosǝǝ, búsu búdufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ nya bɔ́yila búsu Kos. Se kale dísiǝǝ, búsu búdufǝ Rodo nya búdu mfo búsu Patara.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Mfo nya búfi ɔklɔ wǝ ntɔ lǝ Foinike, nya búdu.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Se bɔ́ntɔɔ, búnyǝ Kipro eto kasɔ, nya bɔ́yɛ boofe mfo nya búdu bɔ́yɛ ukúe eto lekpake búsu búdufǝ Tiro lǝ Siria eto kasɔ. Nya búsu kaka boolǝkǝ bikǝ bia nkpe lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se lefe nǝ ele bǝ budu lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ léyoo, bafokanɔfɔ mfó ku mǝ besio ku bebi kenke ákpa bo esu bétsyǝ kusu lǝ lekpo kɛnyɛ, nya bomblɛ lése akonki bóto ola.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: Nya bútsyǝ bǝtsyuǝ esu fɛ búdufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo, nya mǝǝ tsya dísinkli bésu mǝ ayo.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Se búdu lǝ Tiroo, búnyǝ lǝ Ptolemai. Mfo búsu búsiǝsǝ bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe mfo, nya búsiǝ lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ diyi lɔni.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Nya se kale dísiǝǝ, búdu mfó búsu Kaisarea. Búsu bɔsɔnɔ Ditiki Sɛɛ eto otsyabe Filipo. Uyifo bawunsǝkotsyuǝ ekuanse ba bǝlǝkǝ lǝ Yerusalem eto ɔni.

english_verse: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
translation_verse: Ɔkɛ bebisiobi ena ba lensi tɔ bǝsǝ. Beyifo Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Nya búsiǝ mfó eyi ǝtsyuǝ. Lǝ lefe nǝ bɔkɛ mfóo, Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Agabo étsyi Yudea úbǝ mfó.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Nya se úbǝ bo ɔflɔɔ, ónyankli abɔsu nya Paulo lékle lǝ nkɛ nya úfi óklǝ wǝ kosate eto ani ku akpa nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ lɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Nko ke Yudafɔ lookle abɔsu nyamfo eto osate lǝ Yerusalem nya boofi wǝ besu bakpe lǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ eto beninǝ eto ani nɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nya se bɔ́nɔ etiki nyamfóo, bo ku betidi ba nkpe mfó kenke álɛ Paulo lekpakpa boǝ wensu Yerusalem.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi bo nkǝ, “Beso bǝǝnlɛ nku buwi nya bɛnlɛ mɛ ani bolo mǝ? Ntɔnɔ miǝ bakle mɛ lǝ Yerusalem, lǝ nsu nkpǝ lǝ Bonamute Yesu eso.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Nyaso lefe nǝ búnyǝ boǝ boǝmbofo wǝ nsusu bɔtsyi kawuninsǝǝ, bóyani wǝ nya bɔ́tɛyi wǝ boǝnkǝ, “Bonamute Yesu eto lelabi bǝ itǝ.”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi nyamfo eto kamaa, bomblɛ ǝmuǝ bikǝ nya búdu búsu Yerusalem.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Nya Kaisarea eto bafokanɔfɔ eto bǝtsyuǝ tsya dítǝnko bo. Bákpa bo besuko Kipro eto uninǝ wǝ baakpo Mnason bǝ lǝ búsu bɔsɔnɔ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó. Uninǝ ǝmfo eyifo ofokanɔnyǝ koko.

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Nya se búdufǝ Yerusalem bafokanɔfɔ nkpe mfó áfo bo kukɔnɔ tinti. Paulo Esu Nkǝ Lǝ Úsu Unyǝ Yakobo.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Se kale dísiǝǝ, Paulo ákpa bo nya búsu búnyǝ Yakobo ku Yerusalem eto bafokanɔfɔ eto beninǝ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nya se búsiǝsǝ mǝ bólosǝǝ, Paulo átɛyi mǝ asa nnya kenke Onanto lɛyɛ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ úyifo lǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ nti.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Se bétsyue wǝ ato balosǝǝ, mǝmblɛ kenke átɛsǝ Onanto. Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Ɔyimi lala, ǝnyǝ kase Yudafɔ akpi akpi kpǝ díkafo bánɔ nya banlɛ Moses eto afi lǝ kɔni kekleke.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Bǝtsyuǝ átɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ fǝ lɛtɛyi Yudafɔ ba nkpe lǝ nlebe nlebe fǝ bensidi Moses eto afi ku bo akpayifoyifo, nya fátɛyi tsya fǝnkǝ mba ni Yudafɔ tsya mensila mǝ bebi alo.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eso be ele bǝ buyifo mɔmɔ? Buyi boǝ bǝǝbǝ esu bɔbɛbɛ kǝbǝ. Itsyise bɔɔnɔ bǝ ǝbǝ ákpe lǝ bo ɔflɔ mfo.”

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Nya bǝtǝ wǝ nsusu bǝnkǝ, “Bokpe betidi ena bǝtsyuǝ lǝ bo mfo, ba díkaka ntamu.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Yɛnko mǝ esuko Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo mfó nya lǝ fǝ nku lǝ bɛkpi mi esi nya lǝ awunsǝko mǝ lǝ bafo mǝ disikpinkpi eto kayo. Nyamfo luutǝ utidi saa nyǝ nkǝ sidia bátɛyi lǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ, nyaso fintsya lɛ Moses eto kɔfi budi lǝ kɔni.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Nya bafokanɔfɔ ba lenni Yudafɔɔ, bɔ́ɔwɔni ekue bútǝ mǝ bɔ́tɛyi mǝ boǝ, bayɛ̀ edisa nnya béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi ku kɔtɔ ku bebuki ba baawaninsǝ simuǝ kaalo eto sinǝ eto budi, lǝ bayɛ sɛsɔnɔ budi.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Nyaso se kale disiǝǝ, Paulo ákpa basani bamǝ nya bésu béyifo disikpinkpi eto lekpa nǝmǝ. Nyamfo eto kamaa, úsu Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo úsu útǝ besumunyǝ bote eyi nnya kama fɛ disikpinkpi eto lekpa nǝmǝ loolo itǝ lǝ befi kedi eto bikǝ baboko lǝ beyifo keditǝtǝ eto lekpa lǝ mǝ disi. Bǝmúǝ Paulo Lǝ Yudafɔ Eto Disumuyo.

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Nya se íbu sɛkɛ lǝ eyi akuanse nyamǝ lǝ eyoo, Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ étsyi Asia eti bǝbǝ bǝnyǝ Paulo lǝ Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo mfo. Nyaso bákpadi betidi bǝtsyuǝ básɔ lebo nya bǝmuǝ wǝ.

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Nya bǝńyǝ mfó banlɛ kudu bokpo bǝnkǝ, “Israel eto basani, bɛwunsǝko bo! Osani ǝmfo ni nwǝ nlɛ botsyilama kate betidi kenke lǝ nlebe nlebe asa nnya ńyǝ lǝ bo betidi ku afi ku Disumuyo nǝmfo ǝsuǝ. Nya kama tsyaa, woakpa mba lenni Yudafɔ kedufǝnko Disumuyo nnya nlɛ bote nkǝ bákpe bo Yudafɔ eto kakɛnkɛkɔ kamfo kɔfiɔ!”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Bátɛyi nko itsyise lefe nǝ lefe mǝǝ, bǝnyǝ bǝ Paulo ku Trofimo wǝ ni Efesonyǝ balɛ bɔyɛ kafe lǝ Yerusalem. Lǝ nyaso béfi nsusu bǝ ókpa wǝ úsuko Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo mfó.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Nyaso se banlɛ kudu bokpo nkoo, étakasǝ betidi kenke lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ nya bátaka ku kudu nya bétsyetsyi bésu bátsya lǝ kusumukɔ mfó. Bǝtsyuǝ ǝnǝ Paulo babɔnko lǝ Disumuyo nya béti biku kenke.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bála bǝ lǝ balo wǝǝ, Romafɔ eto bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ánɔ nkǝ Yerusalemfɔ kenke átaka bǝńyǝ balɛ bulǝ bokpe.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfo, ókpa bamamanyǝ ninǝ ku bamamanyǝ bǝtsyuǝ ɔ́yɛnko nya bésu kaka banlɛ bulǝ bokpe mfó. Nya se mba ntɔnko mba nlɛ bulǝ bokpe dínyǝ bamamanyǝ bamfóo, báyɛ Paulo bɔblɛ.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Mfó nya ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmǝ dítǝ kusu nkǝ bǝmuǝ Paulo nya béfi amana ǝnuǝ bákpe wǝ. Nya fɛ ɔ́tɔ ɔ́nɔ utsyǝ eto okle utidi ǝmfo ni ku lesa nǝ úyifo.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Nya betidi bamfó eto utsyuǝ saa eto lesa nǝ ɔ́tɛyi lɛbɔ lete. Nya se bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko ǝmfo mámfo ditiki saa nǝ banlɛ bɔtɛyi kasɔ bɔnɔ itsyi lǝ kudu ko betidi bamǝ nlɛ bokpo esoo, ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ bakpa Paulo besu beti bakpe lǝ bamamanyǝ eto diyo.

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝ baala bǝ befi wǝ besu beti bakpe lǝ diyoo, Paulo átɛyi bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ nkǝ, “Ǝǝntǝ mɛ kusu lǝ ndinko fǝ etiki sɛkɛɛ?” Nya bamamanyǝ eto uninǝ ǝmǝ lɛtɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Bee! Aanɔ Sigriki fɛ?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Nya Paulo dílǝkǝ kɛnyɛ nkǝ, “Yudanyǝ mini. Bále mɛ lǝ Tarso lǝ Kilikia eto kasɔ. Nya umǝ ǝmfóo, enni umǝ kɛkɛ. Lǝ nyasoo, lekpakpa tǝ kusu lǝ ndi ko betidi bamfo etiki.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Nyaso uninǝ ǝmfo átɔnɔ útǝ Paulo kusu nkǝ udinko mǝ etiki. Nya Paulo lɛbɔ úbǝ úńyǝ lǝ kesie eto atalakpoe mfo nya óbesǝ ani kato nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ bedi kã. Nya se bédi kãã, údi mǝ etiki lǝ Yudafɔ eto sele ǝsuǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “Boantomǝ ku bayimi ba letsya mfo, nlɛ mi taflatsyɛ boto! Bitǝ kusu lǝ nkpasǝ mi etiki nyamfo lǝ bɛnɔ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ bánɔ bǝ Yudafɔ eto sele ɔnlɛ budii, bésinkli báyɛ kudu bokpo, betsyue wǝ ato. Nya Paulo lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Yudanyǝ mini, nya bále mɛ lǝ Tarso lǝ Kilikia eto kasɔ. Fɛɛ Yerusalem mfo mínsu suku. Gamaliel léte mɛ asa. Báte mɛ boantomǝ eto afi ku mǝ akpa kenke. Nya mímfi mɔ lɛkɔɛsi kenke míntǝ Onanto fe kase mi tsya nlɛ buyifo mmi.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Nya idu lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, ntsyǝ kasɔ ku bafokanɔfɔ lǝ kusu fɔfɔ komfo ǝsuǝ butǝkǝko, nya mínla miǝ balo mǝ. Nyaso ḿmuǝ basani ku besio nkpe lǝ Aba eto diyo.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke ku katsyakɔ kamǝ eto uninǝ saa ebudi nya minlɛ bɔtɛyi mfo eto adansiɛ, itsyise mǝ díkawɔni ekue kǝtǝ mɛ bǝ nsunkeyifo asa nyamfo. Nya lefe nǝ míntɔ lǝ Damaskoo, bǝtǝ mɛ ǝsuǝale bǝnkǝ nnyǝ bafokanɔfɔ ba nkpe mfóo, lǝ mmuǝ mǝ, lǝ nkle mǝ mfi mboko Yerusalem bǝ lǝ bǝnǝ mǝ koto. Paulo Édi Etiki Itsyi Lǝ Kase Ɔ́tsyi Lɛkɔɛsi Ɔ́wuninsǝ Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “Lǝ lefe nǝ mínyɛ míntɔ ibu sɛkɛ lǝ ndufǝ Damaskoo, iyifo fe kakɔmi eto lefosi abombo ǝnuǝǝ, lamfolamfo diyǝnde kekleke ditsyuǝ dítsyi kato díbǝ lékpo mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Nya míndu mínɔ mínkpo lǝ kasɔ nya mínɔ se ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ nlɛ etiki budi kátɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Saulo, Saulo, beso fǝńyǝ mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ mmǝ?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: “Nya míntɔ mínkǝ, ‘Bonamute, owoe feni?’ “Nya usi údi mɛ etiki nkǝ, ‘Mɔ ni Yesu Nasaretnyǝ.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Nya betidi ba ku mɛ nyɛɛ, ǝnyǝ diyǝnde nǝmfo. Fɛɛ bánnɔ utsyǝ wǝ nlɛ etiki budinko mfo eto ɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: “Nya míntɔ wǝ minkǝ, ‘Bonamute, be ele bǝ nyifo?’ “Nya usi ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Taka, lǝ esu Damasko. Mfó moote fǝ lesa nǝ mínlǝkǝ fǝ miǝ lǝ eyifo.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Diyǝnde kplɛ nǝmfo ába mɛ ǝnǝmi itǝ bǝ betidi ba bonko mǝ nyɛɛ, lɛkɔbe mɛ lǝ kɔni bálɛ fɛ búsu búdufǝ Damasko.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Nya, osani utsyuǝ akɛ mfó wǝ baakpo Ananias. Uyifo utidi wǝ leebu Onanto tinti, nya woayɛ lǝ bo afi ǝsuǝ tsya. Nya Yudafɔ ba nkpe mfó kenke leebu wǝ tinti.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Utidi ǝmfo ǝbǝ úńyǝ mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Ɔyimisani Saulo, sinkli ǝnyǝ asa!’ Lǝ nya koo, mɔ ǝnǝmi lékpa kɛ, nya mínyǝ wǝ!

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: “Nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Bo bana eto Onanto ni futsyǝ dílǝkǝ fǝ nkǝ laate wǝ lelabi, nya lǝ ǝnyǝ wǝ Ubi Kɛnkɛ lǝ ate wǝ tsya, lǝ anɔ wǝ etiki itsyi lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, kulesa mensi bu. Taka ǝńyǝ, lǝ afo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa. Lǝ akpadi wǝ diye, lǝ uflǝ fǝ abua uwi.’ Onanto Ákpe Paulo Nkǝ Úsu Mba Lenni Yudafɔ Ɔflɔ.

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “Nyamfo eto kamaa, nsinkli nsu Yerusalem. Nya lefe nǝ mínsu Disumuyo minlɛ ola botoo, íyifo mɛ fe selabe minlɛ, nya mínyǝ bulǝkǝkate butsyuǝ.

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Bonamute kosate lɛtɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Kɔse lǝ edu lǝ Yerusalem, itsyise betidi ba nkpe mfo embutsyue fǝ ditiki nǝ fɔɔtɛyi itsyi lǝ mɛ ǝsuǝ koto.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: “Nya míntǝ mbuayɛ minkǝ, ‘Bonamute, mǝmblɛ yi bǝnkǝ mɔ díkesu katsyakɔ isu katsyakɔ kesu kǝmuǝ bafokanɔfɔ nya mínkablɛ mǝ.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ banlɛ Otsyabe Stefano boloo, mintsya átɔnɔ ku mǝ, nya bǝlǝkǝ mǝ awu kato bǝtǝ mɛ mínlɛ fɛ bákato wǝ aba kalo.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: “Nya Bonamute lɛtɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Taka, lǝ ama kusu itsyise kaka minlɛ fǝ botsyese atsyo tinti lǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ nti!’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo kenkee, betidi bamfó ńyǝ kpoo banlɛ wǝ ditiki ato butsyuǝ. Fɛɛ lefe nǝ údi wǝ ditiki nǝmfo ólosǝǝ, mǝmblɛ ákpo kudu bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Aa! Lo wǝ ku wǝ ditsyǝbi kenke! Yensile bǝ ɔkɛ lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo kon!”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Nya mǝmblɛ lekpo kudu bembembe ku lɛblɔfi bála mǝ awu lǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bákpa utembu bǝsǝ wǝ, nya utembu ǝmǝ lébe kato ófo mfo kenke.

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Nya Roma eto bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmǝ dítǝ nkǝ bákpa wǝ bedufǝnko diyo kafo lǝ bablɛ wǝ lǝ babɛbɛ lesa nǝ eso Yudafɔ bamfo nlɛ wǝ kudu bokpo.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lefe nǝ bákle wǝ bǝǝbǝ wǝ bɔblɛɛ, Paulo átɔ omamanyǝ ninǝ ɔni wǝ dińyǝ mfó nkǝ, “Kusu kpe bǝ bablɛ Romanyǝ wǝ bánkponko tɔ lekpo?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Se uninǝ ǝmfo lɛnɔ nyafoo, úsu ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Be biǝ bieyifo mfo mmǝ? Osani ǝmfoo, Romanyǝ uni lo!”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Nyaso bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmfo dísu Paulo ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Be fanlɛ bɔtɛyi? Romanyǝ feni?” Nya Paulo dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Ee!”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Nya ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmfo lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Mintsya Romanyǝ mini. Fɛɛ mɔɔ, atabi kpǝ míntǝ fɛ mimfiani Romanyǝ!” Nya Paulo dítǝ wǝ mbuayɛ bio nkǝ, “Mɔ alɛɛ, mɔ anto ku ambe kenke eyifo Romafɔ.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Lamfolamfo se betidi ba bǝ bablɛ wǝ lǝ batɔ wǝ kɛnyɛ eto etiki banɔ léte bǝ Romanyǝ unii, difufu ǝmuǝ mǝmblɛ kenke nya bányi besu kama. Nya lefe nǝ mǝ ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ léte nkǝ Romanyǝ unii, sikpi ǝmuǝ wǝ tinti, itsyise wǝ ni futsyǝ dítǝ kusu nkǝ bakpe wǝ lǝ amana ǝsuǝ. Bákpa Paulo Bésuko Bakponkpo Ɔflɔ.

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmfo ála nkǝ waate lesa nǝ díbǝ fɛ béfi etiki lǝ Paulo ǝsuǝ. Nyaso se kale dísiǝǝ, ókpadi Yudafɔ eto besumunyǝ ninǝ ku mǝ bakponkpo kenke nya mǝmblɛ díbǝ bakɔ. Nya útǝ nkǝ banyankli Paulo nya bákpa wǝ báboko mǝ ǝnǝmi bǝ lǝ bedufǝ lǝ wǝ etiki ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ Paulo létsyilamasǝ ǝnǝmii, únyǝ Yudafɔ eto bakponkpo ba díkǝbǝ bensi lǝ lekpokakpokɔ mfó, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bayimi, mɔ lɛkɔɛsi anlɛ mɛ dikpi bolo lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi ibǝ ese lǝ mmi eto diyi nǝ mfo.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Nya se Ananias wǝ ni Yudafɔ eto Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke lǝ mǝ nti mfó lɛnɔ kase Paulo dítǝ mbuayɛ bomfoo, ɔ́tɛyi mba ńyǝ lǝ Paulo ɔflɔ mfo nkǝ basɔ wǝ lǝ kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Mfó Paulo lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Onanto ǝbɔsɔ fǝ nko ke! Fǝ nwǝ ni ofati wǝ bákua diflubi futututu! Nte okponkpo wǝ eto okle fe ni mfo? Nte kɔfi ko eto okle ǝsuǝ fáyɛ fanlɛ mɛ lekpo bokponko, fɛɛ fǝ ǝmǝ ke ńyǝ lǝ ko ǝsuǝ se fǝtǝ kusu fǝ basɔ mɛ!”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Nya mba díńyǝ lǝ Paulo ɔflɔ mfó lɛtɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Onanto eto Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke fanlɛ boka mmǝ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, Paulo ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mɔ bayimi, Israelfɔ, mánte nko minkǝ wǝ ni Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke. Lǝ nko inii, eni méndi wǝ etiki nko. Itsyise bátɛyi lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue nkǝ, ‘Mántɛyi etiki bua saa nnya dińyǝ lǝ mi batɔnkotɔnko ǝsuǝ.’ ”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Nya Paulo ǝnyǝ nkǝ, betidi ba díbǝ bátsya mfó eto bǝtsyuǝ eyifo ditsyukpa nǝ baakpo Sadukifɔ, nya bǝtsyuǝ tsya diyifo Farisifɔ. Lǝ nyasoo, ófã bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔ bayimi, Farisinyǝ mini! Nya Farisinyǝ eto kafo bale mɛ bétsyiko! Nyaso banlɛ mɛ lekpo bokponko mmi nɛ itsyise mfo nnɔ miǝ bǝkpǝnkǝ si bootaka betsyi ɔkɔmǝ bǝbǝ bio!”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Paulo lɛtɛyi nko eto ditiki nǝmfo ólosǝǝ, dikunǝ kǝdiǝ ko dídufǝ lǝ etsyukpa ǝnuǝ nyamfo eto nti, nya báma lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Itsyise Sadukifɔ aatɛyi bǝnkǝ bekpǝnkpǝ ǝnsiǝmbotaka, nya Onanto eto batɔnkpe ku awɔnɔ ǝmbǝ, fɛɛ Farisifɔ áfo nyamfo kenke bánɔ.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Mfo nya kǝdiǝ dídufǝ mǝ lenti nyanklaa. Mfo nya kɔfi eto bateasa ba ni Farisifɔ díńyǝ lǝ Paulo eto ditiki nǝmǝ eto kama bǝ ɔnukualɛ ɔ́tɛyi. Nya bákpo kudu bembembe bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Boǝnyǝ lesa bua saa lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ! Nya lǝ lɛwɔnɔ ditsyuǝ nye Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ nlɛ wǝ etiki budinkoo, be lebia lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ?”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Mfóo, etiki eto kǝnǝ kamǝ ǝbǝ kakɛ ale tinti. Nyaso bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ ǝmǝ áte sikpi nkǝ boonǝ Paulo bésense nyasoo, ɔ́tɛyi wǝ bamamanyǝ nkǝ bakpa wǝ besu beti bakpe lǝ Aba eto diyo.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Nya diyi nǝmfo eto ntsyǝntii, Bonamute ǝbǝ Paulo ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Paulo, kpe utu! Fe kase fǝńyǝ fédi mɔ ditiki nǝmǝ eto adansiɛ lǝ Yerusalem, nko ke fuuńyǝ edi ni lǝ Roma tsya nɛ.” Yudafɔ Ákpe Disi Bǝnkǝ Baalo Paulo.

english_verse: The next morning more than
translation_verse: Nya betidi bamfo ebuyifo fe betidi fosi ana ku kama.

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Nya bésu besumunyǝ ninǝ kenke ku mǝ batɔnkotɔnko ɔflɔ nya bésu bátɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, “Boka ntamu boǝnkǝ boembudi nye bunǝ kulesa isu ese lefe nǝ boolo Paulo.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso mi ku bakponkpo tɛyi bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko bǝ lǝ okpa Paulo oboko katsyakɔ mfó. Biyifo fenkǝ bela biǝ bisi biatɔ wǝ etiki nyamǝ eto lekpasǝ. Nya lǝ bakpa wǝ balɛ bobokoo, lǝ bolo wǝ lǝ kusu.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Nya se Paulo eto utitabo lɛnɔ etiki nya kenke bátɛyi mfo esoo, útsyǝtsyi úsu Aba eto diyo kaka bákpe Paulo nya úsu úyu ɔ́tɛyi wǝ etiki nya bátɛyi.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Nyaso Paulo ákpadi omamanyǝ ninǝ wǝ díńyǝ lǝ mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Kpa okosobi ǝmfo esuko mi ununǝ ɔflɔ. Ɔyɛnko ditiki kekleke nkǝ lǝ ɔtɛyi wǝ.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nya esoo, omamanyǝ ǝmǝ ákpa okosobi ǝmfo úsuko mǝ uninǝ ǝmǝ ɔflɔ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Paulo wǝ bákpe lǝ diyo mfo lekpadi mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ nkpa dikpefi nǝmfo mboko fǝ ɔflɔ lǝ ɔtɛyi fǝ ditiki ditsyuǝ nǝ ɔnyɛnko.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Nyaso bamamanyǝ ninǝ kenke eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko ǝmfo ákɔbe okosobi ǝmfo lǝ kɔni nya ókpa wǝ usuko lɛkpɔ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Be fanyɛnko fǝ atɛyi mɛ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ ákle kɛnyɛ ku mǝ beninǝ bǝnkǝ bǝǝbǝ balɛ fǝǝ lekpakpa bǝ lǝ akpa Paulo aboko katsyakɔ mfó kusɛɛ ku nsusu bǝ lǝ batɔ wǝ etiki ǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko ǝmfo ǝkǝ wǝ ato nkǝ, “Mǝntǝ lǝ kutsyuǝ saa lǝ usi ɔnɔ ditiki nǝ fátɛyi mɛ mfo blɛmblɛ!” Nya útǝ okosobi ǝmfo kusu dídu. Bákpa Paulo Bésuko Aba Eto Unyɛsiǝ Felike Ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Bitǝ Paulo batɔmini lǝ usiǝ lǝ bekpa wǝ bɛyɛnko buɛ bisuko Aba eto Unyɛsiǝ Felike ɔflɔ.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́wɔni kukue kutsyuǝ nkǝ befi besuko unyɛsiǝ ǝmǝ nkǝ,

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ dímuǝ utidi ǝmfo nya yoofo budi bǝ bala bǝ baalo wǝ fɛ mintǝ bamamanyǝ miǝ bafo wǝ, itsyise nnɔ bǝ Romanyǝ uni.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Nyaso nkpa wǝ nsuko mǝ katsyakɔ bǝ lǝ banɔ lesa nǝ uyifo.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Nnyǝ ntsyǝ lekpo minkǝ nnya eso bǝmuǝ wǝ mfo yɛ lǝ mǝ Yudafɔ eto afi ǝsuǝ nya inni bota kebu bo eso ele bǝ bakpe wǝ lǝ diyo nye balo wǝ.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ utsyuǝ lɛtɛyi mɛ ǝnkǝ balɛ disi bubu lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ balo wǝǝ, lamfolamfo mbu disi minkǝ lǝ nkpa wǝ mboko fǝ ɔflɔ nya lǝ ntɛyi mba wǝ ku mǝ lɛkɛ etiki miǝ befi kabua ka uyifo baboko fǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Nyaso bamamanyǝ bamfó eyifo lǝ lesa nǝ bátɛyi mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso ketsye kamfóo, bákpa Paulo báyɛnko taa Antipatri.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Nya se kale dísiǝǝ, bamamanyǝ ba nyɛ lǝ akpa ésinkli bésu Aba eto diyokakpekɔ mfó, nya bǝtǝ mba lǝǝsiǝ lǝ batɔmini bǝ bayɛnko Paulo besuko Kaisarea.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Se bésu bédufǝǝ, bákpa Paulo ku kukue ko bǝtǝ mǝ mfo bésu bǝtǝ Aba eto Unyɛsiǝ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ lefe nǝ ɔnlɛ kukue komǝ boklaa, ɔ́tɔ Paulo kaka útsyi. Se ɔ́nɔ nkǝ Paulo etsyi Kilikiaa,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: usi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Muudufǝ lǝ fǝ etiki nyamfo ǝsuǝ nnɔ nya kukɔnɔ lǝ lefe nǝmǝ ayo lǝ fonko betidi ba lɛkɛ etiki mfó ǝbǝ.” Nya usi útǝ wǝ bamamanyǝ ba ńyǝ mfó nkǝ bákpa Paulo besu beti bakpe lǝ diyo nǝ Oka Herodes léto lǝ bakase wǝ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Eyi anɔ eto kamaa, Usumunyǝ Ninǝ Kenke Ananias ku Yudafɔ eto beninǝ bǝtsyuǝ ku mǝ Otsyiami wǝ bákpo Tertulo ǝbǝ Kaisarea. Bǝbǝ Felike ɔflɔ bǝ lǝ bǝbǝ babɔ wǝ kabua ka Paulo nlɛ buyifo.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Nya se bákpadi Pauloo, Tertulo ákpe kɛnyɛ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bo Ububutidi, lefe kpǝ ni nǝmfoo, diyuǝ akɛ bo lenti ɛyɛ lǝ fǝ kobe ku bote butsyue bo fefi fanyɛnko bo eso. Nya bunyǝ boǝ bɔtsyikawuninsǝ sɛɛ kpǝ tsya ǝbǝ lǝ bo kasɔ mfo kenke.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso bo Yudafɔ kenke lɛ fǝ ani bɔsɔ.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Nya bɔlɛ fǝ lekpakpa bɔlɛ boǝ lǝ bɔyɛ fǝ lefe bobiasǝǝ, lǝ etsyue ato anɔ etiki nya boǝbǝ bɔtɛyi itsyi lǝ osani ǝmfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: “Se bunyǝ wǝǝ, bunyǝ boǝ uyifo nwǝ laakpe bulǝ, nya woatsyilama kafe lefe saa akabiasǝ Yudafɔ eto nsusu lǝ kalebe saa lǝ kasɔ kamfo kenke bǝ lǝ bǝnǝ asa bua baboko lǝ bǝnyǝ Aba lǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya wǝ ni ditsyukpa nǝ baakpo Nasaretfɔ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Nyaso ebofo wǝ bɔtɔ lǝ anɔ lǝ etiki nnya bɔ́tɛyi mfo eyifo sidia.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Nya Yudafɔ bamǝ kenke lɛkɔ bédi adansiɛ bátɛyi bǝtǝkǝ lǝ nnya kenke Tertulo lɛtɛyi bǝ iyifo ɔnukualɛ. Paulo Édi Etiki Ókpansǝ Wǝ Etiki Lǝ Felike Eto Ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Aba eto unyɛsiǝ emfo diyifo Paulo kɔni nkǝ udi etikii, Paulo ǝtsyǝ kasɔ nkǝ, “Anto, nnyi miǝ fǝ ni futsyuǝ laakpo akpo lǝ Yudafɔ nti alɛ kpǝ ni nyamfo. Lǝ nyaso iyifo mɛ disuǝyuǝ bǝ ndi etiki nlǝkǝ mɔ ǝsuǝ kasɔ lǝ fǝ ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Yuuyifo fǝ bote bǝnkǝ iyifo fe lefosi eyi ǝnuǝ ni nyamfo se mímbǝ Yerusalem mímbǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo mfó bǝ lǝ ntǝ kedi.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Nya kuutsyuǝ mǝnyǝ se bonko kuutsyuǝ nlɛ etiki bunǝ nye mínkpe bulǝ saa lǝ Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo mfó nye lǝ Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ tsya nnye lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ kafo.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Nya betidi bamfo eto kuutsyuǝ saa ǝmbofo kulesa bunyǝ kate sitinti, bǝ etiki nnya bátɛyi mfo eyifo sitinti.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Nyaa, ditiki nǝ ǝsuǝ mɔɔtɔnɔ ku mǝ ni bǝnkǝ nńyǝ lǝ boantomǝ eto Onanto eto bofokanɔ eto kusu ko mǝ bǝnkǝ iyifo ditsyukpa nǝ nńyǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nnya ni bǝ mmfo Moses eto kɔfi nnɔ kekleke ku asa nnya báwɔni bákpe lǝ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto ekue ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Mfi ǝnǝmi ntǝkǝ lǝ Onanto ǝsuǝ fe kase mǝ tsya dífi mǝ ǝnǝmi bǝtǝkǝ bǝ betidi ba kenkee, bǝ basɛɛ ku babua tã ebotaka betsyi ɔkɔmǝ bǝbǝ.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, mfi mɔ ǝsuǝale kenke nkpe bǝ nlǝkǝ mɔ nsusu nte lǝ nkpa lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝ lǝ Onanto ku betidi kenke eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: “Alɛ kpǝ ni nyamfo mensikɛ lǝ Yerusalem. Nya misinkli mimbǝ miǝ mufi ǝtǝsa ntǝ bakombo ba ni mɔ kosate eto betidi nya lǝ ntǝ Onanto kedi tsya.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ mínyifo disikpinkpi eto lekpa mínlosǝǝ, Yudafɔ bǝtsyuǝ ǝbǝ bǝnyǝ mɛ lǝ Disumuyo mfó. Nya bonko mba laakpo kudu kakpe bulǝ saa mányɛ. Nya mɔ ǝmǝ tsya mánkpe bulǝ saa.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Yoofo budi bǝ Yudafɔ ba ditsyi Asia eto kasɔɔ, mǝ ele bǝ bebǝ fǝ ɔflɔ. Nya lǝ mɔ ku mǝ kpe etiki ǝtsyuǝǝ, nnya boolǝkǝ batɛyi nɛ.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Nnye betidi ba nsi mfóo, yoofo budi bǝ bɔɔtɛyi asa bua nnya eto dikpi nǝ bálo mǝ lefe nǝ Yudafɔ eto bakponkpo ninǝ ba lɛkɛ mfó fɛ bákponko mɛ lekpo.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Enso ditiki nǝ mímbesǝ ɔkɔɛ kato míntɛyi lǝ lefe nǝ míńyǝ lǝ mǝ ǝnǝmi minkǝ, ‘Mbǝ katsyakɔ mfo lǝ nlǝkǝ mɔ ǝsuǝ kɛnyɛ minkǝ mfo nnɔ minkǝ bǝkpǝnkpǝ si bootaka betsyi ɔkɔmǝ bǝbǝ’ eso békponko mɛ lekpo?”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Nyaso Felike wǝ nyi kusufɔfɔtsyɔntsyɔ eto ditsyukpa nya ófo ditiki nǝmfo ɔ́nɔ tintii, átɛyi Yudafɔ bamfo nkǝ, “Bisinkli bɛyɛ isu ese lefe nǝ bamamanyǝ eto ɔtɔnkotɔnko kplɛ Lisia luubǝ fɛ lǝ nla ditiki nǝmfo nlǝkǝ.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Nya útǝ kusu nkǝ omamanyǝ ninǝ kpa Paulo usu okpe lǝ diyo. Fɛɛ balɛ wǝ ola kukɔnɔ, lǝ bǝtǝ wǝ bayɛtsyuǝ ba luubǝ bǝ bǝǝnyǝ wǝ nnye baboko wǝ asaa, kusu. Paulo Átɛyi Felike Ku Drusila Wǝ Etiki.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi asiani ǝtsyuǝ eto kamaa, Felike ku wǝ usiǝ Drusila wǝ ni Yudanyǝ ǝbǝ, nya útǝ nkǝ bakpa Paulo baboko, nya údi mǝ etiki itsyi lǝ Yesu Kristo eto bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ unsi ɔnlɛ asa kasɔ bulǝkǝ kate mǝ itsyi lǝ nkpǝ sɛɛ siǝsiǝ ku ǝsuǝ ola bɔlɛ ku Onanto eto lekpo kponkpo nǝ lǝǝbǝ bubǝ kama kamaa, sikpi alɛ Felike tinti. Mfóo, nya Felike lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Taka lǝ edu. Nya lǝ nsi nkɛ lefe sɛɛ, muutǝ bakpadi fǝ bǝtǝ mɛ.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Nya se úbu nkǝ Paulo ebuyifo nte butsyuǝ utǝ wǝ esoo, úkakpe nkǝ bákakpadi wǝ lefe saa lǝ wonko wǝ lǝ bǝkǝdi etiki lǝ bǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Lǝ alɛ ǝnuǝ eto kamaa, bǝlǝkǝ Felike bédunko nya báboko Porkio Festo lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ. Nya se Felike lɛbɛbɛ nkǝ Yudafɔ la wǝ asa esoo, útǝ kusu nkǝ Paulo si ɔkɛ diyo nǝmǝ mfó.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Eyi ǝtsyǝ eto kama se Festo díbǝ údufǝ lǝ wǝ sininǝ kedikɔɔ, údu lǝ Kaisarea úsu Yerusalem.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Mfó nya besumunyǝ ninǝ ku Yudafɔ eto beninǝ lɛtɛyi wǝ etiki itsyi lǝ Paulo ǝsuǝ. Nya bálɛ wǝ lekpakpa bǝnkǝ

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: okpa Paulo oboko Yerusalem. Nya mǝ nsusu ni bǝnkǝ bɔɔbiɔ bǝsǝkǝ wǝ lǝ kusu. Nya lǝ ubǝ ɔlɛ bofee, lǝ balo wǝ.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Nya Festo lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Paulo labe lǝ diyo lǝ Kaesarea. Nya mintsya luubǝ mfo lefe kliminti eto kama.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Nyaso bitǝ lǝ mi beninǝ lǝ bǝbǝ mfó lǝ bǝbǝ bǝlǝkǝ kabua ka úyifo bate.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi mflamfla nye lefosi eto kamaa, Festo ésinkli úsu Kaesarea. Se kale dísiǝǝ, ókpadi Paulo eto lekpo nǝmfo.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Nya se Paulo díbǝ údufǝ lekpokakpokɔ mfóo, Yudafɔ ba ditsyi Yerusalem ǝbǝ bǝńyǝ bátsyilama wǝ nya báwe etiki kpǝ bǝtǝkǝ wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ nya bámfo kɛnyɛ bulǝkǝ kate ɔnukualɛ wǝ nkpe lǝ nnya.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Paulo édi etiki ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Ményifo kabua saa nta Yudafɔ eto kɔfi nye Disumuyo nye Okankplɛ Kaisare eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ mbu.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Nya se Festo léla nkǝ lǝ Yudafɔ la wǝ asa esoo, ɔ́tɔ Paulo nkǝ, “Ala fǝnkǝ eesu Yerusalem lǝ nsu nkponko fǝ lekpo nǝmǝ mfó?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Owo. Iyifo mɛ kusutǝtǝ bǝnkǝ kasɔ kamfo eto Okankplɛ Kaisare kosate kponko mɛ lekpo nǝmfo. Fǝ kosate yi sitinti fǝnkǝ manta kɔfi sa mbu nyifo kabua sa lǝ Yudafɔ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyifo lesa nǝ díńyǝ itǝ bǝ balo mǝǝ, mɔɔtɔnɔ miǝ balo mɛ! Fɛɛ lǝ mánta kɔfi saa mbuu, yembutǝ kusu bǝ fǝ nye utidi bamba lǝ ufi mɛ utǝ betidi bamfo lǝ balo. Lǝ nyaso mfi mɔ etiki nlɛ butsyǝ lǝ Okankplɛ Kaisare eto ǝnǝmi!”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Festo ku wǝ beninǝ álɛ etiki nyamǝ bábe nya ɔ́tɛyi Paulo bǝnkǝ, “Se féfi fǝ etiki fanlɛ Okankplɛ Kaisare butǝ esoo, ebusu Okankplɛ Kaisare eto ǝnǝmi.” Oka Agripa Ku Berenike Ánɔ Paulo Eto Etiki.

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi ǝtsyuǝ eto kamaa, Oka Agripa ku wǝ ɔyimisio wǝ baakpo Bernike ǝbǝ Festo ɔflɔ lǝ Kaisarea bǝ lǝ bǝbǝ bǝnyǝ wǝ bǝsiǝsǝ.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Bǝsiǝ mfó eyi kpǝ nya Festo dífi Paulo eto etiki ɔ́tɛyi Oka ǝmfo nkǝ, “Osani utsyuǝ labe lǝ Aba eto diyo mfo, nya Felike léyani utsyǝ.

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ mínsu Yerusalem, besumunyǝ ninǝ ku mǝ batɔnkotɔnko átɛyi mɛ etiki nnya bála bǝtǝkǝ wǝ, nya bátɛyi mɛ bǝ ntǝ kusu lǝ balo wǝ dikpi.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: “Nya mínlǝkǝ kasɔ mínte mǝ miǝ Romafɔ eto kɔfi mǝntǝ kusu bǝ balo utidi dikpi fɛ lǝ bakponko wǝ lekpo. Ele bǝ bǝtǝ wǝ kusu lǝ wǝ ku betidi ba lɛkɛ etiki lǝ batsya batɛyi nya fɛ lǝ balo wǝ dikpi.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bǝbǝ mfo lǝ etiki nyamfo ǝsuǝǝ, mintsya mémfi nnya ntsyǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ. Lǝ nnya kǝsiǝsǝ eto olesiǝǝ, ntǝ miǝ bakpa osani ǝmfo baboko mɔ lekpokakpokɔ.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ etiki nnya béfi lɛ wǝǝ, menyifo etiki bua nnya mímbu miǝ nnya boofi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Asa nnya bátɛyi mfó eyifo etiki nnya ditsyi lǝ mǝ kafokanɔ ku utidi wǝ baakpo Yesu wǝ díkpǝ, nya Paulo, wǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ okpe nkpǝ ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Iyifo mɛ basaa nya mante kase mɔɔyɛ nla etiki nyamǝ. Lǝ nyaso ntɔ Paulo lǝ woola nkǝ woefi ditiki nǝmǝ usuko Yerusalem lǝ usu ulǝkǝ etiki nyamfo kɛnyɛ lǝ Yerusalem mfó.

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lɛtɛyi nkǝ óla nkǝ woefi wǝ etiki usuko Okankplɛ Kaisare lǝ udi, lǝ nyaso ntǝ miǝ beti wǝ bakpe lǝ diyo isu ese lefe nǝ mootsyese wǝ Okankplɛ ǝmǝ ɔflɔ.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Nya Oka Agripa lɛtɛyi Festo nkǝ, “Nla miǝ mɔ kosate lǝ nnɔ wǝ ditiki nǝmǝ.” Nya Festo lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Ɛbɔnɔ ditiki nǝmfo kusɛɛ!” Paulo Ǝbǝ Agripa Eto Ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Nya se kale disiǝǝ, Oka Agripa ku Berenike álɔkɔ ku bekankǝ bǝbǝ lekayo nya bédufǝ lekpokakpokɔ mfó. Nya bamamanyǝ ku umǝ ǝmǝ eto beninǝ dítǝkǝko mǝ bǝǝbǝ. Nya Festo dítǝ ǝsuǝale nkǝ bakpa Paulo baboko.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Nya Festo lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Oka Agripa ku mi mba kenke díbǝ bétsya mfo, osani ǝmfo ni utidi wǝ Yudafɔ ba nkpe lǝ Yerusalem ku Kaisarea mfo kenke tsya léla bǝ balo!

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ mɔ nsusu ǝsuǝǝ, wényifo kulesa nǝ dítǝ bǝ balo wǝ dikpǝnkpi. Fɛɛ wǝ ǝmǝ tsya nkǝ úfi wǝ etiki útǝ bo Okankplɛ. Nya mintsya mánni nko.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ be ni lesa nǝ ele bǝ nwɔni ntǝ bo Okankplɛ ǝmfo? Itsyisee, wényifo tɔ kabua saa mǝ. Lǝ nyaso nkpa wǝ mboko mi ku fǝ Oka Agripa titlu, minkǝ lǝ bidufǝ lǝ wǝ ditiki ǝsuǝ lǝ bete mɛ ditiki nǝ ele bǝ nwɔni ntsyese wǝ.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Itsyisee, yanlɛkɛ bǝ nkpa nwǝ bákpe lǝ diyo ntsyese kasɔ kamfo eto Okankplɛ lǝ lefe nǝ eto lefe mǝnnyǝ kabua ka úyifo ka mɔɔwɔni ntsyese wǝ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Nya Agripa lɛtɛyi Paulo nkǝ, “Kusu kpe bǝ di fǝ etiki.” Lǝ nnya esoo, Paulo ábesǝ wǝ kɔni kato nya údi etiki ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Bo Oka Sɛɛ Agripa, disi ɛkpɛ kpe mɛ mmi bǝ moofo mɔ ditiki bulǝkǝ kate lǝ fǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ nnya eso Yudafɔ dífi etiki lǝ mɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: Itsyise nyi miǝ fǝǝ, eyi Yudafɔ eto kǝnǝ ka nkpe lǝ mǝ nti ku mǝ akpa kenke kukɔnɔ. Nyamfo esoo, minlɛ fǝ lekpakpa nɛ. Kpe utu lǝ anɔ mɔ etiki ǝtǝ mɛ.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Lǝ bɔɔtɛyi ɔnukualɛɛ, nko seka mísiǝ nkpǝ fe Farisinyǝ itsyi taa mɔ sikpefi lefe nɛ. Nya Yudafɔ eto afi ku akpa kpe ale eso nsumu bamba saa eto ale kenke.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso ete béfi etiki nyamfo banlɛ butǝkǝ mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ nɛ lǝ kase mińyǝ minlɛ kusu bobe itǝ kɛnyɛ ka Onanto lɛtɛyi bo bana mǝ utsyǝ eso.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Israelfɔ eto lefosi atobi ǝnuǝ nnya kenke lǝ bokplatsya kekleke kǝtsyǝ ku kakɔmi bǝ lǝ mǝ tsya lǝ bǝnyǝ kusu ko minlɛ bobe mfo. Bo Oka Sɛɛ, nyamfo eso Yudafɔ nlɛ mɛ kɛnyɛ bola kǝtǝkǝ mɛ etiki lǝ ǝsuǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Beso ele bǝ ilǝ bǝ mi mba nkpe mfo kenke fo bɛnɔ biǝ Onanto aatakasǝ bǝkpǝnkpǝ?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “Lefe nǝ léfe mmǝǝ, mfi nnya ntǝkǝ lǝ mɔ ǝsuǝ miǝ muuyifo lesa saa nǝ moofo lǝ nńyǝ lǝ Yesu wǝ ni Nasaretnyǝ eto mba ntǝkǝko wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ eto besumunyǝ ninǝ ǝtǝ mɛ ǝsuǝale bǝ mmuǝ betidi kɛnkɛ kpǝ nkpe lǝ diyo lǝ Yerusalem. Nya mba bálo lǝ mǝ nti tsyaa, íyifo mɔ lelabi bǝ ibǝ nko

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Ntǝkǝko mǝ nya mínkpɔ mǝ nya mínte mǝ ohiã ɛyɛ isu kǝmǝ bamba ka letsyo kulaa tsya. Nya míntǝ miǝ beyifo mǝ asafimfi lǝ Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ kenke. Nya míńyǝ mǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ ku lɛblɔfi miǝ bedi mbusiotiki nnya dińyǝ lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ. Paulo Átɛyi Kase Útsyi Ɔwuninsǝ Útǝkǝko Yesu Kama.

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Nko eto ditsyǝbi ǝsuǝ minkɛ minyɛ mintɔ lǝ Damasko ku ǝsuǝale fe kase besumunyǝ ninǝ dítǝ mɛ.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Bo Oka Sɛɛ, kase minyɛ kusu lǝ kakɔmi eto lefosi ǝnuǝ lefee, nnyǝ diyǝnde nǝ lekpa lenso mfenti eto kofe ditsyi kato nya lékpo létsyilama bonko mba nyɛ bɔntɔ.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Nya mfó se bomblɛ dídu bɔ́nɔɔ, nnɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto sele ǝsuǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Saulo, Saulo, beso fǝńyǝ mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ nko? Lǝ áta biyu kokpaa, fǝ kosate ete loofo lefabe.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: “Nya míntɔ wǝ minkǝ, ‘Bonamute, owoe fe ni?’ “Nya ɔkɔɛ ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mɔ ni Yesu wǝ fanlɛ bɔkpɔnko kekleke.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, taka ǝńyǝ! Se mínkǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ mínte fǝ esoo, muufi fǝ nyifo mɔ uyifoyifo. Ɛbɔtɛyi kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke lesa nǝ fǝnyǝ mfo ku asa nnya muukǝlǝkǝ nkate fǝ.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Nya kaka minlɛ fǝ botsyesee, moose fǝ, nlǝkǝ fǝ ntsyiko lǝ fǝ betidi ku lǝ bele bamba tsya nti.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: Muutǝ asini mǝ ǝnǝmi nnya luutǝ bǝ ebulǝkǝ mǝ etsyiko lǝ Obonsam eto ǝsuǝale ku ketuntu ka bankpe ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ bafo Onanto eto ǝsuǝale ku diyǝnde nǝ ǝsuǝ onkpe banɔ. Bǝ lǝ Onanto lǝ ufi mǝ abua ɔtsyɛ mǝ itǝ bǝ lǝ bǝnyǝ kǝsiǝkɔ kato fe mba úlǝkǝ óboko wǝ akɔ báfiani mba úlǝkǝ utsyǝ itsyise báfo mɛ bánɔ eso.’ Paulo Édi Etiki Itsyi Wǝ Keyifo Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Ntsyiko Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ budi kate Damaskofɔ lǝ nnya kama Yerusalemfɔ, lǝ nnya kama Yudafɔ kenke ku mba lenni Yudafɔ tsya miǝ besinkli lǝ mǝ abua ǝsuǝ lǝ befi mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Onanto, lǝ bate lǝ mǝ asayifoyifo ǝsuǝ bǝ bátsyi báwuninsǝ sitinti.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso Yudafɔ dímuǝ mɛ lǝ Yudafɔ eto Disumuyo nɛ. Nya bála bǝ eni baalo mɛ.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Onanto ǝlǝkǝ mɛ utsyiko lǝ mǝ ani. Nyaso minsi minkpe nkpǝ minlɛ adansiɛ nyamfo budi kate mi beninǝ ku betidi kenke tsya nɛ. Lesa nǝ minlɛ bɔtɛyi mfoo, nnya tututu ni nnya Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ku nnya Moses lɛtɛyi bǝ yuubǝ itǝ nɛ.

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nnya ni bǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ ebubǝ unyǝ kanyi. Nya wǝ ni utidi tutɛ wǝ lootaka utsyi ɔkɔmǝ ubǝ, nya wǝ luunǝ diyǝnde oboko Yudafɔ ku mba lenni Yudafɔ kenke.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, Festo áfa ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Paulo, disi ábia fǝ! Fǝ sikuente kplɛ siamǝ ábiasǝ fǝ disi!”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ububutidi Festo, disi mambia mɛ. Biemfi ditiki nǝmfo fe ayɛtiki. Etiki tinti minlɛ bɔtɛyi.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Sitinti, Oka Agripa yi etiki nnya minlɛ bɔtɛyi mfo. Nyaso minlɛ wǝ etiki budinko ku utu faa nɛ. Mfo nnɔ miǝ nyamfo eto kulesa menyifo wǝ akulasa, uyi nnya lesa saa.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Oka Agripa, áfo nnya Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi lɛtɛyi ánɔ? Nyi miǝ áfo mǝ ánɔ.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Nya Agripa lɛtɔ Paulo nkǝ, “Ala fǝ ntsyi nwuninsǝ mfiani Ukristonyǝ lǝ lefe kliminti nǝmfo ǝsuǝ?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Nya Paulo dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Lefe kliminti dini o-o, lɛkɛntsyɛ dinii, nlɛ ola boto mmi minkǝǝ, mi mba nkpe mfo kenke tsyi bɛwuninsǝ fe mintsya kǝ. Fɛɛ biunyǝ kanyi bakpe mi amana fe kase mɔ nlɛ bunyǝ mǝ!”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Nya Oka Agripa ku Festo ku Bernike ku betidi bamba bamfó kenke létaka bǝńyǝ.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Nya se bataka bábɔ lǝ mfoo, bákpe disi lǝ Paulo eto ditiki nǝmfo ǝsuǝ bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Utidi ǝmfo menyifo lesa saa bua nǝ ele bǝ balo wǝ nye bakpe wǝ lǝ diyo.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Nya Agripa lɛtɛyi Festo nkǝ, “Eni boofo wǝ boyani lenni nya úkefi ditiki nǝmfo útsyǝ Okankplɛ Kaisare lǝ ǝnǝmi eso.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ báse uyi bǝnkǝ ele bǝ busu Roma lǝ Italiaa, bákpa Paulo ku mba bákpe lǝ diyo ku babamba bákpe lǝ omamanyǝ ninǝ wǝ baakpo Yulio eto ani. We ni Okankplɛ eto bamamanyǝ eto ditsyukpa eto uninǝ.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Nya búfi ɔklɔ wǝ dítsyi Adramitio nya útǝ ǝnǝmi Asia eto lekpake eti. Nya bo ku Makedonianyǝ wǝ baakpo Aristarko wǝ ditsyi Tesalonika dísiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ kale dísiǝǝ, bóyo Sidon eto lekpo kɛnyɛ. Mfó Yulio dítǝ Paulo kusu kafo faa nkǝ usu umǝ lǝ usu unyǝ wǝ bayɛtsyuǝ itǝ lǝ bǝtǝ wǝ asa nnya wuufi ɔyɛ kusu.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Nya se busi búdu mfóo lǝ ɔklɔ ǝsuǝ nya lǝ kase ufiebi ǝmǝ lɛkɛ kɛnyɛ ale esoo, bɔyɛ Kipro eto Ɔnwɛdukue eti kaka bɔ́bɔ lǝ ufiebi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Nya se bófe mfóo, bɔ́yɛ lǝ Kilikia ku Pamfilia eto lekpo kɛnyɛ, nya búsu búńyǝ lǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Mira lǝ Likia eto lekpake.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Lǝ mfó omamanyǝ ninǝ wǝ nyɛnko bo ǝnyǝ ɔklɔ utsyuǝ wǝ ditsyi Aleksandria ɔntɔ lǝ Italia eto kasɔ. Nya útǝ búsiǝ lǝ wǝ.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ eyi kpǝ eto kamaa, ílǝ fɛ búsu bóyo umǝ wǝ baakpo Knido. Nya lǝ ufiebi wǝ nlɛ bɔsɔ kekleke esoo, bóamfo bɔyɛ kesu sɛtɔ. Nyaso se búdufǝ Salmonee, bɔ́tsyi bɔ́yɛ búsu Kreta ɔflɔ kaka kasɔ kamǝ díti ufiebi ǝmǝ eso wánkɛ kɛnyɛ ale.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Disiale fɛ bɔ́yɛ búsu búdufǝ kaka baakpo Ɔklɔ eto Kǝńyǝnkɔ ka lɛlɛkɛ ka dimensitsyo ku umǝ wǝ baakpo Lasea.
