english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Nya se búkedi eyi kpǝ lǝ kusu ko bɔnyɛ esoo, Yudafɔ eto kɛnyɛ leklefe ébofe. Nya bɔyɛ lǝ ntu ǝsuǝ lǝ lefe nǝmfo ebubǝ ɛkɛ ale eso. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, Paulo álɛ mǝ kɔlɛ nkǝ,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Boanto mǝǝ, nnyǝ minkǝ bo kusu bɔyɛ idu mɔmɔ ɛntɔ ǝbǝ ale bɔkɛ kaso, itsyise ɔklɔ ǝmfo ebudufǝ lǝ bulǝ ǝsuǝ, nya asa kpǝ ku betidi tsya ebɔtsylɔ.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bamamanyǝ eto uninǝ ǝmfo áfo ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto okanka ku ɔklɔ sate eto lesa nǝ bátɛyi ɔ́nɔ enso nnya Paulo lɛtɛyi.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Nyaso se ɔnwɛkǝtsyǝkɔ kamǝ mánlɛkɛ itǝ lefe kentsyɛ se lɛyɔ lefe nlɛ bubǝ esoo, mba wǝ ku mǝ nyɛ kusu átɛyi bǝnkǝ bala bǝ baayɛ besu bedufǝ Foinike bomu. Mfoti ebɔlɛkɛ itǝ mǝ bɔkɛ lǝ lɛyɔ lefe nǝmǝ. Itsyise Foinike eyifo umǝ lǝ Kreta eto kasɔ eto kaka kakɔlɔ kpǝ lǝǝtsyǝ ɔnwɛ nnya leeti ufiebi kusu lǝ kofe eto diyo kedufǝkɔ eti. Ufiebi Kekleke Ásɔ Lǝ Lekpo Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfó ufiebi sɛɛ utsyuǝ etsyiko bɔsɔ ketsyi Ɔnwɛdikue eti, nya iyifo fe yɔɔlɛkɛ itǝ kusu bɔyɛ. Lǝ nyaso bǝmuǝ ǝsuǝ nya bédu báyɛ lǝ Kreta ɔflɔ.

english_verse: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lamfolamfoo, ufiebi bua utsyuǝ ko dítsyiko bɔsɔ itsyi lǝ kodiani eto lekpake itsyi lǝ Kreta eti.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́sɔ útsyi ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto sɛtɔ óbesǝ kato. Bala bǝ lǝ yɔɔlɛkɛ lǝ batsyi ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto ǝnǝmi bawuninsǝ besuko kasɔ eti, tsya bamfo. Nyaso báyani wǝ nya ufiebi ǝmǝ lɛsɔ útsyi wǝ ɔ́yɛnko.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Nya kama kamaa, búsu búnyǝ kabiɔnkɔ sɛkɛ kǝtsyuǝ lǝ kasɔ ka ntu letsyilama ka baakpo Kauda eto kutsyǝ kɔni. Nya disiǝale fɛ bófo bo ɔklɔ kɛkɛ ǝmǝ botsyasǝ lǝ ɔkplɛ wǝ ǝsuǝ bunsi.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Nya se bǝnyǝ bǝ bulǝ saa ensibǝǝ, béfi kekuǝ bákle ɔklɔ kplɛ ǝmǝ tsya. Báte sikpi bǝnkǝ ufiebi ebɔsɔ utsyi wǝ usu okpe lǝ kota eto kɔwunimi bua ǝsuǝ lǝ Libia eto lekpo kɛnyɛ. Nyaso bábokosǝ ufiebi nto mfia bo bátsyasǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ. Lǝ nyamfo eso ufiebi ǝmǝ ǝnǝ wǝ ɔ́yɛnko.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Se kale dísiǝ fɛ ufiebi ǝmǝ dímanyɛ tɔ bɔsɔɔ, bétsyiko bikǝ bia nkpe lǝ wǝ kafo bulǝkǝ kato kakpe lǝ ntu ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ diyi tsyǝfǝ ǝsuǝǝ, bétsyiko ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto biyifokǝ kpǝ tsya bɔsɛ kato kakpe.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi kpǝ nnya dítǝkǝǝ, ufiebi ǝmǝ ásɔ kekleke isi eso itǝ bǝ bóǝnyǝ kofe nye befandebi. Nya itǝ bǝ bo kpǝ ménsibe kusu boǝ busi bɔɔkɛ nkpǝ kon!

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Nya lefe kpǝ ǝbǝ léfe bankɛ kɔni bendi lesa, mfó Paulo létaka úńyǝ lǝ mǝ ǝnǝmi nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔ bayimi, lǝ nnya ete bɛnɔ mɔ etikii, eni bóendu lǝ Kreta, enni asa nyamfo kenke mǝmbǝ itsyǝ bo mmǝ.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Nyaso nlɛ mi lekpakpa tinti minkǝ bekpe utu, ɔklɔ ǝmǝ ebonya, fɛɛ bo kuutsyuǝ saa embukpǝ!

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Itsyise kǝnǝ ku ketsyee, Onanto wǝ mbǝ mɛ ini wǝ meesumuu, wǝ ɔtɔnkpe ǝbǝ úńyǝ mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, ‘Paulo, mensite sikpi. Ele bǝ efi fǝ ditiki ǝsuko Kaisare ǝflɔ! Itsyise Onanto kpe kafo ɛkpɛ, úńyǝ fonko mba nyɛ kusu kaminsǝ eso mi kutsyuǝ saa embonya.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Nyaso mɔ betidi, bimuǝ utu bɛlɛ! Mfo Onanto nnɔ, lesa saa ebubǝ itǝ fe kase ɔ́tɛyi mɛ.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ele bǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ su ɔsi lǝ kɔwunimi ǝsuǝ lǝ kasɔ ka ntu letsyilama.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Nyaso bala kekue ka lɛkɔbe bitu bia lǝǝmuǝ ɔklɔ kǝnyǝnsǝ, nya bányankli kekue ka lɛkɔbe ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto nnya laate wǝ kusu lǝ ɔlɛ bɔyɛ. Nya bábesǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto mfia bo ufiebi leetsyi itǝ lǝ ufiebi ǝmǝ lǝ unǝ mǝ ɔyɛnko usuko.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Nyaso se ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto beyifoyifo léla bǝ lǝ betsyetsyi bedu esoo, bǝlǝkǝ ɔklɔ kɛkɛ ǝmǝ bǝtǝkǝ lǝ ntu ǝsuǝ nya báko bátɛyi bǝnkǝ bála bǝ babe lǝ ɔklɔ kplɛ ǝmǝ eto sɛtɔ sia bákpe bitu bia nkpe kalende ebofo ɔklɔ ǝmǝ bumuǝ kǝtsyǝ.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Nya se báte sikpi bǝnkǝ ufiebi ebɔsɔ utsyi ɔklɔ ǝmǝ usu ɔsɔ lǝ abansǝ ǝtsyuǝ ǝsuǝ esoo, báyani bitu bena bia nkpe kalende tinti lǝǝmuǝ ɔklɔ kǝńyǝnsǝ lǝ kalebe kani. Nya bǝńyǝ nya bákato ola kekleke bǝ kale lǝ kǝsiǝ mlǝ.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Paulo átɛyi bamamanyǝ ku mǝ uninǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ betidi bamfo mankɛ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmfo kafoo, biukpǝ.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Nyaso bamamanyǝ bamfo ála ukuǝ wǝ lɛkɔbe ɔklɔ kɛkɛ ǝmǝ ɔnlɛ, nya báyani wǝ bákpe lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nyaso nlɛ mi lekpakpa minkǝ, bidi lesatsyuǝ lǝ binyǝ ǝsuǝale itǝ bǝ biembukpǝ. Itsyise lɛ ɔsɔsɔ ɔni embusiǝ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Nyaso Paulo éfi didisa, nya se ɔ́sɔ Onanto ani lǝ mǝmblɛ eto ǝnǝmi mfóo, úbudi lesa nǝmǝ ditsyuǝ údi.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Lamfolamfoo, utsyuǝ saa eto utu ǝsiǝǝ kasɔ nya mǝmblɛ dítsyiko lesa nǝmǝ budi.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Nya se bédi bálosǝǝ, betidi bamǝ esi bǝlǝkǝ bidikǝ bia kenke díbu lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo bákpe lǝ ntu ǝsuǝ, itǝ bǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ átaka kalende. Ɔklɔ Ǝmǝ Ába Úsensǝ.

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ kale dísiǝ kakpaa, bǝnnyǝ lekpo kɛnyɛ bǝtsyǝ lekpo mfó. Fɛɛ bǝnyǝ ɔklɔ eto ɔnwɛkǝtsyǝkɔ kǝtsyuǝ lǝ lekpo kɛnyɛ mfó kaka beebu bǝ yɔɔlɛkɛ bǝ bayɛ lǝ besu bǝńyǝnsǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Nya se báyɛ bantɔɔ, ɔklɔ ǝmǝ ésu ɔ́si lǝ kɔwunimi ǝsuǝ itǝ bǝ wénsifo bɔyɛ bio. Nya se ufiebi tsya nlɛ bɔsɔ kǝkǝ wǝ kama esoo, óba úsensǝ kenke.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Nya se bamamanyǝ bamfo díbu bǝ mba bákpe lǝ Aba eto diyo bamǝ ebutsyetsyi beduu, bǝlǝkǝ nsusu bǝ lǝ basɔ mǝ balo.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se mǝ ɔtɔnkotɔnko ǝmfo léla nkǝ woafo Paulo lǝ nkpǝ esoo, wántɔnɔ ku mǝ. Nyaso útǝ kusu nkǝ nwǝ kee loofoo, lǝ basa bedufǝ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ bake babɔ.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Nya mba luubu tsyaa, lǝ bǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto kǝtsyǝ ku lesa nǝ kee loofo mǝ butsyi kayɛnko lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ kabɔsǝ kesuko utuflɔ. Lǝ nyaso bomblɛ éyifo nko nya bɔ́bɔ búsu lekpo kɛnyɛ mfó, bo kutsyuǝ saa ménkpǝ.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bɔ́bɔ bófe lǝ bulǝ bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, búsu bóte boǝnkǝ mfó ni kasɔ ka ntu letsyilama baakpo Malta.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Nya kasɔ kamfo eto betidi léfo bo kukɔnɔ. Lefe nǝmfo eyifo ɔsɔmi ku lɛyɔ lefe, nyaso bátsyaka ute kplɛ lǝ lekpo kɛnyɛ bǝtǝ bo nya báfo bo kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ Paulo nlɛ atɔni ǝtsyuǝ bɔsɛ lǝ ubǝ okpe lǝ ute ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ, owa utsyuǝ ko lɛbɔ lǝ atɔni nyamǝ nti itsyi lǝ ute ǝmǝ eto lɛyilabi eso, nya óbe ɔ́yɛ ókle Paulo eto kɔni.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Nya se mfó eto betidi dínyǝ bǝ owa ǝmfo faka lǝ wǝ kɔni ǝsuǝ, bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Utidi ǝmfo eyifo nwǝ laalo betidi. Nyaso itǝ bǝ ɔbɔ utsyi lǝ lekpo eto ukpǝ kǝmiǝ tsyaa, bo Ufiǝ ɔnukualɛ embɔtɔnɔ nkǝ usiǝ nkpǝ.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lɛwunya wǝ lǝ wǝ kɔni ókpe lǝ ute ǝmǝ kǝmiǝ nya kulesa saa ményifo wǝ tsya.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Nya betidi ba díńyǝ banlɛ wǝ bobee, díbu bǝ wɔɔfɔ nye wɔɔsɔ kasɔ ukpǝ lamfolamfo. Nya se bǝńyǝ bábe wǝ ɛklɛɛ fɛ kulesa mǝmbǝǝ, bétsyi mǝ nsusu lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ báwuninsǝ nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ ufiǝ utsyuǝ uni.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Nya kaka búsu bɔ́yifǝ mfóo, éyifo kasɔ ka lɛfi Oka Publio eto diyo. Nya oka ǝmfo tsya léfo bo ku disuǝyuǝ, nya óbe bo itǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfo tututu, Publio eto anto ánɔ bufi. Atikesi ǝmuǝ wǝ, nya ɔnlɛ kafo boto tsya. Nya se Paulo dísu wǝ ɔflɔ lǝ diyo nya óto ola nya úfi ani útǝkǝ lǝ wǝǝ, bufi bomǝ áyɛ wǝ.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Se bánɔ bǝ bufi bomǝ áyɛ wǝǝ, befimfi ba kenke nkpe lǝ kasɔ kamǝ mǝmblɛ ábɔ bǝbǝ, nya Paulo létsya mǝ.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Nya lǝ eyifosa nyamfo esoo, bátsyɛ bo asa kpǝ. Nya se lefe léyo bǝ budu mfoo, báboko bo asa kpǝ bǝ bufi bɔyɛ kusu. Bédu Lǝ Malta Bésu Roma.

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Búsiǝ mfó éfo afa ǝtsyǝ fɛ búdu. Lefe nǝ bɔnlɛ buluu, búsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ wǝ baakpo bǝnkǝ Bafimfi Eto Bǝfiǝ wǝ dítsyi Aleksandria wǝ díbǝ úńyǝ lǝ kasɔ kamfó lǝ lɛyɔ lefe nǝmǝ mfó.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Se búdu mfóo, umǝnkplɛ wǝ búsu búńyǝ ni Sirakusa. Nya búsiǝ mfo tsya eyi ǝtsyǝ.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Se búdu mfóo, bɔyɛ búsu búdufǝ Regio. Nya se kale dísiǝǝ, ufiebi sɛɛ etsyiko bɔsɔ ketsyi bo kama wǝ lɛwunsǝko bo, wǝ lékpa bo úsuko Puteoli lǝ diyi nuǝfǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Nya mfóo, búsu búnyǝ mba bafokanɔfɔ bǝtsyuǝ, nya bálɛ bo lekpakpa bǝ busiǝ lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ eyi akuanse. Lǝ nyamfo eto kama se búdu mfóo, búsu búdufǝ Roma.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Nya se bayimi ba nkpe lǝ Roma tsya lɛnɔ bǝ bokpe kusu bɔlɛ bubǝǝ, bǝtsyuǝ ba dítsyi faa ǝbǝ bákɔ lǝ bo lǝ Apia eto asi. Nya bǝtsyuǝ tsya díbǝ bákɔ lǝ bo lǝ kaka baakpo Nsɔnɔkɔ Ntsyǝ. Se Paulo dínyǝ mǝǝ, ɔsɔ Onanto ani, nya isi ékpe wǝ nkɛale enso. Bésu Bédufǝ Roma.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝ búsu búdufǝ Romaa, bǝtǝ Paulo kusu bǝ úsiǝ kaka woala. Nya bǝtǝ kusu bǝ bamamanyǝ bamǝ eto ɔni kase wǝ.

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ kamaa, Paulo ákpadi Yudafɔ eto batɔnkotɔnko ba nkpe lǝ mfó kenke bǝbǝ básɔ lebo. Se bátsyaa, ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔ bayimi, Yudafɔ ba nkpe lǝ Yerusalem dímuǝ mɛ nya befi mɛ bésu bǝtǝ Romafɔ eto Aba bǝnkǝ okponko mɛ lekpo lǝ lefe nǝ eto lefe nǝ ményifo bo Yudafɔ eto kutsyuǝ saa kulesa nye ńta bo banamǝ eto lekpa saa mbu.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Romafɔ bamfó ákponko mɛ lekpo nya bála bǝ lǝ bayani mɛ. Itsyisee, bǝnnyǝ lesa nǝ ǝsuǝ bɔɔyɛ balo mɛ nye balo mɛ dikpi fe kase Yudafɔ eto beninǝ ba dimuǝ mɛ lela.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Nya lefe nǝmfo se Yudafɔ bamfó dímantɔnɔ lǝ nyamfó ǝsuǝǝ, ɔ́tɔnɔ nkǝ ntɛyi mǝ lǝ nla miǝ bakpa mɛ lǝ nsu nnyǝ Okankplɛ Kaisare kosate ɔflɔ. Nya yenyifo mɔ lelabi miǝ maala bo kasɔ eto betidi kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso mínkpadi mi lǝ ndiko mi etiki nɛ. Itsyise Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ ini Israelfɔ kenke eto nwǝ banlɛ kusu bobee, wǝ ni nwǝ mimfo minɔ miǝ wǝ ni nwǝ eso baakpe mɛ lǝ amana ǝsuǝ nɛ.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Bo alɛɛ, bóamfo tɔ kulesa itsyi lǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya bóamfo kukue nye kɛnyɛ bua saa lǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ itsyi lǝ bayimi ba dítsyi Yudea bǝbǝ bo ɔflɔ.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bola boǝ boanɔ lesatsyuǝ itsyi lǝ lesa nǝ fáfo fánɔ mfo ǝsuǝ. Itsyise sintintii, baantɛyi kulesa sɛɛ sa itsyi lǝ ditsyukpa nǝmfo ǝsuǝ ku kalebe saa.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Nyaso bǝtsyǝ wǝ diyi lɔni, nya betidi kpǝ díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ. Mfo nya údi mǝ etiki itsyi lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ǝsuǝ. Nya ókpa mǝ nsusu úsuko kaka Moses ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi lɛwɔni asa bǝtsyǝ itsyi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ. Nya údi mǝ etiki idu lǝ olesiǝ isu ese lǝ ditsyitsyo lefe.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Nya betidi bǝtsyuǝ lǝ mǝ nti léfo wǝ bánɔ, fɛɛ bǝtsyuǝ tsya dímamfo lesa nǝ ɔ́tɛyi banɔ.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Nya etiki nyamfo léboko kǝnǝ lǝ mǝ nti. Nya se banlɛ busǝnsǝ keluu, Paulo átɛyi mǝ etiki fɛfɛ nǝmfo nkǝ, “Ɔnukualɛ uni bǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ lɛyɛ lǝ Yesaya ǝsuǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi bo banamǝ bǝnkǝ,

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: ‘Bisu bikatɛyi Yudafɔ bǝnkǝ, “Biɔnɔ fɛ biǝmbɔnɔ kasɔ, nya buinyǝ nya biǝmbunyǝ fɛ bisu bete.”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Itsyise betidi bamfo eto ketu kpe ale, nya mǝ ato etidi, nya béti mǝ ǝnǝmi tsya. Lenni nko innii, eni ele bǝ mǝ ǝnǝmi nnyǝ, nya mǝ ato tsya ebɔnɔ, nya mǝ nsusu tsya ebokpa kɛ̃ bǝ lǝ batsyi bawuninsǝ lǝ bǝbǝ mɔ ɔflɔ bǝ lǝ mɔ, Onantoo, lǝ ntsya mǝ.’ ”

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lélosǝ wǝ etiki nyamǝ nkǝ, “Ele bǝ ntɛyi mi lǝ bete biǝnkǝ, ditiki nǝ Onanto luufi ofo betidi lǝ nkpǝǝ, béfi ni bésuko mba lenni Yudafɔ. Nya mǝǝ, boofo ni banɔ!”

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo lɛkɛ mfoó alɛ ǝnuǝ lǝ diyo nǝ ófo úkafó kayo, nya úkafo betidi ba kenke ba díkǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Óbe Onanto eto sekakedikɔ letsya ku wǝ utu ku ǝsuǝale útǝ mǝ, nya óte mǝ asa nnya lɛyɛ lǝ Bonanto Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ. Nya kutsyuǝ saa ménti wǝ kusu.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Mɔ, Paulo, wǝ ni Yesu Kristo eto uyifoyifo, nya mini nwǝ bátsyese ku Onanto dílǝkǝ nkǝ mbé wǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ letsyaa, mɔ ni nwǝ lɛwɔni kukue komfo nɛ.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ ɔ́tɛyi wǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba úlǝkǝ útsyǝ nkǝ wuulǝkǝ ote kawunsiǝ fe kase báwɔni bákpe lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: Nya ɛyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto ǝlǝkǝ wǝ óte betidi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ nkǝ wǝ ni wǝ ubi wǝ ótakasǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Nya ɛyɛ lǝ Kristo ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto ǝnyǝ bo kaminsǝ nya úfi bo úyifo wǝ batɔnkpe itǝ Kristo bǝ lǝ butǝ kawunsiǝ eto betidi lǝ bafo wǝ banɔ lǝ beyifo wǝ lelabi.

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Nya mimba nkpe lǝ Roma tsya, eyifo betidi ba Onanto lékpadi nkǝ bibǝ befiani Yesu Kristo eto bale.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso minlɛ kukue komfo bɔwɔni kǝtǝ mi mba kenke nkpe lǝ Roma nya Onanto laala mi, nya ókpadi mi nkǝ, bibǝ befiani wǝ bale nɛ. Boanto Onanto ku Bonamute Yesu Kristo eto lefonǝ ku diyuǝ lǝ disiǝko mi. Paulo Ála Nkǝ Lǝ Ubǝ Unyǝ Mǝ.

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Ditutɛɛ, nyɛ lǝ Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ nlɛ mɔ Onanto ani bɔsɔ lǝ mi disi, itsyise kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke ánɔ kase béfo Onanto bɛnɔ.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Onanto wǝ eto keyifo meeyifo ku kafo faa, nwǝ eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ maabe letsya itsyi lǝ wǝ ubi ǝsuǝǝ, wǝ ni futsyǝ luulǝkǝ ote nkǝ maanta mi ǝsuǝ kulefe lǝ nlɛ ola boto nɛ.

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: Nya lefe saa nǝ minlɛ ola botoo, maalɛ Onanto lekpakpa minkǝ lǝ iyifo wǝ lelabii, lǝ nyǝ kusu lǝ mbǝ nyǝ mi lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Iyifo mɔ lelabi kekleke bǝ lǝ mbǝ nnyǝ mi lǝ nwunsǝko mi lǝ lɛwɔnɔ ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ binyǝ ǝsuǝale.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Lesa nǝ minlɛ bɔtɛyi mfo ni bǝnkǝ lǝ bokpe bo ǝsuǝ nkɛale lǝ lefe nǝmfo kemiǝ, lǝ mi bofokanɔ lǝ bokpe mɛ nkɛale, lǝ mintsya lǝ nkpe mi nkɛale.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Mɔ bayimi, nla miǝ bete biǝnkǝ iyifo mɔ ketsyue lǝ lefe kpǝ ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ mbǝ mi ɔflɔ, fɛɛ yǝntǝ mɛ kusu nko. Iyifo mɔ lelabi bǝnkǝ nwunsǝko betidi lǝ bafo Kristo banɔ lǝ mi nti fe kase mínyifo lǝ betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ bamba nti.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eso maabɛbɛ kekleke bǝ mbǝ ntɛyi mi mba nkpe lǝ Roma tsya Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo. Ǝsuǝale Nya Ditiki Sɛɛ Nǝmfo Nkpe.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ eto bɔtɛyi anlɛ mɛ kofani, itsyise iyifo Onanto eto ǝsuǝale nya lǝǝtǝ utsyuǝ saa wǝ laafokanɔ lǝ unyǝ nkpǝ. Yudafɔ ni betutɛ ba lɔɔnɔ fɛ mba lenni Yudafɔ lǝ bǝtǝkǝ.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo ǝǝlǝkǝ kusu sɛɛ ko lǝǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto lelabi kate. Nya kusu sɛɛ komfo etsyi lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ idu lǝ kasɔ ketsyikokɔ isu ese lǝ kalosǝkɔ. Lǝ fe kase báwɔni lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Utidi wǝ ke ni ɔsɛɛ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi ɛyɛ lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝǝ, wǝ hã luusiǝ nkpǝ nɛ.” Onanto Ǝǝnyǝ Ɔblɔ Lǝ Lebua Saa Eto Okle Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Onanto ǝǝlǝkǝ wǝ lɛblɔfi itsyi taa kato kate lǝ asa nnya dińyǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ ku asa bua eto eyifosa nnya leeti betidi kusu bǝ lǝ bayɛ ɔnukualɛ ǝmǝ kosate bote.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Wǝǝnyǝ ɔblɔ itsyise betidi ǝǝnyǝ Onanto eto asa nnya úyifo ketsye lekpo wuee. Itsyise Onanto kosate dílǝkǝ nnya wuee óte mǝ.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Itsyise idu lǝ lefe nǝ Onanto díyifo kawunsiǝǝ, asa nnya kenke úyifo utsyǝ lǝ kee, ǝǝlǝkǝ Wǝ ǝsuǝale manlo nnya boǝnnyǝ ku wǝ ditsyǝbi boǝnnyǝ ku bo ǝnǝmi kate. Nyaso kuutsyuǝ saa bio ǝmbofo bɔtɛyi nkǝ woamfo Onanto ɔnɔ.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Ite bǝ betidi yi Onanto tsyaa, beemfi dibu nǝ dińyǝ itǝ wǝ kǝtǝ wǝ. Nya baansɔ wǝ ani tsya. Lǝ nnya kɛnyɛɛ, asa bimba eto disi ete beebu. Nsusu sɛ saa ǝmbǝ mǝ nya mǝ nsusu leena tũũ.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Nya bǝǝnyǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ fe babesibe, fɛɛ bamumu beni.

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: Nya baayɛ Onanto wǝ ni nkpǝ kenke busumu, kefi atɛsǝ kǝtǝ asa bimba nnya lǝmbǝ nkpǝ. Beefi asa fe utidi nye baklɔbɛ nye bebuki akpa ana nye bebuki ba laakpi lǝ kasɔ fe owa eto okle kesumu bon.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, Onanto ǝlǝkǝ mǝ ani, nkǝ beyifo lesa nǝ kee baala. Nya beefi mǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ keyifo asinsa ku afanisa ku mǝ betsyuǝ.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Nya beefi bidiatɛyitɛyi kǝtsyǝ lǝ Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ eto kɛnyɛ. Bomu lǝ kedi bufi kǝtǝ Onanto eto kɛnyɛɛ, beefi ke kǝtǝ asa nnya Onanto ǝmǝ díyifo. Fɛɛ Onanto kosate wǝ dińyǝ utǝ atɛsǝ lefe saa, wǝ ele bǝ besumu nɛ. Ibǝ itǝ nko.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Asa nyamfo eto okle eto buyifo eso Onanto léyani betidi bamfó nɛ, nya beeyifo asa nnya ni mǝ sinǝ eto alasa nnya lǝǝnǝ mǝ kakpe lǝ afanisa ǝsuǝ. Nya besio kulaa tsya laayani basani ketsye kesu kalabeko mǝ besiotsyuǝ nnya lanyɛ lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Nya nko ke ini bǝ mǝ basani tsya laayani besio ketsye nya beeyifo afanisa nyamfo eto okle kalabeko bǝtsyuǝ fe sisǝ ku sisiǝ seka. Nya lǝ nyamfo esoo, Onanto ǝnǝ mǝ koto ko dińyǝ itǝ mǝ lǝ mǝ ditsyǝbi bua nǝ baate mfo eso.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Nya se báni bǝ bembokase asa nnya dińyǝ itǝ itsyi lǝ Onanto ǝsuǝ esoo, Onanto ǝlǝkǝ mǝ ani, nkǝ béyifo asa nnya dimalɛkɛ léfo mǝ nsusu kenke. Nya beeyifo asa nnya yanle bǝ beyifo.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Nya asa bua saa yifoyifo eto okle lefo mǝ nkpǝ kenke. Nya asa bua nyamǝ ni nsusu bua fifi, kunǝnkpǝ ku kabua eto bɔbɛbɛ kǝtǝ utsyuǝ. Nnya disi iyi mǝ nkpǝ ǝsuǝ ni kǝnǝnsɛ, betidilolo ku ekiti kpɔnkpɔ ku bidiatɛyitɛyi ku kekisi ku mǝ bǝtsyuǝ eto ketikimadidi.

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: Nya beedi etiki bua kabiasǝ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ eto aye. Beyifo betidi ba leekisi Onanto. Baaka bǝtsyuǝ, nya baabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato, nya baate ǝsuǝ. Nya baabɛbɛ kabua kǝtǝ bǝtsyuǝ lǝ esu kpǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya bǝǝmbu mǝ anto ku ambemǝ.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Beeyi bǝnkǝ lǝ Onanto eto afi ǝsuǝǝ, betidi ba lǝǝsiǝ nkpǝ nkoo, Onanto ebolo mǝ dikpi. Fɛɛ mfe bǝǝńyǝ kekleke lǝ mǝ asa bua nyamfo buyifo ǝsuǝ nɛ. Nya inni nyamfo mate, bomu beekeko betidi ba leeyifo asa nyamfo tsya.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Nyaso, mɔ ɔyimi, fǝ nlɛ betidi bamba lekpo bokponko? Niǝ, niǝ, niǝ inii, ǝmbofo bɔtɛyi fǝ, fǝ yɛ lǝ kusu sɛɛ ǝsuǝ. Lǝ álo fuutsyuǝ dikpii, nko ke foolo fǝ ǝsuǝ dikpi nɛ. Itsyise asa nnya beeyifoo, nnya okle ke fintsya leeyifo nɛ.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Buyi boǝ Onanto hã ni ɔsɛɛ nya okpe ǝsuǝale bǝ okponko betidi ba leeyifo asa nyamfo eto okle lekpo.

english_verse: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ fǝ mɔ ɔyimi, wǝ ni kawunsiǝ kamfo eto utidi wǝ leeyifo asa nyamfo eto kakle lɛ betidi bamba dikpi boloo, ebu fǝ ebofo butsyetsyi kedu lǝ Onanto eto lekpokponkpo ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Nye ebu fǝ bimba ete ini bǝ Onanto leeyifo fǝ kafoɛkpɛ nya onkpe dibusǝ ku disuǝtsyǝ itǝ fǝ? Enyi sitinti fǝ, Onanto eeyifo fǝ kafoɛkpɛ kamfo itsyise woala nkǝ woawunsǝko fǝ lǝ atsyi awuninsǝ.

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Nya se fetidi ato, fáni fǝ ǝmbɔtsyi fǝ lɛkɔɛsi awuninsǝ esoo, fǝ kosate dítǝ Onanto lǝǝbǝ fǝ koto bunǝ kon lǝ diyi nǝ ǝsuǝ wookponko kawunsiǝ kenke lekpo tsyɔntsyɔ. Diyi nǝmfo nya wuulǝkǝ wǝ lefonyi ote nɛ.

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: Nya Onanto loobofo utsyuǝ sa kayo lǝ wǝ keyifo ka úyifo kumǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Betidi ba laakpe otsyekle lǝ kasɛyifoyifo ǝsuǝ nya baabɛbɛ Onanto eto diyǝnde ku dibu ku nkpǝ manloo, Onanto ebutǝ mǝ nkpǝ manlo.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ betidi ba ini kǝdiǝ hã baabɛbɛ nya beentsyue ɔnuakualɛ ǝmǝ koto, nya baala asa bua buyifoo, Onanto ebunyǝ ɔblɔ okpe mǝ unǝ mǝ koto kekleke.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Betidi ba kenke difi asa buayifoyifo betsyi disii, ebudi ohiã nya boonyǝ kanyi. Yudafɔ luudi betutɛ, nya mba lenni Yudafɔ luutǝkǝ.

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mba leeyifo asa sɛɛ, Onanto ebutǝ mǝ kato eto diyǝnde ku dibu ku diyuǝsǝ. Yudafɔ luudi betutɛ, nya mba lenni Yudafɔ luutǝkǝ.

english_verse: God doesn't have any favorites!
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto ambe utidi eto ǝnǝmi fɛ woala etiki kǝlǝkǝ.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Betidi ba kenke dímante Mose eto afi fɛ béyifo abuaa, tsya ebukpǝ banya, ite bǝ Onanto embufi afi nyamǝ okponko mǝ lekpo tsyafɛ. Fɛɛ mba nyi Moses eto afi nyamǝ fɛ beeyifo asa buaa, Onanto ebokponko mǝ lekpo ɛyɛ lǝ afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ bote bǝ Onanto éfi asa nnya nkpe lǝ Moses eto afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝ ókpe lǝ mǝ akɔɛsi ǝsuǝ. Nya mǝ akɔɛsi tsya lɛ bote nko. Nya lefe ditsyuǝǝ, mǝ nsusu aalo mǝ dikpi nye ǝǝlǝkǝ mǝ kasɛyifoyifo kate.

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ aakpadi fǝ ǝsuǝ fǝ Yudanyǝ, nya faayɛ lǝ Moses eto afi ǝsuǝ, nya faabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato fǝ, fǝ eyifo Onanto eto ɔyɛtsyuǝ.

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: Eyi lesa nǝ Onanto laala, nya fenyi lesa nǝ ni lɛsɛ, fe kase fákase nnya lǝ kɔfi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: Eebu fǝ ǝsuǝ fe oteesu itǝ benǝmba nya feni lekpasǝ itǝ betidi ba nkpe lǝ kutuntu.

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: Efi fǝ ǝsuǝ eyifo nwǝ leekulǝsǝ mba lenyi kobe nya faate asa mba ǝnǝ dimankpa tɔ. Eyi kukɔnɔ fǝ kobe ku ɔnukualɛ sɔ̃ɔ̃ nkpe lǝ Moses eto aafi nyamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: Beso fǝ laate betidi bamba asa, fɛ faante fǝ ǝsuǝ asa nyamǝ? Aabe letsya fǝ benyu nya fǝ kosate enyuu?

english_verse: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
translation_verse: Aatɛyi fǝ bankpo kusiǝ nye kusǝ, fɛɛ fǝ kosate nkpǝ? Ǝfiǝsa aalɛ fǝ sɛsi, fɛɛ eeyu asa nnya beefi kakpa wǝ.

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Aabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato fǝ eyi Onanto eto afi, fɛɛ enyi fǝnkǝ, fǝǝ ke laata Onanto eto afi kebuu, ǝǝntǝ wǝ dibu?

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝǝ, bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Mi Yudafɔ eto eyifosa eso mba lenni Yudafɔ laakpadi Onanto diye bua.”

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Lǝ eeyifo lǝ afi nyamfo ǝsuǝǝ, fǝ lelolala eyifo lesa nǝ nkpe kutǝkǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ eenyifo lǝ afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nyalɛ lelolala nǝmǝ eyifo lesa bimba.

english_verse: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ utsyuǝ wǝ lenni Yudanyǝ, wǝ dímanla lelo eeyifo lǝ afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto ebunyǝ wǝ fe utsyuǝ wǝ lela lelo.

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, nwǝ lenni Yudanyǝ, fɛ weeyifo lǝ kɔfi komǝ ǝsuǝǝ, ebokpe kofani fǝ Yudanyǝ wǝ laata kɔfi ko báwɔni bǝtsyǝ kebu ite bǝ bála fǝ lelo tsyafɛ.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: Bomu utsyǝ ni Yudanyǝ tinti ni futsyǝ wǝ laate lǝ wǝ eyifosa ǝsuǝ nkǝ úfi wǝ lɛkɔɛsi útǝ Onanto. Inni nnya lɛyɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sia bála ɛyɛ lǝ kɔfikpekpe eto lekpa eto ditiki ǝsuǝ. Bomu nnya lɛyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝsuǝ. Nnya ini kawunsiǝ eto kuutsyuǝ wǝ laabesǝ utidi ǝmfo eto diye kato, bomu Onanto.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ite nkoo, nte kutǝkǝ ko Yudanyǝ wǝ bála lelo luunyǝ enso mba lenni Yudafɔ? Nte kutǝkǝ ko nke lǝ lelolala ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Lǝ esu kpǝ ǝsuǝǝ, kutǝkǝ kpe. Tutɛɛ, Yudafɔ eto ani nnya Onanto dífi wǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ ókpe.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ ibǝ bǝ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ mámfo bánɔ? Afo anɔ fǝ lǝ nyaso Onanto embuyifo lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ ka útsyǝ ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Kulefe! Lǝ ibǝ bǝ utsyuǝ saa eeyifo odia kulaa tsyaa, te fǝ Onanto eeyifo lǝ wǝ ditiki ǝsuǝ. Fe kase báwɔni lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝ nnya nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Lesa saa aate bǝ fǝ kɛnyɛ eto etiki eyifo ɔnukualɛ. Nya lǝ bakponko fǝ lekpoo, buutǝ fǝ lɛbɛ.”

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: Lǝ bo etikididi ǝsuǝǝ, boatɛyi boǝnkǝ bo abua nnya boeyifoo, ǝǝtǝ bǝ betidi nyǝ asa sɛɛ nnya Onanto leeyifo ketsye lekpoo, ntsyǝ ini nnya tsya? Lǝ nyasoo, bɔɔtɛyi boǝnkǝ yɛmbɔlɛkɛ bǝ Onanto nǝ bo kotoo? Kulefe! Fɛɛ nko bǝtsyuǝ leebu nɛ!

english_verse: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Onanto eni nwǝ leeyifo asa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝǝ, be ǝsuǝ wuunyǝ okponko kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke lekpo?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Bǝtsyuǝ aala bɔtɔ bǝ, “Fɛɛ lǝ mɔ bidia bia maatɛyi ǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto ɔnukualɛtiki kate wee, itǝ bǝ batɛsǝ wǝǝ, nyalɛ beso baalo mɛ dikpi bǝnkǝ utidi bua mini?”

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Lǝ bufi nnya nkoo, boofo bɔtɛyi boǝnkǝ, “Bǝtǝ lǝ bukeyifo asa bua lǝ asɛɛ lǝ ɛbɔ itsyi lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ.” Fɛɛ betidi bǝtsyuǝ lɛ bo abie busǝ kaka bo, nya banlɛ bɔtɛyi bǝnkǝ bo bomu nlɛ etiki budi nko. Betidi ba luufi nnya nkoo, bǝńyǝ itǝ bǝ Onanto lo mǝ dikpi. Betidi Ba Kenke Eeyifo Kabua.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, be boofo bɔtɛyi lǝ bomba ni Yudafɔ ǝsuǝ? Bɔtɛyi bɔnlɛ boǝ bo ale alɛkɛ eso mba lenni Yudafɔ eto alee? Kulefe! Itsyise mɔ́tɛyi ntɔnsǝ koko minkǝ kabua ébudi Yudafɔ ǝsuǝ ku mba lenni Yudafɔ kenke tsya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Betidi ba kenke etsyi kama bakpe Onanto, nya mǝmblɛ kenke ébota bébu. Nya kutsyuǝ saa ǝmbǝ wǝ leeyifo kasɛɛ. Lɛ ɔni kplon ǝmbǝ!

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: Mǝ akuɛlonlobi eyifo fe kudi ko lɛsini kɛnyɛ kunte. Nya mǝ kadiakami ńyǝ itǝ bidia bɔtɛyi. Nya mǝ nyɛ eyifo fe owa eto keni.

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: Nya mǝ nyɛ kenke diyi ekudi ku kalo kpinikpini.

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: Mǝ akpa aataka mlǝ mlǝ lǝ utidi eto bolo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
translation_verse: Bensi nkpǝ sɛɛ saa nya ini kanyinyǝnyǝ hã bǝǝnǝ kaboko.

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: Nya benyi kase ele bǝ bǝsiǝ nkpǝ lǝ diyuǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: Nya mǝ ditsyǝbi ante bǝ baate Onanto sikpi.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, buyi boǝnkǝ lesa saa nǝ bátɛyi lǝ Moses eto afi ǝsuǝǝ, bátɛyi nnya bǝtǝ betidi ba nkpe lǝ afi nyamǝ kalɔ, bǝ lǝ utsyuǝ saa lǝ ɔyɛ wǝ ǝsuǝ kɛnyɛ bulǝkǝ, bǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke kakɛ lǝ Onanto eto lekpokpo eto kalɔ.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: Nyasoo, kuutsyuǝ saa embuyifo ɔsɛɛ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi bǝ ɔyɛ lǝ afi ǝsuǝ eso. Bomuu, lesa nǝ afi nyamfo leeyifo ni bǝ yǝǝlǝkǝ kate utidi nkǝ uyifo obuayifo. Onanto Ku Kawunsiǝ Eto Betidi Eeyifo Bani Ɛyɛ Lǝ Kristo Bofokanɔ Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kusu ko ǝsuǝ Onanto lɛyɛ úlǝkǝ óte nkǝ betidi eyifo basɛɛ, mányɛ lǝ afi ǝsuǝ. Afi ku Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ǝlǝkǝ nyamfo báte wuee.

english_verse: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Onanto ǝtǝ nkǝ betidi ba léfo Kristo bánɔɔ, yifo basɛɛ. Úyifo nyamfo útǝ betidi ba kenke léfo Kristo bánɔ. Nya wóankpe kabakefe lǝ betidi nti.

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: Betidi baa tã eyifo kabua, nya bánnyi báfi Onanto eto diyǝnde blɛmblɛ kon.

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Onanto, ɛyɛ lǝ Kristo Yesu wǝ léfo kayo lǝ bo disi ǝtǝ nkǝ bofiani basɛɛ lǝ wǝ lefonǝ ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Úyifo nyamfo nkǝ lǝ ote bo lefe nǝmfo nkǝ wǝ kosate eyifo nwǝ diyifo ɔsɛɛ, nya weebu nwǝ léfo Yesu ɔ́nɔ tsya nkǝ wuuntsya eyifo ɔsɛɛ lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Nya be ǝsuǝ boofo buńyǝ kasɔ lɛkɔɛsi? Bumbǝ kulesa! Bǝ boayɛ lǝ afi ǝsuǝ eso lee? Owo, bomu ɛyɛ lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto laalɔ bonko wǝ eto nti.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Itsyise buyi wuee boǝ, ɛyɛ lǝ bo bofokanɔ ǝsuǝǝ, nya utidi laafiani ɔsɛɛ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi, nya inni lǝ wǝ bɔyɛ lǝ afi nyamfo ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Nyee, Yudafɔ hã ete ni Onanto eto bale? Nya unni mba lenni Yudafɔ tsya eto Onanto? Ee, uyifo mǝ tsya eto ole.

english_verse: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
translation_verse: Onanto ɔni kplo nkpe. Wǝ luubu Yudafɔ nkǝ beyifo basɛɛ ɛyɛ lǝ mǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ. Nko ke eso wuubu mba lenni Yudafɔ tsya basɛɛ, ɛyɛ lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Nyaso be nyamfo nlɛ bote? Bote ɛnlɛ bǝ bóyani afi nyamǝ butsyǝ bǝ lǝ bofokanɔ lǝ bofo busiǝ lǝ nnya kɛnyɛ? Kulefe! Inni nko, bomu bǝ lǝ bɔlɛ afi nyamǝ lǝ kɔni kekleke.

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Lǝ ɛyɛ lǝ keyifo ka úyifo ǝsuǝ eso Onanto dínyǝ nkǝ Abraham eyifo ɔsɛɛ, nyalɛ okpe keńyǝnkɔ woofo bɔtɛyi nko kasɔ lɛkɔɛsi. Fɛɛ umbǝ kǝńyǝnkɔ nko lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi, itsyise inni Abraham eto keyifo inni únyǝ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue kemiǝǝ, bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Abraham áfo Onanto ɔ́nɔ. Lǝ nnya eso Onanto ǝnyǝ nkǝ uyifo ɔsɛɛ.”

english_verse: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
translation_verse: Lǝ utsyuǝ eyifo keyifoo, wǝ kayofofo eni atsyɛsa, bomu iyifo lesa nǝ dińyǝ itǝ wǝ lǝ wǝ keyifo kamǝ eto kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
translation_verse: Fɛ lǝ utsyuǝ afo ɔnɔ nkǝ Onanto eeyifo utidi wǝ laante wǝ sikpi woafiani ɔsɛɛ, lǝ wóenyifo keyifo saa tsyaa, fɛ ini lǝ wǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ esoo, Onanto eefi wǝ fe ɔsɛɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Nko eso Oka David lɛtɛyi nkǝ utidi wǝ Onanto dibu nkǝ uyifo ɔsɛɛ, fɛ yányɛ lǝ wǝ keyifo ka úyifo esoo, uyifo utidi wǝ baakpo atsyɔ. Oka David átɛyi nkǝ,

english_verse: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
translation_verse: Onanto ákpo atsyɔ utidi wǝ eto abua Bonamute dímanwɔni útsyǝ wǝ blɛmblɛ.”

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Nya atsyɔkpokpo eto etiki Oka David lɛtɛyi mfoo, mba léla alo ete luunyǝ ni nnya? Kulefe! Eka mba dímanla alo tsya, itsyise Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Abraham áfo Onanto ɔ́nɔ, nyaso Onanto léfo wǝ nkǝ uyifo ɔsɛɛ nɛ.”

english_verse: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
translation_verse: Nya dimamfe ini Onanto dínyǝ wǝ nkǝ uyifo ɔsɛɛ nko? Lefe nǝ Abraham dímanla tɔ lelo lee, lee lefe nǝ úkala lelo eto kamaa? Inni lǝ lefe nǝ óla lelo. Bomu fɛ wóla lelo.

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Lelolala eto lekpa nǝ Abraham díyifo mfóo, eyifo lekpotsyǝtsyǝ nǝ laate bǝ Onanto áfo wǝ. Itsyise wǝ bofokanɔ ǝtǝ bǝ Onanto áfo wǝ nkǝ uyifo ɔsɛɛ fɛ óla lelo. Nya ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝǝ, Abraham afiani mba léfo bánɔ kenke eto anto. Nya Onanto áfo mǝ nkǝ beyifo basɛɛ lǝ lefe nǝ eto lefe bánla lelo kulaa tsya.

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: Abraham eyifo anto itǝ mba bála alo tsya. Fɛ inni fe kase bála alo eso, bomu bǝ besi nkpǝ lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ fe kase Boanto Abraham dísiǝ fɛ óla lelo eso. Onanto Átɛyi Kɛnyɛ Utsyǝ Útǝ Abraham.

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Onanto átɛyi kɛnyɛ utsyǝ útǝ Abraham ku wǝ kafo eto bebi nkǝ boodi kawunsiǝ kamfo eto bikǝ fe benake. Wǝ kɛnyɛ ka ɔ́tɛyi útsyǝ mfo manka kase Abraham lɛyɛ lǝ afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝ eso. Bomuu, ɔ́tɛyi utsyǝ nko itsyise ófo Abraham fe ɔsɛɛ ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ite bǝ mba leeyifo lǝ afi ǝsuǝ loofo Onanto eto kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ eto asaa, nyalɛ bofokanɔ mensi fe itǝ, ete kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ tsya ensi ǝmbɔkɛ nɛ.

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: Itsyise afi ǝǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto lɛblɔfi kate. Fɛ lǝ afi ǝmbǝǝ, nyalɛ kɔfi botakebu tsya ǝmbɔkɛ.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ kamfo aka bafokanɔfɔ nya ini kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ itǝ Onanto eto betidi nya ini wǝ lefonǝ nǝ úfi útǝ betidi ba kenke ni Abraham eto kafo eto betidi. Nya inni mba Onanto dítǝ afi mate hã ete ni wǝ kafo eto betidi. Bomu mba léfo bánɔ tsya fe Abraham. Itsyisee, Abraham eyifo mba léfo bánɔ kenke eto anto.

english_verse: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
translation_verse: Nya fe kase Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyii, “Mfi fǝ ntsyǝ ese kpǝ eto anto.” Nya kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ kamfo díbǝ itǝ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi. Onanto ǝmfoo, wǝ Abraham léfo ɔ́nɔɔ, kpe ǝsuǝale itsyise wǝ lootakasǝ bǝkpǝnkpǝ besinkli bǝbǝ nkpǝ. Nya wǝ lǝǝtǝ asa nnya dímankɛ kɔ lǝǝbǝ kakɛ!

english_verse: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
translation_verse: Ite bǝ kusubebe si kɔkɛ tsyaa, Abraham esi úkabe kusu kokoko, nkǝ woofiani ese kpǝ eto betidi eto anto fe kase Onanto lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Fǝ kafo eto bebi tsya ebufui kpenkpenkpe fe befandebi.”

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nyamfo mánlo wǝ ani lǝ wǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ. Bomu ɔ́kɔbe Onanto eto kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ ɔ́lɛ kekleke, nya ókpe nkɛale lǝ wǝ bofokanɔ bomfo ǝsuǝ nya úfi atɛsǝ útǝ Onanto.

english_verse: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
translation_verse: Nya óte wuee nkǝ Onanto kpe ǝsuǝale woofo lesa nǝ ke ɔ́tɛyi utsyǝ buyifo.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Nya inni wǝ mate ǝsuǝ ete báwɔni etiki nyamfo betsyiko bǝnkǝ, “Onanto ǝnyǝ nkǝ uyifo ɔsɛɛ.”

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
translation_verse: Bomu eka bomba tsya Onanto dínyǝ nkǝ bo tsya eyifo basɛɛ ɛyɛ lǝ kase bófo Onanto wǝ létakasǝ Bonamute Yesu utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ bɔ́nɔ eso.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Onanto ǝtǝ kusu bálo Kristo lǝ bo abua nnya búyifo eso. Nya Onanto létakasǝ wǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ bǝ lǝ wǝ Onanto lǝ unyǝ bo basɛɛ lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, se Onanto díyifo bo basɛɛ ɛyɛ lǝ bo bofokanɔ ǝsuǝ esoo, Onanto ǝtǝ diyuǝ édufǝ bonko wǝ nti ɛyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: Nya se bófo Kristo bɔnɔ esoo, útǝ nkǝ bofo Onanto eto lefonǝ nǝ ǝsuǝ bonkpe mɔmɔ. Nya bɔnlɛ disuǝyuǝ bunyǝ sitinti, itsyise bɔlɛ kusu bobe boǝ buunyǝ kesiǝkɔ lǝ Kristo ɔflɔ lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eto diyǝnde ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, boǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ bɔlɛ kanyi bunyǝ itsyise buyi boǝ kanyinyǝnyǝ kamfo eto okle lǝǝtǝ boǝńyǝ kekleke nɛ.

english_verse: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
translation_verse: Nya bo buńyǝ kekleke, ǝǝtǝ bo ditsyǝbi sɛɛ. Nyamfo luutǝ bǝ bunyǝ kusubebe.

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: Nya kusubebe komfo ǝntǝ boedi kofani. Itsyise Onanto áyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nǝ útǝ bo ǝsuǝ útǝ nkǝ wǝ lelabi ba didufǝ bo lǝ ǝsuǝ kenke.

english_verse: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ diyifole saa dímensi fo bo bɔkɛɛ, Kristo ǝbǝ lǝ lefe sɛɛ ǝsuǝ nya úbǝ úkpǝ lǝ bo babuayifo eto disi.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, iyifo lesa nǝ nkpe ale bǝ utsyuǝ kpǝ lǝ utidi wǝ ni ɔsɛɛ eto disi. Fɛɛ yoofo budi bǝ utsyuǝ ebofo bɔtɔnɔ nkǝ lǝ ukpǝ lǝ utidi wǝ lɛlɛkɛ eto disi.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Onanto ǝlǝkǝ óte bo wuee kase woala bo. Itsyise lefe nǝ bɔ́kɛ lǝ lebua ǝsuǝǝ, útǝ nkǝ Kristo kpǝ lǝ bo disi.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Nya se ɛyɛ lǝ Kristo eto kɔtɔ ǝsuǝ nya úyifo bo basɛɛ lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmii, ukpǝ ǝmfo ebulǝkǝ bo utsyiko lǝ Onanto eto lɛblɔfi kplɛ ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Onanto álɔ bonko wǝ nti ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ ubi eto ukpǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ lefe nǝ búyifo wǝ balontidi mfóo, nyalɛ lefe nǝ bonko wǝ eto nti díkalɛkɛɛ, Onanto ebunyǝ bo kaminsǝ, úlǝkǝ bo kulaa tsya oso itsyise Kristo kpe nkpǝ.

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Nya inni nyamfo mate ete, bomu buunyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti ku wǝ itsyise Onanto kosate, ɛyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo wǝ Onanto álɔ bonko wǝ eto sete nti eso. Nnya Ǝsuǝ Adam Nkpe Lete Lǝ Kristo Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
translation_verse: Lebua áyɛ lǝ utidi ɔni ǝsuǝ díbǝ kawunsiǝ, nya lebua nǝmǝ dínǝ ukpǝ léboko kawunsiǝ. Nyamfo esoo, ukpǝ lǝǝbǝ utsyuǝ saa lǝ ǝsuǝ. Itsyise utsyuǝ saa éyifo lebua.

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: Lebua akɛ lǝ kawunsiǝ fɛ Onanto dítǝ bo Moses eto afi. Fɛ lǝ kaka afi lǝmbǝǝ, Onanto anlo utidi dikpi.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ idu lǝ Adam lefe ibǝ Moses eto lefee, ukpǝ ákaso ani kawunsiǝ kenke eto betidi ku mba dímǝnyifo lebua fe kase Adam díyifo se wényifo lǝ Onanto eto afi ǝsuǝ. Nya Adam ǝmfo ńyǝ itǝ utidi wǝ lǝǝbǝ bubǝ.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mǝ inuǝ bamǝ menyifo bani. Itsyise Onanto eto kafo faa eto etǝsa so Adam eto lebua nǝ úyifo. Adam ni utidi ɔni wǝ eto lebua nǝ úyifo eso betidi kpǝ díkpǝ. Fɛɛ Onanto eto lefonǝ nǝ amɔ leso nya nko ke ini wǝ kafo faa eto ǝtǝsa itǝ betidi kpǝ tsya ɛyɛ lǝ utidi ɔni ǝsuǝ nɛ, nya wǝ ni Yesu Kristo nɛ.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Nsi nlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ ku asa nnya lɛbɔ itsyi abua nnya utidi ǝmfo díyifo mankate. Itsyise abua dinǝ lekpokponkpo ku dikpilolo eboko nɛ. Fɛɛ lǝ botakebu kpǝ eto kamaa, Yesu eto lefonǝ ǝtǝ bǝ betidi eyifo basɛɛ.

english_verse: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
translation_verse: Ɛyɛ lǝ utidi ɔni ǝmfo eto lebua ǝsuǝ nya ukpǝ dítǝkǝ betidi kato nɛ. Nko ke ɛyɛ lǝ Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝǝ, nya betidi ba kenke dínyǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ nǝ díyifo mǝ basɛɛ, bǝnyǝ nkpǝ manlo ku sekakedidi enso kenke nɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Nyasoo, se ɛyɛ lǝ lebua lɔni nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ bálo betidi kenke dikpii, nko ke yɔɔyɛ lǝ kasɛɛ kani ǝsuǝ Onanto luufi ɔtsyɛ betidi nya wuutǝ mǝ lɛbɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Nya ɛyɛ lǝ kase utidi ɔni ǝmfo létaka úńyǝ lǝ Onanto eto ditiki ǝsuǝ nya betidi kenke lefiani babuayifoo, nko ke ɛyɛ lǝ kase utidi ɔni ǝmfo dítsyue wǝ ditiki kotoo, Onanto ebutǝ betidi kpǝ fiani basɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Se afi nyamǝ díbǝǝ, itǝ bǝ betidi ǝnyǝ mǝ abuayifoyifo bǝtsyǝ lekpo. Nya se bǝnyǝ mǝ abua kpǝ nyamǝ bǝtsyǝ lekpoo, nko ke Onanto eto lefonǝ nye kaminsǝnyǝnyǝ ka úfi útǝ bo tsya leefui kakpe nɛ.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Nyaso kase lebua dítǝ ukpǝ dídi betidi ǝsuǝǝ, nko ke ini bǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ ebutǝ bǝnkǝ bofiani basɛɛ lǝ budufǝ lǝ nkpǝ manlo ɛyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ nɛ.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, be ele bǝ bɔtɛyi? Yɔɔlɛkɛ bǝ busukosǝ bukeyifo abua bǝ lǝ Onanto lǝ ukǝtǝ bo wǝ lefonǝ kawunsǝǝ?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Owo lo! Kulefe! Se búkpǝ itǝ lebua esoo, yensile bǝ butǝ lebua di bo ketu! Beso ele bǝ busi busiǝ nkpǝ ekeyifo lebua?

english_verse: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bófo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa ɛyɛ lǝ Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ buyifo bani ku wǝǝ, biyi biǝ ntu bo bǝflǝsǝ bo mfo ǝtǝ bǝ búyifo lɔni ku wǝ lǝ wǝ ukpǝ ǝsuǝ tsya.

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Nya ɛyɛ lǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ bomǝ esoo, ete bǝ bo tsya ǝkpǝ nya bébikǝ bo ku wǝ. Nya Onanto lɛyɛ lǝ wǝ diyǝnde ku ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ ótakasǝ Kristo utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ bǝ lǝ bo tsya lǝ botaka busiǝ nkpǝ fɔfɔ bomǝ ku wǝ.

english_verse: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
translation_verse: Nya se bonko wǝ díkeyifo bani lǝ wǝ ukpǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nko ke buyifo bani ku wǝ lǝ wǝ botaka ǝsuǝ nɛ.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Nya buyi boǝ básɔ bo ditsyǝbi koko nǝmǝ bámanklasǝ lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝ ku Kristo itǝ bǝ bo ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sia laala abuayifoyifo lǝ sɛtsylɔ kenke bǝ bɔyɛ ndomufɔ budi kǝtǝ lebua bio.

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ utidi ǝkpǝǝ, lebua ensiǝmbokɛ ǝsuǝale lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ bio.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ búkpǝ ku Kristo, bófo bɔ́nɔ boǝ buusiǝ nkpǝ ku wǝ tsya.

english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Buyi boǝ se Onanto létakasǝ Kristo utsyiko ɔkɔmǝǝ, woensiǝmbukpǝ bio. Ukpǝ ensibǝ ǝsuǝale lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ bio.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Kristo eto ukpǝ wǝ úkpǝǝ, úkpǝ itǝ sɔni hã bǝ lǝ lebua ensiembutǝkǝ kuutsyuǝ saa kato. Nya nkpǝ bo unsi mɔmɔɔ, usi mu ku Onanto.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Nya nko ke ele bǝ itsyǝ nɛ bǝ, mi tsya ǝkpǝ nya yanle bǝ lebua si ditǝkǝ mi kato. Nyaso ele bǝ bisiǝ nkpǝ bitǝ Onanto lǝ mi lɔniyifoyifo ku Kristo Yesu ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Nyaso biǝntǝ lǝ lebua fo lekpe lǝ mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sia lǝǝbǝ bukpǝ mfo ǝsuǝ, bǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ lǝ sitǝkǝ mi kato lǝ asa bua buyifo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Nye biǝntǝ kusu lǝ abua lǝ idi mi utu lǝ ǝsuǝ eto uyi eto kukalebe saa lǝ lebuayifoyifo ǝsuǝ. Bomu bifi mi ǝsuǝ bitǝ Onanto fe betidi ba Onanto dílǝkǝ utsyiko lǝ ukpǝ kemiǝ óboko nkpǝ ǝsuǝ. Beyani mi ǝsuǝ eto kalebe saa bitǝ Onanto fǝnkǝ mba bǝlǝkǝ betsyiko ukpǝ kɛnyɛ baboko nkpǝ. Beyani mi ǝsuǝ bitǝ Onanto lǝ ufi mi uyifo asa sɛɛ eto keyifo.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Yanle bǝ lebua si ditǝkǝ mi kato bio diyifo mi eto onamute, itsyise binsibǝ lǝ afi kalɔ. Bomu bekpe lǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ kalɔ. Bifi Mi Ǝsuǝ Bitǝ Asasɛɛ Buyifo.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Nya be bɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi? Bǝ bunsibǝ lǝ afi kalɔ itsyise bokpe lǝ Onanto eto lefonǝ kalɔ eso, nyaso ele bǝ bukeyifo abua? Owo lo. Inni nko ini!

english_verse: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
translation_verse: Biyi sintinti biǝ, lǝ bifi mi ǝsuǝ bekpe lǝ nwǝ eto keyifo bieyifo kalɔɔ, nyalɛ biyifo utidi ǝmǝ eto beyifoyifo nye ndomufɔ. Lǝ lebua eto sele bianɔɔ, nnya laabɔ ketsyiko mfó ni ukpǝ. Lǝ Onanto eto sele bianɔɔ, nya laabɔ ketsyi mfó ni kasɛɛ yifoyifo.

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Boasɔ Onanto ani, bǝ lǝ lefe nǝ léfe mǝ ǝsuǝǝ, bíyifo babuayifo, nya mɔmɔɔ, bifi mii lɛkɔɛsi kenke béfo Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ mínte mi.

english_verse: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
translation_verse: Nya bɛbɔ lǝ lebua ǝsuǝ, béfloma nya biyifo ndomufɔ itǝ asasɛɛ buyifo.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Nlɛ mi etiki budi ɛyɛ lǝ kase betidi leedi ǝsuǝ, lǝ nlǝkǝ nyamfo kasɔ nte mi lǝ befo nnya kasɔ bɔnɔ. Lǝ lefe nǝ léfe mmǝǝ, béyani mi ǝsuǝ nya béfiani ndomufɔ bitǝ abua buyifo ku asa nnya dimankpa ku disiale eto asa bua saa eto okle buyifo. Nko ke ini bǝ mɔmɔɔ, bífi mi ǝsuǝ bífianisǝ ndomufɔ bitǝ asa sɛ buyifo nya ete bǝ biyifo mba leeyifo asa kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ bíyifo ndomufɔ itǝ lebua, yánkle mi bǝ biyifo kasɛɛ.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Nya nte kutǝkǝ ko bínyǝ itsyi lǝ asa nnya bíkeyifo mfó nnya díyifo mi afanisa mɔmɔ? Asa nnya, nyamfó kenke lǝǝnǝ kaboko ete ni ukpe.

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, Onanto ǝlǝkǝ mi utsyiko lǝ lebua eto ani, nya bíbǝ bíyifo wǝ ndomufɔ. Kutǝkǝ ko bínyǝ se bífi mi ǝsuǝ bítǝ wǝ kenke nya bíyifo kɛnkɛ ni bǝ, lǝ kalosǝkɔɔ, buinyǝ nkpǝ manlo.

english_verse: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_verse: Lebua eto kayofofo ni ukpǝ. Fɛɛ Onanto eto ǝtǝsa nnya wǝǝtǝ bo kafo faa ni nkpǝ bo lǝmbǝ kalosǝkɔ ɛyɛ lǝ Kristo Yesu wǝ ni Bonamute eto lɔniyifoyifo ku bo ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
translation_verse: Sitintii, mɔ bayimi, bɛnɔ nnya mǝǝbǝ bɔtɛyi mfo eto kasɔ, itsyise mimblɛ yi afi nyamǝ. Afi aakle utidi wǝ nkpe lǝ nnya kalɔ lǝ lefe nǝ unsi nkpǝ hã ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
translation_verse: Tǝ mfi nyamfo nte ditsyǝbi nǝmǝ. Usio wǝ nsi kusǝǝ, sisǝ ku sisiǝ eto kɔfi aakle wǝ kamanklasǝ lǝ wǝ usǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ lefe nǝ osani ǝmǝ nsi nkpǝ ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ osani ǝmǝ ǝkpǝǝ, kɔfi komǝ mensikle usio ǝmǝ.

english_verse: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
translation_verse: Nyaso lǝ usio ǝmfo esu usiǝ ko osani bamba lǝ lefe nǝ wǝ usǝ nsi nsi nkpǝǝ, betidi ebɔtɛyi bǝ okpo kusǝ. Fɛ lǝ usǝ ǝmǝ ǝkpǝǝ, kɔfi komǝ mensikle wǝ. Nya onkpe kusu wǝǝsiǝ kusǝ bamba nya wǝmbudi usǝnkpo.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Mɔ bayimi, nko ke ini itǝ mi tsya nɛ. Itsyise lǝ afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝǝ, ɛlɛ bote bǝnkǝ bíkpǝ ku Kristo itsyise biyifo Kristo eto lekpà ditsyuǝ, nya biefiani mi bǝtsyuǝ eto bale. Nya mɔmɔɔ befiani wǝ, nwǝ bátakasǝ betsyiko ɔkɔmǝ eto ole, bǝ lǝ biyifo keyifo sɛ ka luubǝ kutǝkǝ itǝ Onanto.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝ búsiǝ nkpǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo eto nkpǝ eto lɛkɔɛnde ǝsuǝǝ, lebua eto lɛkɔɛnde nǝ kɔfi lǝǝnǝ bo ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ kakpee, ábesǝ bo abuayifoyifo kato ekpe. Nya asa nnya lɛbɔ lǝ nkpǝ bomfó siǝsiǝ ǝtǝ bo ukpǝ.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Fɛ mɔmɔɔ, se búkpǝ nya bɔ́bɔ lǝ asa nnya lékle bo itsyǝ esoo, afi nyamǝ ensibǝ ǝsuǝale lǝ bo ǝsuǝ. Lǝ nnya eso boensiembuyifo kulesa ɛyɛ lǝ kusu koko ku ko kɔfi ǝsuǝ. Bomuu, bo eyifosa ebɔyɛ lǝ kusu fɔfɔ ko laayɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝsuǝ. Afi Ǝtǝ Bǝ Bunyǝ Lebua Butsyǝ Lekpo.

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ nyamfo esoo, be ele bǝ bukatɛyi? Boǝ, nyalɛ afi nyamǝ ǝbǝ kɔnɔɔ? Inni nko ini! Lǝ sitintii, ɛyɛ lǝ afi nyamfo ǝsuǝ nya boǝnyǝ lesa nǝ ni lebua ketsye lekpo nɛ. Lǝ kɔfi komfo ete dímantɛyi nkǝ, “Mansɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ utidi bamba eto asa ǝsuǝǝ,” eni yoofo budi bǝ míntante kase basɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ sas ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ɛyɛ lǝ kɔfi komǝ ǝsuǝǝ, lebua ǝnyǝ kusu lesanklisǝ kǝnǝnsɛ saa eto okle lǝ mɛ ǝsuǝ. Nya lǝ kɔfi saa ǝmbǝǝ, eni lebuayifoyifo tsya ǝmbɔkɛ ǝsuale saa.

english_verse: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
translation_verse: Mɔ kosate ǝsiǝ nkpǝ bomfo eto okle lǝ lefe nǝ léfe mmǝ nǝ mánte afi nyamǝ. Fɛ lefe nǝ mímbǝ mínte afi nyamǝ nya yénsitǝ mɛ kusu bǝ nyifo asa kpǝǝ, abuayifoyifo tsya átaka íńyǝ lǝ mɛ ǝsuǝ nya mínyǝ miǝ nkpǝ.

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: Nya afi nnya eni ele bǝ itǝ mɛ nkpǝǝ, nnya bomu leboko mɛ ukpǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Nya ɛyɛ lǝ afi ǝsuǝǝ, abuayifoyifo ǝnyǝ kusu ɛwɛ mɛ ekpe nya élo mɛ.

english_verse: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, afi alɛɛ, lesa kɛnkɛ ini fɛ, nya asa nnya yaate tsya eyifo kɛnkɛ, nya ɛnyɛ lǝ kusu sɛ ǝsuǝ, nya ɛlɛkɛ.

english_verse: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
translation_verse: Nyalɛɛ, bɔtɛyi bɔnlɛ boǝ lɛsa sɛ nǝmfo ni nǝ léboko mɛ ukpǝ? Inni nko ini! Bomu bǝ lǝ bunyǝ butsyǝ lekpo boǝ lebuayifoyifo eyifo lesa bua esoo, lebuayifoyifo ayɛ lǝ lesa nǝ nkpe kɔnɔ ǝsuǝ nya dínǝ ukpǝ léboko. Nya ɛyɛ lǝ afi nyamfo ǝsuǝǝ, lebuayifoyifo ǝlǝkǝ ni ditsyǝbi kosate léte. Lema Nǝ Nkpe Lǝ Ǝsuǝ Eto Sinǝ Ku Lɛwɔnɔ Lenti.

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Bomblɛ yi boǝ Kɔfi eyifo lɛwɔnɔ eto lesa. Fɛɛ nkpǝ bo minsi eyifo kawunsiǝ kamfo eto utidi eto nle, nya lebuayifoyifo diyi mɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Maamfó asa nnya meeyifo kasɔ bɔnɔ. Itsyise lesa nǝ maala miǝ lǝ nyifoo, inni ni meeyifo. Bomu asa nnya meekisi sitintii, nnya ni nnya meeyifo nɛ.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ asa nnya maanla miǝ meeyifo nnya ni nnya meeyifoo, nyalɛ ɛlɛ bote bǝ ntɔnɔ miǝ afi nyamfo alɛkɛ.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyasoo, ɛlɛ bote bǝ inni mɔ kosate nlɛ lesa nǝmfo buyifo, bomu lebua nǝ nkpe lǝ mɛ ni nnya eso meeyifo nko.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: Nya nyi lǝ mɔ kosate eto ǝsuǝ kemiǝ miǝǝ, lesa sɛɛ saa ǝmbǝ lǝ mɛ. Nya nlɛ nyamfo bɔtɛyi itsyi lǝ mɔ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto ditsyǝbi koko nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ eso. Itsyise maala sitinti miǝ ele bǝ eni nyifo kasɛɛ, fɛɛ meenyifo nko.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Nyaso lǝ meeyifo asa nnya maanla miǝ meyifoo, inni mɔ kosate lǝǝtsyǝ keyifo nnya. Bomuu, lebua nǝ nkpe lǝ mɛ leeyifo nnya.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, nsu nnyǝ lesakpa ditsyuǝ ntsyǝ lekpo. Lǝ nla miǝ meeyifo kasɛɛ, lebua hã meeyifo kekleke.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Lǝ kusu bambaa ǝsuǝǝ, lǝ mɔ utu kemiǝ sitintii, Onanto eto afi aakpe mǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti.

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nnyǝ miǝ asa bamba nnya laabɔ ketsyi lǝ mɛɛ, aakpɔ lema ku lesa nǝ mɔ nsusu laate mɛ nkǝ ɛlɛkɛ. Nya lǝǝtǝ abuayifoyifo lɛbɔ lǝ mɛ nya yeeti mɛ kusu fe nwǝ bákpe lǝ diyo.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Mɔ ǝsuǝ kpe kaminsǝ tinti. Nya owoe luulǝkǝ mɛ utsyiko lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto abua nya kɔ, nya laakpa mɛ kesuko ukpǝ kemiǝ mfo?

english_verse: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_verse: Maasɔ Onanto ani, itsyise nnyǝ bulǝkǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo ǝsuǝ! Nya mɔmɔɔ, lǝ mɔ nsusu ǝsuǝǝ, nyifo nwǝ leeyifo lǝ Onanto eto afi ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ mɔ lǝ utidi ǝmǝ eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ǝsuǝǝ, meeyifo lǝ lebua eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, mba nsi nkpǝ lǝ lɔniyifoyifo ku Kristo Yesu ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto embolo mǝ dikpi.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: Itsyise ɛyɛ lǝ Kristo Yesu ǝsuǝǝ, afi nnya nyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nǝ lǝǝtǝ betidi nkpǝ, ǝtǝ nkǝ mbɔ lǝ afi nnya lǝǝnǝ utidi kakpe ku ukpǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
translation_verse: Lesa nǝ kɔfi ko Onanto dítǝ Moses dímamfo buyifo, se utidi lɛyɔkpɔ nya wóanfo ko budi esoo, nnya Onanto díyifo mɔmɔ nɛ. Ótsyese wǝ ubi fǝnkǝ utidi wǝ nkpe lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ǝsuǝ sia lebua laakpɔ nko. Úbǝ lǝ betidi eto abua eto disi nya úfi wǝ ǝsuǝ útǝ fe kedi, nya útǝkǝ lebua kato nya úsensǝ lebua nǝ nsi lǝ bo eto ǝsuǝale.

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: Onanto éyifo nyamfo útǝ bo mba nsi nkpǝ lǝ kase Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ laala, inni lǝ kase ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto ditsyǝbi koko nǝmǝ laala. Úyifo asa nyamfo kenke bǝ lǝbusiǝ nkpǝ lǝ kusu sɛɛ ǝsuǝ fe kase wǝ afi lete.

english_verse: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
translation_verse: Betidi ba nsi nkpǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ eto ditsyǝbi fe kɔɔ, lǝ kase baalaa, mǝ nsusu si buteke lǝ asa nnya mǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ laala ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ mba nsi nkpǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝsuǝǝ, mǝ nsusu teke lǝ nnya Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ laalaa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Utidi wǝ eto nsusu nteke lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto asa ǝsuǝǝ, wǝǝnyǝ ukpǝ. Fɛɛ utidi wǝ eto nsusu nteke lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝsuǝǝ, wǝǝnyǝ nkpǝ manlo ku diyuǝsǝ.

english_verse: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
translation_verse: Mba dítǝ bǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ tǝkǝ mǝ katoo, baamfo Onanto eto lelabi buyifo.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Lǝ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ afo disiǝ lǝ mi ǝsuǝǝ, nyalɛ binsisi nkpǝ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto lɛkuɛnde ǝsuǝ, bomu fe kase Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ laala. Nya lǝ Kristo eto Lɛwɔnɔ mamfo disiǝ lǝ utsyuǝ ǝsuǝ, nyalɛɛ unni Kristo eto ole.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔ lǝ wǝ Kristo afo disiǝ unsi lǝ mii, mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ebukpǝ lǝ lebua eso. Fɛɛ mi lɛwɔnɔ ebusi disiǝ nkpǝ itsyise Onanto eyifo mi basɛɛ eso.

english_verse: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ nǝ létakasǝ Yesu ditsyiko ɔkɔmǝ si lǝ mi ǝsuǝǝ, wǝǝ létakasǝ Kristo Yesu utsyiko ɔkɔmǝǝ, wǝ ni futsyǝ luutǝ mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sia luukpǝ mfoo, nkpǝ, ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ Lɛwɔnɔ nǝ nsi nkpǝ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
translation_verse: Lǝ bitǝkǝko bisiǝ nkpǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto lelabi ǝsuǝǝ, biukpǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ bɛyɛ lǝ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ ǝsuǝ belo mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto ditsyǝbi bua nǝmǝǝ, buisiǝ nkpǝ manlo.

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: Itsyise betidi ba Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ laayɛnkoo, mǝ ni Onanto eto bebi nɛ.

english_verse: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
translation_verse: Onanto mǝntǝ mi ndomufɔ eto lɛwɔnɔ nǝ luutǝ bikate sikpi. Bomu útǝ mi Lɛwɔnɔ nǝ ni bebi eto lele nya diǝtǝ boakpadi Onanto boǝ, “Abba” eto kasɔ ni, “Nto lala.”

english_verse: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
translation_verse: Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ ku bo awɔnɔ kenke lɛkɔ iyifo kɛnyɛ ka nlɛ bote bǝ buyifo Onanto eto bebi.

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: Nya lǝ buyifo wǝ bebii, nyalɛ buyifo mba leedi wǝ bikǝ. Nya bo ku Kristo ebudi Onanto eto bikǝ bɔkɔ. Nya fe kase búnyǝ kanyi fe Kristo, nko ke bo tsya luuyǝnle fe wǝ nɛ. Buunyǝ Disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
