english_verse: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
translation_verse: Itsyise bisi bisi nkpǝ fe betidi ba difi mǝ nsusu bǝtǝkǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo hã eto asa ǝsuǝ. Nya lǝ kenǝmisɛ si kakpe lǝ mi nti nya biǝdiǝ nniǝ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nyamfo mate kulaa ate nkǝ bisi bisi nkpǝ itǝ kawunisiǝ kamfo eto asa.

english_verse: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
translation_verse: Lǝ mi nti utsyuǝ esi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔɔ, Paulo eto kama mintǝkǝ,” nya obamba tsya nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔɔ, Apolo eto kama mintǝkǝ,” bieyifo asa fe betidi ba kawunsiǝ kamfo eto eyifosa léfo disiǝ dinsi lǝ mi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ nyamfo kenke kamaa, owoe ni Apolo? Nya owoe ni Paulo? Onanto eto beyifoyifo ete buni. Nya bɔ́yɛ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ lǝ bofo bɔnɔ. Nya bo nti eto utsyuǝ saa nlɛnko keyifo ka Onanto dítǝ wǝ nkǝ uyifo.

english_verse: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
translation_verse: Mɔ ni dítsyiko keyifo kamǝ kasɔ fe kase beetsyi diyibibi. Nya Apolo lékpo ni ntu. Fɛɛ Onanto ni nwǝ dítǝ lébe lɛbɔ disi.

english_verse: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, utidi wǝ dítsyii, ku utidi wǝ lékpo ni ntuu, mǝmblɛ ményifo kulesa kinki. Nya Onanto bomu ni nwǝ díyifo, itsyise wǝ ni nwǝ dítǝ lébe lɛbɔ disi.

english_verse: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
translation_verse: Bobakefǝ kubutsyuǝ saa ǝmbǝ lǝ utidi wǝ leetsyi kuyi ku nwǝ laakpo ko ntu nti. Onanto ni futsyǝ wǝ loofo mǝ kayo lǝ mǝ keyifo eto kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
translation_verse: Itsyise buyifo beyifotsyuǝ lǝ keyifo kani ǝsuǝ. Nya boakɔ ani keyifo ke kǝtǝ Onanto. Nya biyifo Onanto eto ɔkuɛ wǝ ǝsuǝ bunyǝ bɔnlɛ keyifo. Nya bini diyo nǝ tsya Onanto léto.

english_verse: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Onanto eto kǝtǝ ka útǝ mɛ ǝsuǝǝ, ǝtǝ mfiani fe diyo eto otonto sɛɛ. Nya mintsyǝ diyo nǝmǝ kasɔ lǝ leba ǝsuǝ. Nya utidi bamba díbǝ ɔnlɛ boto kǝtǝkǝ lǝ ni. Fɛɛ utsyuǝ saa wǝ dibǝ ɔnlɛ boto kǝtǝkǝ, ele bǝ ɔlɔ obe kase ɔnlɛ diyo nǝmǝ boto kayɛnko.

english_verse: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ diyo nǝ utsyuǝ saa léto alɛkɛ nye dianlɛkɛɛ, Kristo eto disumuyo nǝmǝ ni nnǝ luulǝkǝ lete nɛ. Itsyise lǝ disumuyo nǝmǝ ǝsuǝǝ, ute buufi bǝlǝkǝ utsyuǝ saa eto keyifo lǝ lekple bate. Ute ǝmfo ni nwǝ luubǝ ukedi keyifo saa obe, lǝ obe lǝ keyifo kamǝ alɛkɛ.

english_verse: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
translation_verse: Lǝ utsyuǝ eto bikǝ bia úfi óto diyo nǝmǝ ǝńyǝǝ, lǝ ute áto ɔ́yɛ mfo ófee, otonto ǝmfó ebofo kayofofo.

english_verse: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ utsyuǝ eto bikǝ bia úfi óto diyo nǝmǝ ato utee, wǝmbofo kayofofo saa. Fɛɛ wǝ kosate ebunyǝ bulǝkǝ fe nwǝ dítsyetsyi ɔ́bɔ lǝ ute ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ. Mi Kosate Eyifo Onanto Eto Disumuyo Kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
translation_verse: Ele bǝ bete lǝ ekpako mi wuee biǝ mi kosate eyifo Onanto eto disumuyo. Nya Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ nkpe lǝ mi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto eto disumuyo eyifo kakɛnkɛkɔ, nya mi ni wǝ disumuyo nǝmǝ. Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ ata Onanto eto disumuyo nǝmǝ ubuu, Onanto ebɔtsylɔsǝ wuntsya.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
translation_verse: Kuutsyuǝ saa manwɛ wǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ mi nti. Lǝ utsyuǝ lǝ mi nti eebu wǝ ǝsuǝ fe kawunsiǝ kamfo eto obesibee, ufi wǝ ǝsuǝ uyifo ubuki bomu lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmi lǝ itǝ bǝ woofiani obesibe tinti lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
translation_verse: Itsyise lesa nǝ kawunsiǝ eto betidi leebu fe kobetetee, nyamfó eyifo ebukisa lǝ Onanto eto ǝnǝmi. Nyaso Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Babesibe eto asayifoyifo ǝsuǝ nya Onanto laayɛ kemuǝ mǝ lǝ kubu ǝsuǝ nɛ.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
translation_verse: Nya besi báwɔni bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Onanto yi nkǝ babesibe eto nsusu bufi manle kulesa saa kon.”

english_verse: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
translation_verse: Paulo nye Apolo nye Petro nye kawunsiǝ kamfoo, nye ukpǝ ku nkpǝǝ, mɔmɔ nye lefe nǝ luubǝǝ, asa nyamfo kenke eyifo mi ale.

english_verse: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
translation_verse: Nyaa, biyifo Kristo eto bale. Nya Kristo tsya diyifo Onanto eto ole.

english_verse: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa kǝǝnyǝ bo fe Kristo eto beyifoyifo ba béfi Onanto eto etiki kulakula lǝ lefe nǝ léfe mmǝǝ, bákpe kɔni bǝ bɔlɛ nya ola.

english_verse: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
translation_verse: Nya lesa nǝ lefé itǝ nwǝ laabe Boanto Onanto eto asa ǝsuǝ ni bǝ úyifo keyifo kamǝ lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
translation_verse: Lǝ mɔ kutsyee, yǝnlǝ mɛ bǝ kuutsyuǝ kponko mɛ lekpo lǝ mɔ ditiki ǝsuǝ nye ɛyɛ lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo eto kobe ǝsuǝ unyǝ nkǝ nta mbu. Maankponko mɔ ǝsuǝ lekpo kulaa tsya lǝ ditiki nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
translation_verse: Mɔ lɛkɔɛsi anlo mɛ dikpi saa. Fɛɛ nyamfo anlɛ bote nkǝ meenyifo kabua saa, eso mbɔbɔ mfe lǝ kabua ku lǝ mɔ etikididi kemiǝ. Itsyisee, Bonamute wǝ loofo mɛ lekpo bokponko nɛ.

english_verse: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
translation_verse: Nyaso yanle bǝ kuutsyuǝ saa kponko wǝ utsyuǝ lekpo isu ese lefe nǝ Bonamute Yesu eto dibǝfe nǝmǝ leyo. Lǝ ubǝǝ, wǝ ni futsyǝ lookponko utsyuǝ saa lekpo nɛ. Nya wǝ ni nwǝ luulǝkǝ asa bua kenke nnya lɛkula lǝ lekple ote nya wuulǝkǝ asa bua nya betidi nlɛ bubu tsya ote. Mfo nya Onanto lɔɔtɛsǝ utidi saa dińyǝ itǝ atɛsǝ.

english_verse: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
translation_verse: Mɔ bayimi, bǝ lǝ befo asa nnya bɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi ɛyɛ lǝ mɔ ku Apolo ǝsuǝ kasɔ bɔnɔɔ, nwɔni nyamfo bǝ lǝ bebe bekase. Nya lǝ bekase asa nnya eto kasɔ etiki nyamfo nlɛ bulǝkǝ kate nya báwɔni bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bikeyifo asa bikayɛ lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ tlolololo.” Lǝ nyamfo esoo, biembu betidi bǝtsyuǝ bakplǝnyǝ beso babamba.

english_verse: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
translation_verse: Owoe díyifo fǝ nya fankpǝ kutǝkǝ fanso fuutsyuǝ? Asa nnya nkpǝ fǝ kenkee, Onanto ni nwǝ dífi útǝ fǝ. Lǝ nko intee, beso faabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato fenkǝ inni Onanto ni wǝ dítǝ fǝ asa nnya nte fǝ.

english_verse: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
translation_verse: Lee asa nnya bela biǝ biakɛɛ, bíuńyǝ nyamblɛ kokoo? Nye batabinyǝ bíkafiani? Lee bíofiani baka lǝ lefe nǝ eto lefe bo lenni tɔ baka? Ɛlɛkɛ! Eni seka ele bǝnkǝ bikedi pɛfɛ bǝ lǝ bo tsya lǝ budi se ku mi!

english_verse: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
translation_verse: Itsyise ele mɛ fenkǝ bo mba ni batɔnkpee, kesiǝkɔ ka Onanto dífi utǝ bo ni kafɛfɛkɔ. Nya buyifo fe betidi ba bálo dikpi bǝ lǝ balo mǝ lǝ mba kenke eto ǝnǝmi. Nya buyifo abesa nnya kawunsiǝ kenke ku Onanto eto batɔnkpǝ ńyǝ baanlɛ bobe.

english_verse: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
translation_verse: Buyifo bamumu lǝ Kristo eso. Fɛɛ mi eyifo babesibe se biyifo bani ku Kristo eso! Bo eyifo betidi ba lɛyɔkpɔ eso bumbǝ ale. Fɛɛ mii, biyifo mba nkpe ale. Buyifo betidi bimba lǝ betidi eto ǝnǝmi. Fɛɛ mii, bekpe dibu.

english_verse: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
translation_verse: Ibǝ ese mɔmɔɔ, boǝsiǝ kukpɛnsǝ ku utukuɛ. Nya bikǝ kpãã saa ǝmbǝ bo boalɛ. Nya betidi laablɛ bo. Nya bumbǝ kakɛkɔ saa boǝsiǝ.

english_verse: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
translation_verse: Nya boeyifo keyifo kekleke kabe bo ǝsuǝ. Lǝ betidi ékudi boo, boo, boakpo mǝ atsyɔ. Lǝ ibǝ bǝ bǝǝtǝkǝko bo lǝ bate bo ohiãa, bo boakpe nkɛale.

english_verse: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
translation_verse: Lǝ báka bo, boefi kafo buɛɛ eto etiki kǝtǝ mǝ mbuayɛ. Betidi ǝǝnyǝ bo fe kuku. Buyifo kawunsiǝ kamfo eto kuku ku ekubi ibǝ ǝsǝ mɔmɔ.

english_verse: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
translation_verse: Minlɛ mi kukue komfo eto okle bɔwɔni bǝ lǝ mfi nkpe mi kofani. Bomu bǝ lǝ nlɛ mi kɔlɛ fe mɔ bebi lala seka.

english_verse: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ ite bǝ bekpe betidi akpi lefosi ba laalɛ mi ola kulaa tsya lǝ mi bofokanɔ eto nkpǝ bo binsi ǝsuǝǝ, bimbǝ antomǝ kpǝ. Itsyise fe kase binsi nkpǝ nya biyifo lɔni ku Kristo esoo, mɔ ni mi anto, se mɔ ni Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ boboko mi eso.

english_verse: and I want you to be like me.
translation_verse: Lǝ nya esoo, nlɛ mi lekpakpa miǝ lǝ bitǝkǝko mɔ ditsyǝbi kama.

english_verse: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
translation_verse: Nyamfo ǝsuǝ eso minlɛ Timoteo botsyese mi nɛ. Uyifo mɔ ubi lala wǝ leeyifo Bonamute Yesu eto keyifo lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ. Wuubǝ oklomasǝ mi kase mǝǝsiǝ nkpǝ fe Kristo eto nle bo eto kusu mǝǝtǝkǝko. Nya nyamfo maate bafokanɔfɔ kenke kaka kee minsu.

english_verse: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
translation_verse: Mi nti eto bǝtsyuǝ átaka bǝńyǝ abesǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ kato eso mǝ bǝtsyuǝ fɛfɛ, itsyise bébu bǝnkǝ, mensi embusinkli mbǝ mi ɔflɔ.

english_verse: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Bonamute Yesu átɔnɔɔ, muubǝ nyǝ mi lǝ lefe kliminti ǝsuǝ. Nya mɔ kosate muubǝnyǝ ǝsuale nnya betidi ba laabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato nkpe, nya moobe lǝ inni etiki bimba banlɛ budii.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto eto sekadidi aanyɛ ku etiki bimba budi. Bomu yaayɛ ku ǝsuǝale.

english_verse: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
translation_verse: Bela biǝ mbǝ mi ɔflɔ lǝ mbǝ nnǝ mi kato lee, lee mbǝ mi ɔflɔ lǝ lelabi ku diyuǝ ǝsuǝǝ?

english_verse: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, betidi lɛ bɔtɛyi kpǝ bǝnkǝ, sɛsɔnɔdidi kpe lǝ mi nti. Nya mamfokanɔfɔ kulaa tsya anla nnya okle buyifo. Bátɛyi mɛ bǝnkǝ osani utsyuǝ ku wǝ anto eto usiǝ ákɔ okla.

english_verse: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
translation_verse: Nya beso biate ǝsuǝ nko? Keku bomu ele bǝ bikewi. Nya utidi wǝ díyifo nyamfo eto okle, ele bǝ bilǝkǝ wǝ lǝ mimba léfo bɛnɔ nti.

english_verse: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
translation_verse: lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo eto ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ. Lǝ betsya bisii, ele bǝ bete biǝ mintsya kpe mi lenti lǝ lɛwɔnɔ ǝsuǝ. Nya Bonamute Yesu Kristo eto ǝsuǝale ebɔkɛ bo lenti.

english_verse: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
translation_verse: Ele bǝ beyani utidi ǝmfo bitǝ Obonsam lǝ ɔtsylɔsǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ. Nya lǝ ukpǝ kula tsyaa, wǝ lɛwɔnɔ lǝ dinyǝ bofo lǝ diyi nǝ ǝsuǝ Yesu lookponko kawunsiǝ kenke eto betidi lekpo lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
translation_verse: Yanle bǝ bikabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato. Binyi biǝ ǝyǝnkisǝsa sɛkɛ ete laatsyakasǝ ku nnamfo bo banwɔ nnya lǝǝtǝ boataka kabe kato?

english_verse: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
translation_verse: Bilǝkǝ ǝyǝnkisǝsa saa, nnya ni lebua, bitsyiko lǝ mii nti. Nya biyifo nnamfo fɔfɔ bo bánwɔ, bo ǝsuǝ ǝyǝnkisǝsa saa lǝmbǝ, fe kase minyi miǝ bite seka sitinti. Itsyise bo Ukpǝ Asa Bo Ɔ́yila eto Diyi eboyo. Nya mɔmɔɔ, boolo bo Ukpǝ Asa Bo Ɔ́yila eto Lesambi nǝ ni Kristo.

english_verse: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Nyaso, biǝntǝ lǝ budi bo Ukpǝ Asa Bo Ɔ́yila eto Diyi ku abolo nnya ǝsuǝ ǝyǝnkisǝsa koko nkpe, nnya nsusu bua ku asa bua boeyifo. Bomu bitǝ lǝ budi diyi nǝmfo ku abolo wǝ ǝsuǝ ǝyǝnkisǝsa saa lǝmbǝ nya lǝ bote boǝ boenyifo ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi. Bomu lǝ bɔyɛ ku Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ kukue ko míwɔni mintǝ mi kafoo, ńtɛyi mi minkǝ bianyɛ ku betidi ba ni basɔnɔdi.

english_verse: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, inni betidi ba dimamfo banɔ ete minlɛ. Betidi ba minlɛ ni basɔnɔdi nya baasɛsǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ asa ǝsuǝ, mba ni beyu, leeyu betidi eto atabi kedi, ku mba lǝǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi tsya. Nya lǝ atɛyi fǝ ǝǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ kenke, ete bǝ ele bǝ lǝkǝ ǝsuǝ etsyiko lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo kenke ǝsuǝ!

english_verse: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
translation_verse: Bomu lesa nǝ minlɛ bɔtɛyi ni bǝ nwǝ laakpo wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ ɔyimi wǝ díkafo ɔnɔ fɛ weedi sɛsɔnɔ nye onkpǝ kenǝmisɛ nye lǝǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi nye laakpo betidi aye bua nye leekpǝ ntǝ nye leeyu, lǝkǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ wǝ. Nyanle bǝ fǝ ku wǝ kɔ ani lǝ ɔkpɛ ɔni bidi lesa kulaa tsya.

english_verse: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
translation_verse: Lǝ etiki edufǝ lǝ ofokanɔnyǝ ku wǝ utsyuǝ eto ntii, yanle bǝ ufi etiki nyamǝ usuko manfokanɔfɔ ɔflɔ nkǝ bedufǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ wǝ. Bomu mi nti eto ɔni, wǝ ele bǝ udi etiki nyamǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
translation_verse: Biyi biǝ Onanto eto betidi ni mba lookponko kawunsiǝ eto betidi lekpo. Nya lǝ biyi biǝ mi ele bǝ bekponko kawunsiǝ eto betidi lekpoo, ele bǝ befo budufǝ lǝ etiki wɛlɛwɛlɛ esuǝ bɛlɔ bitsyǝ.

english_verse: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
translation_verse: Biyi wǝǝ biǝ bo bafokanɔfɔ bo ni mba lookponko kato eto batɔkpe lekpo. Lǝ nyamfo esoo, yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ kawunsiǝ mfo eto etiki wɛlɛwɛlɛ, so bo ani ku bɔlɛ kabe.

english_verse: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
translation_verse: Nlɛ mi etiki nyamfo bɔtɛyi bǝ lǝ kofani lǝ kɔlɛ mi. Nya bɔtɛyi bɛnlɛ biǝ obesibe saa ǝmbǝ mi mba lefo bɛnɔ nti wǝ loofo etiki nyamfo bɔlɔ ketsyǝ.

english_verse: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ ofokanɔnyǝ aakpa wǝ ɔyimitsyuǝ usuko betidi bamba ba ni mamfokanɔfɔ ɔflɔ nkǝ lǝ bedi mǝ etiki bǝtǝ mǝǝ, nyamfo alɛkɛɛ?

english_verse: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
translation_verse: Asa nyamfo mi bǝtsyuǝ díkeyifo kɔ. Fɛɛ Bonamute Yesu ébuflǝ mi asa bua úwui lǝ mi ǝsuǝ nya úyifo mi kɛnkɛ útǝ Onanto. Nya úsinkli ɔ́lɛkɛsǝ mi ku Onanto eto nti ɛyɛ lǝ Bonamute Yesu Kristo ku Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ ǝsuǝ. Bɛlɛ Mi Ǝsuǝ Ola Kukɔnɔ Nya Lǝ Bifi Dibu Bitǝ Onanto.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
translation_verse: Mi nti eto utsyuǝ ebɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Nkpǝ kusu meeyifo lesa saa.” Sitinti ini. Fɛɛ inni lesa saa lɛlɛkɛ itǝ fǝ. Moofo bɔtɛyi minkǝ, “Nkpǝ kusu meeyifo lesa saa.” Fɛɛ yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ lesa saa di mɛ utu.

english_verse: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
translation_verse: Utidi bamba tsya ebofo bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Buyifo béyifo didisa itǝ kafo, nya kafo tsya nkpǝ itǝ didisa.” Sitinti ini, fɛɛ Onanto ebutǝ nkǝ nyamfo kenke eto lefe lǝ dibǝ lefe. Nya inni bǝ lǝ bufi bo ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ bukeyifo akɔɛnsa eto asa eso ete. Bomu bǝ lǝ bufi nnya bukeyifo Boanto Onanto eto keyifo. Nya wuutǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ lesa nǝ dińyǝ itǝ se.

english_verse: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
translation_verse: Fe kase Onanto létakasǝ Bonamute Yesu utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ, nko ke wootakasǝ bo tsya uńyǝnsǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝale nɛ.

english_verse: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
translation_verse: Biyi biǝ mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eyifo Kristo eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sitsyuǝ. Nya yanlɛkɛ bǝ efi Kristo eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sitsyuǝ akɔ ku utidi wǝ difi sɛsɔnɔdi uyifo wǝ keyifo.

english_verse: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
translation_verse: Biyi tsya biǝ lǝ utidi éfi wǝ ǝsuǝ ɔkɔ ku nwǝ nsi nkpǝ lǝ sɛsɔnɔdidi ǝsuǝ, uyifo ɔni ku wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ǝsuǝ. Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ Eto Kukuǝ átɛyi ékpa wuee nkǝ, “Mǝ inuǝ bamǝ ebuyifo bani lǝ sinǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ utidi wǝ dífi wǝ ǝsuǝ omanklasǝ lǝ Bonamute ǝsuǝǝ, uyifo ɔni ku wǝ lǝ lɛwɔnɔ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
translation_verse: Biensiyifo abua ɛyɛ lǝ sɛsɔnɔdidi ǝsuǝ. Lebua bamba saa eto okle nǝ utidi díyifo, diammankla lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ utidi saa wǝ leedi sɛsɔnɔɔ, weeyifo lebua keńyǝ lǝ wǝ kosate eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
translation_verse: Biyi wuee biǝ, mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eyifo Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nkpe lǝ mi eto kǝsiǝkɔ nya ini Onanto dífi wǝ útǝ mi. Inni mi mbǝ mi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto áfo kayo kpǝ lǝ mi disi. Nyaso bifi mi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ bikeyifo asa nnya laakpe Onanto dibu.

english_verse: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
translation_verse: Nya ɛyɛ lǝ kukue ko bɛwɔni bítǝ mɛ eto etiki ǝsuǝǝ, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ lǝ osani utsyuǝ si nkpǝ nya waanla nkǝ weefi kusiǝ, bete biǝ utsyue nnya kukɔnɔ.

english_verse: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se sɛsɔnɔdidi lɛbɔ esoo, ele bǝ osani saa fi kusiǝ. Nya usio saa tsya lǝ usiǝ kusǝ.

english_verse: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
translation_verse: Yanle bǝ osani ni nkǝ wǝǝmbɔkɔ okla ku wǝ usiǝ. Nko ke yanle bǝ usio ni nkǝ wǝǝmbutsyǝ wǝ usǝ okla.

english_verse: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
translation_verse: Itsyise usio saa eto ǝsuǝ eni wǝ fate eto ale. Bomu iyifo wǝ usǝ tsya eto ale. Nko ke ini bǝ osani eto ǝsuǝ tsya eni wǝ fate eto ale. Bomu wǝ usiǝ tsya eto ale ini.

english_verse: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
translation_verse: Lǝ mǝ nti eto ɔni aala nkǝ weesu wǝ utsyuǝ ɔflɔɔ, ele bǝ wǝ unuǝfǝ tɔnɔ utǝ wǝ enso bǝǝ, lǝ iyifo mǝ inuǝ bamǝ eto ketsyue ketsyuǝ ɛtɔnsǝ bǝ lǝ kusu kutsyuǝ ǝsuǝ itǝ lefe kliminti, fe itǝ olatoto seka esoo, fɛ yembutǝ kusu nɛ. Nya lǝ nnya kamaa, ele bǝ besinkli bǝsiǝ nkpǝ fe kase bísiǝ kɔ. Lǝ biyifo nkoo, biembudufǝ lǝ Obonsam eto bulǝ saa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
translation_verse: Nyamfo menyifo kɔfi minlɛ mi butǝ. Bomu nsusu butsyuǝ ete minlɛ mi butǝ.

english_verse: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
translation_verse: Lǝ sitintii, nta nla miǝ utsyuǝ saa siǝ fe kase minsi. Fɛɛ utsyuǝ saa ku wǝ ditǝsa nǝ Onanto dítǝ wǝ. Utidi saa ku wǝ lele nǝ bǝtǝ wǝ.

english_verse: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, itǝ mba lemfi tɔ bǝsiǝ nye lensi tɔ bǝsǝ ku bakɔbǝsiǝ nye bakɔsee, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ lǝ bisiǝ kusiǝ memfimemfi, kusǝ mǝnsiǝmǝnsiǝ fe kase minsii, ɛlɛkɛ itǝ mi eso.

english_verse: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ biǝmbofo mi ǝsuǝ ola bɔlɛ kǝsiǝ lǝ asa nnya lookpe mi bulǝ ǝsuǝ esoo, biofo bǝsiǝ bufi nye besǝ busiǝ. Itsyise lǝ eyifo nkoo, ɛlɛkɛ itǝ utsyu ǝmǝ eso bǝ okayɛ katsyilama kedi sɛsɔnɔ ukafe.

english_verse: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
translation_verse: Nya mi bǝsǝ ku bǝsiǝ, kɔfi ko minlɛ mi bokpe mfo eni mɔ kole. Bomu, Boanto Onanto nlɛ mi ko bokpe. Nnya ni bǝ kusiǝ manni kusǝ.

english_verse: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ ɔ́ni wǝ ǝsuǝǝ, ele bǝ wǝ mate siǝ nye wǝ ku wǝ usǝ sinkli bǝsiǝ. Nya yanle bǝ osani tsya ni wǝ usiǝ.

english_verse: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
translation_verse: Nya mi bafɛfɛɛ, nlɛ mi nyamfo bɔtɛyi, fɛɛ inni Bonamute eto kɔfi. Lǝ mi mba lefo bɛnɔ fi kusia ko dímamfo ɔnɔ nya usio ǝmǝ lɛtɔnɔ nkǝ bǝǝsiǝ fe usǝ ku usiǝǝ, osani ǝmǝ manni wǝ.

english_verse: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
translation_verse: Itsyise osani wǝ dímamfo ɔ́nɔ ǝmǝǝ, Onanto ebuyifo wǝ kɛnkɛ ɛyɛ lǝ usio wǝ léfo ɔnɔ ǝmǝ eso. Nya nko ke ini bǝ Onanto ebuyifo usio wǝ dímamfo ɔ́nɔ tsya kɛnkɛ lǝ wǝ usǝ wǝ léfo ɔ́nɔ eso. Lenni nkoo, mǝ bebi embuyifo kɛnkɛ. Fɛɛ fe kase intee, Onanto eeyifo bebi bamǝ kɛnkɛ.

english_verse: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ utidi wǝ ni mamfokanɔnyǝ aala nkǝ wanyani nwǝ ni ofokanɔnyǝǝ, kusu kpe itǝ nko faa. Nya lǝ kusu komfo eto oklee ǝsuǝ, usio nye osani wǝ ni ofokanɔnyǝǝ, kpe kusu weeyifo lesa nǝ kee ola. Onanto ákpadi bo nkǝ bubǝ busiǝ nkpǝ lǝ diyuǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
translation_verse: Lesa nǝ maate lǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto etsyukpa kenke nti, ni bǝnkǝ lǝ utsyuǝ saa lǝ usu sɛtɔ lǝ usiǝ nkpǝ lǝ kusu ko Bonamute dítǝ wǝ ɛyɛ lǝ kase unsi lǝ lefe nǝ Onanto lékpadi wǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: If you are already circumcised, don't try to change it. If you are not circumcised, don't get circumcised.
translation_verse: Lǝ ála lelo fɛ Onanto lékpadi fǝǝ, nyalɛ sukosɛ nko. Nya lǝ ámanla tɔ lelo fɛ Onanto lékpadi fǝ, mantɛyi tsya fǝ esi esu ala lelo.

english_verse: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ ála lelo nye ámanla leloo, nyamfo menyifo kulesa. Bomu nnya lefe ni kase ele bǝ boola lesa nǝ Onanto léte bo nkǝ buyifo, nnya ni lesa nǝ lefe nɛ.

english_verse: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
translation_verse: Nyaso siǝ nkpǝ lǝ ditsyǝbi nǝ ǝsuǝ fakɛ fɛ Onanto lékpadi fǝ.

english_verse: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
translation_verse: Lǝ ndomunyǝ feni fɛ Bonamute lékpadi fǝǝ, eyifo Bonamute eto nwǝ nsi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya lǝ eyifo nwǝ nsi lǝ fǝ esuǝ ǝsuǝ fɛ Bonamute lékpadi fǝǝ, afiani Bonamute ndomunyǝ.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
translation_verse: Onanto áfo nya kayo kpǝ lǝ fǝ disi. Nyaso mensiyifo fǝ ǝsuǝ ndomunyǝ ǝtǝ utidi bamba saa.

english_verse: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
translation_verse: Mɔmɔɔ, itsyi lǝ nnya bɛtɔ itsyi lǝ mba lensitɔ bǝsǝ ǝsuǝǝ, mámfo kɔfi saa itsyi lǝ Bonamute ɔflɔ. Fɛɛ se Bonamute dínyǝ mɛ kaminsǝ nya únyǝ mɛ nwǝ dińyǝ utǝ esoo, nnyǝ mɔ nsusu lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
translation_verse: Lǝ utidi ebu obe nya unyǝ kase asa nlɛ ale bɔkɛ lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝǝ, nnyǝ miǝ ɛlɛkɛ itǝ bǝ osani siǝ nko lǝ ɔyɛ kusiǝ bufi.

english_verse: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ éfi kusiǝǝ, mantɛyi fǝ aani wǝ. Nya lǝ émemfi tɔ kusiǝ, mantɛyi fǝ eesu abɛbɛ usio lǝ efi.

english_verse: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ ébufi kusiǝ, émenyifo kabua. Nya lǝ usio wǝ dímǝnsiǝ tɔ kusǝ esu usiǝ kusǝǝ, wényifo kabua saa. Fɛɛ nla minkǝ lǝ nlǝkǝ mi ntsyiko lǝ bulǝ bo leetu usǝ ku usiǝ lǝ nkpǝ bomfo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
translation_verse: Mɔ bayimi, lesa nǝ minlɛ mi bɔtɛyi ni bǝnkǝ, lefe nǝ díbu mensi kulǝ. Nya idu mɔmɔ ɛntɔɔ, ele bǝ mimba mfi bǝsiǝ, siǝ nkpǝ fe bimfi bǝsiǝ.

english_verse: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
translation_verse: Nya betidi ba bulǝ nlɛ butuu, beyifo fe bulǝ saa anlɛ mǝ butu. Nya mba lǝǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ, beyifo fe banlɛ disuǝyuǝ bunyǝ. Nya betidi ba laaya asaa, beyifo fe asa nnya baaya kenke eni mǝ kosate eto ale.

english_verse: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
translation_verse: Nya mba difi kawunsiǝ kamfo eto bikǝ bɔbɛbɛ betsyi lǝ disii, bemfi mǝ ǝnǝmi bǝtǝkǝ lǝ asa nyamfó ǝsuǝ. Itsyise fe kase boǝnyǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo eto bikǝ mɔmɔɔ, inni nko biubǝ bitsyǝ lǝ lefe kliminti nǝ luubǝ kama.

english_verse: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
translation_verse: Nla miǝ bilǝkǝ mi nsusu bitsyiko lǝ lesa saa ǝsuǝ. Itsyise osani wǝ lemfi kusiǝǝ, eefi wǝ nsusu kǝtǝkǝ lǝ Bonamute eto keyifo ǝsuǝ nya woakpe nkɛale keyifo lesa nǝ lɔɔkɛ Bonamute ɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ osani wǝ mfi kusiǝǝ, wǝ nsusu ǝtǝkǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo eto asa ǝsuǝ. Itsyise woala nkǝ woeyifo asa nnya lɔɔkɛ wǝ usiǝ ɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
translation_verse: Nko ini nɛ, bǝ okpe lǝ asakpa ǝnuǝ eto nti, lekpa nǝmfo ku nǝnse, ditsyuǝ saa lɛ wǝ bunǝ. Nya usio wǝ lensi kusǝ nye kebitɛ, eefi wǝ lefe kǝtǝkǝ lǝ Bonamute eto keyifo ǝsuǝ. Itsyise woala nkǝ woefi wǝ ǝsuǝ kenke ku lɛwɔnɔ utǝ wǝ. Nya usio wǝ nsi kusǝ, wǝ nsusu ǝǝtǝkǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ eto asa ǝsuǝ, itsyise woala nkǝ woeyifo asa nnya lɔɔkɛ wǝ usǝ ɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
translation_verse: Nla miǝ lǝ nwunsǝko mi. Nyamfo eso meedi mi etiki lǝ asa nyamfo ǝsuǝ nɛ. Inni kɔfi saa mimfi minlɛ mi bokpe. Bomu nla miǝ lǝ bisiǝ nkpǝ lǝ kusu sɛɛ ǝsuǝ. Nya biofo mi ǝsuǝ kenke bufi kǝtǝ lǝ Bonamute hã kenke ku mi ketu kenke.

english_verse: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ osani átɔ usio disi nkǝ weefii, nya únyǝ nkǝ bembofo busiǝ kpo eyo lefe nǝ beebuu, boofo bǝtsyuǝ bokpa. Nyamfo menyifo lebua.

english_verse: But it is better to have self-control and to make up your mind not to marry.
translation_verse: Fɛ osani wǝ difi nsusu kekleke nkǝ weembufi tɔ usio ǝmǝ fɛ woofo busiǝ sɛkɛ, woofo ko bɔyɛ bokpa etɔ. Osani ǝmfo tsya éyifo ɛlɛkɛ.

english_verse: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
translation_verse: Nyaso, osani wǝ dífi kusiǝǝ, eyifo ɛlɛkɛ. Fɛɛ nwǝ ǝmǝ dímemfi kusiǝ mfó. Wǝ ale alɛkɛ eso nwǝ.

english_verse: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Usio wǝ díkesiǝ kusǝǝ, umbǝ kusu unsi wǝǝsiǝ kusǝ bamba lǝ lefǝ nǝ w'usǝ nsi nsi nkpǝ. Nya lǝ usǝ ǝmǝ ǝkpǝ, nya fɛ woofo kusǝ bamba ko woala busiǝko nɛ. Fɛɛ ele bǝ osani ǝmǝ yifo nwǝ lefo ɔnɔ.

english_verse: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ nnya ete woofo busiǝ kusǝ mǝnsiǝ, eni weebunyǝ disuǝyuǝ enso nko. Nko mǝ mɔ nsusu nlɛ mɛ bɔtɛyi nɛ, nya mfo nnɔ miǝ iyifo Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ eto lelabi tsya.

english_verse: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, nla miǝ maatɛyi mi lesatsyuǝ itsyi lǝ didisa nǝ béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi ǝsuǝ. Boatɛyi boǝ bo utsyuǝ sa yi kobe. Fɛɛ buyi tsya boǝ kobe komfo ǝǝtǝ bǝ utidi abesǝ ǝsuǝ kato nya woate ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ lesa nǝ laakpe betidi nkɛale ni lelabi.

english_verse: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
translation_verse: Utidi saa wǝ leebu nkǝ uyi lesaa, lǝ sitinti, unyi ni fe kase ele bǝ ote.

english_verse: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ utidi wǝ laala Onantoo, Onanto yi wǝ.

english_verse: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
translation_verse: Nya nyamfo ni lesa nǝ minla miǝ maatɛyi mi itsyi lǝ didisa nǝ béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi lǝ kedikǝtǝkɔ ǝsuǝ. Buyi boǝ asa nnya betidi díyifo bǝtsyǝ bǝtǝ kedii, eyifo asa bimba lǝ kawunsiǝ kamfo. Itsyise buyi sitinti boǝ Onanto ɔni hã nkpe.

english_verse: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
translation_verse: Lǝ betidi lɛ etikii, baatɛyi bǝnkǝ bǝfiǝ kpe lǝ kato nye kasɔ mfo nya bǝfiǝ akpa akpa bamfo fui ku mǝ banamute kula tsya.

english_verse: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
translation_verse: Tsyafɛ boo, buyi boǝ Onanto ɔni hã ete nkpe. Wǝ ni Anto wǝ dílǝkǝ asa nyamfo kenke. Nya we ni nwǝ bunsi nkpǝ butǝ. Nya Bonamute ɔni hã wǝ nkpee, wǝ ni Yesu Kristo wǝ ǝsuǝ Onanto lɛyɛ úyifo asa nnya kenke. Nya ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ nya bunsi nkpǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
translation_verse: Inni utidi saa nyi Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo. Asa nnya betidi dilǝkǝ bǝtsyǝ beesumuu, iyifo mǝ bǝtsyuǝ fe sitinti ini ibǝ ese lǝ mmi, bǝ lǝ bedi lesa nǝmfo eto oklee, beebu bǝ bǝfiǝ eto lesa dini bédi. Nya mǝ nsusu bo lǝmbǝ ale laalo mǝ dikpi bǝ edisa nyamfo ǝǝtǝ baabiasǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
translation_verse: Inni didisa nǝ boedi nǝ loobiasǝ bonko Onanto eto nti. Nya yembobiasǝ kulesa lǝ búdi ni. Nya nko ke ini boǝmbunyǝ kulesa lǝ budi lesa kuditsyuǝ.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
translation_verse: Bebe kukɔnɔ lǝ kusu ko benkpǝ ǝsuǝ, bǝ lǝ bɛyɛ utidi wǝ eto bofokanɔ lǝmbǝ ale bunǝ kakpe lǝ lebua ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
translation_verse: Bu abe lǝ utidi wǝ eto bofokanɔ lǝmbǝ ale nyǝ fǝ nwǝ nyi nyamfo ni nwǝ ńyǝ nlɛ lesa nǝ béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi budi lǝ difiǝyo. Wuntsya ebunyǝ ǝsuǝale bǝ lǝ udi lesa nǝ béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi mfó.

english_verse: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
translation_verse: Nyaso lǝ lesa nǝ meedi ebutǝ mɔ ɔyimitsyuǝ lǝ uyifo lebua, mensiembudi lesa nǝ béfi bǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi kulefe lǝ kase yuunǝ mɔ ɔnyimitsyuǝ ekpe lǝ lebua kǝmiǝ eso.

english_verse: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Bibu biǝ mimbǝ kusu bǝ nyifo lesa nǝ maala? Nye minni ɔtɔnkpe nya mǝnnyǝ Bonamute Yesu lee? Nya inni mi ni eyibibi nnya lɛbɔ itsyi lǝ mɔ keyifo ka minyifo mintǝ Bonamute Yesu ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
translation_verse: Lǝ ibǝ bǝ bǝtsyuǝ embofo mɛ fe Bonamute Yesu eto ɔtɔnkpe, mi áfo mɛ nko. Nya fe kase bíyifo lɔni ku Bonamute Yesu, nyamfó mate áte bǝnkǝ mi kosate lǝ nyamfo bulǝkǝ kate wuee biǝnkǝ nyifo Bonamute Yesu eto ɔtɔnkpe.

english_verse: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
translation_verse: Ɛyɛ lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ bǝ bǝtǝ bo didisa ku ǝnǝsa nye ntu.

english_verse: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
translation_verse: Itǝ kusu bǝ bo tsya yɛnko bo bǝsiǝ fe kase batɔnkpe bamba ku Bonamute Yesu eto bayimisani ku Petro tsya leeyifo.

english_verse: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
translation_verse: Lee mɔ ku Barnaba mate ete ele bǝ buyifo keyifo bobe bo ǝsuǝ?

english_verse: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
translation_verse: Omamanyǝ kuutsyuǝ enyifo keyifo kafo wǝ ǝsuǝ wǝ kayofofo. Nya ɔkuɛmfo saa, kpe kusu weedi bidikǝ bia únyǝ utsyiko lǝ wǝ ɔkuɛ. Nya nwǝ lǝǝtsyǝ bebuki kpe kusu bǝ unǝ mmintu bo beeke betsyiko lǝ bebuki bamǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
translation_verse: Boakla lǝ Moses eto afi eto kukue esuǝ bǝ, ubuki wǝ fakpa fesuko keyifo ɔnlɛ keyifo kǝtǝ fǝǝ, mankle wǝ kɛnyɛ lǝ uńyǝ ɔlɛ keyifo. Fɛɛ inni bebuki bamfo tinti ǝsuǝ nya Onanto nlɛ etiki budi.

english_verse: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
translation_verse: Bo ǝsuǝ eso Onanto nlɛ ditiki nǝmfo bɔtɛyi nɛ. Lǝ sitintii, bo eso báwɔni ditiki nǝmfo nɛ. Utidi wǝ lekpe bidikǝ ku utidi wǝ lǝǝfǝsǝ nye lǝǝlǝkǝ bidikǝǝ, eeyifo nko ku nsusu nye kusubebe nkǝ utsyuǝ saa ebunyǝ bidikǝ biamǝ eto bitsyuǝ udi.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
translation_verse: Boto lɛwɔnɔ sɛɛ eto eyibibi bokpe lǝ mi nti. Nya lǝ mi tsya efi ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto bikǝ bitǝ boo, ebiasǝ lesa ǝtsyuǝ?

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Le betidi bamba ǝǝnyǝ kusu kenyǝ kutǝkǝ kutsyuǝ ketsyi lǝ mi ɔflɔɔ, nyalɛ ele bǝ bo nyǝ kutǝkǝ kpe enso mǝ kulaa tsya. Fɛɛ boo, boanyɛ lǝ kusu komfó bǝ lǝ bunyǝ kulesa lǝ mi ɔflɔ. Bomu bufi lesa saa butǝkǝ lǝ bo ǝsuǝ itǝ lǝ Kristo eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ lǝ lɛyɛ lǝ kusu sɛɛ ǝsuǝ lǝ disensǝ disu kalebe saa.

english_verse: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
translation_verse: Biyi wuee biǝnkǝ betidi ba leeyifo keyifo lǝ Onanto eto disumuyoo, mfó bǝǝnyǝ mǝ didisa ketsyiko nɛ. Nya betidi ba leefi mǝ keditǝtǝ eto asa kesu kǝtǝkǝ lǝ kedikǝtǝkɔ eto leba ǝsuǝǝ, nnya ǝtsyuǝ bǝǝtǝ mǝ fe mǝ kedikɔ nɛ.

english_verse: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
translation_verse: Nko ǝmǝ ke ini sitinti bǝ Bonamute dítǝ kɔfi nkǝ betidi ba laabe Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝme letsya be kǝtǝ betidii, letsya bobe nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ ele bǝ bedi lesa nɛ.

english_verse: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ménfi kusu komfo nyifo keyifo saa ntǝ ǝsuǝ. Nye bɔwɔni minlɛ mi kukue komfo bǝ lǝ ntɔ lesatsyuǝ ntsyiko mi ɔflɔ? Lǝ nyamfó esoo, lǝ nkpǝ kulaa tsya alɛkɛ itǝ mɛ eso. Itsyise manla miǝ lesanǝ ǝsuǝ itǝ mɛ kusu bǝ mbesǝ ǝsuǝ katoo, utidi utsyuǝ yɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ uyifo nnya etiki bimba.

english_verse: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
translation_verse: Yǝntǝ mǝ kusu bǝ nkabesǝ ǝsuǝ kato bǝ maabe ditiki sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya eso. Boanto Onanto ebunǝ mɛ koto lǝ mambe Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya ntǝ betidi!

english_verse: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya ete iyifo bǝ mɔ kosate ete dílǝkǝ keyifo kamfo mintǝ mɔ ǝsuǝǝ, eni moobe kusu miǝ ele bǝ bafo mɛ kayo lǝ nnya disi. Fɛɛ iyifo keyifo ka Boanto Onanto dílǝkǝ utǝ mǝ nkǝ ele bǝ nyifo. Itsyise iyifo keyifo ka Onanto dífi mɛ utsyǝ lǝ ke kɛnyɛ.

english_verse: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
translation_verse: Nya nte kayo ka baafo mɛ lǝ ke disi? Kayofofo ka mǝǝnyǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ ni maabe Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya kafo faa. Nya membunyǝ kayofofo saa lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ meedi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
translation_verse: Kusu saa asini kutǝ mɛ se mimbǝ lǝ kuutsyuǝ kalɔ eso. Fɛɛ nyifo mɔ ǝsuǝ fe utidi wǝ leeyifo betidi ba tã eto keyifo bǝ lǝ nnǝ betidi kpǝ moko Boanto Onanto.

english_verse: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
translation_verse: Lǝ nlɛ keyifo lǝ Yudafɔ ntii, mǝ ǝsiǝ nkpǝ fe Yudanyǝ bǝ lǝ itǝ bǝ moofo mǝ bunǝ kaboko Boanto Onanto. Itǝ bǝ mimbǝ lǝ Moses eto kɔfi kalɔ tsyaa, mǝǝsiǝ nkpǝ nko seka ku mba leetsyue wǝ kɔfi koto lǝ lefe nǝ minkpe lǝ mǝ nti itǝ lǝ mfo mǝ bunǝ kǝtǝ Boanto Onanto.

english_verse: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
translation_verse: Nko ke meeyifo ku betidi ba lenni Yudafɔ tsya nɛ. Nya mǝǝsiǝ lǝ mǝ nti fe utidi wǝ lenni Yudanyǝ. Nya meentsyue Yudafɔ eto kɔfi koto itǝ lǝ nnǝ mǝǝntǝ Bonamute Boanto Onanto. Nyamfo aante bǝ ntaka ńyǝ lǝ Onanto eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ itsyise lǝ sitintii, Kristo eto kɔfi ǝsuǝ nya meetsyue koto nɛ.

english_verse: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
translation_verse: Lǝ nkpe lǝ mba eto bofokanɔ lǝmbǝ ale ntii, meeyifo mɔ ǝsuǝ fe mǝ ke bǝ lǝ nnǝ mǝ mboko Boanto Onanto. Meeyifo ǝsuǝ kakatesǝ ko betidi ba eto nti minkpe itǝ lǝ nyɛ lǝ kusu saa ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ nnǝ betidi mboko Boanto Onanto.

english_verse: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
translation_verse: Meeyifo asa nyamfo kenke itǝ lǝ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ eso bǝ lǝ nyǝ disuǝyuǝ nǝ laabɔ ketsyiko lǝ ni keyifo buyifo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
translation_verse: Biyi wuee biǝ betidi kpǝ lǝǝkǝ ketsyetsyi etsyi. Fɛɛ mǝ nti utidi ɔni hã lǝǝtǝkǝ kato kenyǝ nnya kayofofo. Nyaso bitsyetsyi nko ǝmǝ itǝ lǝ bitǝkǝ kato lǝ binyǝ nnya kayofofo.

english_verse: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
translation_verse: Utidi saa wǝ lǝǝmuǝ ǝsuǝ fɛ weesu ketsyetsyii, waabokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ kakase lesa nǝ baate wǝ ku nnya esu kenke bǝ lǝ ofo kato butǝkǝ kafo lekankpakpa nǝ laanklɛ fɛ laa bia. Fɛɛ boo, boǝmuǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ bofo lekpakpa nǝ lǝmbobia kulefe.

english_verse: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eso meetsyetsyi sidididi kesu sɛtɔ bǝ lǝ nsu nla ukuǝ wǝ nkpǝ sɛtɔ. Nya nyifo utidi wǝ laalɔ kabe fɛ woato kekpe nya maanto kekpe waata kebu.

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: Mǝǝtǝ miǝ kekpe lɛ mɛ, bǝ mɔ ǝsuǝ lǝ inyǝ kanyi saa eto okle. Itsyise lǝ mbe Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo letsya ntǝ betidi bamba nya bubǝ botsya bokatesǝ ǝsuǝ bobee, mannla miǝ bǝlǝkǝ mɛ lenti bǝ mǝnyǝ ntǝ kayofofo!

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: Mɔ bayimi, nnla miǝ bekloma lesa nǝ díbǝ lǝ bo bana ba dítǝkeko Moses ǝsuǝ. Lɛsɔmiloku lɛyɛ ko mǝ, nya lése mǝmblɛ kenke. Nya báyɛ lǝ ditumbukplɛ sia eto nti bafe.

english_verse: This was like being baptized and becoming followers of Moses.
translation_verse: Onanto éfi lɛsɔmiloku ku ditumbukplɛ sia úyifo fe mǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa fe betidi ba dítǝkeko Moses kama seka.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Mǝmblɛ edi Lɔwɔnɔ eto didisa nǝmǝ tutuutu,

english_verse: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
translation_verse: nya bǝnǝ Lɛwɔnɔ eto ntu bomǝ tsya ke. Bǝnǝ ntu betsyiko lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ eto kobansǝ nǝ lɛyɛnko mǝ lidufǝnko mfo. Nya kobansǝ komǝ ni Kristo ǝmǝ kosate.

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝmfó kemiǝ kulaa tsya, Onanto mǝnnyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ mǝ kpǝ eto ditsyǝbi ǝsuǝ. Lǝ nyasoo, bǝkpǝ lǝ ntɛnti.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Asa nyamfo nnya ele bǝ bunyǝ bokase asa bǝ lǝ abua buyifo eto kamini lensiǝmbɔkɛ bo lǝ ǝsuǝ kakpe bo bulǝ,

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: nye bufi ǝsuǝ butǝ bǝfiǝ busumu eto asayifoyifo fe mǝ ke. Nyamfo eto okle Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Betidi bamfo álo bebuki, bédi bǝnǝ ku sitǝkpǝ ku sɛsɔnɔdidi eto asa, nya báke béwui ǝfiǝkǝ.”

english_verse: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
translation_verse: Yanle bǝ ibǝ bǝ bola asɔnɔsa buyifo fe kase mǝ betidi díyifo, nya betidi akpi fosi ǝnuǝ akpi ǝtsyǝ díkpǝ lǝ diyi lɔni ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: Baatɛyi lekpa bǝnkǝ, “Kusu kpe bǝ buyifo lesa saa.” Sitinti ini. Fɛɛ inni lesa saa lɛlɛkɛ bǝ buyifo. Kusu kpe bǝ buyifo lesa saa, fɛɛ inni lesa saa laaboko bo kutǝkǝ.

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Nya biǝntǝ lǝ boto Boanto bobe. Nko ibǝ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ díyifo nya bawa dínǝ mǝ nya békpǝ.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Nya yanle bǝ botaka buńyǝ lǝ Onanto ǝsuǝ fe kase mǝ bǝtsyuǝ díyifo, eso nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ nkpe ukpǝ eto ǝsuǝale lɛtsylɔsǝ mǝ.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Nyamfo kenke ǝbǝ itǝ bǝ lǝ bufi bokase kobe. Nnya bǝ báwɔni nnya bǝtsyuǝ bǝ lǝ bufi bɔlɛ bo kɔlɛ. Itsyise lefe nǝ ǝsuǝ bonkpǝ mfoo, eboyo kalosǝkɔ.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ leebu nkǝ uńyǝ keklekee, be kukɔnɔ nkǝ weedu ɔnɔ.

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Nyaso, mɔ bayimi, bilǝkǝ mi ǝsuǝ bitsyiko lǝ bǝfiǝ busumu saa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Nlɛ mi etiki budi fe betidi ba nyi kobe. Nyaso bitsyue asa nya maatɛyi mi mfo koto lɛ bɛnɔ.

english_verse: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
translation_verse: Lɛkɔbe nǝ boefi kasɔ Onanto anii, ńyǝ itǝ Boanto eto kɔtɔ. Nya abolo nnya boedi lǝ bɔlɛ Boanto eto lesa kɛnkɛ budii, aate bǝ bo ku Kristo eyifo bani.

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Ite bǝ bufui tsyaa, lǝ sitintii, buyifo ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sɔni kplo. Itsyise abolo ani ete ini boedi.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Bibu Israel bebi ba leedi lesa nǝ befi bǝtǝ Onanto kedi, beyifo bani ku Onanto. Itsyise wǝ uni befi asa bǝtǝ kedi lǝ wǝ keditǝtǝ eto leba ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Nya mɔmɔɔ, bubu fanlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi minlɛ miǝ bǝfiǝ ba befi ani batoo, nya mǝǝtsya eyifo Onanto kosate? Nye lesa nǝ béfi bǝtǝ kedi bǝtǝ asa bamba, nyamfo eyifo lesa tinti ditsyuǝ?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Owo lo! Lesa nǝ minlɛ mi bɔtɛyi, nya ni bǝ, asa nnya béfi bǝtǝ kedi lǝ kedikǝtǝkɔ eto aba ǝsuǝ, befi nya bǝtǝ kedi bǝtǝ awɔnɔ bua, inni Onanto. Nya manla miǝ biyifo bani ku awɔnɔ bua nyamfo.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Yanlɛkɛ bǝ binǝ asa itsyi lǝ Boanto eto lɛkɔbe kafo nya bisi bisu binǝ itsyi lǝ awɔnɔ bua tsya eto lɛkɔbe kafo. Yanle bǝ edi lesa lǝ Boanto eto kedikǝtǝkɔ nya esi esu edi lesa lǝ awɔnɔ bua eto kedikǝtǝkɔ tsya.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Nye bela biǝ Boanto lǝ unyǝ ɔblɔ okpe bo? Biembu disi biǝ bokpe ǝsuǝale beso wǝ.

english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Bekpe kusu biaya sinǝ saa sia banlɛ bote lǝ asi kayimi. Nya yanhiã bǝ lǝ bɛtɔ bɛnɔ sinǝ sia eto okle sini fɛ lǝ beya.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Itsyise Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Kawunsiǝ ku asa nnya nkpe lǝ ke kenkee, Boanto eto ale ini.”

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Lǝ manfokanɔnyǝ utsyuǝ akpadi fǝ nkǝ bǝ lǝ bidi lesa nya fatɔnɔ fesu, lesa nǝ ke dii, di ni nya mantɔ kulesa anɔ lǝ disa nǝmǝ eyifo kɛnkɛ lee dienyifo.

english_verse: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
translation_verse: Itsyise inni fǝ nwǝ lidi lesa nǝmǝ eto utu eso yuukǝlǝ bomu nwǝ nse. Utidi utsyuǝ ebofo bɔtɔ nkǝ, “Beso lǝ nkpe kusu meeyifo lesa ǝtsyuǝ fɛ ini utidi bamba yookpe bulǝ lǝ lɛkɔɛsii, nya ele bǝ nyɛ lesa nǝmǝ buyifo?

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: Lǝ nsɔ Onanto ani lǝ lesa nǝ míndi ǝsuǝǝ, beso ele bǝ utidi bamba bu nkǝ nta mbu lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝ se minsɔ Onanto ani?”

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kloma fǝnkǝ lesa saa nǝ utsyuǝ leeyifo nye leedi nye lǝǝnǝǝ, ele bǝ uyifo nnyamblɛ kenke bǝ lǝ befi dibu bǝtǝ Onanto.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Bienyifo kulesa nǝ luuńyǝ lǝ Yudafɔ nye mba lenni Yudafɔ nye Onanto eto bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
translation_verse: Biyifo fe kase meeyifo lesa saa. Maabe kusu miǝ mǝmbuyifo kulesa nǝ luuńyǝ lǝ kutsyuǝ saa ǝsuǝ. Mambe kusu itǝ mɔ lɛlɛkɛsǝ, bomu itǝ lesa nǝ lɔɔlɛkɛ itǝ utsyuǝ saa bǝ lǝ bǝnyǝ bulǝkǝ.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Bebe lǝ bekase mɔ ditsyǝbi fe kase mintsya laabe Kristo eto lele. Kristo Ni Utsyuǝ Saa Eto Disi.

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Maatɛsǝ mi itsyise biakloma mɛ lefe saa nya biakloma asa nnya minte mi nya biayɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ lefe saa.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nla miǝ bekloma wuee biǝ, Kristo eyifo utidi saa eto disi. Nya usǝ tsya eyifo disi itǝ usio. Nya Onanto ni disi itǝ Kristo.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Nyaso osani wǝ laato ola nye laabe Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ letsya lǝ Onanto eto Disumuyo nya úfi lesatsyuǝ úti disi, wǝǝntǝ Kristo dibu.

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: Nya usio wǝ laato ola nye laabe Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ letsya lǝ Disumuyo nya weemfi ofiamɛ keti disii, wǝǝntǝ wǝ usǝ dibu. Nya letekɛkɛ saa ǝmbǝ lǝ wǝ ku usio wǝ lɛkpi disi nya wanlɛ wǝ ǝsuǝ ola eto nti.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
translation_verse: Lǝ usio ǝmfo mémfi ofiamɛ úti disi, nyalɛ ete bǝnkǝ ɔ́kpi disi. Nya se iyifo afanisa bǝnkǝ usio kɔ disi nye ɔkpi disi esoo, ele bǝ ufi ofamɛ uti disi.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Yanle bǝ osani fi kulesa uti disi lǝ Disumuyo itsyise Onanto éyifo osani lǝ wǝ ditsyǝbi ǝsuǝ. Nya wǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto dibu kate. Fɛ kusiǝ wǝǝ, ǝǝlǝkǝ osani eto dibu kate.

english_verse: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto mánka usio buyifo ketsyiko osani ǝsuǝ. Bomu usio úyifo útsyiko lǝ osani ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: Nya inni usio eso Onanto díyifo osani. Bomu osani eso Onanto díyifo usio.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Onanto eto batɔnkpǝ esoo, ele bǝ usio fi ofiamǝ uti disi lǝ ete bǝ okpe lǝ osani eto kalɔ.

english_verse: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ bo bofokanɔ eto nkpǝsiǝsiǝ ǝsuǝǝ, usio ǝnsiǝ lete lǝ osani ǝsuǝ, nya osani tsya ǝnsiǝ lete lǝ usio ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Itsyise fe kase bǝlǝkǝ usio betsyiko lǝ osani ǝsuǝǝ, nko ke ini bǝ usio tsya lele osani nɛ. Fɛɛ lǝ nyamfo kenkee, Onanto ɔflɔ itsyi.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Mi kosate bu bebe lǝ ɛlɛkɛ bǝ usio ńyǝ ukato ola lefe nǝ eto lefe weefi kulesa uti lǝ disi?

english_verse: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ usio eto sɛwɔni ekulǝ seyoma nkoo, iyifo dibu itǝ wǝ. Itsyise Onanto ǝtǝ wǝ sɛwɔni sia dikulǝ fe lesa nǝ díti wǝ disi.

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ utidi utsyuǝ ala nkǝ wǝǝnǝ etiki lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝǝ, lesa nǝ ete mɔɔtɛyi wǝ ni bǝ bo Onanto eto bafokanɔfɔ eto etsyukpa kenke ensibǝ kusumu eto ketsyue bamba saa enso nkamfo.

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Itsyise ditutɛ, nnɔ bǝ lǝ betsya fe bafokanɔfɔɔ, biama mi ǝsuǝ lǝ akpa akpa. Nya iyifo mɛ fe nyamfo eyifo ɔnukualɛ.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Kusu kuni bǝ kǝnǝ dufǝ mi lenti bǝ lǝ ilǝkǝkǝ mba ni bafokanɔfɔ ba ni banikualɛtidi lǝ mi nti ete bǝ lǝ betidi lǝ bǝnyǝ mǝ bǝtsyǝ lekpo.

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ bibǝ bekpe disii, biǝnnyǝ kǝsiǝkǝ mi bǝtsyuǝ fɛ utsyuǝ saa leefi wǝ lesa kedi. Nyamfo ǝtǝ bǝ kukpɛnsɛ aalɛ bǝtsyuǝ nya bǝtsyuǝ lǝǝnǝ nte kekpǝ.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Nyamfo lɛ bote nkǝ lefe saa nǝ biudi nye biunǝ lǝ lɛkɔbe nǝmfo kafoo, bɛlɛ Bonamute Yesu eto ukpǝ bokloma isu ese lefe nǝ usi wuubǝ.

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ wante lesa nǝ Bonamute Yesu eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ni fɛ udi abolo nyamǝ nye unǝ utsyiko lǝ lɛkɔbe nǝmǝ kafoo, weedi nya wǝǝnǝ lekpokponkpo kaboko ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Nko ke ini lǝ didisa nǝmǝ eto kamaa, úfi lɛkɔbe nǝme nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Lɛkɔbe nǝmfo ni Onanto eto ketsyue fɔfɔ ka mɔ kɔtɔ úfi óse uyi. Nya lefe saa nǝ biunǝ lǝ ni, binǝ lǝ bifi bikakloma mɛ.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Itsyise lesa nǝ mímfo mintsyiko lǝ Bonamute Yesu ɔflɔɔ, ni ni nnǝ minte mi nɛ. Nya lǝ ketsye ka ǝsuǝ fɛ boofi wǝ bakpee kɔni, úfi abolo,

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: nya se ɔ́sɔ Onanto anii, úbudi nnya nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nnyǝ nyamfo ni mɔ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sia muufi ntǝ Onanto kedi lǝ mi disi. Befo lǝ bidi lǝ bifi bikakloma mɛ.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Nyaso utidi saa wǝ luudi Bonamute Yesu eto lesa nǝmfo nye wuunǝ lǝ lɛkɔbe nǝmfo kafo lǝ kusu ko dimantsyɛ ǝsuǝǝ, ebote bǝ uyifo lebua se wǝǝntǝ Bonamute Yesu eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ ku kɔtɔ dibu eso.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Nyamfo dítǝ bǝ mii nti eto betidi kpǝ ǝmbǝ ǝsuǝale nya bǝtsyuǝ diyifo befinfi. Nya kpǝ tsya díkpǝ nɛ.

english_verse: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ bote kobe nya butsyiko bo ǝsuǝ budufǝ kobee, yuutǝ bǝ Onanto embokponko bo lekpo.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ Bonamute Yesu aakponko bo lekpo nya wǝǝnǝ bo koto bǝ lǝ Onanto lǝ ɔyɛ bo bɔtsylɔsǝ ku manfokanɔfɔ.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo esoo, mɔ bayimi, lǝ betsya biǝ biedi Bonamute Yesu eto lesaa, binyǝ bisiǝkǝ utsyuǝ.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ kukpɛnsɛ lɛ utsyuǝǝ, ele bǝ udi lǝ wǝ diyo bǝ lǝ Onanto wankponko mi lekpo lǝ betsya fe ditsyukpa seka. Nya lǝ etiki bamba kpe, lǝ mbǝ mfóo, bɔɔlɛ nnya bobe.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Biyi wuee biǝ lefe nǝ bíante tɔ Onanto, bítǝ asa nnya lǝmbǝ nkpǝ kédi mi nya bíkesumu asa nyamfó nya bénya kusu komǝ.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Nya ele bǝ bete biǝ utidi saa wǝ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nsi lǝ ǝsuǝ, ǝǝbǝmbofo etiki budi nkǝ, “Mbusiotidi ni Yesu.” Nko ke tsya ini bǝ kuutsyuǝ saa embofo bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Yesu ni Boanto,” lenni Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ nsi wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ ǝǝtǝ ǝtǝsa kpǝ, fɛɛ Lɛwɔnɔ lɔni nǝmǝ ete diini.

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: Esu kpǝ kpe nnya ǝsuǝ boeyifo keyifo kǝtǝ Bonamute, fɛɛ Bonamute ɔni ǝmǝ ete uni nɛ.

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: Nya nyifo akpa akpa akpa tsya kpe, fɛɛ Onanto ɔni ǝmǝ ete lǝǝtǝ ǝsuǝle utsyuǝ saa weeyifo ke.

english_verse: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
translation_verse: Ǝtǝsa nnya utsyuǝ saa nkpee, Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ ni nwǝ dítǝ wǝ ǝtǝsa nyamǝ bǝ lǝ betidi ba kenke lǝ bakɛ wǝ ǝsuǝ eto kutǝkǝ.

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: Nya obamba kpe bofokanɔ kekleke ɛyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmfo ke ǝsuǝ. Nya utsyuǝǝ, okpe ǝtǝsa nnya weefi katsya bufi ɛyɛ lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ ke ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Utidi bamba aafo ayanisa buyifo kate. Nya utsyuǝǝ, lǝ kase wuudi Onanto eto ditiki nǝmǝ. Nya utidi bambaa, bǝ lǝ unyǝ awɔnɔ akpa akpa utsyǝ lekpo. Nya utsyuǝ, bele bele eto bɔtɛyi. Nya obamba tsyaa, bele bele eto kasɔ bulǝkǝ.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: Asa nyamfo kenke, Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ lɔni nǝmǝ eto eyifosa ete ini. Lɛwɔnɔ ni leesie ǝtǝsa nyamǝ kǝtǝ utsyuǝ saa ɛyɛ lǝ kase waala ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The body of Christ has many different parts, just as any other body does.
translation_verse: Kristo eyifo utidi eto ǝsuǝ eto uyi wǝ nkpe ǝsuǝ eto akpa lete lete kpǝ. Etidisuǝ ani ete ini tsya ǝsuǝ eto akpa lete letee, lɛkɔ iyifo ani.

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Nko ke ini lǝ bomba ni Yudafɔ ku ǝsǝ bamba eto betidi ku betidi ba ni ndomunyǝ ku betide ba nsi lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ ǝsuǝ tsya eto nti nɛ. Lɛwɔnɔ lɔni nǝ ete béfi béyifo bo ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpaa, ni ǝmǝ díyifo bo ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ sɔni nɛ. Nya Lɛwɔnɔ lɔni nǝmǝ eto ntu ete béfi bǝtǝ bomblɛ dínǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Itsyise etidisuǝ ani eenyifo utidi. Bomu ǝsuǝ eto akpa lete lete kpǝ béfi bákɔ fɛ íyifo utidi ɔni.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Lǝ kokpa átɛyi nkǝ, “Se minni kɔni esoo, mǝmbɔkɔ ku ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ siamǝ,” nyamfo anlɛ bote sitinti nkǝ kuni ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ eto lekpa ditsyuǝ.

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ koto tsya nkǝ, “Se minni dinǝmi esoo, mǝmbɔkɔ ku ǝsuǝ nyamǝ,” nnya tsya anlɛ bote sitinti nkǝ kuni ǝsuǝ eto lekpa ditsyuǝ.

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ǝsuǝ nyamǝ kenke ete ni dinǝmii, ntsyǝ yuubǝ fɛ diɔnɔ lesatsyuǝ? Nya lǝ koto mate tsya inii, ntsyǝ yuutsyǝ fɛ kɔɔnɔ asa eto bɔnyi?

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ bɔlɛ ola bobee, buunyǝ boǝ Onanto efi akpa lete lete úyifo utidi eto dimu nǝmǝ fe kase wǝ kosate lela nkǝ utsyǝ.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Lǝ ǝsuǝ eto lekpa lɔni ete ni utidii, ntsyǝ wuutsyǝ!

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ fe kase boǝnyǝ, akpa nyamǝ fui. Tsya utidi ɔni ete uni.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ se inni ɔni ete unii, dinǝmi embofo kɔni bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Manhiã fǝ lǝ mɔ kulesa ǝsuǝ.” Nya nko ke disi tsya lembofo kokpa bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Manhia fǝ lǝ mɔ kulesa kǝmiǝ.”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Lǝ kusu kutsyuǝ, akpa nya nte fe nnya lǝmbǝ ale saa, nnya bomu lǝ bǝ imbǝ bo lǝ ǝsuǝǝ, boǝmbofo kulesa buyifo.

english_verse: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
translation_verse: Nya akpa nya boembu kakpe lǝ kulesa kemiǝ, nnya bomu buunyi bǝ ele bǝ bɔlɛ ola nɛ. Nya akpa nnya laannɔ bobe bǝ bulǝkǝ bote betidi lǝ bǝnyǝǝ, nnya boalɛ ola kalɔ kalɛ kukɔnɔ nɛ.

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: Nya boenyifo nko kǝtǝ akpa nnya betidi lǝǝnyǝ lefe saa. Fɛɛ Onanto dífi nnya ɔkɔ ku akpa nyamfó nko nya útǝ nkǝ bufi dibu butǝ akpa nnya bomu boǝnyǝ boǝ bǝmbǝ kutǝkǝ saa.

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: Nyamfo te nko bǝ lǝ ntimama lǝ boyɛ bɔkɛ lǝ akpa nyamǝ nti. Bomu lǝ akpa nyamfo kenke lǝ ifi lɔniyifoyifo itǝ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ǝsuǝ eto lekpa ditsyuǝ lǝ kanyi bunyǝǝ, ǝsuǝ nyamǝ kenke ǝǝnyǝ kanyi nya ǝsuǝ eto akpa bamba tsya lǝǝnyǝ kanyi. Nya lǝ batɛsǝ lekpa ditsyuǝǝ, ǝsuǝ eto akpa nyamfo kenke ǝǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Nyaso mimblɛ lɛkɔ biyifo Kristo eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ siamǝ nya mi utsyuǝ saa eyifo wǝ ǝsuǝ nyamǝ eto lekpa lɔni.

english_verse: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
translation_verse: Nya betidi ateate ba Onanto dífi ókpe lǝ bafokanɔfɔ nti ni tutɛɛ, batɔnkpe bamǝ, nya dinuǝfǝ, bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi, nya ditsyǝfǝ, bateasa, fɛ mba leeyifo ayanisa. Nya mba nkpe ǝsuǝale laatsya bufi nye laawunsǝko betidi nye batɔnkotɔnko nye mba laatɛyi bele bele.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: Inni betidi ba kenke ni batɔnkpe nye bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi nye bateasa. Nya inni utsyuǝ saa nkpe ǝsuǝale laafo ayanisa buyifo kǝlǝkǝ kate.

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Nya inni utsyuǝ saa nkpe ǝsuǝale laafo bufi botsya nye laafo belebele bɔtɛyi nye laafo belebele kasɔ bulǝkǝ kate.

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Nyamfo esoo, bifi mi akɔɛsi bitǝkǝ lǝ asa nnya nkpe kutǝkǝ enso ǝsuǝ. Asa nnya nkpe kutǝkǝ enso nyamblɛ kenke ni nnya muubǝ nte mi mfo nɛ.

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya ete moofo bele bele bia betidi ku Onanto eto batɔnkpǝ kenke latɛyi nya maanla betidii, mɔ etiki nnya meedi kenke eyifo efufu fe kase lǝ basɔ tsyɛnsi eto kakpɛ laale.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ nlǝkǝ Onanto eto kɛnyɛ nte nya maanɔ asakulakula kasɔ nya minyi asa nnya kenkee, nya minkpe bofokanɔ bo moofo bufi kanyi atini ketsyiko lǝ nnya kǝnyǝkɔ fɛ mimbɛ lelabii, minni kulesa.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ mfi mɔ bikǝ bia nte mɛ kulaa tsya ntǝ bakombo nya mintǝ miǝ batonsǝ mɛ kulaa tsya fɛ mimbɛ lelabii, minni kulesa kon.

english_verse: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or
translation_verse: Lelabi kpe dibusǝ nya lenkpe kafoɛkpɛ. Diansɛsǝ ǝnǝmi nya diambesǝ ǝsuǝ kato nya diate ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
translation_verse: Lelabi entidi ato nya dianta asa kǝtǝ ǝsuǝ nye dinyǝ ɔblɔ. Nnya lelabi emfi nsusu bua lǝ utidi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
translation_verse: Nya diǝnnyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ asa bua ǝsuǝ. Bomu diǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ asa sɛɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Love is always supportive, loyal, hopeful, and trusting.
translation_verse: Lelabi ǝntǝ disi lǝ bofokanɔ ku kusubebe ǝsuǝ nya diǝńyǝ kekleke lǝ bulǝ saa bulǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Lelabi ǝmbǝ kasekɔ. Onanto eto kɛnyɛ bulǝkǝ kpe, fɛɛ ekpe ke kalokɔ. Bele akpa akpa bɔtɛyi tsya kpe, fɛɛ biubǝ mi kasekɔ. Kobetete kpe sitinti, fɛɛ ko lefe ebubǝ efe.

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
translation_verse: Itsyise kobetete ku kɛnyɛ lǝkǝlǝkǝ eto kǝtǝ ka Onanto dítǝ bo, mamflɔ.

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ lesa nǝ lɛflɔ kenke abɔ dibǝǝ, lesa nǝ dimamflɔ ensi ǝmbɔkɛ.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ minyifo dikpefi, nkǝdi etiki nya mínkanɔ asa kasɔ nya mínkebu disi fe dikpefi. Mɔmɔ wǝ minkabee, ekpefisa nyamfo mensile mɛ kulesa.

english_verse: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
translation_verse: Asa nnya boǝnyǝ mɔmɔɔ, iyifo flufluflu fe kase boǝnyǝ lesa lǝ ahuhɔɛ ǝsuǝ. Nya kama fɛ buunyǝ nnya wuee, ǝnǝmi ku ǝnǝmi. Nya lesa nǝ minyi mɔmɔɔ, mankpako mɛ elo. Fɛɛ kamaa, muubǝ nyǝ lesa saa nya yeokpa wuee fe kase Onanto nyi mɛ mɔmɔ kenke.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Sitintii, asakpa ǝtsyǝ ni nnya nkpe mfo. Nya nyamfo ni bofokanɔ ku kusubebe ku lelabi. Fɛɛ nnya nso lǝ nnya nti kenke ni lelabi.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Nyaso bitǝ lǝ lelabi lǝ diyifo lesa nǝ mi utsyuǝ saa lɔɔbɛbɛ kekleke nkǝ lǝ ɔkɛ. Nya bifi mi ǝnǝmi bitǝkǝ lǝ lɛwɔnɔ eto ǝtǝsa ǝsuǝ. Nya ǝtǝsa nnya nso bofe kenke nibǝ lǝ afo Onanto eto ditiki sɛɛ kɛnyɛbulǝkǝ.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Utidi wǝ laatɛyi bele bele, weedi etiki ku betidi, bomu Onanto ni nwǝ weedinko etiki. Itsyise utidi kuutsyuǝ ǝmbɔnɔ nnya kasɔ, nya lǝ etikididi ǝsuǝ waatɛyi asa ketsyi lǝ asakulakula ǝsuǝ ɛyɛ lǝ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ eto ǝsuǝale ǝsuǝ

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ utidi wǝ lǝǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto ditiki kɛnyɛ kǝtǝ betidi, waawunsǝ ko mǝ nya waakpe mǝ nya woakpe mǝ nkɛale nya wǝǝyuǝsǝ mǝ utu.

english_verse: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
translation_verse: Utidi wǝ laatɛyi bele bele bia eto kasɔ betidi laanɔɔ, wǝ ǝsuǝ ete waawunsǝko. Fɛɛ utidi wǝ lǝtǝkǝ Onanto eto ditiki kɛnyɛ, waawunsǝko bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpǝnǝ kenke.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Iyifo mɔ lelabi lǝ mimblɛ ebofo bele bele bɔtɛyi. Fɛɛ lǝ biofo Onanto eto ditiki kɛnyɛ bulǝkǝ kǝtǝ betidii, yɔɔlɛkɛ itǝ mɛ eso. Itsyise utidi wǝ lǝǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto ditiki kɛnyɛ kǝtǝ betidii, kpe kutǝkǝ eso utidi wǝ laatɛyi bele bele, siankpa utidi utsyuǝ kpe wǝ loofo asa nnya woatɛyi kasɔ bulǝkǝ kǝtǝ mba nti onkpǝ mfo, itǝ lǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa nǝmǝ lǝ bafo kasɔ bɔnɔ.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Nyamfo esoo, mɔ bayimi, lǝ mbǝ mi ɔflɔ nya minlɛ bele bele bele bia kuutsyuǝ saa laannɔ biɔtɛyii, nte kutǝkǝ ko biunyǝ lǝ nnya esuǝ? Biembunyǝ kutǝkǝ saa, enso lǝ nlǝkǝ Onanto eto kɛnyɛtsyǝtsyǝ kasɔ ntemi, nye minte mi lesa ǝtsyuǝ, nye minlǝkǝ Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ kɛnyɛ minte, nye minte mi asa ǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ utidi wǝ leefe kakpatsyaku amfo ke bufǝ kakpadi asa nyamǝ kukɔnɔɔ, ntsyǝ yuutǝ bǝ betidi fo ǝsuǝ bumuǝ itǝ lema?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Nko ke ete ini bǝ utidi di etiki lǝ sele sitsyuǝ ǝsuǝ sia betidi lembofo lesa nǝ woatɛyi kasɔ bɔnɔ. Bomu wuubǝ uyifo fe ufiebi wǝ lénya údufǝ lǝ difule ǝsuǝ, nya kasɔnɔnɔ lǝmbǝ.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ utsyuǝ annɔ sele sia maatɛyi eto kasɔɔ, ete mfiani ɔfɔ lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ, nya wǝ tsya áfiani ɔfɔ lǝ mɔ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
translation_verse: Se ǝnǝmi lɛsɛ mi lǝ Lɛwɔnɔ eto ǝtǝsa bofo ǝsuǝǝ, bitǝ lǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ ɛsɛ mi lǝ befo ǝtǝsa nnya lɔɔwunsǝko bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa nǝ luunǝ mǝ isuko sɛtɔ enso.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Nya utidi wǝ laatɛyi bele bele, ele bǝ ɔtɔ Onanto lǝ utǝ wǝ ǝtǝsa bǝ lǝ ofo nnya woatɛyi kɛnyɛbulǝkǝ.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Itsyise lǝ nyɛ lǝ kusu komfo ǝsuǝ nto ola, mɔ lɛwɔnɔ ete nlɛ olaa, fɛɛ inni lǝ mɔ nsusu ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Nyaso be ele bǝ nyifo? Mooto ola ku mɔ lɛwɔnɔ kekleke, fɛɛ mooto ola ku mɔ nsusu tsya. Nya muusǝ ǝsǝ ku mɔ lɛwɔnɔ, fɛɛ ǝsǝ nnya muusǝ ebutsyi taa mɔ nsusu kemiǝ tsya.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ǝńyǝ alɛ Onanto ani bɔsɔ itsyi lǝ fǝ lɛwɔnɔ ǝsuǝ kee, ntsyǝ yuuyifo fɛ utidi wǝ ńyǝ fǝ lǝ akɔ mfo lɔɔnɔ lesa nǝ fanlɛ bɔtɛyi fɛ wɔɔtɛyi nkǝ fǝ olatoto lǝ ibǝ itǝ fe kase fanlɛ Onanto ani bɔsɔ se woannɔ lesa nǝ fanlɛ bɔtɛyi mfó?

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ fǝ ani nnya faasɔ Onanto alɛkɛ kula tsya, betidi bamba embunyǝ kutǝkǝ saa betsyiko lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Maasɔ Onanto ani bǝ meedinko Onanto etiki lǝ bele bele ǝsuǝ enso mi nti eto utsyuǝ saa.

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ bo katsyakɔɔ, moola miǝ maatɛyi etiki anɔ nnya eto kasɔ betidi lɔɔnɔ lǝ mfi nnya nte betidi asa enso bǝ nkǝdi etiki kpǝ lǝ bele bele ǝsuǝ sia betidi laannɔ kasɔ.

english_verse: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
translation_verse: Mɔ bayimi, bienyifo mi ǝsuǝ fe bekpefi lǝ mi disibubu ǝsuǝ. Bomu biyifo mi ǝsuǝ fe beninǝ lǝ mi disibubu ǝsuǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ abua eto esu ǝsuǝǝ, biyifo mi ǝsuǝ fe bekpefi seka.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝǝ, Boanto Onanto átɛyi nkǝ, “Muudi etiki ku mɔ Kusǝ eto betidi ɛyɛ lǝ betidi ba laatɛyi bele bele ǝsuǝ. Nya muudi etiki ɛyɛ lǝ bafɔ eto kɛnyɛ ǝsuǝ, Fɛɛ bembutsyue mɔ ɔkɔɛ koto.”
