translation_verse: Mfo nya óto ɔkɔɛ bembembe fe tsyata seka. Nya se ófã nkoo, bánɔ bukumi sekuanse eto ɔkuɛ kato.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ mínɔ bukumi bomfoo, nla lǝ nwɔni asa nnya mínɔ ntsyǝ. Fɛɛ nnɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ wǝ lɛbɔ utsyi kato lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ti akulasa nnya bukumi sekuanse siamǝ lɛtɛyi ǝtsyǝ, manwɔni nnya ǝtsyǝ.”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Ete mínyǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ nńyǝ lǝ kasɔ ku lekpo ǝsuǝ lébesǝ wǝ kodiani kato.

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: Nya óka ntamu lǝ Onanto wǝ nsi nkpǝ lefe saa, wǝ díyifo kato ku nnya nkpe kasɔ mfo ku asa nnya nkpe lǝ ke, lekpo ku asa nnya nkpe lǝ ni eto diye ǝsuǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Lefe biasǝbiasǝ saa ensibǝ.

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Nya lǝ ɔtɔnkpe kuansele ǝmǝ efe wǝ kakpatsyaku koo, ete Onanto eto ketsyue kulakula ka bábe letsya bǝtǝ wǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba ni wǝ beyifoyifo ebubǝ itǝ.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, ɔkɔɛ wǝ ditsyi kato dídinko mɛ etiki esi ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Su afo kukue kɛkɛ ko básini bǝtsyǝ nkpe lǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ nńyǝ lǝ lekpo ku kasɔ ǝsuǝ eto kɔni.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya mínsu ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ ɔflɔ nya míntɛyi wǝ minkǝ ufi kukue kɛkɛ komɛ utǝ mɛ. Ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Fo lǝ ayimi ko. Kɔɔkɛ kalo lǝ fǝ kafo, fɛɛ lǝ kɛnyɛ kɔɔkɛ kamini fe bablɔ seka.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Mmfo kukue kɛkɛ komǝ lǝ wǝ kɔni, nya míyimi ko, nya kɔkɛ kamuni fe bablɔ lǝ mɔ kɛnyɛ. Fɛɛ lefe nǝ mímmini ko mínlosǝǝ, kɔkɛ kalo lǝ mɔ kafo kǝmiǝ.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ átɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Ele bǝ esi abe Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya ǝtǝ ese kpǝ, betidi kpǝ, bele bele ku baka kpǝ.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Fɛɛ mensusu diyo nǝmǝ eto lekpata, itsyise béfi mfo bǝtǝ mba lenni Yudafɔ. Mǝ lǝǝbǝ botaka kǝńyǝ lǝ umǝ kɛnkɛ ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ itǝ afa fosi ana afa ǝnuǝ.

english_verse: My two witnesses will wear sackcloth, while I let them preach for
translation_verse: Mootsyese mɔ badansiɛ inuǝ ba lɔɔkɛ lǝ nkumfia ǝsuǝ babé mɔ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya itǝ eyi kɔkpi alofa ǝnuǝ awosi akua (

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi inuǝ bamfo ni nnuǝbi eto eyi ǝnuǝ ku kakandiɛyi inuǝ nnya ńyǝ lǝ kawunsiɛ eto Onamute eto ǝnǝmi nɛ.

english_verse: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Lǝ utsyuǝ ala nkǝ weeyifo mǝ kabuaa, ute aabɔ ketsyi lǝ mǝ nnyɛ katsylɔsǝ mǝ. Nyaso utidi wǝ ke laala nkǝ weeyifo mǝ kabuaa, kusu komfo ǝsuǝ bɔɔyɛ balo wǝ nɛ.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Bakpe ǝsuǝale baafo ɔsɔmi kusu buti bǝ woanni lefe nǝ banlɛ Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya bobe. Bakpe ǝsuǝale tsya lǝ etubiǝ kenke ǝsuǝ nya bootǝ bǝ ntu bomǝ fiani kɔtɔ. Bakpe ǝsuǝale tsya bǝ kanyinyǝnyǝ ate ate bǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke ǝsuǝ lǝ lefe nǝ kee baala ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Lefe nǝ ke babé Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ letsya balosǝǝ, ubutubuki kplɛ wǝ lɛbɔ utsyi lǝ dikpembiǝ kplɛ nǝmǝ ubǝǝ, ebɔkpɔnko mǝ utǝkǝ mǝ kato olo mǝ.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Mǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ kpenkpe siamǝ ebolabelǝ obia lǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ bǝtǝkǝ diye kulakula bǝ Egipte nye Sodom eto obia. Mfo básɔ mǝ Onamute lǝ uyikabie ǝsuǝ nɛ.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ ku kutsyǝ ǝsuǝǝ, betidi ebutsyi ese saa ese, nlɛ saa nlɛ, bele tɛyitɛyi saa beletɛyitɛyi ku kawunsiǝ eto betidi ba tã bǝbǝ babe mǝ bakɔ nya bǝmbɔtɔnɔ bǝ bebikǝ mǝ.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Nya kaawunsiǝ eto betidi ebunyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ betidi inuǝ bamfo eto ukpe eso. Booto keyudu, bǝtǝ bǝtsyuǝ asa, itsyise Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi inuǝ bamfo ekpe esoo, lǝ kanyi kpǝ ka bǝnǝ baboko betidi ǝsuǝ eso.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ ku kutsyǝ kama, ufiebi wǝ lǝǝtǝ nkpǝ ditsyi Onanto ɔflɔ ǝbǝ údufǝ lǝ mǝ nya bátaka bǝńyǝ bǝbǝ nkpǝ! Sikpi ǝmuǝ mba kenke dínyǝ mǝ tinti.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi inuǝ bamǝ ánɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ wǝ lɛbɔ útsyi kato wǝ lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bibǝ kato mfo!” Kase mǝ balo ńyǝ banlɛ mǝ bobee, bédufǝ lǝ lɛsɔmiloku ǝsuǝ nya dítsyi mǝ disuko kato.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Lǝ lefe nǝmfó ǝsuǝ tututuu, kasɔ étinkǝ kekleke nya umǝ eto lekpafosile léba lékpo kasɔ muɛmuɛmuɛ, nya betidi akpi akuanse díkpe. Sikpi ǝmuǝ betidi fɛfɛ bamǝ nya bátɛsǝ Onanto wǝ nkpe kato eto diye!

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Kanyinyǝnyǝ nuǝfǝ ébubǝkafe, fɛɛ kǝtsyǝfǝ teke kama ɛlɛ bubǝ kitikpo. Béfe Kakpatsyaku Kuansele.

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe kuansele éfe wǝ kakpatsyaku, nya mínɔ kakɔɛ bembembe lǝ kato ka nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Kawunsiǝ kamfo eto sekakedikɔ áfiani Boanto Onanto ku Wǝ Nwǝ Bákpo Ɔhile Bǝtsyǝ eto kale, nya wuudi seka idu manlo isu manlo!”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: banlɛ bɔtɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bonamute wǝ ni ǝsuǝalenyǝ kenke, wǝ nkpe nya ɔkɛ! Bɔasɔ fǝ ani, itsyise éfi fǝ ǝsuǝale kplɛ nyamǝ nya fétsyiko seka budi.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Kafo ányi betidi ba dímamfo fǝ bánɔ. Lefe nǝ ele bǝ lǝkǝ fǝ lɛblɔfi ate eboyo. Lefe nǝ ele bǝ bakponko bekpenkpe lekpo eboyo. Lefe nǝmǝ eboyo bǝ lǝ afo fǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ba ni fǝ beyifoyifo, bamǝ kayo sɛɛ, ku fǝ betidi kenke ba nkpe dibu itǝ fǝ, bakplɛnyǝ ku betidi wuewue ta kenke. Nya lefe eboyo bǝ lǝ atsyɔlɔsǝ betidi bua ba lélo fǝ betidi lǝ kawunsiǝ.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Básini Onanto eto kedikǝtǝkɔ kɛnkɛ ka kato, nya mínyǝ kɛnyɛklekle eto lelaka nǝmǝ mfó. Nya difule léto ɔsi díńyǝ ute ku kudu kplɛ. Kato ékumi nya kasɔ dítinkǝ nya etubankli kplɛ kpǝ lélo.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Usio ǝmǝ lɛ kafo. Kafo kamǝ lɛ wǝ bunǝ, nya se kafo nlɛ wǝ bunǝ esoo, úkafa bembembe.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Ekpinsa bamba esi ɛbɔ lǝ difule bio! Owa kplɛ wǝ nkpe sikpi, lɛsɛ, nkpe esi akuanse ku akpɛ lefosi, nya wǝ disi saa ntsyi lekankpakpa tsya lɛbɔ.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Úfi wǝ uke únǝ befandebi eto lekpa tsyǝfǝ utsyiko difule nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ kato ókpo lǝ kasɔ ǝsuǝ. Nnya kamaa, ótaka uńyǝ lǝ usio ǝmǝ eto sɛtɔ nkǝ lǝ ole ubi ǝmǝ koo, lǝ umuǝ wǝ ɔmini.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Nya usio ǝmǝ léle osaibi, wǝ luudi seka lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke ǝsuǝ, ku situ eto oka eto uyi. Fɛɛ Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ étsyi ubi ǝmǝ údunko mlǝ úsuko Onanto ku wǝ lekankpomɛ nǝmǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Usio ǝmǝ étsyetsyi úsu mfim nti kaka Onanto lɛlɔ kǝsiǝkɔ utsyǝ wǝ, kaka bɔɔlɛ wǝ ola itǝ eyi kɔkpi kɔni eyi alofa ǝnuǝ eyi fosiakua (

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Mfo nya lema díbǝ lǝ kato. Nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ninǝ kenke wǝ baakpo Mikael lɛtɔnko Onanto eto batɔnkpe bátaka lema lǝ owa kplɛ ku wǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ ǝsuǝ nya batsyaka lema.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Bǝtǝkǝ owa kplɛ ku wǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ kato, nya bénsitǝ wǝ ku wǝ batɔnkpe bamǝ kusu bǝ bǝsiǝ lǝ kato mfó bio.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Bála owa kplɛ ǝmǝ bǝlǝkǝ lǝ kato mfó. Wǝ ni owa koko kplɛ ǝmǝ baakpo olo ǝmǝ nye Obonsam nɛ, wǝ lɛwɛ kawunsiǝ kenke okpe nɛ. Nya bála wǝ ku wǝ batɔnkpe kenke bátsyese kasɔ mfo.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nya mínɔ ɔkɔɛ kplɛ utsyuǝ lǝ kato nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔmɔɔ, Onanto eto bofokǝtǝ utidi ebubǝ! Onanto eto ǝsuǝale ku sekadidi ebubǝ. Wuulǝkǝ wǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ eto ǝsuǝale ote. Itsyise bǝlǝkǝ nwǝ díkala bo bayimi kɛnyɛ ketsye ku kakɔmi kǝtǝ Onanto báto bákpe itsyi kato.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Bǝtǝkǝ wǝ kato ɛyɛ lǝ Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto kɔtɔ ku ɔnukualɛtiki nnya ǝsuǝ bábe letsya. Itsyise bátɔnɔ faa bǝ bookpe nya béfi mǝ nkpǝ bǝtǝ fe keditǝtǝ seka.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Nyaso binyǝ disuǝyuǝ mimba nkpe kato mfo! Fɛɛ mǝǝtǝ kasɔ ku lekpo mbɔbɔɛ! Itsyise Obonsam áyifǝ úbǝ mi ɔflɔ ku lɛblɔfi. Itsyise uyi nkǝ lefe sɛkɛ hã dibu itǝ wǝ.”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Se owa kplɛ ǝmǝ dínyǝ utsyǝ lekpo nkǝ bála wǝ báto bakpe lǝ kasɔ mfóo, ɔ́ti usio wǝ léle osaibi ǝmǝ kama.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Nya béfi kɔlikpo eto mbaba kplɛ nuǝ bǝtǝ wǝ lǝ ufunini usu kaka wɔɔkɛ lǝ mfiminti kaka bɔɔlɛ wǝ ola itǝ alɛ ǝtsyǝ ku kutsyǝ bǝ lǝ ɔbɔ ofe lǝ owa kplɛ ǝmǝ eto ani.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, owa kplɛ ǝmǝ ala ntu kpenkpenkpe ntsyuǝ bǝ lǝ bɔbɔdi fe ketu kplɛ busu butsyi usio ǝmǝ budunko.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kasɔ áfo kǝtǝ usio ǝmǝ nya kéminkli kɛnyɛ kámini ntu boǝ kenke owa kplɛ ǝmǝ léla.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Ete owa kplɛ ǝmǝ dínyǝ ɔblɔ kekleke ókpe usio ǝmǝ, nya údu úsu nkǝ weesu ɔkpɔnko usio ǝmǝ eto befluflu ba díbu ba leetsyue Onanto eto etiki koto nya báfo ɔnukualɛ wǝ bǝlǝkǝ bate mǝ lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ bánɔ.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Nya owa kplɛ ǝmǝ díńyǝ lǝ lekpo kɛnyɛ mfó.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nnyǝ ubutubuki utsyuǝ nlɛ bɔbɔ ketsyi lekpo kǝmiǝ. Okpe esi akuanse ku aakpɛ lefosi. Lɛɛkpɛ saa tsyi lekankpakpa. Nya báwɔni mbusiotiki nnya ńyǝ lǝ Onanto ǝsuǝ bǝtǝkǝ lǝ wǝ disi saa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Ubutubuki ǝmǝ ale ɔkplɛle, nya wǝ akpa te fe ukiti eto ale, nya wǝ kɛnyɛ te fe tsyata eto kale. Owa kplɛ ǝmǝ éfi wǝ kosate eto ǝsuǝale, wǝ lekankpomɛ ku wǝ sekadidi eto ǝsuǝale kplɛ nyamǝ útǝ ubuki ǝmfo.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Ubutubuki ǝmǝ eto disi lɔni te fenkǝ léfo dikpemfabe kekleke ditsyuǝ, fɛɛ lefabe nǝmǝ ébukpe. Nyamfo éyifo kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke naa, nya bǝtǝkǝko ubutubuki ǝmǝ kama.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Betidi ésumu owa kplɛ ǝmǝ, itsyise kúfi ko ǝsuǝale kútǝ ubutubuki ǝmfo. Bémusu ubuki ǝmǝ bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Owoe nte fe ubutubuki ǝmfo? Nya owoe loofo wǝ bɔkpɔnko?”

english_verse: The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ ubutubuki ǝmǝ ǝsuǝale údi emutiki nya lǝ Onanto ǝsuǝ, nya ditǝ únyǝ ǝsuǝale itǝ afa fosi ana afa ǝnuǝ.

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Útsyiko Onanto eto diye, wǝ kǝsiǝkɔ ku betidi ba kenke nsi lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ kato bukudi.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ wǝ kusu bǝ ɔkpɔnko Onanto eto betidi lǝ utǝkǝ mǝ kato, nya besi bǝtǝ wǝ ǝsuǝale lǝ ese saa ese, betidi saa betidi, bele saa bele ku kafo saa kafo ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Nya betidi ba kenke nsi lǝ kasɔ mfo ebusumu wǝ enso betidi ba hã eto aye báwɔni fɛ béyifo kawunsiǝ bákpe lǝ nkpǝ eto kukue ko ni Lesamubi nǝ bálo eto bale.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Nwǝ ato nkpee, lǝ ɔnɔ.

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Utidi wǝ ele bǝ bǝmuǝ, boomuǝ wǝ. Nya nwǝ tsya ele bǝ balo ku lesiabi tsyaa, ele bǝ balo wǝ nko. Nyamfo esoo, mi Onanto eto betidi, ele bǝ bińyǝ kekleke lǝ kanyinyǝnyǝ ǝsuǝ nya lǝ bidi ɔnukualɛ bitǝ wǝ.

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Mfo minsi mínyǝ ubutubuki bua bamba wǝ lɛbɔ utsyi kasɔ. Ukǝ aakpɛ ǝnuǝ lǝ disi fe lekposubi eto ale, fɛɛ údi etiki fe owa kplɛ ǝmǝ seka.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Ófo ǝsuǝale kplɛ nnya nkpe wǝ utsyiko lǝ ututɛ ǝmǝ ɔflɔ, nya úyifo nnya keyifo lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi. Útǝ nkǝ betidi ba nsi kasɔ mfo ku ke betidi kenke lǝ besumu ubutubuki bua tutɛ ǝmǝ wǝ bǝtǝkǝ dikpemfabe kekleke fɛ díkǝkpǝ.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Ubutubuki nuǝfǝ ǝmfo éyifo ayanisa kplɛ. Útǝ ute kulaa tsya ábɔ utsyi kato úbǝ kasɔ lǝ betidi ba kenke eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Ɔ́wɛ betidi ba kenke nsi lǝ kasɔ lǝ ayanisa nnya bǝtǝ wǝ kusu bǝ uyifo lǝ ubutubuki tutɛ eto ǝnǝmi eso. Ubuki ǝmǝ átɛyi kawunsiǝ eto betidi nkǝ bato ubutubuki bua wǝ eto disi bǝtǝkǝ lefabe ku lesiabi fɛ ɔkɛ nkpǝ eto ditsyǝbi lǝ befi bakpe wǝ dibu.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Bǝtǝ bǝ ubutubuki bua nuǝfǝ ǝmǝ nkpe ǝsuǝale mfoo, wɔnɔ nkpǝ okpo lǝ ubukitonto tutɛ ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ ofo etiki budi nya lǝ utǝ lǝ balo betidi ba kenke lembusumu wǝ.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Ubutubuki ǝmǝ ǝtǝ nkǝ bǝtǝkǝ betidi ba kenke, betidi wuewue ku beninǝ, batabinyǝ ku bakombo, ndomufɔ ku mba nsi lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ ǝsuǝ leba lǝ mǝ kodiani lee lǝ mǝ nto.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Kuutsyuǝ saa embofo lesa bote lee oya lǝ bǝntǝkǝ wǝ leba nǝmfo, ini ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ eto diye nye leklabi nǝ nyǝ itǝ wǝ diye.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Ditiki nǝmfo atɔ itǝ kobetete. Utsyuǝ saa wǝ laanɔ asa kasɔ bu ubutubuki bua eto leklabi nǝmfo obe. Itsyise leklambi nǝmfo ńyǝ ditǝ utidi eto diye. Ni leklabi ni alofa akua awosi akua akua (

english_verse: I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nnyǝ Lesamubi nǝmǝ se dinyǝ lǝ Sion eto kɔtini ǝsuǝ. Nnyǝ betidi akpi kolofa awosi ana ana (

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Ete mínɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ wǝ ditsyi kato fe ntu bo nlɛ boto lǝ ketsyue kplɛ nye ofamfa seka bembembe. Ɔkɔɛ wǝ minɔ esi ole fe betidi nlɛ kasanku bɔsɔ.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Betidi akpi kolofa awosi ana ana bamǝ ńyǝ lǝ lekankpomɛ nǝmǝ ɔflɔ, asa ana nya nsi nkpǝ ku beninǝ bamǝ. Balɛ kusǝ fɔfɔ ko mǝ hã lekase busǝ. Lǝ betidi kenke ntii, mǝ hã ni mba dinyǝ bofo nɛ.

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Mǝ ni basani ba lɛlɛ mǝ ǝsuǝ kɛnkɛ betsyiko lǝ sɛsɔnɔdi ǝsuǝ. Mǝ ni mba dítǝkǝko Lesamubi nǝmǝ kaka ke lɛntɔ. Mǝ disi báfo kayo nɛ lǝ betidi nti nya mǝ ni betidi tutɛ ba befi bǝtǝ Onanto ku Lesamubi nǝmǝ nɛ.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Bantɛyi tɔ sidia bǝnyǝ, nya bǝnyǝ tɔ kabua saa lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝnyǝ. Yohanes Ǝnyǝ Kato Eto Batɔnkpe Itsyǝ Bǝtsyuǝ.

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nnyǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe bamba utsyuǝ wǝ nlɛ bufunini kabe kato fanlofanlo ku nkpǝ manlo eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ itǝ betidi ba nkpe kasɔ eto lekpake saa, ese saa, bele saa ku nsɔ saa nsɔ.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Ófá ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bibu Onanto lǝ bɛtɛsǝ wǝ sɛkplɛ siamǝ! Itsyise lefe éboyo bǝ okponko kawunsiǝ kenke lekpo. Bisumu wǝǝ diyifo kato ku kasɔ, lekpo ku ntu bo laayifǝ saa eto okle!”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Onato eto ɔtɔnkpe nuǝfǝ ǝtǝkǝko ututɛ nya ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Oba o-o! Babilonia wǝ lefo diye ǝmǝ aba sitinti! Útǝ betidi kenke ba díkpe nte ɛyɛ lǝ mǝ sɛsɔnɔdidi eto lebua ǝsuǝ ǝkpǝ nte fe bunǝ bǝnǝ mu bǝkpǝ!”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe tsyǝfǝ ǝtǝkǝko betutɛ inuǝ kama nya ɔnlɛ bofa bembembe katɛyi nkǝ, “Utsyuǝ saa ke lusumu ubutubuki ǝmǝ ku wǝ awesa nyamǝ nya utǝ bǝtǝkǝ leba lǝ wǝ kato nye lǝ wɛ kɔni,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: ebunǝ Onanto eto lɛblɔfi ku wǝ lefonyi kekleke nǝmǝ fe nte bo nkpe ale lɛkɔbetɔ nǝ béfi bákpo lǝ wǝ lɛblɔfi ǝsuǝ! Boofi ute ku ute eto leba bǝtǝkǝ mba diyifo nyamfo bǝnyǝ kanyi lǝ Onanto eto batɔnkpe kɛnkɛ ku Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto ǝnǝmi.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Ute wǝ nlɛ mǝ botonsǝ eto kuyuǝ ko nlɛ bɔbɔ ketsyi lǝ kanyinyǝnyǝ kamɛ ǝsuǝ ebukabe kato idu manlo isu manlo. Nya lefe saa, ketsye ku kakɔmii, betidi ba dísumu ubutubuki ǝmǝ ku wɛ awesa ku mba ǝsuǝ wǝ diye eto leba ntǝkǝ embɔkɛ diyuǝ kulefe.

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: “Nyamfo lɛ bote nkǝ mba ni Onanto eto betidi nya beetsyue Onanto eto etiki koto nya báfo Yesu banɔ, ńyǝ kekleke lǝ mǝ kanyinyǝnyǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Nya mínɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ kato ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Wɔni nyamfo ǝtsyǝ bǝ idu mɔmɔ ɛntɔɔ, bákpo betidi ba diyifo bani ku Bonamute fɛ békpe atsyɔ!” Nya Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ léfo lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ nkǝ, “Ee, bootsyǝ ɔnwɛ lǝ mǝ nyifo ǝsuǝ. Nya boonyǝ mǝ kayofofo.” Kawunsiǝ Eto Bɛkuɛnke Eto Lefodife Eboyo.

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, nnyǝ asɔmiloku flubia ǝtsyuǝ, nnya ǝsuǝ lele utidi eto ditsyǝbi nsi. Úti sika eto lekankpakpa lǝ disi nya ɔnlɛ sɛyikpɛbe sia nkpe kobe lǝ kɔni.

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya ɔtɔnkpe bamba utsyuǝ lɛbɔ utsyi lǝ Disumuyo nǝmǝ kafo nya ófã bembembe ótsyese nwǝ nsi lǝ asɔmiloku nyamǝ ǝsuǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bike biamǝ lefodife éboyo, nyaso fi fǝ sɛyikpɛbe lǝ afodi bidike biamǝ itsyise kasɔ eto bidike biamǝ eboyo lefodife!”

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Nya nwǝ nsi lǝ lɛsɔmiloku nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ lɛwunya wǝ sɛyikpɛbe siamǝ ofia nya ófodi kawunsiɛ eto bɛkuɛnke biamǝ kenke.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Nnya kama, nnyǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe bamba wǝ lɛbɔ utsyi Onanto eto kedikǝtǝkɔ, nya wǝ tsya nlɛ sɛyikpɛbe sia nkpe kobe.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe bamba tsya wǝ nsi lǝ ute eto kɛnyɛ, lɛbɔ utsyi kedikǝtǝkɔ eto leba mfó úbǝ. Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmfo áfã ku ɔkɔɛ bembembe ɔ́tɛyi ɔtɔnkpe wǝ nlɛ sɛyikpɛbe nkǝ, “Fi fǝ sɛyikpɛbe sia nkpe kobe lǝ alɛ nte eto eyibibi nnya nkpe lǝ kawunsiǝ eto ɔkpɔ ǝmǝ kafo, itsyise eyibibi nyamǝ ebobe itǝ bɔlɛ.”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Nyaso Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ awunya wǝ sɛyikpɛbe siamǝ ofia nya ɔlɛ ebibi nyamǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke ókpo lǝ kaka bǝǝkǝ eyibibi eto ntu kakpo ka ni Onanto eto lɛblɔfi kplɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Nya béké eyibibi nyamǝ lǝ kaka ǝsuǝ beeke eyibibi eto ntu kakpo ǝmǝ lǝ umǝmflɔ, nya kɔtɔ lɛbɔ kɔ́bɔdi fe esunkpo lofa ǝnu sɔ̃ɔ̃ lǝ dikulǝbi ǝsuǝ nya kotsyo tsya fe akpa anɔ.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nsi nnyǝ ǝnyǝsa kplɛ bamba kato nnya díyifo ǝnyǝsa nnya lɛkɛ ale. Nnyǝ Onanto eto batɔnkpe ekuanse ba lɛkɔbe kanyinyǝnyǝ akuanse fɛfɛ banlɛ, itsyise nnya dílǝkǝ Onanto eto lɛblɔfi fɛfɛ éte nɛ.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Nya mínyǝ lesa nǝ nte fe ahuhɔɛ eto lekpo nnya letsyaka ku nte wǝ lɛsɛ hee. Nnyǝ betidi ba tsya dílǝkǝ ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku wǝ asawenwe nyamǝ ku nwǝ eto diyee, aklansa dílǝkǝ éte. Bǝńyǝ lǝ ahuhɔɛ eto lekpo nǝmǝ nya banlɛ kasanku ka Onanto dítǝ mǝ.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Nya banlɛ Moses, Onanto eto uyifoyifo ku Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto kusǝ busǝ katɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bonamute Onanto, Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke, ntsyǝ fǝ eyifosa lɛmɔ iyifo yanii mmǝ! Ǝse nnya kenke eto Oka, nya fǝ asayifoyifo lɛflɔ nya fǝ etiki diyifo ɔnukualɛ.

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: Owoe loofo buńyǝ fǝ lǝ ǝnǝmi nya wǝmbote fǝ sikpi, O Bonamute, nya wembufi atɛsǝ utǝ fǝ dinye? Itsyise fǝ hã diyifo kɛnkɛ, nya kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke luubǝ bǝtǝ fǝ dibu. Itsyise bǝlǝkǝ fǝ asasɛ yifoyifo bate utsyuǝ saa ǝnyǝ.”

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, ńnyǝ se Kedikǝtǝkɔ lǝ kato ku kaka ni Kakɛnkɛkɔ Kenke lǝ mfó lɛsini nte.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Nya Onanto eto batɔnkpe ekuanse ba nlɛ kanyinyǝnyǝ akuanse nyamǝ lɛbɔ betsyi lǝ Disumuyo nǝmǝ. Bakpe awu flubia nya nlɛ buyǝnde nya béfi sika sia eto kekue bákle mǝ akɔɛsi.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Mfo nya asa ana nyamǝ nsi nkpǝ eto ɔni dífi sika sia eto ekplibi akuanse nnya díyi ku Onanto wǝ nsi nkpǝ manlo manlo eto lɛblɔfi útǝ batɔnkpe ekuanse bamǝ.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Nya kuyuǝ ko lɛbɔ kutsyi lǝ Onanto eto diyǝnde ku ǝsuǝale nyamǝ diyi Disumuyo nǝmǝ kenke, nya kuutsyuǝ saa mamfo Disumuyo nǝmǝ budufǝ ese lefe nǝ efimbi akuanse nya batɔnkpe ekuanse bamǝ lébokodibǝ éfe.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Mfó minɔ ɔkɔɛ bembembe utsyuǝ lɛbɔ utsyi Disumuyo nlɛ batɔnkpe ekuanse bamǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Bisu bɛwuninsǝ Onanto eto lɛblɔfi eto ɛkpɛ ekuanse nyamǝ bekpo lǝ kawunsiǝ ǝsuǝ!”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe tutɛ ǝmǝ esu ɔwuninsǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ ǝmǝ okpo lǝ kawunsiǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyamfo ǝtǝ bǝ afabe nya nkpe kɛnyɛ ale, leefi, abɔ ekpo lǝ mba ubutubuki ǝmǝ eto leba ntǝkǝ ku mba disumu wǝ awenwesa.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Nya ɔtɔnkpe nuǝfǝ lɛwuninsǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ ókpo lǝ lekpo ǝsuǝ, nya ntu bomǝ lɛtsyi bɔwuninsǝ bofiani fe betidi kpenkpe eto kɔtɔ seka, itǝ bǝ asa nnya nkpe nkpǝ lǝ lekpo nǝmǝ kenke ekpe.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Nya ɔtɔnkpe tsyǝfǝ lɛwuninsǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ ókpo lǝ ntu ku etubiǝ kenke ǝsuǝ nya nyamblɛ léfiani kɔtɔ.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Mfo mínɔ se ɔtɔnkpe wǝ laabe ntu lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “O ɔkɛnkɛnyǝ, fǝǝ nsi nkpǝ idu taa kokolefe ke, fǝ akpokponkpo kenke aayɛ lǝ kusu tsyɔntsyɔ ǝsuǝ lo!

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Betidi bamfo ákpo fǝ betidi ku fǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto kɔtɔ kasɔ. Nyaso fǝtǝ mǝ kɔtɔ fǝ bǝnǝ nɛ. Kotonǝnǝ komfo ǝńyǝ kutǝ mǝ tinti!”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Mfo mínɔ ɔkɔɛ lɛbɔ utsyi Onanto eto kedikǝtǝkɔ eto leba ǝsuǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Bonamute Onanto, Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke! Fǝ lekpokponkpo yɛ lǝ kɔtsyɔntsyɔsu ku lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe nale áwuninsǝ nnya nkpe lǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ ǝsuǝ ókpo lǝ kofe eto ǝnǝmi ǝsuǝ nya Onanto dítǝ ko ǝsuǝale nkǝ otonsǝ betidi ku ko utefofo.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Ute eto ǝsuǝale átonsǝ mǝ nya békudi Onanto eto diye. Onanto kpé ǝsuǝale lǝ kanyi kamfo ǝsuǝ, fɛɛ báni bǝ bǝmbɔtsyi bawuninsǝ lǝ mǝ abua ǝsuǝ batɛsɛ wǝ sɛkplɛ.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Ɔtɔnkpe nɔnde ǝmǝ áwuninsǝ nnya nkpe lǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ mǝ ǝsuǝ ókpo lǝ ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ eto lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ. Kale ko léna lǝ ubutubuki ǝmǝ eto sekakedikɔ kenke nya betidi lɛyimi anyi lǝ kanyi ka banlɛ bunyǝ eso.

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: Nya betidi bamǝ díkudi Onanto wǝ nkpe kato lǝ kanyi ka banlɛ bunyǝ lǝ afabe nyamǝ eso. Tsyaa, báni bǝ bǝmbɔtsyi bawuninsǝ lǝ mǝ abua ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe kuande ǝmǝ áwuninsǝ asa nnya nkpe lǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ ǝsuǝ ókpo lǝ Ketukplɛ Frate ǝsuǝ. Ketu kamǝ ákpi kǝtǝ baka ba ditsyi kofekatokɔ kusu lǝ bayɛ bafe.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Mfó nya mínyǝ awɔnɔ bua ǝtsyǝ nnya nte fe asasa lɛbɔ itsyi lǝ owa kplɛ ǝmǝ, ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku bidiabidia eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi ǝmǝ eto nnyɛ.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Beyifo awɔnɔ bua nnya leeyifo ayanisa. Awɔnɔ bua etsyǝ nyamfo leesu kakpadi kawunsiǝ eto baka kakɔ itǝ lema bɔkpɔ lǝ Boanto Onato, Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke ǝmǝ, eto Diyi Kplɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: “Bɛnɔ! Muubǝ fe uyu seka! Disuǝyuǝ itǝ nwǝ luutǝkǝ ǝnǝ nya umuǝ ǝsuǝ utsyǝ ku wǝ awu bikelɛlɛ wǝmbɔyɛ leyaleya okpe wǝ ǝsuǝ kofani lǝ betidi nti.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Mfó nya awɔnɔ bua nyamǝ lékpa baka bamǝ bǝtsyǝ lǝ kaka baakpo Armagedon lǝ Hebri eto sele ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe kuansele ǝmǝ lɛwuninsǝ nya nkpe lǝ wǝ ɔkpɛ ǝmǝ kafo okpo lǝ difule ǝsuǝ. Ete ɔkɔɛ kplɛ utsyuǝ lɛbɔ utsyi lǝ lekankpomɛ nǝ nte lǝ Disumuyo nǝmǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Elo o-o!”

english_verse: There were flashes of lightning, roars of thunder, and the worst earthquake in all history.
translation_verse: Mfóo, difule ko ditsyiko ɔsi boto, kafa kekumi nya kasɔ ditinkǝ kekleke. Kasɔ butinkǝ kamfo eto okle mǝmbǝ tɔ kǝnyǝ idu lǝ kawunsiǝ eto kasɔ ketsyikokɔ.

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ ába lǝ akpa ǝtsyǝ, nya ese ese eto kǝmǝnkplɛ léba. Onanto ákloma Babilonia kplɛ nya utǝ únǝ nte útsyiko lǝ wǝ lɛkɔbe nǝmǝ kafo wǝ lɛblɔfi nǝmǝ eto nte.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Nsɔ bo ntu letsyilama kenke ánya vi, nya atini kenke tsya lénya.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Mfo batɔnkpe ekuanse bamǝ eto ɔni wǝ nlɛ kakpɛ ekuanse dibǝ ɔtɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Bǝ lǝ nte fǝ kase boonǝ ɔsɔnɔdi koto. Uyifo umǝnkplɛ wǝ bato bǝnyǝnsǝ lǝ ntu bɔdibɔdi kpǝ ɔflɔ.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Nya kawunsiǝ eto baka laa labe ko wǝ fe kase utsyuǝ leesu ɔsɔnɔdi ɔflɔ, nya kawunsiǝ eto betidi eto le bua buuyifo te fe kase bǝǝnǝ nte kekpe seka.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ ǝsiǝ lǝ mɛ ǝsuǝ nya ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ lékpa mɛ úsu ko mfiminti. Mfó minyǝ usio utsyuǝ nsi lǝ ubutubuki sia utsyuǝ wǝ aye nya báwɔni bǝtǝkǝ lǝ ubuki ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ kenke diyifo aka itǝ Onanto. Ubuki ǝmǝ kpe esi akuanse ku akpɛ lefosi.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Usio ǝmǝ kpe awu nya diyifo tũũ asiasia kpakpa akpa nya balɔkɔsǝ wǝ ku sika sia ku aba ku atsyabi nya lefo koya nya ɔnlɛ sika sia eto kɔpo lǝ kɔni ko diyi ku asa nnya laanɔ bobe ku asinsa úńyǝ utsyiko lǝ wǝ sɛsɔnɔdidi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Bawɔni lɛkulaye bǝtǝkǝ lǝ wǝ kato nǝ eto kasɔ ni bǝnkǝ Umǝnkplɛ Babilonia, Ɔsɔnɔdi kenke eto ambe ku asa nnya beekisi lǝ kawunsiǝ eto ambe.

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: Nya minyǝ usio ǝmǝ mfo wǝ dikpǝ nte ku Onanto eto betidi eto kɔtɔ ko únǝ ku mba bálo eto kɔtɔ itsyise bǝtǝkǝko Yesu kama eso. Se minyǝ wǝǝ, iyifo mǝ yanii tinti.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Mfo nya ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ lɛtɔmɛ nkǝ, “Beso iyifo fǝ yanii mǝ? Muulǝkǝ akulasa nya nkpe lǝ usio ǝmfo ku ubutubuki esi akuanse ku aakpɛ lefo wǝ ǝsuǝ unsi nte fǝ.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Ubutubuki ǝmfo ébusiǝ nkpǝ uńyǝ, fɛɛ unsisi. Ola nkǝ eni lǝ ɔbɔ utsyi lǝ ukpe eto dibiǝ kplɛ nǝmǝ kafo udu, nya lǝ bátsylɔsǝ wǝ. Betidi ba disiǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ ba eto aye báwɔni bakpe lǝ nkpǝ eto kukue fɛ Onanto diyifo kawunsiǝ, yuuyifo mǝ naa lǝ bǝnyǝ ubutubuki ǝmǝ. Itsyise úsiǝ nkpǝ uńyǝǝ, fɛ unsibǝ, nya usi wuubǝ bio.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: “Nyamfo ahiã itǝ kobe ku kasɔnɔnɔ. Esi akuanse nyamǝ ńyǝ itǝ atini akuanse nya ǝsuǝ usio ǝmǝ lǝǝsiǝ. Beyifo baka ekuanse tsya.

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: Baka ɛnɔ bamǝ ébusiǝ nkpǝ bǝnyǝ, ɔni si lǝ lekpomɛ, nya ɔfɛfɛ mǝmbǝ tɔ. Lǝ ubǝǝ, lefe kliminti hã ete wuusiǝ.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Nya ubutubuki wǝ dísiǝ nkpǝ ibǝ efe fɛ unsisii, wǝ kosate eyifo oka flamflale wǝ ni ekuanse bamǝ eto ɔni wǝ bantɔɔ katsylɔsǝkɔ.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: “Aakpɛ lefosi nya fǝnyǝǝ, eyifo baka lefosi ba eto sekadidi fe dimanyo tɔ. Fɛɛ bootǝ mǝ kusu bedi seka itǝ lebombosɔsɔ lɔni ku ubutubuki ǝmǝ.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Nsusu bɔni ete nkpe baka lefosi bamǝ, nya boofi mǝ ǝsuǝale ku mǝ kɛnyɛbusiǝ bǝtǝ ubutubuki ǝmǝ.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Bɔɔkpɔnko Lesamubi nǝmǝ ku wǝ betidi ba bákpadi, ba ulǝkǝ, mba léfo wǝ banɔ ntǝkǝko wǝ, ebutǝkǝ mǝ kato, itsyise wǝ ni banamute eto Onamute, ku baka eto oka.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ tsya atɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Ntu bo fǝnyǝ bo ǝsuǝ ɔtsyetɔ ǝmǝ nsi, eyifo ese ese, betidi betidi, nle nle ku bele bele.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Aakpɛ lefosi nyamǝ fǝnyǝ ku ubutubuki ǝmǝ, ebukisi otsyetɔnyǝ ǝmǝ. Bɔɔlɛ lesa saa nǝ nte wǝ bafo, usiǝ leyaleya. Bɔɔyimi wǝ ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ nya bootonsǝ sinǝ sia dibu ku ute.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Itsyise Onanto éfi nyamfo okpe mǝ lǝ utu kǝmiǝ nkǝ lǝ beyifo lesa nǝ wǝ ala ǝsuǝ. Nya útǝ nkǝ beyifo bani lǝ befi mǝ sekadidi bǝtǝ ubutubuki ǝmǝ, isu ese lefe nǝ Onanto eto etiki luubǝ itǝ

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: usio wǝ fǝnyǝ mfo, eyifo umǝnkplɛ wǝ nlɛ seka budi lǝ kawunsiǝ eto baka ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, nńyǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe bamba utsyuǝ wǝ ditsyi kato ɔnlɛ kasɔ bubǝ. Okpe ǝsuǝale tinti nya wǝ diyǝnde díyǝnde lékpo lǝ kawinsiǝ kenke ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Ófa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Óba o-o! Babilon wǝ léfo diye sitinti nso aba kenke! Ófiani awɔnɔ bua kenke eto ɔkplɛ eto kabiɔkɔ. Mfó nya baklɔbɛ bua saa eto okle ba bendi kenke nsi nɛ.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Itsyise kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke ǝnǝ wǝ nte bo nkpe ale ini nnya wǝ sɛsɔnɔdidi eto lebua dínǝ éboko. Kawunsiǝ eto baka álabeko wǝ, nya kawunsiǝ eto mba laasa asi léfiani batabinyǝ ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ setsyetɔ kekleke siamǝ ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Nya minsi mínɔ ɔkɔɛ bamba ditsyi kato nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Mɔ betidi, bɛbɔ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ bibǝ! Bǝ lǝ bɛyɛ wǝ abua nnya ɔnlɛ buyifo buyifo. Kase biǝmbɔkɛ lekpake lǝ kotonǝnǝ ko luubǝ lǝ wǝ betidi ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: Itsyise wǝ abua etsyue itǝkǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ itǝ bǝ ísu ítidi taa kato, nya Onanto laakloma wǝ esu bua nyamǝ.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Biyifo wǝ fe kase wuntsya díyifo mi eto kɛnyɛ. Tisǝ wǝ oko sinuǝ lǝ nnya úyifo mi eto kɛnyɛ. Lɛkɔbe nǝ kafo békuti nte bo nkpe ale bǝtǝ fǝǝ, kuti ni sinuǝ ǝtǝ wǝ.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Bitǝ lǝ unyǝ kanyi, lǝ ɔkɛ lǝ lɛwɛnlo ǝsuǝ fe kase inyǝ itǝ, lǝ wǝ kɔlɔkɔ kekleke ku nkpǝ minimini bo údi ǝsuǝ. Itsyise wǝǝsiǝ katɛyi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, ‘Besio eto Oka mini mfo. Minni ɔkɔsiǝ nya membusiǝ nkewi keku kulefe!’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Mmǝ eso bule bo ni kanyinyǝnyǝ ebutu wǝ diyi lɔni. Wuudufǝ lǝ kanyi ka ni bufi, ukpe ku kukpɛnsɛ kplɛ ǝsuǝ. Itsyise Bonamute Onanto wǝ laakponko wǝ lekpoo eyifo ǝsuǝalenyǝ.

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: “Lǝ kawunsiǝ eto baka ba dídinko wǝ sɛsɔnɔ, nya bédi nkpǝ ku wǝ ǝńyǝ ute wǝ nlɛ wǝ botonsǝ eto kuyuǝ ko nlɛ botakaa, boofa bewi nku bǝtǝ wǝ.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Boonyǝ kɔsɛsɛ ke lǝ sikpe sia dímuǝ mǝ lǝ kanyi ka bǝnyǝ ɔnlɛ bunyǝ esoo, batɛyi bǝnkǝ, ‘Mbɔbuɛ! Mbɔbuɛ! Umǝnkplɛ Babilonia! Lebombosɔsɔ lɔni ete béfi bǝnǝ fǝ koto mǝ!’

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: “Mba laasa asi kplɛ ba nkpe lǝ kasɔ kamǝ tsya éwi nku bǝtǝ wǝ, ílǝ mǝ itǝ wǝ itsyise kuutsyuǝ saa ensianya fǝ bike.

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: Kuutsyuǝ saa anya mǝ bike bia ni sika sia ku siflubia, atsyabi ku aba nnya lefo koya, mfia sia, nkufia ku mfia bo lǝǝyǝnde, eyi akpa akpa eto kakle ku nnya laanyi fififi, ku asa nnya béfi atonyi ku eyi nnya lefo koya, bitu ku situnaa ku aba nnya lefo koya béyifo tsya.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: “Mba laasa asi átɛyi bǝnkǝ, ‘Babilonia! Asa sɛɛ nnya fábɛbɛ fǝtǝ ǝsuǝ, nnya lɛbɔ fǝ lǝ ani mmǝ nɛ. Fǝ setabikɛ ku sisuǝlɔ tsya ánya, ensiebunyǝ nnya kulefe.’

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Mba laasa asi ba léfiani batabinyǝ ɛyɛ lǝ biketete ǝsuǝǝ, ebuńyǝ kɔsɛsɛ famfamfa itsyise báte sikpi bǝ bofo lekpa lǝ wǝ kanyi ka ǝsuǝ. Boofa nya buwi kamisǝ ku batǝyi bǝnkǝ,

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: ‘Mbɔbɔɛ! Mbɔbuɛ, umǝnkplɛ wǝ lefo diye. Umǝ wǝ bálɔkɔsɛ ku mfia flubia ku nsia eto aflanka, nya bálɔkɔsɛ wǝ ku sika sia ku aba ku atsyabi nnya lefo koya ni nwǝmfo nɛ.

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ lǝ lebombosɔsɔ lɔni kama, setabi siamfo kenke áfiani abiabia!’ “Ntu eto efue eto beninǝ kumba nyɛ kusu lǝ ntu ǝsuǝ, mba laaka ntu eto efue ku mba laasa asi lǝ ntu eto efue ǝsuǝ ǝkǝnǝ bǝńyǝ kɔsɛsɛ.

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: Nya báfá ku keku se bǝnyǝ umǝ ǝmǝ nlɛ ute bunyǝ eto kuyuǝ, nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, ‘Umǝnkplɛ saa mensikɛ tɔ wǝ lekateko nwǝ mfo!’

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Mfó bákpa utembu bákpo lǝ mǝ esi, béwi kaminsǝku bátɛyi sitinti bǝnkǝ, ‘Mbɔbɔɛ, mbɔbɔɛ boǝtǝ umǝ wǝ lefo diye mfo. Wǝ ni umǝnkplɛ wǝ efue kplɛ nnya laayɛ lǝ lekpo ǝsuǝ eto basate lɛyɛ bakɛ atabi ɛyɛ lǝ wǝ setabikɛ ǝsuǝ. Nya lǝ lebombosɔsɔ lɔni ǝsuǝǝ, áfiani umǝbaba.

english_verse: The heavens should be happy with God's people and apostles and prophets. God has punished her for them.”
translation_verse: O mimba nkpe kato kenke, binyǝ disuǝyuǝ. Binyǝ disuǝyuǝ mi Onanto eto betidi kenke ku wǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ku Kristo eto batɔnkpe! Itsyise Onanto ákponko wǝ lekpo ólo wǝ dikpi lǝ nnya úyifo mi eso!’ ”

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, nńyǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ nkpe ale utsyuǝ dítsyi kobansǝ kplɛ kutsyuǝ nya óto ko ókpe lǝ lekpo katɛyi nkǝ, “Mmǝ ke booto Babilonia eto umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ basɔ lǝ kasɔ bembembe, nya bensiembunyǝ wǝ bio kulefe nɛ.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Nya bensiǝmbɔnɔ kasanku, katlowoe ku nkpatsyaku eto kɔsɔ lǝ fǝ kulefe! Bensiembunyǝ ani eto kɔsi eto uyifoyifo saa lǝ fǝ bio. Nya bensimbɔnɔ ǝkuǝ saa eto kɔsɔ lǝ fǝ bio.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
translation_verse: Bensiembunyǝ kakandiɛ eto buyǝnde lǝ fǝ bio. Nya bensiembɔnɔ kusiǝkpakpa eto ese tsya lǝ fǝ. Fǝ mba laasa asi ni bǝsuǝalenyǝ kenke lǝ kawunsiǝ, nya ɛyɛ lǝ fǝ kasayifoyifo ǝsuǝ fawɛ kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke fakpe!

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Bǝnǝ Babilonia eto umǝ koto itsyise bǝnyǝ Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ku Onanto eto betidi ku mba kenke bálo lǝ kasɔ mfo eto kɔtɔ ko bákpo lǝ kasɔ mfo eso!”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: Lǝ nyamfo eto kamaa, nnɔ kudu kplɛ ko lele sitiditu kplɛ eto kɔsɔ kato ko nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Atɛsǝ itǝ Onanto! Bofokǝtǝ, diyǝnde ku ǝsuǝale eyifo bo Onanto eto ale!

english_verse: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
translation_verse: Itsyise woakpo lekpo lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝ, kala etiki kǝlǝkǝ lǝ kusu tsyɔntsyɔ ǝsuǝ. Olo otsyetɔsio bua wǝ nlɛ kawunsiǝ bobiasǝ ku wǝ sɛsɔnɔdi dikpi. Onanto ǝnǝ wǝ koto itsyise ólo Onanto eto beyifoyifo.”

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Nya besi báfa bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Atɛsǝ itǝ Onanto! Kuyuǝ ko nlɛ bɔbɔ ketsyi lǝ ute wǝ létonsǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ lɛ bɔbɔ isu manlo manlo!”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Ete ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ lɛbɔ útsyi lekankpomɛ nǝmǝ ɔflɔ lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Bɛtɛsǝ bo Onanto, mi wǝ beyifoyifo, ku betidi ba ta, bakplɛnyǝ ku betidiwewe kenke ba laate wǝ sikpi.”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, nnɔ ɔkɔɛ utsyuǝ fe sitiditu eto ole nye nwǝ lele ketu eto ntu bo nlɛ boto ketsyi ketsyue kato kakpo lǝ ditumbu ǝsuǝ, nye fe ofamfa wǝ lɛbɔ utsyi kato seka, wǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Atɛsǝ itǝ Onanto! Itsyise Bonamute, bo Onanto Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke, ni oka.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Bitǝ lǝ bunyǝ disuǝyuǝ, bobesǝ wǝ kato, nya bufi atɛsǝ butǝ wǝ! Itsyise Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto kusiǝkpakpa eto diyi nǝmǝ eboyo, nya wǝ kusiǝ komǝ dílɔ wǝ ǝsuǝ utsyǝ itǝ diyi nǝmǝ.

english_verse: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
translation_verse: Béfi aklala flubia nnya lele eto awu nnya nlɛ buyǝnde bǝtǝ wǝ bǝ okpe.” Nya aklala flubia nnya lele ǝmǝǝ, eyifo Onanto eto betidi eto asa sɛɛ yifoyifo.

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: Mfó nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Wɔni nyamfo ǝtsyǝ. Disuǝyuǝ itǝ mba bákpo báboko Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto kusiǝkakpakɔ eto lesakedikɔ.” Nya ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ disi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nyamfo eyifo Onanto kosate eto kɛnyɛ eto etiki.”

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Mfó mínse akonki lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ miǝ lǝ nsumu wǝ, fɛ ɔtɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Menyifo nko! Uyifoyifo ete mini fe fǝ ke ku fǝ bayimi ba léfo Yesu eto ditiki bánɔ ke. Onanto ete ele bǝ busumu!” Itsyise ɔnukualɛ eto asa nnya Yesu dílǝkǝ ótee, nnya ni lesa nǝ lékpe Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi nkɛale nɛ. Yohanes Ǝnyǝ Nwǝ Nsi Lǝ Ɔtɔmli Flubia Ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, nnyǝ se kato lɛsini kenke nya mínyǝ ɔtɔmli flubia utsyuǝ. Baakpo nwǝ nsi lǝ wǝ bǝ Nwǝ leeyifo asa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ ku Ɔnukualɛnyǝ. Woakpo lekpo kayɛnko lǝ kusu tsyɔntsyɔ ǝsuǝ woakpɔ lemma kayɛnko lǝ kusu tsyɔntsyɔ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: Wǝ ǝnǝmi éyifo fe ute wǝ nlɛ buńyǝ nya unti akankpakpa kpǝ lǝ wǝ disi. Báwɔni diye ditsyuǝ bǝtǝkǝ lǝ wǝ nǝ kuutsyuǝ saa lenyi enso wǝ kosate hã.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: Ókpé awu nnya bédi bákpé lǝ kɔtɔ ǝsuǝ. Wǝ diye ni Onanto Eto Ditiki Nǝmǝ.

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: Nya kato eto bamamanyǝ ba nkpe awu nya lɛlɛkɛ iflubi nnya ele, nya bensi lǝ batɔmli flubia ǝsuǝ ntǝkǝko wǝ kama.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Útsyi lesiabi nyɛ nnuǝ yayaya lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ nǝ wuufi ɔkpɔnko, ese ese utǝkǝ nnya kato. Wuufi situ eto uyi udi seka lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ, nya wɔɔnwɔ wain eto ebibi ulǝkǝ nte lǝ mu kawakɔ lǝ Onanto Ǝsuǝalenyǝ Kenke eto lefonyi ku lɛblɔfi kekleke ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Nya báwɔni diye ditsyuǝ lǝ wǝ awu nnya onkpe ku lǝ lekpabu ǝsuǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, Baka eto Oka ku Banamute eto Onamute.

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: Lǝ nnya kamaa, nnyǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ wǝ díńyǝ lǝ kofe ǝsuǝ, ófa bembembe ókpadi baklɔbɛ ba kenke leefunini kato ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bibǝ bɛkɔ lǝ bidi Onanto eto ditsyitsyosa kplɛ nǝmǝ!

english_verse: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Bibǝ bɛyimi baka, bamamanyǝ ninǝ, bamamanyǝ, batɔmini ku betidi ba lǝǝsiǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ, betidi ba kenke, ndomufɔ ku mba nsi lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ ǝsuǝ, bakplɛnyǝ ku betidiwewe eto ǝsuǝ eto sinǝ!”

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Nya mínyǝ ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku kasɔ mfo eto baka ku mǝ bamamanyǝ lɛkɔ bátaka lema lǝ Nwǝ nsi lǝ ɔtɔmini ku wǝ bamamanyǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ bǝmuǝ ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku bidiabidia eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi wǝ díyifo ayanisa ǝmǝ lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi. Ayanisa nyamfó béfi bawɛ betidi ba ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ eto leba nteke ku mba lésumu ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ eto ditsyǝbiwenwe bákpé. Béfi ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku bidiabidia eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi bamǝ báto bakpe ku nkpǝ lǝ ute eto aba nya nlɛ bunyǝ lǝ ditumbu klinklaa kplɛ nǝmǝ kafo.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: Nya nwǝ nsi lǝ ɔtɔmini ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ lélo bamamanyǝ fɛfɛ ku lesiabi nǝ lɛbɔ ditsyi lǝ nwǝ nsi lǝ ɔtɔmi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ nya baklɔbɛ bamǝ kenke díbǝ bétsyuebi sinǝ sia ke boofo butsyuebi kamini.

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nnyǝ akankpomɛ ǝtsyuǝ, nya bǝtǝ mba nsi lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ ǝsuǝale bǝ bakpo akpo. Mǝ ni betidi ba bálo eto awɔnɔ se bédi adansiɛ bǝtǝ Yesu ku Onanto eto Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ eso. Báni bénsumu ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku wǝ ditsyǝbi wenwe nye bǝtǝkǝ wǝ leba lǝ mǝ nto nye mǝ ani. Bátaka bǝsiǝ nkpǝ nya bédi seka ku Kristo itǝ alɛ kɔkpi.

english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Diyuǝ ku Onanto eto atsyɔkpokpo kplɛ itǝ betidi ba dínyǝ kedikɔ lǝ bekpenkpe eto botaka kǝńyǝ tutɛ bomfo kǝmiǝ. Ukpe nnuǝfǝ ǝmbɔkɛ ǝsuǝale lǝ mǝ, bomu booyifo Onanto ku Kristo eto besumunyǝ nya buudi seka ku wǝ itǝ alɛ kɔkpi. Bǝtǝkǝ Obonsam Kato.

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: Nya wuusu kawunsiǝ eto akpɔ ana nyamǝ kenke usu ɔwɛ betidi okpé, ba ni Gog ku Magog. Wookpa mǝ utsyǝ ditsyu itǝ lema bɔkpɔ, nya boofui fe lekpo eto kɔwunimi.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Bésense bakpo lǝ kawunsiǝ kenke nya bátsyalama kaka Onanto eto betidi nsi wǝ ni wǝ umǝkplɛ lala ǝmǝ. Fɛɛ ute áyifǝ utsyi kato úbǝ ótonsǝ mǝmblɛ kenke.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Nya Obonsam ǝmǝ lɛwɛ mǝ ókpee, béfi wǝ báto bákpe lǝ ute ku ute bambi eto ditumbu klinklaa kplɛ nǝmǝ kafo kaka báto ubutubuki bua ǝmǝ ku bidiabidia eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi ǝmǝ bákpe. Mfó boonyǝ kanyi ketsye ku kakɔmi itǝ manlo manlo. Onanto Ákponko Kawunsiǝ Kenke Lekpo.

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: Nya kamaa, nnyǝ lekankpomɛ kplɛ flubia ditsyuǝ ku nwǝ lǝǝsiǝ lǝ ni. Kasɔ ku kato étsyetsyi bánya lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi nya bésinyǝ mǝ bio.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: Nya mínyǝ bekpǝnkpe kpǝ, bakplɛnyǝ ku betidiwewe ńyǝ lǝ lekankpomɛ nǝmǝ eto ǝnǝmi. Básini ekue, nya besi básini kukue kɔni, ko ni mba nsi nkpǝ eto kukue. Bákponko bekpenkpe bamǝ lekpo, utsyuǝ saa lǝ kase úsiǝ nkpǝ fe kase báwɔni bákpe lǝ ekue nyamǝ kafo.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: Lekpo ǝlǝkǝ bekpenkpe ba nkpe lǝ ni óboko, nya ukpe ku ɔkɔmǝ tsya dílǝkǝ bekpenkpe ba nkpe lǝ mǝ bǝtǝ. Nya Onanto lékponko utsyuǝ sa lekpo ɛyɛ mǝ eyifosa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Nnya kamaa, béfi ukpe ku ɔkɔmǝ báto bákpe lǝ ute eto ditumbu klinklaa kplɛ nǝmǝ kǝmiǝ. Ute eto ditumbu klinkla kplɛ nǝmǝ ni ukpǝ nuǝfǝ nɛ.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ eto diye báwɔni bákpe lǝ nkpǝ eto kukue komǝ kafoo, béfi wǝ báto bákpe lǝ ute eto ditumbu klinklaa nǝmǝ kafo.

english_verse: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, nnyǝ kato fɔfɔ ku kasɔ fɔfɔ. Itsyise kato tutɛ ku kasɔ tutɛ ébubǝ kafe nya lekpo tsya lénya.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Nya mínɔ ɔkɔɛ kplɛ utsyuǝ lɛbɔ utsyi lekankpomɛ nǝmǝ ɔflɔ wǝ nlɛ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Bebee! Mɔmɔɔ, Onanto eto kǝsiǝkɔ ébubǝ kakpé lǝ betidi nti mfo. Wuusiǝ lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ nya boofiani wǝ betidi. Onanto kosate ebusiǝko mǝ nya wuuyifo mǝ Onanto.

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: Nya wɔɔsi mǝ binǝntu kenke lǝ ǝnǝmi. Nya ukpe tsya ensiǝmbɔkɛ. Nko ke ini itǝ bolo kǝsiǝ, nku buwi ku kanyinyǝnyǝ tsya nɛ. Akokosa kenke ébubǝ efe.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Ete nwǝ nsi lǝ lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Bebee! Mbusinkli nyifo asa nnya kenke afɔfɔ.” Nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Wɔni nyamfo ǝtsyǝ, itsyise iyifo etiki nnya diyifo sitinti nya utsyuǝ saa loofo bofo kanɔ.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Lesa saa aflɔ. Mɔ ni ututɛ ku ɔfɛfɛ, ketsyikokɔ ku kalosǝkɔ. Utsyuǝ saa ke utukɔɛ nlɛɛ, muutǝ wǝ kusu unǝ nkpǝ eto ntu bo laayifǝ ketsyi ditubiǝ ǝsuǝ nya wǝmbofo kayo saa.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa ke ditǝkǝ kato ebofo nyamfo utsyiko mɔ ɔflɔ. Muuyifo wǝ Onanto nya wuuyifo mɔ ubi.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Fɛɛ betidi ba laate asa sikpi, mba leedu bǝtsyuǝ kǝtǝ, mba leeyifo asinsa, mba laalo betidi ku batsyetɔnyǝ ku bafate, ku mba leesumu bǝfiǝ, ku badia kenke, eto kǝsiǝkɔ ni ditumbu klikla kplɛ nǝ nlɛ bunyǝ ku ute eto aba kafo. Nyamfo ni ukpe nuǝfǝ nɛ.” Yerusalem Fɔfɔ Ǝmǝ.

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Nya kamaa, Onanto eto batɔnkpe nlɛ ɛkpɛ ekuanse ka díyi ku kanyinyǝnyǝ ka nkpe kɛnyɛale ekuanse fɛfɛ eto ɔni ǝbǝ mɛ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Bǝ, lǝ nte fǝ Okafɔ wǝ ni Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto kusiǝ.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ áfo disiǝ lǝ mɛ nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ ditsyi mɛ usuko kɔtini ko letaka fefe eto kato. Óte mɛ Yerusalem, umǝnkplɛ kɛnkɛ ǝmǝ lɛbɔ utsyi Onanto ɔflɔ kato ɔnlɛ kasɔ bubǝ.

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: Nya ɔnlɛ buyǝnde ku Onanto eto diyǝnde. Umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ ǝyǝnde fe leba nǝ lefo koya tinti. Dite fe Yaspa eto leba seka nya lekpa wuee fe ahuhɔɛ seka.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Báto ofati wǝ ditidi nya otaka bátsyilama wǝ, nya bákpe lefosi mbɔsie nnuǝ lǝ wǝ. Nya Onanto eto lefosi batɔnkpe inuǝ ńyǝ lǝ mu nnyɛ. Báwɔni Israel eto lefosi atobi ǝnuǝ nyamǝ eto aye bǝtǝkǝ lǝ nsie bomǝ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Nsie ntsyǝ lǝ kofekatokɔ, ntsyǝ lǝ ɔnwɛ kato, ntsyǝ nkpe lǝ ɔnwɛ dikue nya ntsyǝ nkpe lǝ diyo kedufǝkɔ eti tsya.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Bákpe lefosi aba ǝnuǝ lǝ kasɔ fɛ báto umǝ ǝmǝ eto kafati bǝńyǝnsǝ. Nya báwɔni Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto batɔnkpe lefosi inuǝ bamǝ eto aye bǝtǝkǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ dídinko mɛ etikii, lɛ sika sia eto esususa nnya wuufi ususu umǝ ǝmǝ ku wǝ nsie bomǝ ku kafati lǝ kɔni.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: Akpɔ ana nkpe umǝ ǝmǝ, nya wǝ akpake eto kekulǝ ku lɛblɛbe lekate. Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ ésusu umǝ ǝmǝ ku esususa nyamǝ, nya unyǝ nkǝ umǝ ǝmǝ eto lekpa lɔni ekulǝ esunkpo kɔkpi alofa anɔ. Nya umǝ ǝmǝ eto dikulǝ, lɛblɛbe ku botaka akate pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about
translation_verse: Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ ésusu ofati ǝmǝ tsya, nya únyǝ nkǝ wǝ ditidibi eyifo akpa alofa ǝnuǝ lefosi akpa akua ɛyɛ lǝ esususa nnya ɔnlɛ ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Aba nnya béfi báto ofati ǝmǝ ni yaspa, nya béfi sika sia nso bɔlɛkɛ, sia lekpa fe ahuhuɛ seka báto umǝ ǝmǝ kosate.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Nya béfi aba nya léfo koya akpa akpa bálɔ umǝ ǝmǝ eto aba nnya kasɔ ǝsuǝ báto kafati kamǝ bǝtǝkǝ. Kasɔ eto leba tutɛ ni nǝ baakpo yaspa. Dinuǝfǝ ni nǝ baakpo sapir. Ditsyǝfǝ ni nǝ baakpo yalkedon, nya lenale ni nǝ baakpo smaragdo.

english_verse: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
translation_verse: Lɛnɔnde ni nǝ baakpo sardonik, nya lekuande baakpo sardis. Akuansele baakpo krisolit, nya leflamflale baakpo beril. Lɛwɔnɔmbǝle baakpo topas. Lefosile baakpo krisopra. Lefosi ɔnwɛ baakpo yanikint, nya lefosi unuǝfǝ baakpo ametist.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
translation_verse: Nya mǝnnyǝ Disumuyo saa lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ, itsyise Bonamute Onanto Ǝsuǝalenyǝ ǝmǝ ku Lesamubi nǝmǝ ni wǝ Disumuyo.

english_verse: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
translation_verse: Kofe nye kofande eto bofa nye buyǝnde manfe itǝ umǝ ǝmǝ, itsyise Onanto eto diyǝnde ǝǝyǝnde kakpo wǝ nya Lesamubi nǝmǝ ni wǝ okandiɛ.

english_verse: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
translation_verse: Kawunsiǝ eto betidi ebɔyɛ lǝ ni buyǝnde ǝsuǝ, nya kasɔ eto baka ebufi mǝ setabikɛ baboko wǝ.

english_verse: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
translation_verse: Nya bembuti umǝ ǝmǝ eto nsie ku lefe, itsyise kale tsya embona lǝ wǝ.

english_verse: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
translation_verse: Ese, ese eto betidi kenke ebufi mǝ sɛkplɛ ku mǝ setabikɛ bǝbǝ baboko mfó.

english_verse: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kɔfiɔ eto kulesa embedufǝ lǝ wǝ kulefe. Nya badia ku mba leeyifo afanisa embudufǝ lǝ wǝ. Bomu mba eto aye báwɔni bákpe lǝ Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto nkpǝ eto kukue komǝ kafoo, mǝ hã luudufǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ nɛ.

english_verse: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
translation_verse: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ áte mɛ ketu ka lǝǝtǝ nkpǝ eto ntu. Ketu kamǝ aabɔdi ketsyi lǝ Onanto ku Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ. Ntu bomǝ akpa kɛnkɛnkɛ fe ahuhuɛ.

english_verse: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
translation_verse: Nya bɔnlɛ bɔbɔdi lǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ eto obia kplɛ. Nya lǝ ke etukpenkpe ǝnuǝ nyamǝ ǝsuǝǝ, nkpǝ eto kuyi ńyǝ, nya kuanwɔ kofa saa kofa. Ko afanto eyifo afantosi itǝ ese ese eto betidi kenke.

english_verse: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
translation_verse: Bembunyǝ kulesa nǝ Onanto díkudi lǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ. Onanto ku Lesamubi nǝmǝ eto lekankpomɛ ebɔkɛ lǝ umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ, nya wǝ beyifoyifo luusumu wǝ.

english_verse: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
translation_verse: Boonyǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi nya wǝ diye luutǝkǝ lǝ mǝ nto.

english_verse: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
translation_verse: Kale ensi embolo mfó. Nya bembohiã kakandiɛ nye kofe, itsyise Bonamute Onanto ebudi mǝ diyǝnde nya boodi seka isu manlo manlo. Yesu Eto Dibǝfe

english_verse: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
translation_verse: Mfó nya Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Ɔnukualɛ eto etiki ni nyamfo nya boofo nya bofo kanɔ sitiniti. Nya Bonamute Onanto wǝ leefi wǝ Lɛwɔnɔ kǝtǝ wǝ bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi, wǝ ni létsyese wǝ ɔtɔnkpe nkǝ ote wǝ beyifoyifo asa nnya lǝǝbǝ bubǝ lǝ lefe kliminti ǝsuǝ.”

english_verse: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
translation_verse: “Bebe! Muubǝ lǝ lefe kliminti ǝsuǝ. Disuǝyuǝ itǝ mba leetsyue etiki nnya bábe letsya lǝ kukue komfo kǝmiǝ koto.”

english_verse: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
translation_verse: Mɔ Yohanes, mɔ ni nwǝ lɛnɔ nya mínyǝ asa nyamfo kenke. Nya lefe nǝ mínyǝ nya mínɔ nnya mínlosǝǝ, nse akonki lǝ Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe wǝ dílǝkǝ asa nyamfo óte mɛ eto akɔ miǝ lǝ nsumu wǝ.

english_verse: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Menyifo nko! Mintsyaa, uyifotsyuǝ mini fe fǝ ku fǝ bayimi ba ni Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi ku mba kenke leetsyue etiki nnya nkpe lǝ kukue komfo koto. Onanto mate ete ele bǝ sumu.”

english_verse: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi mɛ nkǝ, “Menfi bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi eto etiki nnya báwɔni bákpe lǝ kukue komfo akula, itsyise lefe nǝmǝ eboyo lelo nyamfo kenke luubǝ itǝ.

english_verse: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
translation_verse: Utsyuǝ saa ke leeyifo asa buaa, sukosǝ lǝ ukeyifo asa bua, nwǝ ke leeyifo asinsa sukosǝ lǝ ukeyifo asinsa, nwǝ kee leeyifo kasɛɛ, sukosǝ lǝ ukeyifo kasɛɛ, nwǝ kee diyifo kɛnkɛ, usukosǝ lǝ uyifo nwǝ diyifo kɛnkɛ.”

english_verse: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
translation_verse: Yesu nkǝ, “Bebe! Muubǝ lǝ lefe kliminti ǝsuǝ! Muubǝ ku kayofofo ka dińyǝ kǝtǝ utsyuǝ saa lǝ keyifo ka úyifo kumǝ.

english_verse: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
translation_verse: Mɔ ni Ututɛ ku Ɔfɛfɛ, Kasɔ Ketsyikokɔ ku Kaalosǝkɔ.

english_verse: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
translation_verse: “Disuǝyuǝ itǝ betidi ba dumuǝ ǝsuǝ bǝtsyǝ fe kase bǝǝflǝ bikǝ kɛnkɛ kǝtsyǝ. Mǝǝ dunyǝ kusu bedi nkpǝ eto kuyi komǝ eto eyibibi nɛ, nya bɔɔyɛ lǝ kesie kamǝ bedufǝ lǝ kato eto umǝnkplɛ ǝmǝ lafo.

english_verse: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
translation_verse: Nya mba luunyǝ lǝ ɔkpɔ ǝmǝ kama ba ni mba leeyifo afanisa, mǝ ni betidi ba ni bafate, mba ni batsyetɔ, mba laalo betidi, mba lǝǝtǝ bǝfiǝ kedi ku betidi ba ni badia ba laawɛ betidi kakpe lǝ etiki ku lǝ eyifosa ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
translation_verse: “Mɔ, Yesu, ni nwǝ létsyese mɔ ɔtɔnkpe miǝ lǝ ubǝ ulǝkǝ asa nyamfo ote mi bafokanɔfɔ eto etsyukpa kenke. Mɔ ni kuyi eto ote kplɛ ǝmǝ wǝ ni Oka David eto ufluflu. Mɔ ni kolensiǝnto eto sefandebi sia lǝǝyǝnde.”

english_verse: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
translation_verse: Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ ku okafɔ ǝmǝ nkǝ, “Bǝ!” Nya nwǝ ke nlɛ nnya bɔnɔ tsya tɛyi nkǝ, “Bǝ!” Nya nwǝ ke utukɔɛ nlɛɛ, ubǝ. Nya nwǝ ke nlɛ ntu bo lǝǝtǝ nkpǝ bɔbɛbɛɛ, ubǝ ofo mu unǝ, kayo saa mamfo mamfo.

english_verse: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
translation_verse: Mɔ Yohanes lɛ utsyuǝ saa wǝ laanɔ etiki nnya nkpe lǝ kukue komfo kafoo ato bukǝ kekleke miǝ utsyuǝ saa ke luufi lesatsyuǝ okpé lǝ nnya ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto ebufi kanyinyǝnyǝ ka báwɔni bakpé lǝ kukue komfo utǝkǝ wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ fe kotonǝnǝ.

english_verse: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
translation_verse: Nya lǝ utsyuǝ tsya ǝlǝkǝ lesatsyuǝ utsyiko lǝ nnya ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto embutǝ unyǝ kayofofo ka báwɔni bákpe lǝ kukue komfo ǝsuǝ. Weebunyǝ kedikɔ ka untǝnyǝ lǝ nkpǝ eto kudiyi komǝ ǝsuǝ ku lǝ umǝ kɛnkɛ ǝmǝ tsya ǝsuǝ.

english_verse: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
translation_verse: Nwǝ nlɛ adansiɛ budi lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝ lǝ bɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Ee, muubǝ lǝ leefe kliminti ǝsuǝ!” Ibǝ itǝ. Bonamute Yesu, bǝ!

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_verse: Bonamute Yesu eto lefonǝ lǝ disiǝko utsyuǝ saa.
